title: MACA: Multi-Axis Covariance Alignment for Robust Distillation of Self-Supervised Speech Models
authors: Dong-Hyun Kim, Jae-Hong Lee, Joon-Hyuk Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has become a standard paradigm for speech representation learning, but modern SSL encoders are computationally expensive, motivating compression for resource-limited environments. Knowledge distillation (KD) has emerged as a promising solution to this; however, standard point-wise feature regression is under-constraining and fails to preserve the teacher's representational geometry. Relation-based distillation adds self-similarity constraints, yet it still captures only a partial view of second-order structure and may amplify spurious correlations under low-resource distillation or domain shift. In this work, we revisit speech SSL distillation from a probabilistic perspective and interpret KD as distribution alignment between teacher and student representations, which decomposes into first-order mean matching and second-order covariance matching. Since full covariance alignment over vectorized sequences is intractable, we propose multi-axis covariance alignment (MACA), a principled extension of relation-based KD that tractably aligns temporal and feature covariances under a matrix-normal surrogate. Concretely, MACA aligns intra-layer covariance matrices by separately matching their diagonal variance and off-diagonal covariance components for stable conditioning and additionally matches inter-layer cross-covariances between consecutive layers to transfer depth-wise dependency structure. To stabilize training, we further introduce an adaptive min–max weighting scheme that balances the second-order alignment terms. Extensive experiments on SUPERB with multiple SSL teacher backbones show consistent gains over representative distillation baselines, especially under low-resource distillation and domain shifts. Analyses of covariance discrepancy and eigenspectra demonstrate the empirical relevance of covariance alignment, while representation autocorrelation and weight-space loss landscapes indicate reduced spurious correlation amplification and flatter, better-centered local geometry, supporting improved generalization.

title: Automation of the Formation and Assessment of Educational Assignments Using the GigaChat Neural Network Model in Engineering Education
authors: Vasiliy Kochetkov, Sergey Petrov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The development of digital technologies stimulates the introduction of intelligent systems into the educational activities of technical universities. One of the promising directions is the automation of the formation and assessment of educational assignments using large-scale language models. This paper describes a prototype system that uses the GigaChat neural network model and an interaction interface implemented via a Telegram bot. The architecture of the solution, as well as mechanisms for question generation and evaluation of student’s answers, are presented. The advantages of integrating LLM technologies into engineering education and directions for further development are identified.

title: A Robust Deep Learning Framework for Automated Digital Image Forgery Detection Under Real-World Post-Processing Attacks
authors: K. Lakshmi, Boya Uday Kumar, Karanam Venkata Raghava, Aashir Musaid Arcot, Boya Mallikarjuna, Varikuntam Sravani
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The active development of image editing software and social media has contributed to the threat of image manipulation in a greater way, and trustworthy forgery detection is one of the key tasks of multimedia forensic research. Although deep learning techniques have enhanced detection accuracy, most of the currently existing methods solve the problem based only on spatial features and do not perform well in images subjected to real-world post-processes which affect the observable information as compression, blurring, and noise additions. To solve such shortcomings, the present study develops a powerful two-domain deep learning framework, which incorporates both spatial and frequency domain features to understand automated image forgery. It is represented as the spatial branch using ResNet50v2 with an augmented attention mechanism to highlight manipulation done, and the frequency branch using Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) coefficients to help identify compression artifacts. The combination of complementary cues when switching between domains (via a feature fusion strategy) and the simulation of typical distortions introduced by post-processing training (robustness-based training) is simulated. Test on CASIA v1, CASIA v2, and Columbia datasets reveal that the proposed approach has an accuracy of 98.9 percent on CASIA v2 that is more than 2.5 percent higher than single-branch CNN baselines. With extremely strong JPEG compression (quality factor 50), the suggested model will fall by a mere 3.1% in performance, whereas conventional CNN detectors will lose over 12% of their performance. Cross-dataset testing also indicates better generalization with accuracy on the unseen data 93.4. Such findings suggest that dual-domain learning and attention-driven refinement can be effectively combined to create a solid and viable application in real-world digital image detection of forgery.

title: Mitigating Latency and Spectral Inefficiency in 5G New Radio via Intelligent Queue-Driven Resource Scheduling
authors: Ali Amer Ahmed Alrawi, Mustafa Maad Hamdi, Khattab M. Ali
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Fifth-generation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex>) New Radio networks must support HetNet services with opposing QoS requirements at the same time. These include ultra-reliable low-latency communications (URLLC), enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB), and massive machine-type communications (mMTC). Schedulers in conventional systems depend on a myopic or static resource allocation policy. Therefore, they cannot adapt efficiently to quick traffic and channel dynamics that result in large delays before the system becomes highly loaded with significant inefficiency of spectral utilization. An adaptive radio resource management framework is proposed for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 G$</tex> downlink scheduling with queue dynamics, delay constraints, power consumption and spectral efficiency considered jointly. A multiobjective optimization model that captures the trade-off between user latency and system-level efficiency forms an objective function of this scheduler. Owing to non-convexities introduced thereby, the scheduler is formulated as a Markova decision process implemented by means of learning based adaptive policy. Extensive simulation using MATLAB under heterogeneous traffic proves that average URLLC latency achieved (over all users)by proposed scheme up to 37 % lower compared to proportional fair baseline where in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5}^{\text {th }}$</tex> percentile tail latency suppressed by about 40 %.In addition it realizes more than 16 % gain on spectral efficiency while <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex> nearly 6 % improvement in fairness at dense network scenarios. Intelligent queue aware can address one key solvable weakness in the 5G systems without any physical layer change or extra spectrum.

title: Reevaluating CNN Filter Dimensions: Experimental Findings on CIFAR-10 and Fashion-MNIST for Modern Network Architectures
authors: Annapoorani S, Anna Anbumozhi, Subashini B, Dhanalakshmi J
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Contemplating Convolutional Filter Sizes in CNNs: A CIFAR-10 and Fashion-MNIST Case Study and Implications for Modern Architectures. This study examined the convolutional filter size a major hyperparameter in order to have a better understanding of the effects on performance and efficiency of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and especially in image classification tasks. The study used a simple controlled CNN with different convolutional filter sizes (3×3, 5×5 and 7×7) and collected data from the dataset, CIFAR-10 and Fashion-MNIST. The data collected showed a significant inverse relationship between accuracy and convolutional filter size. The smallest filter size (3×3) achieved both the best test accuracy on CIFAR-10 (73.04%) and Fashion-MNIST (92.68%). Larger filter sizes (5×5 and 7×7) resulted in more computation time to train and higher computation costs. This study illustrates some trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency, but supports the use of small kernels in well-known and more modern architectures (i.e. VGG and ResNet) and the more notional explanation/concept of using parameterized convolutional architecture. Therefore, the findings from this study can inform the progress in the choice of kernel sizes based on accuracy or efficiency or both.

title: Remaining Useful Life Prediction Based on Nonlinear Tweedie Exponential Dispersion Process Considering Random Effects, Covariates, and Imperfect Maintenance
authors: Yaohui Lu, Shaoping Wang, Rentong Chen, Chao Zhan, Jiashan Gao, Yuwei Zhang, Rui Mu
abstract: Stochastic process models can capture the stochastic dynamics, and they have been widely applied in component degradation modeling. However, the model uncertainty may lead to inaccurate and unreliable prediction of the remaining useful life (RUL). In addition, time-varying working conditions and maintenance should be incorporated into the degradation model to accurately determine the component degradation level. To solve the aforementioned problem, a generalized degradation model, based on the nonlinear Tweedie exponential dispersion process (TEDP) considering random effects, covariates, and imperfect maintenance, is developed. Firstly, the nonlinear TEDP model with random parameters is proposed to construct the component degradation model. Considering the time-varying operating environment, the effect of the continuous and discrete covariates on the component degradation is modeled based on the proportional hazards model. The effect of the imperfect maintenance is also incorporated into the component degradation model. Then, mean-field variational inference, stochastic gradient variational inference, and Expectation-Maximum (EM) algorithms are used together to estimate the unknown parameters in order to improve the mathematical interpretability of the developed models. Finally, simulation studies and two real cases on GaAs laser and hydraulic pump are used to demonstrate the effectiveness and validity of the proposed model and algorithm.

title: A Multilingual Voice-First Generative AI Framework for Financial Literacy in Low-Resource Communities using Lightweight LLMs
authors: Sreesh Raghavendra, Hemanth Sankar, Deeban Chakravarthy V
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Vast sections of low-income society in India are financially excluded, primarily due to lack of literacy, language skills and access to credible sources of financial advice. We present a voice-based, financial literacy coaching system aimed at low-income, low-literate, semi-literate users in rural and semi-urban India. The system provides dialogue support in five Indian languages, with automatic language identification, and does not require users to be able to read or type. It has two well-defined modes of operation: one in which real-time, actionable, easy-to-understand financial advice is dispensed about savings, loans and expenditures, and another in which users can train themselves in making good financial decisions by exploring the advisor’s responses to simulated difficult financial scenarios like crop failure, unplanned medical expenses, seasonal variations in income. The system consists of a Context Extraction Module, a Hybrid Scenario Generation and Reasoning Controller that enforces rule-based, deterministic constraints during scenario navigation but also uses LLMs for flexible dialog management, and Explanation Generation engine that provides easy-to-understand answers to user queries about why certain financial advice is being given. The Advice and Scenario modes of operation of this system are sharply different from other financial advice-giving chatbots that operate only in a question-answer setting.

title: An Agentic AI for Personalized Investment Advisory: Detecting and Mitigating Cognitive Biases in Financial Decisions
authors: Subashri Vazhai Parathan, Aditya Padmanabhan, R Thamizhamuthu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Cognitive biases greatly hinder retail investor's ability to make decisions, leading to huge financial losses that total billions of dollars every year across global markets. Traditional robo-advisors and conversational AI systems give the same investment advice every time, but they don't really get to the root of the problem: the behavioral patterns that lead to bad financial decisions. These current systems view every interaction with a user as a separate event. They give general advice without considering each user's unique psychological profile or learning from past decisions. This study presents an innovative agentic artificial intelligence framework that surpasses traditional reactive advisory models by integrating enduring behavioral memory, predictive action modeling, and dynamically tailored intervention strategies. The architecture we suggest uses specialized agents that work together to find cognitive biases in real time, predict how likely it is that biased behaviors will happen in the future, and give each investor personalized advice based on their unique behavioral fingerprint. The system keeps an eye on what users do with their investments around the clock, builds bias profiles that change over time, and makes probabilistic predictions about how people will make decisions in different market situations. The system helps people find and overcome their cognitive limits by using advanced counterfactual reasoning and emotionally intelligent communication strategies. This research aids in the integration of behavioral economics, autonomous AI systems, and human computer interaction studies, laying the groundwork for future financial advisory platforms that serve as enduring behavioral coaches instead of mere transactional information sources.

title: Knowledge Management Processes and Their Impact on Organizational Innovation for Enabling Artificial Intelligence Applications
authors: Ihsan Mesri, Basim Faisal Abdul Nayef, Abdulrahman Mustafa Taha
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study aims to identify the level of knowledge management (knowledge identification, knowledge generation, knowledge storage, and knowledge distribution) among male and female principals in Ministry of Education Governorate from their perspective. To identify the level of administrative creativity (problem-solving, adaptability, and risk-taking) among male and female principals in Ministry of Education Governorate from their perspective. To explore the relationship between knowledge management and its dimensions (knowledge identification, knowledge generation, knowledge storage, and knowledge distribution and administrative creativity among male and female school principals. The researcher adopted the descriptiveanalytical approach in conducting the study, as this approach is suitable for the nature and objectives of the study. In light of the study's findings, the researcher recommends and suggests administrators in public schools must safeguard the information they acquire and share it with all their colleagues to benefit from it during their work. Adopting a team-based approach to performing tasks both inside and outside the school ensures the utilization of the individual experiences and knowledge of all team members. School administrations should work to attract creative teachers, support them, and provide them with all necessary facilities, given their impact on knowledge management processes within the school. The necessity of knowledge sharing among teachers themselves, and between teachers and their administrators, through brainstorming sessions and discussion groups. Motivating teachers to perform their duties in new and creative ways that ensure effectiveness and prevent boredom from repeating the same routine teaching procedures.

title: A Holistic Optimization and Control Framework of Hydrogen and Battery-based Remote-Area Renewable Energy System with Grid Frequency Support
authors: Haoxin Dong, Haonan Sun, Bo Zhao, Nian Liu
abstract: Remote-area wind–photovoltaic bases are increasingly connected to load centers through voltage-source-converter high-voltage direct-current (VSC-HVDC) links and are expected to co-produce green hydrogen. In such systems, weak interconnection, frequent transitions between grid-connected and islanded operation, and converter-dominated dynamics make frequency security a coupled planning-and-control problem rather than a pure control-tuning problem. This paper proposes a holistic multi-timescale framework for a remote-area renewable energy system comprising wind turbines (WTs), photovoltaic (PV) units, battery energy storage systems (BESSs), electrolyzers (ELs), and a VSC-HVDC link. A two-stage stochastic model co-optimizes capacity configuration and 15-min dispatch under scenario-dependent frequency-security constraints, and a coordinated control strategy combines communication-free DC-link-voltage-based frequency perception with constraint-aware support from BESS, EL, WT, and PV. In the case study, the optimal scheme yields an LCOE of 0.2901 CNY/kWh, and introducing EL reduces the LCOE by 0.0106 CNY/kWh compared with the scheme without EL. Time-domain simulations across three operating scenarios and 1,152 steady-state operating points, with 1,000 disturbance realizations per point, show that the proposed framework satisfies the prescribed frequency-security limits in all tested cases and improves the sending-end frequency nadir relative to the benchmark controllers. The results demonstrate that coordinated use of hydrogen load, battery storage, and VSC-HVDC can simultaneously improve economic performance and frequency security in remote-area renewable energy systems.

title: A Silent, Low-Heat Pinch Valve Driven by a Single Rolled Dielectric Elastomer Actuator_supp1-3697122.mp4
authors: Huichan Zhao
abstract: Fluidic control for untethered soft robots is currently limited by the noise and bulk of solenoid valves. While dielectric elastomer actuators (DEAs) offer a promising soft alternative, existing designs often rely on high-frequency resonance, which induces mechanical vibration, or complex multiactuator stacking to generate sufficient force. Here, we present a miniature (5.0 g) pinch valve driven by a single rolled DEA operating in a nonresonant mode, simplifying the structural and driving system requirements. Our direct-drive design achieves measured sound levels close to the background noise level (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 49 \text{ dB}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and low heat generation (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>C rise) while maintaining a rapid response time (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 20 \text{ ms}$</tex-math></inline-formula>). We further integrate the valve into an untethered pneumatic multilegged robot, demonstrating reliable fluidic control at a locomotion speed of 77.5 cm/min.

title: Structurally-Informed 3D Gaussian Splatting for Limited-Angle CBCT
authors: Haowei Zhou, Zhaohong Pan, Jingjing Dai, Xuan Liu, Weilin Gao, Yaoqin Xie, Xiaokun Liang
abstract: Limited-angle cone-beam computed tomography (LA-CBCT) enables rapid imaging and reduced radiation exposure, but its severely incomplete projection data lead to ill-posed reconstructions with prominent artifacts, limiting clinical applicability. Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3D-GS) have shown promise for efficient tomographic reconstruction, yet its performance remains highly sensitive to initialization. In this work, we present SPARK (Structurally-Informed Projection-Accelerated Reconstruction), a two-stage framework that introduces a generative, structurally informed initialization for 3D-GS. In the first stage, a geometry-conditioned network directly predicts complete 3D Gaussian parameters from a sparse subset of projections, embedding learned anatomical priors to mitigate artifact propagation. In the second stage, the generated scene is refined through physics-based 3D-GS optimization, yielding high-fidelity reconstructions consistent with measured projections. Extensive experiments on public datasets demonstrate that SPARK substantially improves both image quality and convergence speed, achieving superior PSNR/SSIM in severely limited-angle scenarios compared with analytical, iterative, and deep learning baselines. Moreover, SPARK reconstructions provide enhanced inputs for downstream post-processing networks, further boosting image fidelity. These results suggest that SPARK is a promising prior-informed 3D-GS framework for simulated LA-CBCT reconstruction under limited angular coverage, providing an effective bridge between data-driven anatomical priors and physics-based projection-domain refinement.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Hybrid Control Approach for a Class of Underactuated Mechanical Systems
authors: Tengfei Zhang, Yana Yang, Xiaoshuang Zhou, Ying Zhang, Changchun Hua
abstract: Underactuated mechanical systems are pivotal in industrial applications due to their lightweight design and flexibility. Most control methods designed for such systems are either tailored to specific types or feature complex continuous controller structures, whose discretization in practical implementations may lead to performance degradation due to sampling and quantization effects. To this end, an advanced hybrid control approach is developed directly based on a discrete-time model, ensuring consistency and improved performance in digital control environments. Unlike single-method control techniques, the proposed design synergistically integrates feedforward and feedback control, combining the accuracy and disturbance rejection capability of feedback control with the timeliness of feedforward control. Moreover, a reinforcement learning (RL) optimization strategy is then employed to achieve synergistic adaptive adjustment of both the feedforward shaping parameters and the feedback control gains. Notably, a composite signal is designed to incorporate information related to the underactuated state into the feedback controller, enhancing coupling so that the underactuated variables achieve the desired response. Finally, the experimental results on double-pendulum crane and tower crane systems verify the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed approach.

title: Large Scale Crop Mapping in the Sanjiang Plain Using a Deep Spatial-Temporal Fusion Network With Time Series Sentinel-2 Imagery
authors: Xubin Li, Xiaodong Na, Zeyu Zhang, Sunai Ma, Yizhe Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate mapping of crop types is essential for agricultural management and food security. While deep learning using multitemporal imagery achieves high performance, the dimensionality of time-series data often causes feature interference and excessive computational costs, hindering large-scale deployment. To address the issue, a novel deep learning model named the Spatial-Temporal Fusion Network (STF-Net) is proposed in this study. The architecture consists of a dual branch structure and a gated fusion module. The dual-branch structure is responsible for separating and processing spatial and temporal features, where the spatial branch focuses on extracting spatial features of crop planting areas, and the temporal branch focuses on extracting temporal features of crop growth. The adaptive weighting and integration of information extracted from both branches is then handled by the gated fusion module. To evaluate model performance, crop mapping experiments were conducted for major grain crops in the Sanjiang Plain using time-series Sentinel-2 imagery. The results demonstrated that STF-Net delivered efficient crop mapping while effectively reducing salt-and-pepper noise. STF-Net achieved the best accuracy, with an overall accuracy of 92.58%, a mean Intersection of Union of 0.85, and an average Kappa coefficient of 0.89. The producer accuracy (PA) for rice, maize, and soybean is 95.49%, 90.26%, and 91.24%, respectively. The crop mapping results from our model show good agreement with official crop planting area statistics, with coefficients of determination R² exceeding 0.97. These results indicate that the proposed approach is a promising solution for accurate, cost-effective large-scale crop mapping.

title: Rotor-Angle Stability Enhancement Control for MMC-MTDC Systems Without Sending-End Power Reserves
authors: Haobo Zhang, Wang Xiang, Jun Liang, Jinyu Wen
abstract: High-voltage direct current (HVDC) systems are increasingly integrated into AC grids, making rotor-angle stability more critical. Improper HVDC dynamics during disturbances may weaken synchronizing torque and damping, thereby aggravating oscillations. This paper investigates the offshore wind farm (OWF)-based multi-terminal DC (MTDC) system and analyzes the impact of its power injections on grid rotor-angle stability. On this basis, a frequency-based power control logic is first proposed to enhance rotor-angle stability. Building on this logic, Lyapunov-based control laws are derived to coordinate active and reactive power both among multiple terminals (inter-terminal level) and within each converter under capacity constraints (intra-terminal level). To further optimize the coordinated control, an optimization framework is then designed, ensuring effective and secure support. Finally, considering the absence of power reserves at the sending-end OWF, a capacitor energy-based control strategy is proposed for MTDC systems to utilize all MMCs' capacitor energy at the same time for stability support (device level), thereby expanding the available support energy and further improving rotor-angle stability. The effectiveness and robustness of the proposed control are validated on the IEEE 39-bus system via PSCAD/EMTDC and MATLAB co-simulation.

title: Structured Graph Reasoning for Traffic Anomaly Detection
authors: Haoyue Shi, Le Wang, Sanping Zhou, Fei Hui, Zhanheng Gao, Gang Hua, Wei Tang
abstract: Unsupervised traffic anomaly detection aims to detect abnormal frames and objects in dashcam videos given only normal videos during training. Most prior works focus on reconstruction or prediction pipelines, while recent graph-based extensions still rely on graph feature learning or limited statistical interactions. Consequently, reasoning over structured relations and abnormal interactions remains largely unexplored. This paper introduces a structured graph reasoning method that bridges normality learning, anomaly detection, and structured reasoning in a unified framework. Unlike prior works, our approach interprets the graph as a semantic abstraction tailored for comprehensive traffic scene understanding. It includes three innovative components: (1) A domain-specific graph representation that models semantic relationships for road scenarios, providing a holistic description of traffic activities. (2) A graph reasoning detector that detects traffic anomalies by reasoning over structured traffic scene graphs. It integrates node anomaly, i.e., unusual object movements, and edge anomaly, i.e., abnormal object interactions, within a unified graph optimization approach, enabling the effective detection of complex, interaction-driven anomalies. (3) An Anomaly Transformer that predicts future traffic scene graphs by attending node and edge queries to object locations and relation histories, thereby effectively learning individual and interaction-level normal traffic behaviors. Experiments on two public datasets, i.e., DoTA and MM-AU, demonstrate the superior performance of our method and its effectiveness in detecting complex abnormal traffic events.

title: Temporal Relations-Aware Nonnegative Latent Factorization of Tensors for Dynamic Directed Graph Representation_supp1-3700025.pdf
authors: Xin Luo
abstract: Dynamic directed graphs are increasingly used to model complex relational systems across various domains, such as telecommunication networks. Latent factorization of tensors (LFoT) models are effective for dynamic graph representation learning. However, existing LFoT models typically rely on canonical polyadic decomposition and fail to capture the unipartite characteristics of dynamic directed graphs, i.e.,such graphs concern the temporal interactions among a single node set, thereby limiting the node representation accuracy and temporal interaction modeling capability. To address this critical issue in system modeling, this article proposes a novel temporal relations-aware nonnegative LFoT (TRNL) model whose ideas are threefold: 1)building a nonnegative RESCAL-based LFoT model for representing the nodes and unipartite temporal interactions precisely; 2)developing an adaptive parameter-learning scheme to improve training efficiency and practical applicability; and 3)theoretically proving that its convergence is guaranteed under the proposed learning scheme. Experimental results on eight real-world dynamic directed graphs demonstrate that TRNL outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in both representation learning accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Retrieving Delta Permittivity Using Highly Independent Dual-Band Microwave Sensor with Alternately Reference Capabilities
authors: Syah Alam, Indra Surjati, Lydia Sari, R. Deiny Mardian, Fitri Kurnia Sari, Teguh Firmansyah, Muhammad Iqbal, Nugraha, Dian Widi Astuti, Mudrik Alaydrus, Yuyu Wahyu, Zahriladha Zakaria
abstract: This paper proposes a delta permittivity detection using a microwave sensor based on dual-opposite interdigitated split ring resonator with alternately reference capabilities. The proposed sensor has two different sensing areas and has high independent characteristics operating at dual-band resonance frequencies at fr1 = 0.94 GHz and fr2 = 1.59 GHz. The delta permittivity is detected by placing the reference sample and the measured sample in each sensing area alternately and simultaneously in direct contact. The permittivity of the reference material has a fixed value using vacuum (εr = 1) and RO5880 (εr = 2.2), while the permittivity of the measured sample varies in the range 1 - 9.2. From the measurement results, the sensor has a high performance with a maximum ΔF of 0.81 GHz and 0.84 GHz, Frequency Detection Resolution (FDR) of 0.45 GHz and 0.48 GHz and Normalized Sensitivity (NS) of 6.00% and 6.03% for vacuum and RO5880 as reference, respectively. In addition, the average accuracy of the sensor is in the range of 91.46% - 97.40% for the detection of delta permittivity of the sample. Both sensing areas can be used as references interchangeably and have high performance, respectively. Therefore, the proposed sensor can be recommended for several applications such as material quality control, pharmaceutical industry and food quality control.

title: Intelligent WSN Sensor Node for Oil Spill Detection and Classification: A TinyML and IoT Approach
authors: Yan Ferreira Da Silva, Raimundo Carlos Silvério Freire, João Viana Da Fonseca Neto
abstract: This paper proposes a novel intelligent sensor node architecture that integrates embedded systems, machine learning, and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The proposed sensor node plays a crucial role in a Wireless Sensor Network (WSN) designed to detect and classify pollutants from petroleum derivatives in aquatic environments. The operational workflow consists of four fundamental stages: a) observing environmental variables (pH, conductivity, liquid temperature, and turbidity); b) signal processing; c) classification through an artificial neural network; and d) displaying outputs for decision-making. The signals serve as inputs to a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) neural network embedded in an ESP32e microcontroller via TinyML and MicroPython. As a key contribution, this work performs a multicriteria trade-off analysis comparing MLP, RBF, SVM-RBF, and NLR models, evaluating predictive performance, computational cost, and memory footprint. The results demonstrate a classification F-score of 87.32% and an overall accuracy of 85% for diesel and gasoline detection in aquatic environments. Furthermore, the development of an online monitoring stage and energy optimization techniques is presented, where tests using the Deep Sleep mode showed a 44.7% reduction in power consumption, ensuring the node’s survival in hostile aquatic environments.

title: Selectively Generating High-Purity Vortex Beams by a Planar Filtenna with a Compact Integration of Spiral SSPP and HMSIPW
authors: Si-Wen Zhang, Jun Hu, Yue-Fei Hu, Huang-Yan Li, Xiang Wang, Wen Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Orbital angular momentum (OAM) waves exhibit orthogonal phase characteristics and have attracted considerable interest in wireless communications, radar imaging, and electromagnetic sensing applications. This paper presents a compact planar OAM filtenna that integrates an incomplete uniform circular array (UCA), a spiral spoof surface plasmon polaritons (SSPPs) waveguide, and a half-mode substrate integrated plasmonic waveguide (HMSIPW) filter. The SSPPs feed excites sequentially rotated circularly polarized elements to generate vortex waves, while the HMSIPW introduces bandpass filtering without increasing the size. Theoretical analysis establishes the mode relation, revealing the OAM generation mechanism. Simulated and measured results confirm frequency-selective radiation from 4.12 to 5.82 GHz, with six modes and mode purities up to 0.96. The proposed filtenna achieves compact, high-purity, and frequency-reconfigurable OAM radiation, showing great potential for wireless communication and radar imaging systems.

title: Spectral State Fusion Tree Mamba for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Bing Tu, Zhenghao Hu, Bo Liu, Yan He
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) data possess complex spatial structures and high-dimensional spectral information. Mamba has been applied to address the limitations of general methods in HSI classification, including restricted receptive fields and high computational complexity. However, the scan mechanism of traditional Mamba unreasonably constructs spatial distance relationships between neighboring row pixels and fails to adaptively construct the optimal scanning path based on the spectral similarity of pixels. Additionally, the characteristic of traditional Mamba scanning each channel independently overlooks the feature extraction from high-dimensional spectral information. This work proposes a Spectral State Fusion Tree Mamba (SSFTM) architecture to resolve these limitations. The Tree Scan (TS) mechanism computes cosine distances among spatial neighboring pixels and spectral channels to construct adaptive minimum spanning trees in both the spatial and spectral domains, thereby establishing reasonable spatial–spectral relationships and enabling efficient joint feature extraction. The Spectral State Fusion (SSF) mechanism applies multi-layer one-dimensional dilated convolutions along the spectral dimension to the state space vectors, enabling inter-channel interaction and promoting multi-scale spectral feature extraction. The proposed SSFTM demonstrates superior classification accuracy across multiple datasets compared to SOTA methods and exhibits acceptable computational complexity. The code is available at https://github.com/copawloroous/SSFTM.

title: VRFST: Voiceprint-Based Robust Wear Detection for Diamond Abrasive Tools via Fuzzy-Enhanced Spatiotemporal Features
authors: Lili Tang, Hui Tian, Hui Huang, Jialiang Xie, Shan Lou, Paul J. Scott, Wenhan Zeng
abstract: Accurate and robust wear detection of diamond abrasive tools is crucial in Industry 5.0, yet challenging due to inherent fuzziness and uncertainties in multi-condition, high-noise industrial environments. This paper presents a novel voiceprint-based robust method via fuzzy-enhanced spatiotemporal features (VRFST) to address this challenge by exploiting the power of fuzzy neural networks in handling uncertainty. First, to theoretically guide model design, the acoustic-based wear detection problem of diamond abrasive tools is formally described as a multi-objective optimization task. Second, to obtain robust representations, acoustic signals are preprocessed, including time series encoding and frame-level feature aggregation. Critically, the core innovation lies in integrating three synergistic feature extractors: a temporal feature extractor using stacked bidirectional long short-term memory (SBiLSTM) and multi-head attention, a spatial feature extractor with convolutional block and spatial pyramid pooling (SPP) block, and a fuzzy feature extractor utilizing overlapping Gaussian membership functions to suppress multi-source uncertainties adaptively. Third, voiceprint representations are derived by multi-scale convolutional fusion of fuzzy-spatial-temporal features and classified using a theory-guided loss function to enhance accuracy and generalization. Experiments show that incorporating the fuzzy feature extractor significantly enhances robustness, with the extracted temporal, spatial, and fuzzy features offering inherently complementary representations. VRFST achieves exceptional accuracy, ultra-low inference time, and strong generalization under diverse operating conditions and high-noise scenarios across three datasets, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.

title: Event-Triggered Finite-Time Tracking Control for Nonlinear Cyber-Physical Systems with Actuator Faults under Deception Attacks
authors: Haiqing Huang, Yingying Liu, Ben Niu, Yi Niu, Guangdeng Zong, Xiaomei Wang
abstract: This paper addresses the adaptive finite-time fault-tolerant tracking control problem for nonlinear output feedback systems with actuator faults under multiplicative deception attacks. First, a composite observer is developed by integrating a fault compensation mechanism with the compromised output signal. Unlike conventional state observers, the proposed observer can simultaneously estimate unmeasured system states, actuator faults, and external disturbances. Second, a sig function, novel coordinate transformations, and an attack compensator are introduced to develop a nonsingular finite-time adaptive tracking controller under deception attacks. The proposed controller effectively eliminates the singularity problems inherent in traditional finite-time controllers and ensures the desired tracking performance. In addition, an improved finite-time adaptive filter is introduced to enhance control accuracy and relax constraints on design parameter selection, compared to existing filtering methods. To reduce communication burden, a dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM) is incorporated. The proposed control strategy guarantees practical finite-time stability of the closed-loop system while successfully avoiding the Zeno phenomenon. Simulation results further verify the effectiveness of proposed strategy.

title: $\mathcal {C}^{n}$ Trajectory Generation in 3D under Arbitrary Bounds on Curvature and Higher-Order Curvature Derivatives
authors: Samer Raed Aldabbas, Ahmet Talha Çetin, Murad Abu-Khalaf, Emre Koyuncu
abstract: We propose a method to solve the general problem of generating a trajectory in 3D space that is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {C}^{n}$</tex-math></inline-formula> continuous, and satisfies given bounds on the curvature <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa \_{\text{max}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and its higher-order derivatives <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\dot{\kappa }\_\text{max}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ddot{\kappa }\_{\text{max}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa \_{\text{max}}^{(n-2)}$</tex-math></inline-formula> where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n>2$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The core idea is to first synthesize parametrically and geometrically continuous <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {C}^{n}$</tex-math></inline-formula> path segments each lying in a 2D hyperplane that satisfy the constraints on the curvature and its derivatives, then lift these planar segments to 3D via an affine anchor-plane transformation that preserves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {C}^{n}$</tex-math></inline-formula> continuity and the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa$</tex-math></inline-formula>-related constraints. The method supports local replanning, and is amenable to real-time implementation. We demonstrate this approach to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {C}^{4}$</tex-math></inline-formula> trajectory generation in 3D, of practical interest to robotics and aerospace applications, where fourth-order smoothness (snap continuity) together with explicit bounds on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\dot{\kappa }$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ddot{\kappa }$</tex-math></inline-formula> is required for dynamic feasibility. We apply the proposed trajectory-generation pipeline to solve a terrain-following problem for a fixed-wing aircraft.

title: Dual-Function Radar Communication Signal Processing Based on AFDM and Index Modulation
authors: Xuan Fu, Jianlong Tang, Zhiyuan Li, Yongpeng Cui
abstract: Affine frequency division multiplexing (AFDM) is widely adopted in the dual-function radar communication (DFRC) research since its multipath separation capability can alleviate inter-carrier interference. However, current symbol detection algorithms designed for AFDM systems exhibit a relatively high bit error rate (BER) under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. To address this issue, a joint maximum ratio combining (MRC) and message passing (MP) symbol detection algorithm called JMP is proposed, which leverages the rule constraints of index modulation and the feedback characteristics of MP in combination with MRC. In the design of radar signal processing, an algorithm termed constant false alarm rate and orthogonal matching pursuit (CFAR-OMP) is developed, utilizing CFAR threshold decisions as prior guidance to avoid constructing the massive dictionary matrix required by the conventional OMP algorithm. Simulation results show that, compared to conventional algorithms, the proposed JMP algorithm achieves significantly improved communication BER performance at low SNR levels while maintaining low computational complexity. Simultaneously, the CFAR-OMP algorithm ensures reliable radar target parameter estimation performance within a localized parameter search framework.

title: Shaft-integrated Force Sensing with Transformer-based Dynamics Compensation for Telesurgery_supp1-3699214.mp4
authors: Zonghe Chua
abstract: Robot-Assisted Minimally Invasive Surgery (RAMIS) enhances surgeon dexterity, with newer platforms leveraging haptic feedback to further improve performance. Such force information has broader potential to inform performance assessment, tactile localization, and surgical autonomy. This motivates the need for accessible approaches to integrating force sensing into RAMIS tools. This work presents a method for integrating a six-axis commercial force sensor into the distal end of a standard cable-driven surgical instrument, enabling end-effector force measurement while preserving the original mechanical functionality of the device. The proposed design emphasizes reproducibility and accessibility for research applications, requiring no specialized manufacturing tools. A transformer neural network integrates force sensor measurements with robot state information to aid estimation of applied forces at the end-effector, compensating for internal cable forces arising from actuation. Our proposed approach achieved normalized errors below 6%, and generalized to unseen conditions better than purely proximal data-driven sensing approaches. High internal cable forces caused sensor saturation and reduced axial force observability, which can degrade performance along the tool’s major axis and under higher load conditions. Given current levels of performance, the balance of system integrability and performance enables applications and research into timely topics of haptic feedback, skill assessment, and force-informed autonomy in RAMIS. Videos and code are available at https://enhanced-telerobotics.github.io/shaft force sensing.

title: Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Joint Scheduling of EV Charging, Solar PV, and BESS under Real-Time Tariff Feedback
authors: P. Muthukumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of behind-the-meter renewable energy resources and electric vehicle (EV) adoption has increased uncertainty in scheduling and operational planning in modern smart grids. Under conditions of stochastic user behavior, time-varying tariffs, and operational constraints on distributed energy resources (DERs), conventional centralized optimization techniques become difficult to apply. This study presents a Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL) framework for coordinating electric vehicle (EV) charging, photovoltaic (PV) generation, and battery energy storage systems (BESS) under time-varying tariff signals. A federated actor-critic approach with shared global incentive feedback is employed, through which each agent independently learns control actions, including charge/discharge levels, flexible load shifting, and allocation of surplus renewable energy. The learning process accounts for real-time price signals, transformer loading limits, and end-user comfort constraints. The methodology is implemented in the MATLAB Reinforcement Learning Toolbox using the IEEE-33 bus distribution test system and stochastic demand and solar generation datasets. The results show a reduction in peak-hour demand, increased utilization of renewable energy, and lower overall energy costs when compared with rule-based and singleagent control strategies. The proposed architecture demonstrates scalability and coordinated tariff-responsive operation, which are required for practical transactive energy systems.

title: Automated Machine Learning Trainer: A No-Code Framework for Model Development and Explainability
authors: G. Sivagami Devi, P. Kanagalakshmi, A. Dharani Dharan, E. Harini
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Rapid progress in Machine Learning (ML) has accentuated the necessity for an informed available tool, which strives to cut and reconstruct model development and deployment. Traditional ML workflows tend to be too convoluted, requiring specific interventions in data preprocessing, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, and interpretability. We therefore present the Automated Machine Learning Trainer (AMLT), an easy-to-use no-code framework that automates the whole ML pipeline, allowing access to model development for both beginners and professionals. AMLT houses embedded Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods to augment transparency into its predictions, increasing trust in ML applications. Comparison experiments showcased how AMLT proves to be efficacious, cutting down time-to-deploy by 35 % and increasing prediction performance by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{5} \%$</tex> in comparison with existing AutoML frameworks. Our results indicate that AMLT has the potential to democratize the automated application of ML, allowing its wide adoption across various disciplines.

title: Comparative Study of Cnn Architectures for Automated Detection of Lung Diseases from Chest X-Ray and Ct Scan Images
authors: Misbahul Sheikh, Nafisa Tasnim Neha, Naushin Mamun, Samia Habib, Kazi A Kalpoma
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Different types of respiratory disease are common and have affected many people around the world. However, their detection remains challenging due to overlapping symptoms and complex radiographic patterns. The early detection of lung diseases such as Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF) and Pneumonia is critical. Although CT scans are commonly used to diagnose IPF and other lung diseases, they are expensive and not always widely available. Some deep learning techniques, including convolutional neural networks (CNN), are used for lung disease detection and prediction. The basic CNN model performs poorly for images with abnormal orientation. This study proposes a Deep Learning (DL) based approach to initially classify IPF and other lung diseases using both chest X-ray and CT scan images. We employed a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures like VGG19, DenseNet121, and ResNet50 to perform classification tasks using three different approaches. First, we have collected a real-time dataset of X-ray and CT scan images from across Bangladesh and prepared three different datasets: 3-class X-ray classification (IPF, Pneumonia, Normal) using our own dataset, 15-class X-ray classification using NIH Chest X-ray 14 dataset with customized classes, and 3-class CT scan classification (IPF, Pneumonia, Normal) using our own dataset. Our findings demonstrate that DenseNet121 consistently outperformed the other models across all tasks, achieving the highest accuracy of 96% in 3-class X-ray classification, 88% in 15 -class X-ray classification, and 95% in 3-class CT scan classification. As a scalable and budget-friendly alternative for automated lung disease screening, the fusion of deep learning and chest X-rays proves highly promising. Such AI-driven systems could significantly bolster diagnostic workflows in hospitals, particularly within medical infrastructures that face severe resource limitations.

title: AI-Powered Threat Intelligence Framework for Strengthening Cybersecurity in Multi-Cloud and DevOps-Driven Enterprise Environments
authors: Vijay Krishna, Siva Prasad Ponnuru, Jyothi Chandra Nath, Seetha Rama Rao Thirumala, Nagaalekhya Kypa, Rajesh Kamisetty, Shripad V Deshpande
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework, which can be used to promote cybersecurity in multi-cloud and DevOps-based enterprise environments. The framework uses the Hybrid AI Threat Intelligence Pipeline (HATI) that incorporates deep learning, graph neural networks, and large language models to identify advance and emerging threats. Mostly used to support real-time data ingestion, scalable log processing, and automated anomaly detection, Apache OpenSearch with ML-dash and Security Analytics, is used on a variety of cloud environments and CI/CD pipelines. Experimental analysis proves the suggested solution to be highly accurate in detection, low rates of false positive and rapid incident response in comparison with conventional rule-based, single-model and SIEM-based solutions. The combination of hybrid AI and scalable analytics platform will provide advantages of complete correlation of threats, proactive defense, and increased operational resilience. On the whole, the framework can be seen as a dynamic and strong framework to offer a solution to enterprises that expect to have high-level cybersecurity features in more dynamic and distributed infrastructures.

title: EUNet: An Efficient Attention-Enhanced U-Net for Automatic Chronic Wound Segmentation
authors: Esrat Jahan, Md. Darun Nayeem, Rehanuma Tabassum, Md. Masudul Islam, Nishat Nayala Priyanka
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of chronic wounds from clinical photographs is essential for objective assessment and treatment planning, yet remains challenging due to irregular wound boundaries, heterogeneous backgrounds, and varying imaging conditions. This article presents EUNet, an attentionenhanced encoder-decoder framework for automatic chronic wound segmentation. EUNet builds on a ResNet50 backbone and integrates squeeze-excitation attention, residual depth-wise separable convolutions, a dilated bottleneck, and a shallow fullresolution skip connection to jointly capture global context and fine boundary details. The model is trained end-to-end using a combined binary cross-entropy and Dice loss, mixed-precision optimization, and a warmup cosine learning-rate schedule. Experiments conducted on a composite dataset of 2,760 wound images demonstrate that EUNet achieves a Dice score of 89.23% and an IoU of 8 0. 7 9%, outperforming multiple backbone-based U-Net variants. Ablation and multi-seed evaluations confirm the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed architectural components.

title: Research on the Restructuring Path of the Power Material Supply Chain Under the Background of Digital Transformation
authors: Chaoxia Gao, Shucheng Qi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The emerging complexity of the power material supply chain, with the wave of digital transformation, requires data-driven solutions to reorganise and optimise the performance of operations. This paper focused on the analysis of the role of digital transformation on the efficiency, resilience, and decision-making in the power material supply network in terms of a quantitative framework that is implemented by a simulation. An artificial dataset was created that was two years of weekly operations and was analysed in Python (Google Colab) with high-level machine learning models such as Ridge, XGBoost, and LightGBM. Results of the regression showed that cost and lead-time prediction were highly accurate, with the Ridge model having MSE = 3.45, RMSE = 1.86 for cost and MSE = 0.00029, RMSE = 0.017 for lead time. Moreover, the classification models were 99% accurate and F1 = 0.99. The paper concludes that the digital transformation allows for quantifying the improvement of supply-chain efficiency as well as risk mitigation. Managers have been advised to use integrated digital systems and not to use isolated technologies. The results suggest that a form of strategic decision, such as the restructuring efforts, can be implemented using simulationbased analysis. The use of artificial data also restricts the applicability of findings to practice, though.

title: UDF-Net: Uncertainty-Aware Dynamic Fusion Network for Vision-LiDAR Autonomous Systems
authors: Lakshmi S R, Ghousia Sanober Sabreen, Tamilselvi B, K. Keerthana, Manosri T
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, integrated vision and sensor fusion for autonomous systems gained attention because of increasing deployment of autonomous vehicles in complex and dynamic environments. The existing HydraFusion (HF) method employed context-aware sensor fusion for improving accuracy by choosing appropriate sensor combination. However, it depended on single-frame context identification and employe fixed set of predefined fusion branches which leads to imbalance fusion decisions and restricts adaptability in changing environmental conditions. To overcome these limitations, Uncertainty-Aware Dynamic Fusion Network (UDF-Net) for autonomous system is proposed. Initially, data is collected from RADIATE dataset which consist of camera images and LiDAR point clouds and features were extracted. Next, temporal context modeling is introduced employing transformer-based encoder for capturing scene information across many frames where uncertainty estimation enhances reliability of context prediction. Further, dynamic fusion graph is constructed utilizing Graph Neural Network (GNN) which allows flexible and adaptive sensor fusion. Furthermore, context-guided feature fusion is performed using attention-weighted message passing with dynamic graph. Lastly, resulting fused features are processed by same detection network for generating outputs. Therefore, proposed UDF-Net framework obtains enhanced accuracy (89.6 %) and mAP (71.8 %) which is more reliable context estimation and improved fusion stability which validates efficiency for autonomous system perception compared to existing HydraFusion approach.

title: FedThreat-X: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Threat Intelligence Framework for Multi-Cloud Cybersecurity
authors: Md Anisur Rahman Chowdhury, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Aizaada Akylbekova, Sabrina Mohona, Meena Momand, Gohar Masood Khan, Kefei Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: As more organizations distribute their workloads across multiple cloud platforms, detecting coordinated, privacy-sensitive cyber threats has emerged as an urgent challenge. Despite the increasing use of multi-cloud ecosystems, sensitive log data, inconsistent security policies, and heterogeneous cloud architectures make it unattainable to securely and cooperatively share actionable threat intelligence. We suggest FedThreat-X, a federated threat intelligence framework that protects privacy while fostering cooperative learning of attack behaviors without revealing raw surveillance data. Threat-aware adaptive aggregation, proximal stability management, and lightweight local MLP classification are the key components in our model, which is able to train in heterogeneous and non-IID multi-cloud environments. FedThreat-X (using CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM, and GNN-TI as a baseline) is able to achieve the state-of-the-art performance of 98.7 percent in the Microsoft GUIDE dataset containing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 M}$</tex> evidence records, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 M}$</tex> triaged events, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 4 1}$</tex> MITRE ATT&CK approaches. These strong results have a low false-positive rate and high generalization to demonstrate how resilient it is to distribution changes. This work hereby advances the state of the art in multi-cloud cybersecurity by enabling secure, scalable, high-fidelity, federated threat intelligence suitable for real-world SOC operations.

title: Development of a Cloud-Integrated IoT Smart Attendance System Using ESP32 Edge Devices
authors: A S M Shahariar Robin, Sarang Chisim, Md. Hridoy Ahmed, Md. Taslim Arif, Sheak Rashed Haider Noori
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The manual attendance systems are tedious, prone to errors and can be abused by proxy and is prone to mismanagement of data. In order to address the shortcomings, this paper will suggest a cloud-based Smart Attendance System based on the NFC (Near Field Communication) protocol and ESP32 edge devices. Each user is issued with a special NFC card with a special secure identifier (UID), when entering a classroom or a workplace, the card is scanned and its identifier read by the reader. The received data is transmitted to a Node.js backend server, and it is securely stored in a MongoDB database that fills the real-time monitoring and administrative control in a web-based dashboard. Experimental testing under controlled environments revealed that the mean end to end response time is approximately 1 second, and it includes card scanning, cloud transmission, and dashboard update. Additional stress test with 100 simulated HTTP requests ensured that the back end is stable and checking can be obtained at greater load test. The suggested system is a cheap (approximately, 13 USD as the hardware cost), scalable, and efficient alternative to the old strategies of attendance that may be applied to educational institutions and organizational setting.

title: Lightweight and Interpretable Deep Learning for Automated Waste Classification
authors: Saad Ahmed, Razia Sultana, Qazi Masrafi Rahman, Aroshi Ali, Khalid Syfullah
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing volume of solid waste poses serious environmental and public health challenges, especially in rapidly urbanizing countries like Bangladesh. Effective waste classification plays a key role in improving recycling rates and minimizing pollution. In this study, we present a deep learningbased framework for automatic waste classification using an extended version of the BDWaste dataset. We evaluated the performance of four pretrained convolutional neural networks which are MobileNetV2, VGG16, DenseNet121 and Xception alongside a custom lightweight CNN designed for deployment in resource-constrained settings achieving very high accuracay of 98.76%. MobileNetV2 achieved the highest classification accuracy, while the custom CNN showed competitive performance with significantly lower computational requirements. To enhance interpretability, we applied the Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) technique, which offered visual insights into the model's decision-making process. This work supports the development of intelligent waste classification systems that contribute to urban sustainability and environmental protection efforts.

title: Escaping Stability-Plasticity Dilemma in Online Continual Learning for Motion Forecasting via Synergetic Memory Rehearsal
authors: Yunlong Lin, Chao Lu, Tongshuai Wu, Xiaocong Zhao, Guodong Du, Yanwei Sun, Zirui Li, Jianwei Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved remarkable success in intelligent systems such as autonomous vehicles and robots. However, most DNN-based methods suffer from catastrophic forgetting, where the DNN may fail to maintain its performance in previously learned tasks after adapting to new data. This phenomenon gives rise to a critical tradeoff between memory stability of the DNN, that is, the ability to retain learned knowledge, and learning plasticity, that is, the capacity to acquire new information effectively. To address such stability–plasticity dilemma, this study proposes a novel CL method named synergetic memory rehearsal (SyReM). SyReM maintains a compact memory buffer to represent learned knowledge. To ensure memory stability, it employs an inequality constraint that limits increments in the average loss over the memory buffer. Synergistically, a selective memory rehearsal mechanism is designed to enhance learning plasticity by selecting samples from the memory buffer that are similar to recently observed data. This selection is based on an online-measured cosine similarity of loss gradients, ensuring targeted memory rehearsal. We validate SyReM under online CL tasks for motion forecasting. Comprehensive experiments on naturalistic driving datasets demonstrate that, compared to non-CL and CL baselines, SyReM significantly mitigates catastrophic forgetting in past scenarios while improving forecasting accuracy in new ones. The code is available at https://github.com/BIT-Jack/SyReM

title: A DC-DC Converter for Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Fenglian Wang, Guibin Zou, Xiuyan Wei, Shengkun Pang
abstract: High voltage gain non-isolated dc-dc converters are finding an increasingly wide range of applications in clean energy generation systems. In applications, such as electric vehicle charging station, the low ripple input current of dc-dc converters can prolong its service life. Accordingly, this paper Improved and derived a dc-dc converters with low ripple input current and low voltage stress of switch. The converters are characterized by high efficiency, extra-high voltage gain, continuous input current, passive clamping, and common ground. It adopts a unique three-winding coupled-inductor and a switched capacitor to enhance its boost capability, and has three modulation indexes, i.e., the boost turn ratio (n1>1), the buck turn ratio (n2<1), and the duty cycle D. The introduction of buck turn ratio n2 not only avoids an increase in the capacity and size of the coupled-inductor, but also reduces the leakage inductance, total inductance, and core size of the coupled inductor, which in turn reduces the loss of the coupled inductor, and increases the efficiency of converter. This paper studies steady-state characteristics of the converter, deduces the current/voltage relationship for each component, analyses the efficiency of converter. Finally, the feasibility of the proposed converter is verified via experiments.

title: Enhancing Representation Inversion and Alignment for Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval
authors: Feifei Zhang, Jia Chen, Haoliang Zhou, Changsheng Xu
abstract: Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) retrieves a target image by combining a reference image with a textual modification. Existing supervised methods have demonstrated strong performance; however, their dependence on costly, manually annotated triplets limits their scalability and generalizability across diverse applications. Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval (ZS-CIR) addresses this by mapping visual inputs to textual representations, enabling annotation-free composed image retrieval. However, accurately capturing both fine-grained details and high-level semantics remains a key challenge. To address this, we introduce an enhanced visual representation inversion and alignment model designed to bridge the gap between visual and textual modalities. Specifically, we propose a Multi-Level Visual Prompting (MLVP) module that integrates local image information into global representations through both instance-level and feature-level prompting, ensuring a more comprehensive capture of fundamental visual content. We further introduce a Dynamic Alignment (DA) module that complements contrastive learning, ensuring semantic flow is effectively transferred and aligned across different modalities without adding extra trainable parameters, thereby narrowing the semantic gap between the visual and textual representations. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets validate the superiority of our method over existing state-of-the-art approaches, demonstrating robust generalization and adaptability for zero-shot composed image retrieval.

title: Distribution-Agnostic Idle-Age-Aware Joint Channel and Packet-Size Learning in Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Haythem Bany Salameh, Malik Umar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Cognitive radio (CR) enables opportunistic spectrum access while protecting primary users (PUs). This opportunistic transmission is uncertain due to time-varying PU activity (unpredictable PU returns) and heterogeneous channel quality. PU idle times differ across channels, in which some have long mean availability, others only short opportunities, and PU returns vary in severity. In multi-user CR networks (CRNs), contending CR users must coordinate over a control channel to reserve an idle PU channel and select a packet size that maximizes throughput while reducing PU-return-induced forced terminations. Most existing designs optimize channel assignment or packet sizing in isolation, assume memoryless PU idle times, and often ignore control contention/reservation overhead that directly reduces the residual transmission time. This paper investigates joint channel assignment and packet-size control under unknown PU availability while explicitly accounting for realistic control overhead time. We model the Residual PU-idle Time with Idle Age using a distribution-agnostic residual-life survival function and incorporate control overhead into the packet probability of success (PoS). We formulate a maximum-profit, discounted-return problem that maximizes long-term network goodput subject to a required PoS, a received signal-to-noise (SNR) threshold, and exclusive channel occupancy. Due to the uncertainty in PU activity and the long-term nature of the optimization problem, which cannot be solved using conventional optimization methods, we model it as a constrained Markov decision process (CMDP). To solve the resulting CMDP, we propose an on-policy State-Action-Reward-State-Action (SARSA) reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm with convergence-based exploration and a quantized/pruned action-space mechanism that filters infeasible channel–packet-size pairs to reduce online search complexity. The proposed RL-based algorithm learns perchannel PU dynamics online and jointly selects the throughput-maximizing channel and packet size. Our method is a hybrid model-assisted/model-free design, in which online survival/quantile estimates are used only for PoS-based feasibility screening, while SARSA learns the long-term policy directly from interaction without requiring a parametric PU model. Simulations under heterogeneous PU availability/severity show up to 32% throughput improvement, with fewer retransmission attempts than reference schemes.

title: Towards Heterogeneous Multimodal Sentiment Analysis with ASR Errors via Multi-Granularity Contrastive Coordination_supp1-3702501.pdf
authors: Zhiqiong Wang
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis improves the understanding of human emotions by integrating multiple modalities. However, the language modality accessed by the actually deployed MSA systems is obtained through automatic speech recognition (ASR), which can induce text errors and lead to MSA performance degradation. Besides, multimodal heterogeneity and multimodal information imbalance further aggravate the negative impact of ASR errors. To this end, we propose a multi-granularity contrastive coordination framework (MGCCF) for achieving robust MSA under ASR errors. Specifically, an error sentiment awareness calibration (ESAC) unit is devised to learn cross-modal word-level correlation and contextual information, enabling the detection and calibration of erroneous sentiment word embedding. Furthermore, a multimodal contrastive coordination (MCC) paradigm is presented to achieve cross-modal multi-granularity alignment from both global semantic and local temporal aspects. Meanwhile, MCC ingeniously leverages non-verbal features to reinforce the ability of ESAC to correct anomalous sentiment information and refine the expression of language features. Eventually, a multimodal information fusion (MIF) approach is developed to deal with multimodal information imbalance, which interpretably quantifies the contributions of different modalities and achieves multimodal dynamic fusion. Comprehensive experiments on seven MSA datasets demonstrate that our MGCCF achieves remarkable performance and surpasses the current state-of-the-art MSA methods.

title: Dispatch Strategy Against Typhoon-Induced Wind Farm Cut-Outs Considering Resilience: An Adaptive Robust Stochastic Optimization Approach
authors: Haitao Xu, Haotian Zhao, Hongbin Sun, Qinglai Guo
abstract: Typhoon disasters pose serious threats to the secure operation of wind-integrated power systems by damaging transmission lines and perturbing wind power output. To address the typhoon-induced large-scale unplanned wind farm cut-outs, it is necessary to develop an orderly wind curtailment plan in day-ahead dispatch. However, balancing robustness and wind power utilization in such plans remains challenging. Furthermore, it is difficult for a single uncertain optimization framework to simultaneously account for the distinct uncertainties in both transmission contingency and wind power output, leading to strategies with limited practicality. To tackle these challenges, this paper proposes a day-ahead dispatch model incorporating an orderly wind curtailment strategy, using adaptive robust stochastic optimization (ARSO) to handle the dual uncertainties of line failure and wind generation. The model consists of pre-dispatch and re-dispatch stages. The curtailment plan is determined in the form of curtailment thresholds in pre-dispatch stage and implemented in re-dispatch stage. In addition, this paper designs a wind power sample generation method and develops a solution algorithm for the ARSO model based on the column-and-constraint generation (C&CG) approach. Case studies on modified IEEE 39-bus and 118-bus systems verify the necessity of orderly wind curtailment and the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: MET: Electric Toothbrushing Activity Recognition Based on Magneto-Inductive Sensing
