title: DiffusionDxNet: Improving Monkeypox Classification Using Diffusion-Based Data Augmentation and DenseNet121
authors: Md Mohaiminul Islam Sajib, Protik Chakroborty, Md. Adnan Sami, Md. Abdus Sami Shezan, Bishal Prosad
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Monkeypox is an emerging infectious disease whose skin manifestations closely resemble other dermatological conditions such as chickenpox and measles, making early visual diagnosis challenging. This diagnostic similarity may delay early detection, highlighting the need for automated and reliable computer-aided diagnostic systems. In our research, we develop a deep learning model using diffusion-based data augmentation and the DenseNet121 model to classify monkeypox into various categories. Because monkeypox image datasets are most often unbalanced, we create synthetic images of minor categories to develop a balanced dataset. This is followed by fine-tuned four-class classification tasks using a pretrained DenseNet121 model. To make our model more robust for better predictive performance, we investigate the combination of multiple learned models through both the uniform and greedy model soup methods. The experiments were conducted using the Monkeypox Skin Images Dataset (MSID), a publicly available four-class dermatological dataset containing 770 images collected from internet-based sources and curated by the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Islamic University, Kushtia, Bangladesh. The proposed approach was evaluated on an independent test set using standard metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, Matthews correlation coefficient, and ROC-AUC. The model achieved an accuracy of 97.06 %, macro precision of 96.05 %, macro recall of 95.35 %, macro F1-score of 95.65 %, and ROC-AUC of 0.9969. These results indicate consistent performance across all classes and demonstrate the effectiveness of diffusion-based augmentation combined with robust feature learning. In conclusion, the proposed framework demonstrates strong potential as an efficient and scalable solution for automated monkeypox diagnosis and highlights the usefulness of diffusionbased augmentation in medical image analysis.

title: Towards Accurate Autism Detection: a Multimodal Machine Learning Approach
authors: Musfiq Alam Chowdhury, Md. Al-Hasan, Sabiha Alam Chowdhury, Mehedi Hasan, Rubina Aktar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is known as a complex neurodevelopmental condition characterized by a wide range of behavioral and communicative deficits. The detection of ASD has been established as a critical area of research, wherein automated and reliable approaches have been enabled through deep learning methodologies. In this study, a multimodal fusion framework was proposed, in which data from image, audio, video and text modalities was integrated to capture complementary behavioral cues. Specialized deep learning architectures were employed for each modality so that high-level feature representations could be extracted, which were subsequently combined at the decision-making stage to enhance classification robustness. The curated dataset, comprising individuals with and without autism, was used to assess the proposed multimodal model. An exceptional average accuracy of 98.61% and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 98.61% were obtained across 5-fold cross-validation, thereby demonstrating the superior efficacy of integrating heterogeneous data sources compared to single-modality approaches. Interpretability methods, including SHAP, were utilized to elucidate the decisionmaking process, highlighting clinically relevant features such as gaze direction, vocal pitch and semantic content. Through its transparency, robustness and state-of-the-art performance, the proposed model is highlighted as a promising advancement toward practical, AI-driven, non-invasive autism screening tools.

title: Explainable Comprehensive Analysis of Cross-Dataset Phishing Detection Models
authors: Firoze Maliha, Gazi Tahsina Sharmin Jahin, Umme Habiba, Abu Nowshed Chy, M. Jamshed Alam Patwary
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Phishing remains one of the most common cybercrimes that uses various deceptive URLs to obtain users' personal and sensitive information illegally. While machine learning and deep learning methods have been widely used, most studies report high in-sample accuracies without addressing cross-dataset generalization or model interpretability. This paper considers a diverse collection of models, including traditional machine learning classifiers, deep neural and transformer models, and a hybrid CNN+BiLSTM, on two publicly accessible URL-based phishing datasets. The hybrid CNN+BiLSTM model outperformed with 97 % and 95 % accuracy on the respective datasets. However, cross-dataset testing revealed notable performance degradation, highlighting dataset dependency and the challenge of transferability across domains. Feature analysis confirmed that URL length and special characters are robust indicators across datasets, whereas other predictors are dataset-specific. We applied Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME), which illustrates that the models depend on rational URL indicators while also exposing dataset-driven variations. Overall, our findings emphasize that effective phishing detection requires both robust hybrid architectures and interpretable explanations, with future directions focused on domain adaptation to improve generalization.

title: Edge Analytics for Real-Time Process Optimization in Smart Manufacturing: A Hybrid Machine Learning Approach
authors: Rahat Hasan, Md Shohan Bhuiyan, Ayan Barua, Md Nayem Rahman, Rakibul Islam, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid pace of Industry 4.0 has heightened the need for real-time process optimization in smart factories. The proposed framework includes Random Forests for efficient feature selection and interpretation, as well as LSTM for handling complex temporal relationships in multivariable sensor data. The proposed accurate RF+LSTM framework is designed within an edge computing architecture to enable low-latency, realtime analytics and thereby optimize real-time decision-making. To validate the experimental results, the AI4I 2020 Predictive Maintenance challenge dataset, containing 10,000 instances and 14 features across industries, was used. Efficiency is validated to demonstrate that the proposed edge analytics-based framework is effective and accurate within smart factories and has higher values of accuracy at 98.90 % as compared to the existing RF, LSTM, XGBoost, and KNN models on the basis of different evaluation parameters like precision, recall, F1-score, MCC, and AUC values.

title: Cost Effective Sizing of Off Grid PV Systems and Batteries for Residential Energy Systems
authors: Tanjima Akter Mukta, Md Abu Talha, Sancoy Barua, Tahsin Nawroj
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rising demand for electricity and the need for sustainable energy solutions have led to increased interest in solar photovoltaic (PV) systems, particularly for residential applications. This paper focuses on optimizing PV panel and battery sizing for residential energy systems in Bangladesh. Using Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP), the study aims to minimize the total cost of off-grid PV systems while ensuring efficient energy demand. The optimization process was applied to three consumer categories—larger, medium, and smaller—based on their energy consumption profiles. Results demonstrate that the optimized systems provide significant cost reductions, with smaller consumers benefiting from a 90% reduction compared to larger consumers. Additionally, the study shows how PV systems with integrated battery storage can reduce grid dependency and improve energy reliability. The findings highlight the importance of accurate system sizing to balance cost, performance, and energy needs, offering a scalable solution for residential electrification in regions with high solar potential.

title: Data-Driven Psychological Stress Classification Among Higher Education Students: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Ajit Sharma, Dhiman Sarma, Saikat Das Roky
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Psychological stress is a serious issue for students in higher education institutions, especially in Bangladesh. Many students deal with academic pressure, financial problems, and high social expectations. Although mental health issues are increasing, most institutions do not have effective ways to spot or address stress early. These challenges can harm students' academic results and overall well-being. There is a clear need for predictive models that consider personal stress factors like family expectations, financial struggles, and peer pressure. This study fills an important gap in research, since earlier studies have often missed the specific stressors students face in higher education or have not used a wide range of diverse data, like academic records, health information, behavior, and personal challenges. In this research paper, we propose a data-driven technique using machine learning (ML) algorithms to classify stress. To address this, we built a predictive model using 801 student records, with 80% used for training and 20% for testing. We tested five ML algorithms: Random Forest (RF), Naive Bayes (NB), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression (LR). The LR model performed best, reaching 94.56% accuracy and a macro Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 99.54. SVM came next with a slightly lower AUC. Random Forest had 74.15% accuracy, which may suggest overfitting. KNN and Naive Bayes achieved 67.35% and 76.19% accuracy, respectively. The work's potential to address the growing number of mental health issues among students by giving educational institutions a tool for predicting stress before it reaches critical levels makes it socially necessary. Early detection can improve student well-being and lower dropout rates. The model promotes inclusivity by considering socioeconomic factors, which makes it appropriate for a range of student demographics and enhances fairness in mental health support services.

title: Power MOSFETs With Super Safe-Operating-Area
authors: Mark A. Gajda, Ali Ghasemi, Hamza Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: In this work we present a method of eliminating the undesirable effect of premature thermal runaway in MOSFETs operating in linear-mode and show a fully fabricated 100V-class split-gate trench power-MOSFET that achieves the theoretical safe-operating-area entitlement with minimal impact on R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>. The mechanism responsible for thermal runaway is described in terms of competition between charge carrier density, device channel mobility and system geometry. A solution is proposed, which involves integrating two MOSFETs onto the same die to increase the average local thermal mass of active cells, thereby reducing the local thermal impedance. This allows for very fast thermal stabilisation of the device during linear-mode operation by inverting the sign of relevant temperature coefficients and quickly transitioning the device into negative feedback, thus preventing thermal runaway. The operating principles are electrothermal, hence universal, and can be transferred to any base technology, material, voltage rating, and package configuration.

title: A Comprehensive Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Bone Fracture Detection in X-Ray Images Using Optimized CNNs and Transfer Learning
authors: Sumaya Hannan Shova, Rinku Islam, Sarwar Hossain, Md Ishtiaq Ahmed, Md. Abdul Malek, Mst. Mohona Akter, Sadekatul Shihab, Abdullah Al Bassam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Bone fractures, particularly prevalent among elderly populations, demand rapid and precise diagnostic methods to mitigate complications and enable timely intervention. Tradi- tional fracture detection relying on radiologists' manual interpre- tation of X-ray images remains time-consuming and susceptible to human error, especially for subtle or complex fractures. This paper introduces a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates optimized convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with transfer learning to automate and enhance fracture detection in X-ray images. Utilizing the Human Bone Fracture Multi- modal Image Dataset (HBFMID), comprising diverse X-ray and MRI scans, our approach employs pre-trained CNN architectures (ResNet-101, DenseNet-121) fine-tuned via transfer learning to adapt to fracture-specific features, effectively addressing data scarcity challenges. Architectural optimizations including layer pruning, channelwise attention mechanisms, and adaptive pooling reduce computational complexity while improving sensitivity to critical fracture patterns. Augmentation techniques such as rotation, flipping, and noise injection further bolster model generalizability. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-theart performance, with the proposed model achieving the accuracy (ACC) of 95.66%, 99.41%, 97.70%, 99.67%, 99.41% and the value of AUC is 95.54%, 99.98%, 99.78%, 99.99%, 99.98% for Custom Classifier, SVM, RF, LR, XGBoost, respectively.

title: An Ensemble Learning-Based Artificial Intelligence Framework for Postpartum Depression and Suicidal Risk Prediction
authors: Tamanna Akter, Monir Hossen, Najnin Khan Nipa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The birth of a baby brings significant emotional changes; however, many women experience psychological distress such as postpartum depression (PPD) and, in severe cases, suicidal behavior. PPD is a major public health concern that adversely affects maternal well-being and infant development. Early and reliable detection is therefore essential. This study proposes an ensemble learning-based framework for predicting PPD and suicidal risk using demographic and symptom-based data collected from 1,877 postpartum mothers. To address class imbalance, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was applied. Several baseline machine learning classifiers, including Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, and K-Nearest Neighbors, were evaluated and compared with ensemble models such as Random Forest, XGBoost, and an AdaBoost-Extra Trees hybrid. Model performance was assessed using 10-fold cross-validation and multiple train-test splits (60/40, 70/30, and 80/20), with the 70/30 split producing optimal results. Experimental findings indicate that ensemble models consistently outperform individual classifiers, achieving high predictive accuracy for suicidal risk (XGBoost: 94%) and postpartum depression (XGBoost: 95%). These results demonstrate the effectiveness of ensemble learning techniques for supporting early identification of high-risk postpartum women.

title: Dynamic Reinforcement Learning-Driven Event-Aware Adaptive for Education Management System
authors: Rui Guo, Meng Li, Ao Cui, Shulian Zhang, Guangsheng Cao, Qi Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, the rapid growth of online and blended learning environment has increased the need for intelligent Education Management Systems (EMS) capable of adapting to continuously evolving engagement patterns, learner behavior and academic progress. The existing Group-Theoretic Particle Swarm Optimization (GT-PSO) approach uses historical data to evaluate learner behavior but is rely on uses static decision rules and less capable of responding to dynamic events. To address these limitations, this research proposes a Dynamic Reinforcement Learning-driven Event-Aware Adaptive Management System (DREAM-EMS) for managing personalized and continuous educational delivery. Initially, learner interactions from the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD) are modeled as an event-driven sequential decision-making process, while an online RL framework is employed to learn adaptive curriculum adjustment, intervention strategies and pacing modification in real-time. An Event-Triggered Adaptive Reward Reweighting (ET-ARR) mechanism dynamically adjusts optimization priorities based on detected learner events that enables timely engagement recovery, workload balancing and performance improvement. Finally, a closed-loop feedback mechanism continuously updates learner states and policies, which ensures stable and personalized adaptation over academic timelines to provide event-aware education management. The experimental results demonstrates that the proposed DREAM-EMS model achieved accuracy (97.8%), precision (95%) and recall (98%) compared to existing GT-PSO approach.

title:
A New 1200V SiC Trench MOSFET with Shallow Trench and High-Energy Deep P
                    <sup>+</sup>
                    Implantation for Enhanced Gate Oxide Electric Field Shielding
authors: Moufu Kong, Zhaoyu Ai, Hongfei Deng, Qizhi Feng, Mingliang Yang, Xuebing Yin, Bingke Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper proposes a new 1200V silicon carbide (SiC) trench-gate MOSFET. The key structural feature is a 0.4-μm shallow trench formed before the high-energy (1.8 MeV) P<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> implantation. Compared to the conventional device, this design enables a 0.4 μm deeper P<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> doping profile under the same implant conditions without requiring an additional photomask. Benefiting from the shallow trench, a 43% reduction in the maximum gate oxide electric field (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ox,max</inf>) is achieved compared to the conventional device, resulting from the decreased electric field at the gate trench bottom. This substantially improves the gate oxide reliability. Moreover, the shallow trench increases the ohmic contact area for the N+ source. Experimental results show that the proposed device achieves a specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>) of 2.53 mΩ·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, comparable to the conventional value of 2.58 mΩ·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, while both maintain a breakdown voltage above 1500 V. This demonstrates a promising technical approach for high-voltage SiC trench MOSFETs.

title:
Breaking the E
                    <sub>on</sub>
                    and Radiated Noise Trade-off via Deep Split-Gate CSTBT™ with dI/dt Shaping
authors: Kosuke Sakaguchi, Kazuya Konishi, Ryosuke Kobayashi, Gai Fujiki, Kazuki Nishida, Naoki Ikeda, Fumihito Masuoka
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: To effectively reduce both radiated noise and turn-on loss (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>) of CSTBT™, precise optimization of the current waveform during the turn-on period is crucial. In this study, we propose a novel deep split gate (DSG) CSTBT™, which achieved a significant 53% reduction in E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> while simultaneously mitigating radiated noise. This substantial improvement is attributed to the DSG structure’s precise control over negative gate capacitance. This control enables the realization of a high dI/dt during the late stage of the current rise while maintaining a low dI/dt during the initial stage of the turn-on period. Consequently, the DSG structure also demonstrates a total SW loss reduction of 19% at the equivalent DC loss.

title: An Emotion Recognition Framework from EEG Signals Using Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (DGCNN)
authors: Aniruddha Deb, Bishal Roy Chowdhury, Dip Muhuri
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In this paper, a high-performance eEG-based emotion recognition is suggested, using a Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Network (DGCNN). The model was trained using the SEED-IV dataset which is a publicly available multi-session EEG benchmark dataset at Shanghai Jiao Tong University which contains preprocessed, cleaned, and filtered recordings of four emotional states happy, sad, fear, and neutral. In contrast to other deep learning-based EEG signal models, which tend to represent the processed data as fixed grids or sequence of signals, DGCNN inherently views the dynamic and intertwined nature of the brain by making EEG electrodes nodes of a graph and dynamically changing the edges between these nodes as the training progresses. PyTorch v2.3.0 (cu121), Torch-scatter, Torch-geometric are used as the deep learning environment, with easy loading of EEG features, data augmentation, and easy loading of Python 3.10, scikit-learn, matplotlib and seaborn being used to evaluate and visualize the models. It has been experimentally demonstrated that DGCNN is always high-performing and stable, and it performs even better in emotions state classification in comparison with the classical CNN and RNN-based models. The article sheds light on how neural modeling with graphs can be used to decode the affective brain patterns and issues more neuro-sensitive EEGbased applications in the health care, mental health, and humancomputer interface.

title: Deep Learning for High-Fidelity Mpox Classification: From Data to Diagnosis
authors: Tonmoy Debnath, Apurba Adhikary, Sultana Jahan Soheli, Md. Shamim Ahsan, Avi Deb Raha, Yu Qiao, Mohammad Amzad Hossain, Abidur Rahaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The recent surge in monkeypox (Mpox) outbreaks underscores the urgent need for rapid and accurate diagnostic tools. While deep learning (DL) has shown significant promise for analyzing skin lesions, prior research has been constrained by limited datasets and prominent accuracy. Addressing these critical gaps, this paper introduces an enhanced Mpox detection framework. Our approach leverages a novel dataset meticulously compiled from three public Kaggle sources (MSID, MSLD, MSLD v2.0). Through rigorous preprocessing, including a 14 fold data augmentation and a dHash deduplication process, we curated a unique dataset of 16,918 images, representing an approximate 20 times expansion over typical prior datasets. This significantly enhanced data foundation mitigates domain shift challenges and improves model generalization. We systematically evaluated two state-of-the-art Convolutional Neural Networks: ResNet50 and EfficientNetB2. ResNet50 exhibits outstanding classification accuracy, achieving a Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) of 99.98 % and specificity of 99.70 %, alongside a test accuracy of 99.61 %. In contrast, EfficientNetB2 achieves a compelling balance of competitive 99.48 % balanced accuracy with significant computational advantages, including 69.8 % fewer parameters and a 71.59 MB smaller model size, making it highly suitable for resource-constrained environments. The high accuracy on an independent test set for both models further validates their robust generalization capabilities.

title: Comparison between the Secrecy Capacity of Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface Aided and Relay-Assisted Wireless Communication Systems
authors: K. M. Nafis Tajwar, Md. Zahurul Islam Sarkar, Ankon Datta, Ahshan Habib, Asif Ur Rahman Akhand
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper studies the ergodic secrecy capacity (ESC) of two emerging wireless architectures: one assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) and the other using a conventional relay, both in the presence of a passive eavesdropper. For instance, a legitimate source communicates private information to its intended legitimate destination, with a passive eavesdropper co-located in the background, attempting to overhear the signal. Under the considered system setup, the instantaneous signal-to-noise ratio distributions at the intended receiver and the eavesdropper are analyzed to evaluate the ergodic secrecy capacity. Considering independent and identically distributed Rayleigh fading, we derived the closed-form expression of the ergodic secrecy capacity and further supported the analytical results with numerical evaluations. The impact of key system parameters such as signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), number of RIS reflecting elements, and relay gain are examined to understand their influence on secrecy performance. From the simulation outcomes, it is shown that the RIS-assisted transmission achieves higher ergodic secrecy capacity compared to the relay-assisted scheme, particularly in high SNR regions and when a larger number of reflecting element is used.

title: Smart 1.7 kV SiC Power ICs: Monolithic Integration of TCOX UMOSFETs and Tri-Gate CMOS
authors: Chia-Lung Hung, Jing-Neng Yao, Yun-Tin Huang, Ho-Lin Kao, Bing-Yue Tsui, Tian-Li Wu, Yi-Kai Hsiao, Hao-Chung Kuo
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper presents a thick corner oxide (TCOX) located at the trench bottom of a 1.7 kV SiC trench UMOSFET and a monolithically integrated 20 V Tri-Gate SiC CMOS circuit to realize smart control and high-power IC applications for the first time. This integration provides a solution for simultaneously embedding Tri-Gate FET based complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS) circuits into the UMOSFET power device, enabling the realization of smart power management integrated circuit (IC) functions in the future. Trench MOSFETs are key to achieving lower specific on-state resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>) by enabling further scaling of power devices. Among CMOS technologies, FinFET CMOS has been used to further enhance performance due to its well-behaved short-channel effects (SCE) and its ability to overcome device scaling issues. In this work, we successfully demonstrated a process for integrating a 20 V SiC Tri-Gate FET CMOS into a 1.7 kV trench UMOSFET with a TCOX structure. The threshold voltage (Vth) of the Tri-Gate FETs is 1~2 V lower than that of the planar MOSFETs, which enhances circuit speed and reduces power consumption. The Ron,sp of the fabricated UMOSFET can be reduced to 3.5 mΩ-cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, accompanied by a similar density of interface states (Dit) at both the mesa and trench side wall regions. A variety of Tri-Gate FETs based CMOS circuits were designed and their functions verified on this platform, enabling the realization of smart power IC management.

title: Multi-Class Eye Disease Classification using Transfer Learning
authors: Sudan Madhu M P, Deekshith R, Deep Gagan P S, Lalithya Govardhan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Doctors require accurate testing for eye disease diagnosis because early detection of eye diseases, which currently rank among the most common causes of worldwide blindness, must be done through accurate testing. Deep learning methods have shown they can effectively analyse medical images, helping identify retinal diseases. The present research investigates a multi-class eye disease classification system which uses transfer learning through convolutional neural networks that contain SqueezeNet MobileNet-V2, ResNet-18, ResNet-34 and ResNet-50. The research used a large retinal dataset, which contained ten disease categories. The accuracy assessment uses precision, recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score and confusion matrix analysis to evaluate model performance. Experimental results show that ResNet-50 delivered the best classification accuracy, whereas ResNet-34 and ResNet-18 offered the most practical balance between performance and computational cost given the training time and hardware limitations. The study demonstrates that deep learning models, which use transfer learning, can function as dependable diagnostic tools for automatic eye disease detection in multiple classes.

title: LEAF-CT: Lightweight Energy-Aware Forwarding with Controlled Transmission for Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks
authors: Saravanan Subramani, Kiran Kumari Gayam, P. Thiyagarajan, R. Naveenkumar, Shalini Raji A
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Wireless underwater communication incorporating acoustic sensor networks plays a crucial role in various applications that includes ocean monitoring, environmental surveillance, disaster prevention and offshore exploration. However, underwater acoustic channels struggle with issues such as low bandwidth, high propagation delay and other energy constraints that determines reliable and efficient data transmission as a challenging task. Therefore, to resolve these issues, a Lightweight Energy-Aware Forwarding scheme with Controlled Transmission (LEAF-CT) is proposed for underwater acoustic sensor networks. Specifically, the proposed LEAF-CT framework integrates distance-based controlled transmission for short-range communication with a simple rulebased adaptive forwarding mechanism for long-range multi-hop routing. Rather than employing complex metaheuristic optimization, relay nodes are selected through a lightweight scoring function that considers residual energy, progress toward the sink and current forwarding load. Such that this design assists to significantly reduce computational overhead while maintaining energy efficiency and routing reliability. Thus, the simulation-based evaluation using the same network settings as the base model, LEAF-CT demonstrates improved network lifetime, reduced energy consumption, and stable packet delivery performance compared to conventional routing protocols and complex optimization-based schemes.

title: A Comparative Analysis of YOLO Variants with Improved YOLOv12m Model for Students' Classroom Behavior Detection
authors: Souvik Pramanik, Md. Ibrahim Khalil, Ornima Hafizur, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The automated detection of student classroom behavior is critical for enhancing engagement assessment and providing educators with real-time feedback. However, this task remains challenging owing to the prevalence of small, subtle, and often occluded actions. This study presents a robust framework for the real-time recognition of five key student behaviors: raising hands, using phones, bowing heads, bending, and turning heads. We conducted a comprehensive comparative analysis of state-of-the-art YOLO models, including YOLOv9m, YOLOv10m, YOLOv11m, and YOLOv12m. Our findings identified YOLOv12m as the most promising architecture in this domain. Through systematic fine-tuning and the- incorporation of advanced data augmentation, attention mechanisms, and hyperparameter optimization we significantly enhanced the YOLOv12m model. The resulting system achieved a state-of-the-art test mAP50 of 0.916, precision of 0.881, and recall of 0.861, outperforming all its baseline counterparts. Ablation studies confirmed the efficacy of multi-scale feature aggregation and attention modules in mitigating challenges related to scale and occlusion. The proposed framework demonstrated high robustness in real classroom scenarios, offering a practical and intelligent tool for automating behavioral analysis and supporting interactive teaching methods.

title: A High-temperature Three-phase Silicon Carbide MOSFET Power Module with Integrated Low Propagation and High Noise Immunity Gate Driver
authors: Qianheng Dong, Jing Zhu, Yifei Zheng, Bowen Xue, Xiang Fan, Siyang Liu, Long Yuan, Weifeng Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper presents a three-phase silicon carbide (SiC) power module for high temperature that reduces power electronic system volume and cost while enhancing reliability. The proposed module integrates silicon-on-insulator (SOI) gate drivers, featuring low propagation delay, robust transient dV/dt noise immunity and high drive strength, enabling operation at up to 225 °C. The propagation delay, sourcing and sinking current capability and transient dV/dt noise immunity of the gate driver are tested under various ambient temperatures. Furthermore, a three-phase 600V/50A SiC MOSFET power module has been developed using high-temperature packaging technology. The switching performance and protection function behavior of the fabricated power module are fully evaluated at different temperatures. The proposed module shows superior switching speed, transient dV/dt noise immunity and lower switching energy losses compared to the commercial INFINEON IM12S60EA2 SiC power module.

title: Efficient UAV Selection in K-Means Online Routing Protocol (K-Morp) using Heuristics and Weighted Values
authors: Rubiat Hasan Khan, Monir Hossen
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Within the dynamic and fast-changing architecture of aerial networks, the orchestration of several unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) is a multifarious challenge, especially when variables such as mobility, energy and signal reliability fluctuate at the same time. This paper focuses on this challenge and presents an advanced clustering algorithm that is designed to meet realistic operational conditions. The Enhanced K-MORP algorithm is based on the conventional K-means++ algorithm, but it includes telemetry information (energy reserves and signal strength) in the optimization routine. Performance of the proposed scheme was analyzed on the basis of synthetic UAV datasets under Gaussian Markov mobility assumptions under high-level simulation with increasing time. Comparative analyses were carried between Enhanced K-MORP and its baseline counterpart in terms of critical parameters like mean squared error, packet delivery ratio, end-to-end delay, throughput and normalized routing load.

title: A Collaborative Hybrid CNN–LSTM Framework for Real-Time Multichannel Retail Demand Forecasting Using Edge–Cloud Computing
authors: Aashish K C, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Sunil Kumar Goyal, Md Anisur Rahman Chowdhury, Towfika Salam, Mohammad Tajrian Arafat Alvi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Demand forecasting in a modern multichannel retail ecosystem is essential to maximising consumer happiness, simplifying the supply chain, and improving inventory levels due to its precision and reliability. The diffusion of boundaries of online and offline shopping channels results in an extremely erratic, nonlinear, and spatiotemporal demand process that is difficult to accurately predict using statistical and machine learning models like CNNs and LSTMs. Particularly, the centralized cloud computing model possesses inherent scalability and latency bottlenecks in real-time conditions in an omnichannel retail environment of today, and is a major constraint in implementing these models in online applications. To address such problems, the proposed paper describes a Collaborative Hybrid CNN-LSTM architecture to forecast the demand in real-time omnichannel retail with the help of a more efficient edge-cloud computing paradigm. The specific implication of our proposed framework to reduce the latency in computations and bandwidth bottlenecks is the notion of utilizing the collaborative learning process to enhance the latency inference and the enhancement of the data confidentiality in a modern edge-cloud computing framework to support big retail data analytics applications and machine learning modeling applications. Experiments using a large-scale retail dataset demonstrate that our proposed model is significantly better than LSTMs, CNN models, RNN models, and LightGBM machine learning models, achieving 94.7% model accuracy and 0.96 ROC AUC value, respectively, thereby confirming their practical effectiveness in a large-scale modern omnichannel retail setting when applied as a big.

title: Compact Triple-Band Elliptical Monopole Antenna with Modified U-Slot Ground for Sub-6 Ghz 5G Applications
authors: Prabhu. T, Suganya. E, Boopathi Raja. K
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: This paper describes a compact triple-band monopole antenna that uses elliptical radiating elements and a modified U-shaped defected ground structure (DGS) for wireless applications below 6 GHz. The suggested antenna works at three resonant frequencies: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.49 \text{GHz}, 4.35 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 5.12 GHz. These frequencies are for WiMAX, sub- 6 GHz 5 G NR, and WLAN bands, respectively. Geometrically scaled elliptical patches make it possible for the device to work on multiple bands. The modified U-slot etched on the ground plane is also very important for improving inter-band isolation and impedance bandwidth. The U-slot stops surface wave propagation, changes the distribution of ground current, and lowers the antenna quality factor. This makes impedance matching stable and bandwidth wider across all operating bands. The antenna's return loss is better than -23 dB, and its VSWR is less than 1.15. At the resonant frequencies, the highest gains are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 2 8 ~ d B i}, \mathbf{1. 3 5 ~ d B i}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{0. 9 3 ~ d B i}$</tex>. The upper band's relatively low gain is due to substrate losses and current confinement that come with compact slot-loaded structures. A prototype is built and tested with a Vector Network Analyzer and anechoic chamber setup. The results from the simulation and the measurement match up well. The proposed antenna is good for portable and short-range sub-6 GHz wireless applications because it is small, has a simple shape, and can work on multiple bands.

title: An Imperical Study on Predicting Golden Beauty of Human Face Appearance
authors: Sharmin Akter Bithi, M. Akhtaruzzaman, Md. Golam Rabiul Alam, Aseef Iqbal, Md Meftahul Ferdaus
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This study explores the prediction of human facial beauty levels using machine learning models combined with Golden Ratio principles. A dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 0}$</tex> celebrities sourced from the internet, was analyzed using facial landmark detection techniques to compute Golden Ratio (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\phi}$</tex>) based features. The models were evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score to assess classification performances. The Random Forest model outperformed the Decision Tree, achieving higher accuracy as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 0 8 \%}$</tex> over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 9. 6 8 \%}$</tex>. The confusion matrix confirmed that the Random Forest model performed well across all categories. These findings highlight that integrating facial landmark analysis with machine learning enhances automated beauty assessment and underscores the robustness of the Random Forest model in achieving higher accuracy and a balanced trade-off between precision and recall.

title: The BOOL.I Corpus: The Impact of Context on Child and Adolescent Bengali ASR
authors: Kawchar Husain, Samia Preity, Moksedur Rahman Sohan, Anowar Hossain, M. Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper presents a Bangladeshi Bangla speech corpus for children and adolescents and evaluates state-of-theart Bengali Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models on this data. Although significant progress has been made in the construction of Bangladeshi Bangla corpora, dedicated resources for young speakers remain relatively underexplored. Our corpus contains 16,069 segmented utterances from speakers aged 5 to 18 years and is designed to support research on child and adolescent speech in Bangladeshi Bangla. Using a four-stage evaluation framework consisting of preprocessing, unified inference, error computation, and WER distribution analysis, we benchmark four leading pretrained models: bengali-whisper-medium and three wav2vec2-xls-r-300m variants. bengali-whisper-medium achieves the strongest performance with a Micro WER of 0.4092 and a Micro CER of 0.2131, outperforming all wav2vec2 based systems. A fine-grained analysis shows that 16.47% of segments achieve perfect recognition and 22.71% fall within WER < 0.3. Interestingly, performance varies by utterance length: short 1-5 word utterances yield the highest mean WER (0.4981), while longer utterances (16+ words) show substantially better accuracy (mean WER <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.37$</tex>). This finding reveals that contextual information helps ASR systems overcome acoustic variability in children's speech, demonstrating the critical role of context-aware modeling for young speakers.

title: Advanced Deep Learning Framework for Ultrasound Breast Cancer Classification Using CNNs and Vision Transformers with Explainable AI
authors: Nafisa Tasnim, Shahriar Siddique Arjon, Tamanna Yasmin, Nymur Rahaman Antor, Al Arian Ahmad, Mohammad Ali Sakib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Breast cancer remains a leading cause of mortality among women worldwide, making early and accurate classification crucial. Despite being an essential diagnostic tool, manual interpretation of ultrasound images is time-consuming and prone to error. Leveraging Explainable AI (XAI) techniques, this study presents an advanced deep learning framework for automated breast cancer classification using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs). Utilizing a publicly available dataset of 9,016 annotated breast ultrasound images, we evaluate several state-of-the-art architectures, including DenseNet201, ResNeSt50, ConvNeXtV2, ViT-B16, ViTB/32, Swin Transformer V2, and RepVGG. Compared with existing approaches, our proposed ViT-B/32 model surpasses them, achieving 99.67% validation accuracy, 0.9999 AUC, and minimal misclassifications. Transparency for medical practitioners is enhanced through attention mechanisms and LIME-based interpretability, providing clinically meaningful insights into model decision-making. Extensive evaluations, including confusion matrix analysis and t-SNE visualization, validate our framework's robustness. The results demonstrate that transformer-based models integrated with XAI offer superior interpretability and accuracy, making them highly suitable for clinical applications in breast cancer diagnosis.

title: KNN-Based Real-Time Sign Language Recognition Using Flex Sensor Smart Glove with Real-Time Response
authors: Aadhithya T, Sharon Juliet P F, P. Nisha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Communication challenges experienced by individuals with hearing and speech impairments significantly limit their participation in everyday social and professional activities. This paper presents a real-time sign language interpretation system developed using Internet of Things (IoT) and Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) techniques through a wearable smart glove. The proposed system uses flex sensors to detect finger bending, hand orientation, and motion. The sensor outputs are processed using an Arduino Nano microcontroller and classified using machine learning algorithms to convert gestures into text output in real time. Experimental evaluation shows reliable gesture recognition with low latency and improved adaptability to different user signing patterns. The developed solution is lowcost, portable, and easy to use, making it suitable for applications in education, healthcare, workplaces, and public environments. The system can also be connected to a laptop or LCD display to provide immediate visual feedback, thereby enhancing usability and accessibility.

title:
Structural and Dielectric Features of Na, W dualdoped Bi

                    Sr

                    Co
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Ceramics for Energy Storage Applications
authors: Uzma Hira, Tajammul Hussain Tahir, Muhammad Zeeshan Amjad, Zaki Uddin, Nabeela Noor
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: In this paper, a new series of cobaltite materials is designed for energy storage and supercapacitor applications. $\mathrm{Bi}_{2-2 y} \mathrm{Na}_{2 y} \mathrm{Sr}_{2} \mathrm{Co}_{2-y} \mathrm{~W}_{y} \mathrm{O}_{x}$ polycrystalline cobaltite materials have been fabricated using solid-state reaction. All of the prepared samples are phase-pure, according to X-ray powder diffraction (XRD) data, and have monoclinic misfit layered structures in which hexagonal CoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and rock-salt BiSrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layers are alternatively stacked along the unit cell’s c-axis. According to Koop’s theory, the loss tangent and dielectric loss both drop with increasing frequency, indicating Maxwell-Wagner type polarization in the material. Additionally, conductivity relaxation is shown by the hump in the electric modulus plot, and a non-Debye type conduction mechanism is revealed by the Nyquist and ColeCole studies.

title: Asymmetrically Perturbed Dual-Band Patch Antenna for X-Band Applications
authors: Md Faruk Muntasir Banna, Mehedi Hasan Munna, Md Aminul Islam, Fariha Tasnim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth and development of X-band ap-plications-such as satellite communication links and unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) systems has created a strong demand for low-profile, dual-band antennas that ensure stable impedance performance while maintaining simple fabrication. This work presents the design and simulation of a compact microstrip antenna based on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> array of inset-fed rectangular patches incorporating an asymmetric slot perturbation. Using Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\epsilon_{r}=2.2, \tan \delta=0.0009\right.$</tex>, thickness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\mathbf{1. 6 ~ m m}$</tex>), the optimized unit cell resonates at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.235 \mathbf{~ G H z}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.06 \mathbf{~ G H z}$</tex>, achieving return losses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-44 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-28 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> with corresponding peak gains of 8 dBi and 7.96 dBi. In <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times$</tex> 2 array configuration, resonances occur at 8.07 GHz and 9.48 GHz due to inter-element coupling, with return losses of -23 dB and -22 dB, respectively. The corresponding -10 dB impedance bandwidths are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.93-8.24 \text{GHz}(310 \text{MHz}, 3.83 \%)$</tex> and 9.37-9.58 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{GHz}(210 \text{MHz}, 2.22 \%)$</tex>. The array achieves peak gains of 9.5 dBi and 8.1 dBi with radiation efficiencies of approximately 96.2 % and 94.5 % at the respective resonant frequencies. The array demonstrates enhanced directivity and efficient radiation performance across both operating bands. With its single-layer design, compact footprint, and strong dual-band characteristics, the proposed antenna is well suited for integration into modern X-band communication systems.

title: Edge-Based Respiratory Profiling: An Explainable Hierarchical Framework for Automated COPD Screening
authors: Hassan Jubair, Abid Ahasan, Mithela Mehenaz, Nabomita Sarkar, Md. Nurunnabi Mollah, Md. Merajul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Respiratory diseases such as COPD are a leading cause of global mortality, yet automated screening tools are often hindered by the high computational cost of Deep Learning models and a lack of clinical interpretability. Furthermore, many high-performance benchmarks in the literature are inflated by methodological flaws, specifically data leakage during validation. This study proposes a Hierarchical Explainable Framework that decouples event detection from disease diagnosis to achieve high-precision screening on edge devices. First, a lightweight Random Forest classifier, engineered with 156 statistical spectral features, detects adventitious sounds (wheezes/crackles) with an inference latency of 0.67 ms, reducing computational cost by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4}$</tex> times compared to CNN baselines. Second, a patient-level statistical profiling module aggregates these discrete events to generate a robust diagnostic score. Our hierarchical approach achieves a competitive cycle-level accuracy of 55.5% and a Diagnostic AUC of 0.90 in distinguishing COPD patients from healthy controls. Explainability analysis identifies harmonic variability (Chroma features) as a superior biomarker for obstruction compared to standard spectral envelopes. These results demonstrate that computationally frugal edge classifiers can yield high-precision clinical insights when aggregated via robust statistical profiling.

title: Integrating Pap Smear Imaging for Enhanced Deep Learning-Based Cervical Cancer Screening
authors: Pradeep Kumar, Santosh Kumar Choudhary, Manoj Anumolu, Kreshnaa Priiyaa, Sharmitha Vasantham, Manoj Kumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Cervical cancer is a worldwide health concern. In short, the detection of cervical cancer through the Pap smear test will certainly contribute to saving a large number of lives. But the cumbersome process of checking the results of the Pap smear test by the expert only, based on human intuition, results in biased outcomes. A number of deep learning and Machine learning techniques developed but the efficacy is limited for Pap smear images. In short, a novel automatic system for the detection of abnormal cervical cells by incorporating deep learning with Focused Mask Scoring R-CNN is proposed in this research paper which incorporate a lightweight squeeze-andexcitation (SE) block into the mask head to recalibrate channelwise feature responses. In the experiment, a high accuracy of 97.05 % is achieved by the model. In addition, the average precision, recall and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.97,0.98$</tex>, and 0.97 are obtained through the use of the testing data, respectively. Further, the area under the ROC curve is found to be greater than 0.95 by using the novel automatic system introduced in this paper, the experimental output shows the model is performing well as compare to existing state of art techniques.

title: A Transformer-Based Perspective to Bangla-English Code-Mixed Sentiment Analysis
authors: Sandwip Dash Talukder, Debajyoti Das, Mahmudul Hasan Badhan, Rubel Ahmod, Md Saidul Islam, Islam Md Saiful
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Despite Bangla being the seventh most spoken language worldwide, it remains a low-resource language in Natural Language Processing, particularly concerning Bangla-English (BE) code-mixed text. While Banglish (transliterated Bangla) has been studied, sentiment analysis of text containing native Bangla script alongside English words remains underexplored. This paper addresses this gap by introducing BE-CodeMix-3, a manually annotated sentiment dataset of 8,179 social media comments categorized into three sentiment classes. We provide a comprehensive evaluation across various architectures, including traditional Recurrent Neural Networks (LSTM, BiLSTM, GRU) and transformer-based models (mBERT, XLMRoBERTa, and BanglaBERT). Experimental results demonstrate that language-specific pretraining is critical for capturing the nuances of code-mixed text. The BanglaBERT model significantly outperforms all baselines, achieving a peak accuracy and F1-score of 0.90. Our findings highlight that while multilingual models provide a strong foundation, transformers pretrained specifically on large-scale Bangla corpora offer superior semantic and syntactic encoding for real-world, codemixed applications.

title: Investigation of SVD-Based Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Divya Sharma, Kushalatha M R, H. Mallika, Garima Joshi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Over time, research activity in the domain of Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) is steadily pacing, utilizing Matlab and Python interfaces. It involves analyzing input speech to discern the underlying emotional state being conveyed. This process typically undergoes stages like feature extraction, feature matching, classification, and database integration. Emotions recognized by the system encompasses anger, worry, happiness, neutrality, and gloomy. Various models enable the analysis of specific characteristics within the speech signal, allowing the system identification of emotional state expressed. The main objective of this work is to present the implementation of the Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) algorithm in combination with varied classifiers and speech emotion databases for detecting human's emotions and evaluating the performance of SVD-SER with respect to different classifiers. The results show that SVD with CNN SER gives higher accuracy as compared to other classification methods. LSTM also exhibits good precision but gives more number of false negatives.

title: Enhancing Security in Multicasting Using Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface and Selection Combining Diversity Technique
authors: Shafi Abid, Md. Zahurul Islam Sarkar, Muhtasib Farhan, Ahshan Habib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper analyzes physical-layer secure multicasting in a wireless network that utilizes reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) utilizing selection combining (SC) diversity receivers. Although RIS enhances signal directionality, it is still vulnerable to deep fading. This work shows that the integration of SC provides a vital diversity advantage that RIS alone cannot attain. Hence it successfully mitigates deep fading in secure multicast scenarios. Analytical formulations in closed form have been established for the Probability Density Function (PDF), Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF), and Ergodic Secrecy Multicast Capacity (ESMC). The results confirm the study and demonstrate that the proposed RIS-assisted SC technique achieves significant enhancements in secrecy compared to the non-diversity system, especially at higher Signal-to-Noise Ratios (SNR). Moreover, the findings suggest that increasing the number of RIS reflecting elements improves secrecy performance, whereas an increased number of multicast users decreases ESMC due to the selection of the lowest SNR value at the destination user.

title: An Intelligent Student Management System Using Temporal Stability-Aware Adaptive Learning
authors: Aihui Nie, Zhixin Yao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, student management systems played a critical role in modern educational institutions by allowing effectively handling of academic records, attendance tracking and student performance monitoring. In existing big data-driven decision tree-based student management platform is used to classify and monitor student performance and behavior. However, this existing approach struggled with overfitting which is caused by frequent learning updates and restricted scalability while assisting continuous real-time data that limited its efficiency in deployment. To overcome these challenges, Temporal Stability-Aware Adaptive Learning Layer (TSALL) is proposed for an improved student management system. Primarily, data is collected from multiple real-world sources and preprocessed to remove inconsistencies and data is normalized for ensuring reliability. Further, TSALL analyzes student data across many time periods for identifying stable behavior and patterns of performance. Here, learning updates are selectively triggered only when consistent patterns persist over time by avoiding unrequired model complexity and reduces computational overhead. Moreover, data is employed to classify student performance and update individual student's profiles in real-time. Finally, proposed TSALL-based student management system efficiently reduces model complexity by supporting real-time student data processing. Additionally, by learning only from stable student behavior patterns, proposed TSALL obtained better generalization and reliable performance. Therefore, proposed TSALL framework achieved better results in accuracy (95.1 %) and precision (97.4 %) compared to existing approach.

title: AI-Powered Hybrid Management Framework for Autonomous Vendor Evaluation and Procurement Optimization
authors: Mohyedin Hamza, M Swapna, Navdeep Singh, K Bhasakara Rao, Krishna Bhargavi Y, Kala Priydarshini G
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The optimization of procurement is an acute issue that enterprise organizations face working in unstable supply chains and dynamic regulatory environments. The old vendor management systems tend to be based on restrictive and rule-driven appraisals that just cannot evolve to non-linear and complex procurement world. This paper suggests employing AI-based hybrid management and uses rare and advanced algorithms such as a Quantum-Inspired Decision Matrix (QIDM), Swarm-Driven Contractual Risk Assessment (SDCRA), Graph-Based Trust Propagation Network (GTPN) and Evolutionary Procurement Policy Optimizer (EPPO). The model itself analyses vendors, identifies risks, advances procurement policies, and makes transparency available with blockchain-backed audit trails. The comparison of 50 procurement scenarios conducted through experimental analysis demonstrated that QIDM obtained a vendor ranking accuracy of 96, SDCRA had a risk detection precision of 93.3, and EPPO could allow saving up to 13.4 in procurement costs. Moreover, GTPN achieved a mean error of prediction of trust of only 2.65 which is better than conventional evaluation models. Additional support of multimodal sentiment analysis and neuro-fuzzy reinforcement decision layers further enhanced the profile of vendors and minimized conflict in decision by 47%. This model shows a sound, decipherable, and changeable approach to contemporary procurement issues. The next phase of work will involve the adoption of real-time IoT-built supply chains, including moral AI limits, and joint procurement through federated learning architecture.

title: Predicting Genre Crossovers in Book Recommendations Using Hybrid Machine Learning Models
authors: Estiyak Rubaiat, Jahir Uddin Mohammad Babar, Tojammel Jakaria Jenin, Mirza Fahim Abdullah, MD. Saif Al Shahriar, Sharfuddin Mahmood
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Traditional book recommendation systems struggle to predict genre crossover tendencies in modern books that frequently blend multiple genres, as they rely heavily on static metadata like genre tags and titles. This paper proposes a hybrid model that integrates sentiment analysis of user reviews, sequential pattern mining (via Apriori), and machine learning techniques-including XGBoost for feature-based prediction, Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) for modeling reading sequences-connected through a novel sentiment-guided structured hybrid integration layer. The approach captures emotional engagement and longterm user behavior to predict dynamic genre transitions. Experiments on a dataset of 10,872 books and over 52,000 ratings/reviews from Goodreads and Amazon show the proposed model achieves a top- 3 accuracy of 98.3% and an F1score of 78.6% (micro-averaged), significantly outperforming prior single-genre or non-hybrid methods. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of sentiment analysis and pattern mining. This work advances personalized, adaptive book recommendations by better handling genre fluidity in contemporary literature.

title: Research on Power Domain Anti-Jamming Methods Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: XingXiang Huang, Lin Qi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: With the intensifying confrontation between intelligent jamming and anti-jamming technologies and the continuous enhancement of system adaptability, both sides are adjusting their strategies in response to the other’s actions. This paper proposes the anti-jamming method LOLA and its improved version LOLA-DQN based on reinforcement learning in the context of power domain confrontation. Also, we employ game theory to describe agent-agent interactions.This algorithm gains a greater advantage in power confrontation among agents by learning the opponent’s gradient and combining it with its own learning network.

title: Energy-Efficient Green Computing Techniques for Edge Devices Using Attention-Guided Federated Learning
authors: Deepa Dawadi, S R V. Prasad Reddy, R. Deepa, S. Punitha, Aarthy. G
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, an energy efficient green computing methods for edge devices has gained attention because the rapid growth of intelligent Internet of Things (IoT) applications and the rising deployment of edge intelligence in resource needed environment. Whereas in such systems Federated Learning (FL) emerges as an effective pattern for distributed model training while storing data privacy. However, the existing Adaptive Decision Algorithm (ADA) method based on optimization driven resource allocation suffer additional computational and exhibit limited adaptability under dynamic workload and energy conditions. To address these issues, this research presents Federated Attention-Aware Network (FedAA-Net) a learning driven green computing framework was designed for edge devices operating under FL. Whereas, in this proposed system locally generated edge data is processed without centralized transmission and edge devices performs energy aware local training under the coordination of federated server. Then, an attention deep learning controller is embedded at every edge device to regulate computation and communication behavior by prioritizing low energy execution features. Next, this enables adaptive control of transmission scheduling, CPU utilization and federated participation without iterative optimization. Finally, the federated server aggregates lightweight model updates by using standard federated averaging, scalable coordination and providing privacy preservation. Experimental evaluation presents that the proposed framework reduced entire energy consumption and learning completion time while enhancing task level energy efficiency compared to the existing ADA model by confirming its effectiveness for sustainable edge intelligence.

title: Comprehensive Dynamic Modeling and Control of the Flexible Foundation-Active Magnetic Bearings-Rotor System
authors: Yang Yan, Ximing Liu, Kun Wang, Xi Chen, Shiqiang Zheng
abstract: Conventional dynamic models lack the capability to incorporate the intricate coupling effects and resulting dynamic uncertainties when the magnetically suspended flywheel (MSF) is supported by a flexible foundation, leading to restricted performance in current vibration suppression methods. To address this challenge, a comprehensive framework for dynamic modeling and computation is presented in this article. This article introduces velocity spinors to describe system motion efficiently and calculate kinetic energy. This approach significantly simplifies the calculation process in modeling the high-dimensional flexible foundation-active magnetic bearings (AMBs)-rotor system without compromising accuracy. With this model, we quantitatively analyze the influence of flexible support characteristics and flywheel control parameters on system stability and coupled vibration. Furthermore, corresponding methods for stable control and vibration suppression are proposed. The accuracy of the established dynamic model and computational framework is validated through experiments performed on the MSF prototype. This model allows researchers to optimize the support characteristics of the flexible foundation and the flywheel control parameters before on-orbit operation, ensuring the stable operation and active vibration suppression of the MSF under varying support conditions.
