abstract: Road traffic accidents represent a critical public safety challenge, particularly in developing countries where rapid urbanization, increasing vehicle density, and limited enforcement mechanisms contribute to high injury and fatality rates. This study conducts a comprehensive investigation of multi-class road traffic accident severity prediction using machine learning and neural network models based exclusively on categorical accident attributes derived from official accident reports. A cleaned real-world dataset comprising 12,316 accident records from Addis Ababa, Ethiopia (2017-2020) with 14 categorical predictor variables is utilized, and accident severity is formulated as a multi-class classification problem. In addition to a baseline Random Forest classifier and a fully connected neural network, the study incorporates gradient boosting algorithms (XGBoost, LightGBM, Gradient Boosting), imbalance-aware techniques including SMOTE and focal loss, and statistical significance testing. Feature importance analysis using SHAP and permutation importance provides explainability insights. Experimental results show that the Random Forest achieves 84% accuracy, while the neural network attains 85% in the baseline study. Random Forest with SMOTE achieves 69.0% accuracy on sample data, outperforming other models. Despite improvements, minority severity classes remain challenging to predict due to severe class imbalance, indicating the need for further methodological enhancements.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Real-Time Traffic Risk Prediction and Safety Enforcement
authors: Md. Owafeeuzzaman Patwary, Apurbo Biswas, Tonny Shekha Kar, Firoz Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing frequency of road accidents and traffic violations highlights the need for proactive, real-time risk detection systems. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning framework for real-time traffic risk prediction and safety enforcement using traffic surveillance footage. The proposed system integrates YOLOv8 for vehicle detection, DeepSORT for multi-object tracking, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal behavior modeling, and XGBoost for final risk classification. Driving behavior features such as speed variation, braking patterns, and lane changes are extracted as time-series representations and fused with contextual factors including simulated weather conditions and driver fatigue. The lightweight YOLOv8-nano backbone enables efficient real-time deployment, while multimodal feature fusion enhances robustness. Experiments conducted on the UA-DETRAC dataset demonstrate strong detection performance with an mAP@0.5 of 85.3%. The risk prediction module achieves 92.4% classification accuracy and an AUC-ROC of 0.961, indicating reliable discrimination between low- and high-risk driving behaviors. Ablation studies further confirm the importance of temporal modeling and contextual feature integration. The proposed framework provides an effective and scalable solution for intelligent transportation systems, contributing to proactive traffic monitoring and enhanced urban road safety.

title: Explainable Federated Learning Approach for Early Detection and Diagnosis of Brain Stroke in Clinical Healthcare Systems
authors: Siliveri Rajender, Pramod Kumar. P
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Stroke continues to be among the major causes of death and disability globally. On the other hand, stroke predictive models developed using centralized machine learning approaches remain hampered by poor interpretability, issues related to data privacy, and inefficiency in processing decentralized patient data. In this study, to mitigate these challenges associated with stroke predictive tasks, there is a proposal for the development of an Explainable Federated Learning platform incorporated with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to develop a transparent stroke predictive model. Based on the Stroke Prediction Dataset available on Kaggle, the new model was tested among decentralized nodes to maintain data privacy. The new platform exhibited improved communication complexity and quicker model convergence compared to previous centralized approaches. The developed model indicated 98.9% accuracy, 98.6 % precision, 98.2 % recall rate, 99.1 % specificity rate, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98.4 \% \text{F1}$</tex> score. Fundamentally, the Explainable Federated LSTM model offers a good enhancement in predictive accuracy, privacy maintenance, and interpretable results to help clinicians arrive at reliable and transparent decisions for early detection of stroke. This study focuses on the evaluation of an explainable federated learning system for tabular stroke prediction and is not oriented toward the development of new algorithms. The results are indicative that privacy-preserving training coupled with explainability will allow clinicians to make transparent and reliable decisions.

title: GNN-Enhanced Transfer Learning for Joint Power and Spectrum Allocation in Underlay D2D Communications
authors: Linfeng Chen, Yijun Wang
abstract: Device-to-Device (D2D) underlay communications can significantly improve spectrum efficiency, but joint power and spectrum allocation under imperfect Channel State Information (CSI) remains challenging due to complex non-Euclidean interference relationships. This paper proposes a Graph Neural Network (GNN)-enhanced transfer learning framework for joint resource allocation in underlay D2D systems. The framework first employs a transfer learning-based DNN to migrate knowledge from perfect CSI to imperfect CSI scenarios, greatly reducing training data requirements. A novel GNN-based interference graph module is then introduced, which dynamically constructs interference graphs from CSI and user locations. Multi-layer graph convolutions and a lightweight edge-attention mechanism extract high-level spatial embeddings, which are fused with raw features and fed into the transfer learning DNN for end-to-end optimization. Simulations show that the proposed scheme achieves up to 18.4% higher sum-rate, 17.5% improved energy efficiency, and 33.5% lower outage probability compared to the transfer learning baseline, with only 15% additional training overhead, while approaching 93% of CVX optimal performance. Results validate the effectiveness of graph-based modeling for practical D2D resource allocation under imperfect CSI.

title: A Comparative Study of Modern Object Detectors for Robust Apple Detection in Orchard Imagery
authors: Mohammed Asad, Ajai Kumar Gautam, Priyanshu Dhiman, Rishi Raj Prajapati
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: For predicting yields, counting apples, robotic harvesting, and monitoring crops, it is very important to be able to accurately detect apples in orchard images. But changes in light, leaf clutter, thick fruit clusters, and partial occlusion often make performance worse. To provide a fair and reproducible comparison, this study establishes a controlled benchmark for single-class apple detection on the public AppleBBCH81 dataset using one deterministic train/validation/test split and a unified evaluation protocol across six representative detectors: YOLOv10n, YOLO11n, RT-DETR-L, Faster R-CNN (ResNet50-FPN), FCOS (ResNet50-FPN), and SSDLite320 (MobileNetV3-Large). Performance is evaluated primarily using COCO-style mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95, and threshold-dependent behavior is further analyzed using precision-recall curves and fixed-threshold precision, recall, and F1-score at IoU = 0.5. On the validation split, YOLO11n achieves the best strict localization performance with mAP@0.5:0.95 = 0.6065 and mAP@0.5 = 0.9620, followed closely by RT-DETR-L (0.6012 and 0.9506) and YOLOv10n (0.5941 and 0.9501). At a fixed operating point with confidence ≥ 0.05, YOLOv10n attains the highest F1-score (0.780870), whereas RT-DETR-L achieves very high recall (0.970218) but minimum precision (0.224605) because of many false positives at low confidence. These findings show that detector selection for orchard deployment should be guided not only by localization-aware accuracy but also by threshold robustness and the requirements of the downstream task.

title: CSI-RS Overhead Reduction in 6G
authors: Zhengxiang Ma, Zhigang Rong, Jialing Liu, Baoling Sheen, Ruikang Yang, Juan M. Roa, Renjian Zhao, Weimin Xiao, Miguel Dajer, Anthony C.K. Soong
abstract: This paper explores techniques to reduce the CSI-RS overhead for massive MIMO in 6G. A structured mapping between antenna ports and CSI-RS REs is introduced to facilitate lowering the CSI-RS density in terms of RE/RB/port. Its performance advantage over unstructured mapping is studied. Although these techniques are powerful, the performance improvements were limited. Hence, AI/ML based algorithms are also explored and our results showed that significant CSI-RS overhead reduction is possible without impacting performance.

title: Fuzzy Logic-Based Congestion Estimation in Network-on-Chip Routers Under Video Object Plane Decoder Traffic
authors: Denis Kutuzov, Alexey Osovsky, Nataliya Maltseva, Georgy Popov
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper proposes an approach for proactive detection of congestion in routers of Network-on-Chip (NoC) systems based on fuzzy logic. The input parameters of the model include the packet injection rate (PIR), the average occupancy of router input buffers, and the structural metric of Betweenness Centrality (BC), enabling the joint consideration of both dynamic traffic characteristics and the topological importance of nodes. To construct the dataset, cycle-accurate NoC simulations were performed using the Noxim simulator. A regular <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> mesh topology with deterministic XY routing and Poisson traffic injection is considered. As a workload scenario, the VOPD (Video Object Plane Decoder) benchmark is used, representing a directed acyclic task graph with pronounced heterogeneity in both computational and communication demands. The developed fuzzy inference system provides an estimation of the probability of congestion occurrence at network nodes. The fuzzy logic framework enables accurate modeling of nonlinear relationships among system parameters and ensures smooth transitions between different network operating regimes.

title: Technical Advancements and Strategic Frameworks for the Maintenance and Repair of Civil Infrastructure Using Fiber-Reinforced Polymer (FRP) Materials
authors: Ahmed Alshaikha, Hamdy M. Mohamed, Mohamed Abduljalil Alam
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The deterioration of reinforced concrete (RC) infrastructure has emerged as a critical global concern, with maintenance backlogs resulting in substantial financial burdens each year. In the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) region, the combination of extreme temperature variations and high chloride exposure significantly accelerates the corrosion of steel reinforcement, often leading to premature structural degradation well before the intended design life is reached.

title: Business Intelligence Systems as a Strategic Driver of Digital Innovation
authors: Ibrahim A. Abu-AlSondos, Rana Husseini Frangieh, Saeed Hameed Aldulaimi, Worood Ahmad Melhem, Mahmoud Alghizzawi, Aws Raed Mahafdhah
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This work explores how artificial intelligence–when integrated into business intelligence systems-can become a strategic lever for digital innovation, while simultaneously becoming a potential source of trust erosion if accountability and governance principles are absent. Despite the widespread adoption of analytics and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> in business intelligence, the literature tends to divide into two paths: one that examines the impact of business intelligence and analytics on performance, decision-making, and competitive advantage and another that focuses on responsible AI principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, and risk management. This separation results in deficiency in explaining the “missing link” between responsibility as an organizational capacity and trust as an intermediary mechanism, and then between trust and sustainable competitive outcomes within digital innovation systems. This research adopts a theoretical approach based on an integrative literature review and the development of test proposals/hypotheses linking the dimensions of responsible integration and trust with the outputs of digital innovation and competitive advantage. It also identifies contextual variables (such as data governance maturity and organizational pressure) that can strengthen or weaken these relationships. The research contributes theoretically by bridging disparate literatures and practically by providing a guiding model that enables organizations to transform responsibility from a standard slogan into an operational and strategic capability that generates both value and trust.

title: Design of a Virtual Testbed for Modeling and Simulating a Small USV Dynamics in Waves
authors: Dmitriy A. Shlemin, Edgar M. Naissance, Oleg V. Kubryak
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: In this paper, a virtual testbed based on the MCSim Python library is presented for modeling and simulating the behavior of a small unmanned surface vessel (USV) under wave conditions. The developed simulation environment allows comprehensive analysis of the platform’s 6-DOF dynamics, as well as assessing the performance of the path following and motion control algorithms in the presence of wave disturbances.

title: IoT-Enabled Real-Time Power Quality Anomaly Detection and Disturbance Classification for Ghana/Zambia Distribution Networks
authors: Obed Asamoah Nyarko, Anderson Mwamba, Maxim Andreevich Silaev
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: Power quality monitoring in Sub-Saharan African distribution networks faces significant challenges due to unbalanced loading, long radial feeders, and a lack of automated detection infrastructure. This paper proposes a real-time Power quality monitoring framework using a hybrid convolutional neural network and bidirectional long short-term memory deep learning model designed for both utility and educational applications. Using a digital twin of a modified IEEE 13-bus system, parameterized to reflect Ghanaian and Zambian grid conditions, a dataset of 250 disturbance samples was generated at a 20 kHz sampling rate. By employing a sliding-window augmentation strategy, the proposed model achieved an overall classification accuracy of ninety percent, with near-perfect detection of voltage swells and harmonics. The results demonstrate the model's robustness in handling phase unbalance and short-duration transients. Beyond technical monitoring, the framework serves as a "Virtual Power quality Laboratory," bridging the gap between classical power engineering and AI-driven grid management for engineering students in the region

title: Methodology for Studying Noise in Digital Cameras
authors: V. L. Zhbanova, D. A. Filatov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper presents a method for studying digital camera noise. A color target based on the RAL color atlas has been developed. Three digital cameras were selected as research objects. Experimental studies were conducted. High noise levels were detected in the blue channel, caused by the illumination conditions during the experiment. To improve the quality of the experiment, a type A light source, which has a yellowish-orange tint, can be replaced with a source with a neutral white color temperature tint. Qualitative analysis can predict the technical capabilities of the receiver, evaluate the distortion of the input signal and the stability of the matrix. The developed noise determination method should be universal and easy to use, and the results are informative and visual. Such topics pertain to the implementation of information transmission systems and methods, signal reception and processing, as well as technologies for image and signal processing, analysis, and recognition, and computer vision, which constitute strategic directions for the development of science and technology. The proposed method formed the basis of laboratory work for the courses "Digital Image Processing" and "Optical and Light Measurements". Conducting experimental measurements will help students consolidate their knowledge of the alignment of optoelectronic devices and systems, as well as improve their knowledge of lighting engineering.

title: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection
authors: Nivedhidha M, Ramkumar M P, Dharani M, Emil Selvan G S R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) allows multiple participants to collaboratively build models while keeping their individual raw data private. This makes it an ideal fit for Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) deployed in distributed and privacy-sensitive environments. However, IDS that use FL share some weaknesses, including data from clients being non-Independent Identically Distributed "IID," unstable updates, and potential poisoning attacks. The Fed-IDS is an innovative approach to federated intrusion detection that seeks to address the problems posed by trust and these other issues through the use of Principal Component Analysis- (PCA) based clustering and distance-based anomaly measures. In the Fed-IDS framework, the initial pre-processing of the MalMem2022 dataset consists of centralizing missing values, label encoding, normalizing, and using SMOTE to augment data for each client through Dirichlet partitioning. After pre-processing, the dataset is redistributed to each client to create true distributed environments. Feature reduction is performed using PCA, and clusters are established using K-Means+ to determine cluster centroids to be used in the calculated distance from a sample to a centroid. These metrics are used to improve the feature matrix and aid in the classification and reliability estimation of clients. Local client learning is also conducted by calculating the mean cluster distance based upon each clients' client-side data.

title: Development of Software for Calculating Nuclear Reactor Components Using the Finite Element Method
authors: Dmitry E. Egorov, Elena V. Poznyak, Andrey T. Alekseev, Anton A. Tutnov, Evgeny S. Krutko, Vadim S. Tkachenko
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article describes software developed to numerically simulate thermal and mechanical processes in nuclear fuel rod components. Finite element analysis is used to assess the stress-strain state, accounting for transient thermal strains, pressure, and fuel swelling due to radiation. The software was developed as part of a student research project in the Department of Robotics, Mechatronics, Dynamics and Strength of Machines at the National Research University "Moscow Power Engineering Institute" (MPEI), "Applied Mechanics" specialization, educational program "Dynamics and Strength of Machines, Instruments, and Equipment".

title: AI-Transparency and Algorithmic Trust in Smart LMS Adoption: An Explainable-AI Extended TAM Model
authors: Boumedyen Shannaq, Said Almaqbali, Oualid Ali
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: With artificial intelligence (AI) integrated into Learning Management Systems (LMS), there has been academic discussion on problems of transparency, trust, and user anxiety, but these issues have not been properly researched in the conventional Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). This present work responds in this way by adding to the TAM framework with constructs of AI Decision Transparency, Trust in AI Decisions, and positioning AI Anxiety as a moderating variable. The survey was conducted through quantitative methodology, using convenience sampling, a total of 276 valid responses were received and further data analysis was done using SmartPLS. Behavioral Intention was explained by the structural model at 59.5 per cent (R 2 = 0.595). It has been found that AI Decision Transparency has a strong positive correlation with Trust (0.626, p 0.001) and that Trust, in its turn is significantly correlated with Perceived Usefulness (0.432, p 0.001) as well as Behavioral Intention (0.158, p 0.001). Even though the direct effect of AI Anxiety on the intention was substantial (0.321, p 0.001), the hypothesised moderating role was not reinforced. Comprehensively, the results emphasize the fact that transparency-based trust is a key process that enables the adoption of AI-enabled LMS, thus providing credible theoretical evidence and practical suggestions to the ethical use of AI in higher education.

title: Bridge-Selective Saturation-Guided Topology Optimization within a Hierarchical Framework for IPMSM Rotors
authors: Ziran Cheng, Jian Gao, Yufeng Zhao, Zhixiang He, Kun Liu, Shoudao Huang
abstract: This paper proposes a novel bridge-selective saturation-guided topology optimization (TO) method for interior permanent magnet synchronous motor (IPMSM) rotors within a hierarchical optimization framework. First, the outer-loop parameter optimization provides a baseline rotor configuration, and the nonlinear magnetic behavior of the rotor core is incorporated into the TO framework through a saturation-consistent material interpolation model. Then, the effects of element variation on the saturation states of both the main flux path and the flux-barrier bridge region are simultaneously considered. Next, a region-dependent saturation indicator is embedded into the sensitivity analysis and design updating scheme, such that excessive saturation in the main flux path is suppressed while a higher saturation level in the flux-barrier bridge region is promoted to increase local magnetic reluctance and reduce leakage flux. Finally, a 3-kW IPMSM prototype is built to validate the proposed method, demonstrating its effectiveness and accuracy.

title: A CUDA-Accelerated Iterative Inversion Framework for High-Resolution Brain MRI Reconstruction
authors: Anjali K S, Pankaj Krishna, Nagadevi S, Anand M, Sathyapriya L
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The reconstruction of high-resolution brain MRI is critical for accurate neurological diagnosis; however, conventional CPU-based reconstruction techniques incur substantial computational cost and prolonged processing time. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a cloud-based reconstruction framework implemented using a Jupyter Notebook environment (Notebook Everywhere), enabling scalable and hardware-flexible experimentation. The reconstruction pipeline is executed on both CPU and GPU platforms to facilitate systematic performance evaluation. GPU acceleration is assessed using NVIDIA RTX 3050, RTX 3060, and RTX 4060 GPUs. The framework employs optimized large-scale matrix operations and iterative solvers, including Conjugate Gradient and Regularized Least Squares methods, applied to raw k-space MRI data to generate high-resolution 2D and 3D brain images with improved noise suppression and spatial fidelity. Performance is quantitatively evaluated using GPU reconstruction time (ms), peak GPU memory usage (MB), throughput (images/sec), Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM). Experimental results indicate significant reductions in reconstruction time and improved throughput on GPU platforms while maintaining comparable PSNR and SSIM values, thereby preserving diagnostic image quality. These findings demonstrate that cloud-enabled GPU acceleration substantially enhances computational efficiency and supports near real-time MRI reconstruction for clinical and research applications.

title: An Explainable Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Approach for Personalized and Health-Constrained Meal Recommendation
authors: Bhavesh P, Nayantra Ramakrishnan, Nagendra Prabhu S, G. Shivashankar, Moorthi K, Vaissnave Vaissnave
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Personalized dietary recommendation systems must balance nutritional adequacy, health constraints, and user preferences while ensuring long-term adherence. However, existing approaches often struggle to model sequential dietary behavior and multi-nutrient dependencies simultaneously. To address these challenges, this work proposes a Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Model (HRM) for adaptive dietary recommendation. The framework integrates heterogeneous nutritional graph representations with transformer-based semantic food encoding to capture both structured nutritional relationships and contextual food understanding. A high-level policy generates long-term nutritional goals based on user health conditions, while a low-level policy selects meals that satisfy these goals under dietary constraints. The model is optimized using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to enable continuous learning from user feedback and evolving nutritional states. Experiments are conducted on real-world nutritional datasets combined with simulated dietary profiles representing diverse health conditions such as diabetes and general wellness. The proposed HRM is evaluated against heterogeneous graph neural networks, transformer-based recommenders, and neural collaborative filtering. Results show that HRM outperforms all baselines, achieving an 11.3% improvement in cumulative reward and a nutritional balance score of 88.9%. The framework also ensures high dietary safety with a 98.5% allergen-safe recommendation rate and improves user adherence to 92.6%. These findings demonstrate that hierarchical reinforcement learning provides an effective and adaptive framework for personalized dietary planning by jointly optimizing nutritional balance, safety, and user engagement.

title: Design of a Spaceborne Phased Array Antenna for Ultra-Low Orbit
authors: Shilin Dong, Jie Zhang, Yakai Dong, Zhanchun Fan
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The Earth’s atmosphere in the ultra-low orbit is characterized by relatively higher density and higher atomic oxygen concentration. In order to make the satellite fly better in the ultra-low orbit, all the equipment on the surface of the satellite needs to carry out low profile design and atomic oxygen protection design. Antenna is a necessary equipment on the surface of satellite. To solve the above problems, we proposed a design scheme of antiatomic oxygen tile-type phased array antenna in this paper. We adopt a tile-type design to reduce the projection area of the antenna in the direction of flight. To address the need for atomic oxygen protection, we proposed an improved scheme that adjusts the size of the antenna’s radiating element to offset the impact of atomic oxygen protection on the antenna’s resonant frequency, which can effectively reduce the loss of the antenna’s electrical performance. According to actual measurement verification, both the antenna’s standing wave and EIRP have been improved, which proved the proposed scheme has good engineering application value.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)

title: Mixed-Integer Programming for Multi-Agent Motion Planning and Encirclement of Moving Targets in 3D Space
authors: Runhui Long, Yun Wang, Hanyue Fan, Yifan Shen, Zhongqiang Ren, Sutthiphong Srigrarom
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This paper considers a Moving-Target Multi-Agent Motion Planning (MT-MAMP) problem that seeks collision-free paths for multiple agents to collectively encircle a few moving targets along straight lines, and each target has a requirement on the minimum number of agents for encirclement. MT-MAMP is challenging as it involves not only inter-agent collision avoidance, a fundamental challenge in MAMP, but also encirclement of moving targets that involves task allocation in a dynamic environment. While various approaches have been developed for MAMP and its variants, we are not aware of any prior research on MAMP that considers encirclement of moving targets. To address MT-MAMP, we formulate the problem as a mixed-integer program (MIP) by leveraging several existing techniques in the field of MIP for motion planning, and introduce a notion of shrinking encirclement, which allows the agents to tighten their encirclement as time evolves. We verify our MIP with up to 15 agents and 4 targets in various cases, which verifies the correctness and effectiveness of our approach for MT-MAMP.

title: Enhancing ACO Stability and Performance for Urban VRP via Clustering and Intersection Penalty
authors: Jin Xu, Jinxing Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This study addresses a complex variant of the Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) with capacity constraints and split deliveries. To address this issue, this paper proposes the Clustering and Intersection-aware ACO Algorithm, an Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) algorithm that incorporates a state transfer strategy based on clustering and modifies the pheromone updating strategy through the introduction of an Intersection Penalty Factor. This reduces redundant path crossings and improves solution quality. The experimental results demonstrate that the Clustering and Intersection-aware ACO Algorithm outperforms the traditional ACO algorithm in urban distribution across a range of map sizes, markedly reducing the overall cost of distribution paths, and yielding a highly stable solution. This research presents a novel approach for optimizing complex VRPs, offering valuable insights for practical logistics scheduling.

title: Drag Free and Attitude Control System design for gravitational wave detection satellites
authors: Haoyue Zhang, Dong Ye, Peng Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: This paper focuses on the Drag-Free and Attitude Control System (DFACS) design for space-based gravitational wave detection missions. First, based on the stringent scientific requirements, the control objectives and the operation scheme in science mode are formulated. Second, the environmental and actuation noises acting on the spacecraft are modeled in the frequency domain using transfer functions, and a complete dynamical model of the spacecraft with two test masses is established. To address the strong couplings inherent in this system, a decoupling procedure is performed, resulting in single-input single-output (SISO) control loops. Subsequently, for each loop, sensitivity and complementary sensitivity functions are constructed according to the control objectives, and a $H_{\infty}$ suboptimal control problem is formulated within the frequency domain framework. Considering that solar radiation pressure (SRP) dominates in the ultralowfrequency band of the deep-space environment, an additional feedforward compensation channel is introduced on top of the traditional $H_{\infty}$ controller to pre-cancel the induced forces and torques, thereby alleviating the burden on the control loops, releasing robustness margins and improving the overall control performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed DFACS effectively suppresses environmental disturbances and actuator noises, ensuring that both the residual accelerations of the test masses and the inter-satellite pointing errors satisfy the stringent scientific specifications, thus verifying the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed control scheme.

title: Gradient Design and Verification of Lightweight High-Temperature Thermal Protection Components
authors: Haoning Shi, Le Mi, Kun Liu, Teng Liu, Fankong Meng, Yu Jin, Yun Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Manned lunar exploration spacecraft face significant challenges in high-temperature thermal protection due to extremely high heat flux plume during landing and takeoff, as well the high-temperature radiation caused by multiple engine coupling. To meet the high-temperature thermal protection requirement and lightweight constraints of spacecraft, a gradient design method of high-temperature thermal protection component is proposed, which employs lightweight materials with high-performance. A transient thermal analysis model for high-temperature thermal protection components is established by coupling radiation conduction and interlayer thermal conduction. The optimized lightweight design can be conducted based on the established model. Focusing on the typical thermal protection requirements of a manned lunar exploration spacecraft, the design of high-temperature thermal protection components is completed. Experimental verification indicates that the components can satisfy the thermal protection requirements of a surface temperature of $850^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ on the outer surface for 930 seconds, with a temperature rise on the inner side not exceeding $55{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The areal density of the proposed hightemperature thermal protection components is less than 3.2 $\mathrm{kg} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$. This research provides a reference for the design of hightemperature thermal protection systems for spacecraft.

title: A Multi-Segment Logarithmic Spiral Continuum Robot With a Stretchable Central Backbone: Design and Coupled Kinematic Modeling
authors: Huaicheng Huang, Pingsong Deng, Guolei Wang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel continuum robot design inspired by the logarithmic spiral, uniquely characterized by its stretchable central backbone and segment-based actuation. This bio-inspired structure grants the robot exceptional flexibility and an extensive workspace. The robot is characterized by its stretchable elastic backbone and segment-based actuation. We developed a detailed coupled kinematic model based on an enhanced piecewise constant curvature (PCC) framework, which incorporates dynamic compensation for backbone length and a systematic analysis of coupling effects. This allows for mapping high-level shape commands directly to actuator controls. A 12-DoF physical prototype was constructed to validate our model experimentally. The qualitative results demonstrate the robot's extensive workspace and its remarkable ability to achieve complex configurations, including S-shapes and spatial spirals, confirming the design's high flexibility. Furthermore, a coupled kinematic model is developed to effectively map shape commands to actuator controls, enabling the robot to achieve complex configurations. Experimental validation confirms the model's effectiveness in controlling the robot's unique morphology in open-loop scenarios.

title: Numerical Simulations of Particles Fluidization and Transportation in the Asteroid Sample
authors: Xu Kong, Yuncheng Lin, Jianfeng Man, He Zhang, Fuming Zeng, Fan Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The gas-driven fluidization and transportation of particles are simulated using Euler-discrete element method in the asteroid sample. The distributions of velocities of gas and particles are predicted in the sample. Two zone structure with a core and an annulus are appeared along radial direction, achieving particles fluidization. The pneumatic conveying of particles consists of an induction time and initial fluidization and transport time under the gas driven condition. The maximum collecting solid mass flux exists at the time of about 2.5 s. Simulated exit solid mass flux agree with experimental measurements at the atmospheric environment.

title: Dynamic Load-Aware Task Scheduling Method for Aerospace Situation Simulation with DMA-LSTM Prediction
authors: Runnan Qin, Wenming Xie, Wenlin Tang, Xiao Zheng, Jingru Dong, Jingyi Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: To meet the demand for large-scale data-model fusion computation in aerospace situation simulation, we propose a task scheduling method capable of perceiving dynamic load changes. Based on a container cloud architecture, this method utilizes a designed DMA-LSTM (Double Moving Average - Long Short Term Memory network) network to predict node load, enabling dynamic load migration to optimize resource allocation. It effectively enhances node scaling efficiency and ensures load balancing during large-scale computation. Experimental results demonstrate that this task scheduling method outperforms mainstream algorithms-including elastic scaling algorithms (HPA, DMA-HPA) and task scheduling algorithms (round-robin, pure random, weighted random methods)-in terms of node scaling efficiency, task response time, and load balancing, achieving superior resource allocation and availability.

title: A Soft Voting Ensemble Approach for Early Prediction of Cardiovascular Disease Risk
authors: V. Chandana Sri, P. Amrutha Nagavalli, James Deva Koresh Hezekiah
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Cardiovascular disorders remain among the most serious health challenges worldwide, making early risk identification extremely important. This study proposes a machine learning–based framework for predicting the likelihood of heart disease by combining XGBoost and Random Forest algorithms into a unified ensemble model. The dataset is carefully prepared through median-based handling of missing values, normalization of input features, and correction of class imbalance using the SMOTETomek method. A soft voting mechanism is adopted to combine the outputs of both models, allowing the system to benefit from their complementary strengths. The ensemble achieves a testing accuracy of 99.05%, demonstrating strong predictive capability and consistent classification performance. The results indicate that integrating multiple learning algorithms can significantly enhance reliability in heart disease risk prediction tasks.

title: Comparative Analysis of Detection and Segmentation Architectures using YOLO Variants and Deep Segmentation Models for Gastrointestinal Polyp Detection and Segmentation
authors: Sourav Das Polok, Md Zillur Rahman Saimon, Salil Batra, Pravallika Kondapalli, Muhammad Aumlanul Abrar, Gouri Shankar Chakraborty
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Gastrointestinal polyps are benign growth in the form of abnormal tissue structures that grow on the inner lining of the digestive system and have been found to be the main cause of colorectal cancer. These polyps need to be identified and carefully defined in endoscopic images early, to enable timely diagnosis, and to decrease mortality rates of cancer. Nevertheless, the automated analysis is quite a difficult task because of differences in the polyp size, shape, texture, and the illumination conditions. Comprehensive comparative analysis of advanced deep learning architectures gastrointestinal polyp detection as well as gastrointestinal polyp segmentation is performed on the Kvasir-SEG dataset in their study. In the detection task, the YOLOv9, YOLOv10, and YOLOv11 are evaluated based on preciseness, recall, F1-score, and the mean Average Precision (mAP). To segment, U-Net, U-Net++ and TransUNet are compared in their accuracy, Dice coefficient and Intersection over Union (IoU). Experiments prove that YOLOv11 has the highest detection accuracy, 93.8% whereas TransUNet has a better segmentation accuracy 96.9%. The results indicate the trade-off between predictive accuracy and model complexity and offer useful advice on how one can pick efficient architectures in clinical environments. The following work is to consider a hybrid segmentation architecture combining detection-based localization with a transformer segmentation network that promotes additional performance improvement and reduced computing costs.

title: Evaluating the Performance of Machine Learning Frameworks for Operating in Secure Enclaves
authors: A. A. Bardovsky, S. G. Fomicheva
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The general task of secure scaling, updating, and (pre)training deployed ML models is formalized, from which the particular optimization task of automated selection of “edge/fognode + enclave + ML-framework + ML-model” architectural configurations for edge/fog computing environments is highlighted. A comparative analysis of modern machine learning frameworks in the context of their readiness to function in crowded enclaves is carried out. The results of an experimental evaluation of the performance of these frameworks in Intel SGX enclaves are presented. The unavailability of the Tensor Flow and Tensor Flow Lite frameworks for functioning in these enclaves for real-time object detection is noted.

title: Automated System for Assessing the Degree of Deterioration of Road Markings Based on Deep Learning and Multiparametric Aggregation
authors: Artem Bagdasaryan, Sergei Gaponenko, Ksenia Polyantseva
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The paper presents a method for automated assessment of the condition of road markings based on computer vision and deep learning methods. An approach is proposed that combines neural network semantic segmentation with algorithmic formalization of defects in the form of a set of calculated features, including erasure, fading, fragmentation, sharpness of borders and geometric integrity. For segmentation, the U-Net, FCN, and DeepLabV3 architectures have been studied, fine-tuned on a combined dataset using combined loss functions and taking into account class imbalance. An algorithm for the integral assessment of the condition of road markings based on weighted aggregation of features with subsequent classification into three levels of suitability has been developed. Experimental results show that FCN and DeepLabV3 architectures provide more stable segmentation compared to U-Net. The proposed system makes it possible to obtain a reproducible and interpretable assessment of the condition of road markings and can be used in the tasks of automated monitoring of road infrastructure.

title: RIS Element Phase-Offset Calibration based on RSRP Reports
authors: Narayan Prasad, Tao Luo, Meilong Jiang, Kobi Ravid, Junyi Li
abstract: Calibration of a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) in cellular systems is constrained by a practical obstacle: low-complexity user equipment (UE) may report only reference signal received power (RSRP), in contrast to the complex-valued channel state information (CSI) or in-phase/quadrature (I/Q) measurements assumed by most prior art. This paper develops a complete framework for RIS phase-offset calibration from magnitude-only feedback. We formulate RSRP-based calibration as a constrained phase-retrieval problem under a single-offset-per-element impairment model, and propose a family of phase-only calibration codebooks based on—Hadamard-randomization, adjacent-element grouping, dither-plus-binary-quantization, and a hybrid schedule—that explicitly balance reflected power toward a designated buddy receiver against the pattern diversity required for magnitude-only recovery. We introduce a one-shot manifold-optimization solver that jointly updates the element drifts and unknown measurement phases on a single torus manifold, and extend it to an uncertainty-aware variant that accommodates bounded cascade-channel errors. We further adapt a zero-shot calibration principle from phased arrays to RISs for commissioning-time initialization. Simulations on quaternary RISs with one or more buddy receivers show that the proposed codebooks and solvers substantially outperform naïve and independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) random baselines and closely approach a genie upper bound.

title: Sequence Modeling Architectures: Foundations [Special Issue on the Mathematics of Deep Learning]
authors: Felix Sarnthein, Arshia Afzal, Razvan Pascanu, Antonio Orvieto, Caglar Gulcehre, Jonas Geiping, Francesco Tonin, Albert Gu, Volkan Cevher
abstract: With the success of deep learning in the past decade, neural networks transitioned from simple layer stacking to sophisticated, highly complex designs. This article provides an overview of such deep learning architectures for sequence modeling, with a focus on their core mathematical principles: 1) we review the evolution of common sequence models from recurrent neural networks (RNNs), to Transformers, and modern state-space models (SSMs), exploring their foundational components such as gating, attention, and linear dynamical systems mechanisms. 2) We present general linear sequence mixers as a unifying framework to highlight the relationship between architectures and compare them in terms of inductive biases and algorithmic complexity. 3) We examine how practical considerations such as parallelizability and hardware awareness dictate their success. Finally, we discuss their impact on diverse fields of signal processing, including time-series analysis, natural language, and computer vision.

title: A Current-Driven SSHI for Piezoelectric Vibration Energy Harvesters
authors: Luigi Costanzo, Alessandro Lo Schiavo, Massimo Vitelli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: An SSHI-based interface circuit for piezoelectric vibration energy harvesters is presented and experimentally validated. The developed solution relies on current-driven regulation of the rectifier output voltage downstream of a parallel SSHI stage. By measuring only the rectifier output current, the proposed Current-Driven SSHI (CD-SSHI) interface directly and continuously sets the rectified DC voltage to its optimal value under time-varying vibration conditions, without resorting to the perturbative approaches of classical Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) techniques. The CD-SSHI reaches and tracks the Maximum Power Point (MPP) without the steady-state oscillations typical of perturb and observe MPPT, and without the power interruptions characteristic of the fractional open circuit voltage MPPT. Moreover, the proposed CD-SSHI interface significantly increases dynamic performance compared to an SSHI coupled with a conventional implementation of the P&O MPPT control. A theoretical analysis based on a general equivalent circuit model is developed to support the proposed circuit. Experimental results obtained from a prototype implementation demonstrate the effectiveness of the CD-SSHI interface in terms of both steady-state efficiency and dynamic response.

title: An Integrated Simulation-Experimental Framework for Prediction of Pollution Flashover on Railway Catenary Insulators_supp1-3702664.mp4
authors: Huai Zhao
abstract: The pollution flashover is a key factor influencing the electrical performance of catenary insulators. The current studies about pollution flashover prediction of insulators based on the flow field and electric field are fragmented. In this work, a systematic prediction framework for pollution flashover of catenary insulators is developed by combining flow-electric coupling simulations and laboratory experiments. The temporal evolution of contamination mass density on the insulator surface is obtained from flow-field simulations and wind tunnel experiment. A mapping relationship between surface contamination and conductivity under water-saturated conditions is then established. By simulating the electric-field at different conductivity levels, the framework accurately predicts the evolution of the leakage current. The pre-flashover leakage-current amplitude is determined from flashover tests and used as the threshold for predicting the flashover instant. This work provides a theoretical basis for scheduling maintenance and enhancing the safety of railway catenary systems.

title: Memristor-Based Sensitization Nonassociative Learning Circuit and Its Application in Overheat Protection of Industrial Robots
authors: Junwei Sun, Mengfan Wu, Zicheng Wang, Yanfeng Wang
abstract: Most memristor-based nonassociative learning circuits only focus on habituation, while neglecting the research of sensitization and secondary sensitization. Therefore, this article designs a memristor-based sensitization learning circuit, which mainly consists of a habituation module, sensitization module, voltage control module, and secondary sensitization module. After receiving the signal from the voltage module, the habituation module and the sensitization module implement their functions, respectively. Second, this study investigates the influence of voltage stimuli on the formation rates of habituation and sensitization. Moreover, the functions of secondary sensitization and long/short-term sensitization processes have been successfully realized through the synergistic effect of the sensitization module and the secondary sensitization module. Finally, PSPICE software is used to verify the correctness of the design. The outcomes of simulations demonstrate that the circuit is capable of simulating the complex biological mechanisms mentioned earlier. Meanwhile, the circuit also realizes the application simulation of overheating protection for industrial equipment.

title: PositiveAssist: Energy-Gated Control for Positive Power Assistance of Exoskeleton in Walking, Running, and Stair Climbing
authors: Juncheng Zhou, Weibo Gao, Jin Sen Huang, Kurt Lin, Wanqi Wu, Hao Su
abstract: Assistance timing is a critical factor in exoskeleton control. This article introduces the energy-gated controller, which uses only two thigh-mounted inertial measurement units to estimate hip kinematics and predicted mechanical power so that assistive torque is delivered primarily during positive work phases. Our proposed method combines a power-based gating mechanism with a biomechanics-informed torque synthesis strategy that reconstructs extension assistance from a delayed flexion profile, without gait-phase detection or task-specific mode switching. Experimental results from six participants <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(n = 6)$</tex-math></inline-formula> show that using a single fixed parameter set selected a priori, over <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$97.8\% \pm 1.2\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> of delivered mechanical power remained positive during walking and above <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$94.8\% \pm 1.6\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> during running, across 0.75–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.5\,\mathrm{m/s}$</tex-math></inline-formula> without parameter retuning. Under the same fixed parameters, the controller was further tested in five overground nonperiodic locomotion tasks and showed smooth assistance during the tested gait transitions, indicating potential for real-world deployment.

title: Relative Pose Estimation for Nonholonomic Robot Formation With UWB-IO Measurements
authors: Kunrui Ze, Wei Wang, Shuoyu Yue, Guibin Sun, Kexin Liu, Jinhu Lü
abstract: This article studies the problem of distributed formation control for multiple robots by using onboard ultra wideband (UWB) distance and inertial odometer (IO) measurements. Although this problem has been widely studied, a fundamental limitation of most works is that they require each robot’s pose and sensor measurements are expressed in a common reference frame. However, it is inapplicable for nonholonomic robot formations due to the practical difficulty of aligning IO measurements of individual robot in a common frame. To address this problem, first, a concurrent-learning-based estimator is first proposed to achieve relative localization between neighboring robots in a local frame. Different from most relative localization methods in a global frame, both relative position and orientation in a local frame are estimated with only UWB ranging and IO measurements. Second, to deal with information loss caused by directed communication topology, a cooperative localization algorithm is introduced to estimate the relative pose to the leader robot. Third, based on the theoretical results on relative pose estimation, a distributed formation tracking controller is proposed for nonholonomic robots. Both 2-D and 3-D real-world experiments conducted on aerial robots and grounded robots are provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: DDGA: Domain Distance Guided Active Domain Adaptation for Unpaired Super-Resolution
authors: Zai-Zuo Tang, Yu-Bin Yang
abstract: While additional training samples enhance the performance of unpaired super-resolution (SR) networks, they inevitably escalate training costs. To address this issue, we propose a Domain Distance Guided Active Domain Adaptation (DDGA) method for unpaired SR, which intelligently selects additional training samples to maximize network performance improvement while minimizing the number of selected samples. In the sample selection process, to solve the problem of incompatibility between the additional samples and the target domain, we propose a three-fold metric that combines compatibility, complementarity, and representativeness to align the distribution of the additional samples with that of the target domain. Additionally, a domain representation method based on relative offset is proposed, transforming the task of texture detail representation into a score-based representation, effectively reducing the reliance of active domain adaptation (ADA) methods on texture details for unpaired SR tasks. Extensive experiments validate DDGA's effectiveness, demonstrating a 0.538 dB performance improvement in unpaired SR networks while introducing only 2% additional samples.

title: Bridging the Short-Term and Long-Term Gap: A Cross-Task Continuous Learning Person Re-Identification Problem
authors: Wei Liu, Xin Xu, Kui Jiang, Zheng Wang, Chia-Wen Lin
abstract: Person Re-IDentification (ReID) plays an important role in the application of intelligent security systems. Significant advancements have been made in Short-Term (ST) ReID tasks, where person appearances remain relatively constant. Similarly, progress has been achieved in Long-Term (LT) ReID tasks, which are characterized by drastic changes in person appearances. However, a vital yet overlooked challenge in real-world ReID is maintaining the continuity of the retrieval process. When transitioning from ST to LT ReID, simply discarding the ST model in favor of the LT model can lead to a severe performance drop during the early retrieval stage, due to the loss of valuable appearance-related knowledge in the ST model. Conversely, discarding the LT model and relying solely on the ST model may significantly reduce accuracy during the extended retrieval stage, as it overly depends on appearance-related knowledge. Therefore, we delve into a novel ReID problem with practical ramifications, namely short-term to long-term ReID (S2L-ReID), which continuously learns from ST to LT task. Existing continuous learning methods, only designed to mitigate domain differences across ST domains, face substantial challenges when dealing with the significant task gap in the S2L problem. These challenges manifest as severer catastrophic forgetting due to knowledge conflicts between ST and LT tasks and mutual constraints during optimization between over-coupling new knowledge adaptation and old knowledge non-forgetting tasks. To address these challenges, we propose a unified Meta-Knowledge Accumulation framework (MKA), which enables continuous learning of universal meta-knowledge for all tasks. It comprises a plug-and-play Meta-knowledge Purify Module (MPM) to alleviate knowledge conflicts by filtering out task-specific knowledge. Additionally, a transferable Peer Learning Strategy (PLS) is included to weaken mutual constraints by alternating between old and new models and enabling mutual learning. Finally, considering that real continuous application processes need to process the retrieval from ST to LT data, we propose a new Joint-Tests evaluation for evaluating the performance of the model on the more realistic hybrid data. Notably, MKA requires only a small computational resource usage with good portability. Empirical evaluations on all test settings exhibit that our MKA attains the best performance and is highly portable.

title: Hybrid Compression of LiDAR Point Clouds Using Density-Based Partitioning
authors: Nuno A. B. Martins, Abel Mendes, Fernando J. P. Lopes, Luís A. Da Silva Cruz
abstract: LiDAR point clouds exhibit large density variations, and these variations have a direct effect on compression efficiency. Geometry-based codecs such as the MPEG Geometry-based Point Cloud Coding standard (G-PCC) perform well on sparse point clouds, while learning-based methods such as the JPEG Pleno Verification Model (JP-PCC) are more effective on dense point clouds. Hybrid coding exploits this complementarity by selecting the most suitable codec for each subset of points according to local density. This work evaluates five point cloud partitioning methods based on distance, fixed density thresholds, adaptive density quantiles, neighbourhood-based refinement, and morphological smoothing. Experiments are conducted on three LiDAR point cloud datasets with distinct global density levels: KITTI, Ford, and Flowbotic, a new industrial indoor dataset introduced in this study. Density-based clustering is used to reduce biases caused by intra-dataset density variations, and all evaluations focus on practical operating points below 4 bits per point. Results show that density-based hybrid coding consistently improves compression efficiency over single-codec operation. Among the tested methods, adaptive density thresholding with a 0.25 quantile achieves the best average performance for the KITTI and Ford datasets, while neighbourhood-based sparse-to-dense refinement gives the best results for the Flowbotic dataset. These findings show that density-aware partitioning supports effective hybrid coding across diverse LiDAR acquisition conditions.

title: Quantum Computing for Insurance Fraud Detection
authors: Yang Bai, Wenyu Jiao, Chao Ju, Xinjie Feng, Jiawei Lu, Nuo Qian, Lingge Kong, Qi Ao, Jiajun Zhang, Feifei Zhu, Lian Zhou
abstract: Collusive insurance fraud causes large financial losses for insurance companies. While community detection algorithms based on modularity can be used to detect collusive insurance fraud, the related optimization problem is NP-hard, motivating the application of quantum computers. In this work, we employ a specialized quantum computer, the Coherent Ising Machine (CIM), for the insurance fraud detection problem. By modeling insurance fraud detection as a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) problem, we efficiently solve it on a physical CIM with over 1500 qubits. Our results demonstrate the applicability of CIM to insurance fraud detection and its significant acceleration capability compared to classical methods. Furthermore, CIM can obtain degenerate ground-state and near-optimal solutions due to its sampling capability and the community partitions of CIM manifest diversity while each solution maintaining a competitive fraud detection performance. We explore how this solution diversity can be used to strengthen CIM fraud detection performance and demonstrate that, when top 2% or less nodes are reviewed, the precision and recall rate of the solution ensemble are better than that of the best single solution up to 2.5 times and 2 times, respectively.

title: STFD-SNN: A Physics-Constrained Spiking Neural Network Framework for Maritime Radio Environment Map Reconstruction
authors: Liu Yi, Youchen Fan, Yufei Guo, You Fu, Shengliang Fang, Ming Lei, Qichen Wang
abstract: The escalating disparity between the supply and demand of maritime radio spectrum resources necessitates the construction of high-fidelity Radio Environment Maps (REM) for effective spectrum situational awareness and dynamic management. However, this task is severely hampered by distinctive maritime challenges, including extreme data sparsity, highly dynamic electromagnetic propagation characteristics, and complex spatio-temporal correlations, which significantly degrade conventional terrestrial REM reconstruction methods. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a hierarchical REM reconstruction framework that synergistically integrates Spiking Neural Networks with physical constraints. Our contributions are threefold. First, we devise an adaptive Unmanned Aerial Vehicle sampling strategy based on a refined Ant Colony Optimization algorithm, incorporating a hierarchical priority decision mechanism and joint heuristic function to improve data collection efficiency under sparse sampling. Second, we architect a Frequency-Spatio-Temporal Attention (FSTA) -enhanced Spiking Neural Network (SNN) model that captures spatio-temporal dynamics from sparse observations for high-precision 2D REM completion. Third, we introduce a physics-guided knowledge distillation paradigm that embeds maritime electromagnetic propagation models as multi-stage soft constraints through three coordinated mechanisms, which direct input correction via height-weighted physical deviation terms, and loss-level supervision penalizing physically inconsistent 3D reconstructions. Extensive simulations conducted in a high-fidelity maritime scenario demonstrate, which is constructed using real geographic environments, GMTED digital elevation data, and representative meteorological conditions. Our framework outperforms tensor completion U-Net and PINN baseline across various sampling rates. Notably, at sampling rates of 20%, 50%, and 80%, the proposed framework consistently attains superior Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Normalized Mean Square Error (NMSE), with the Physical Residual Metric (PRM) further serving as a diagnostic indicator confirming internalization of physical priors.

title: Embroidery Actuator Utilizing Embroidery Patterns to Generate Diverse Fabric Deformations
authors: Yuki Ota, Yuki Funabora
abstract: This paper presents a novel Embroidery Actuator, a fabric-integrated pneumatic actuator that enables diverse and controllable deformations through embroidery pattern design. Unlike conventional fabric actuators that rely on fiber- or thread-shaped actuators, the proposed actuator is fabricated by directly stitching an inflatable tube onto the fabric using a cord-embroidery technique. The embroidered thread and the fabric jointly form a sleeve that constrains the expansion of the inflatable tube, converting internal pressure into targeted bending or stretching deformations. By varying the embroidery pattern, such as zigzag or cross configurations, different geometric constraints can be realized, allowing for flexible control of deformation direction and magnitude. Analytical deformation models based on the Neo-Hookean model and Lagrange's equations were developed to predict the relationship between pneumatic pressure and bending angle. And then,experiments demonstrated that the actuator achieved 47 degrees of flexion on the fabric surface side and 165 degrees on the reverse side by altering the embroidery pattern. Additionally, the created model expressed deformation with an error margin of several degrees.

title: Creating Empowering Counter-Narratives through Collective Digital Art by Disabled People

authors: Agata Stanczyk, Mindy Tran, Tarini Saka, Yixin Zou, Veelasha Moonsamy
