title: Decoupling Control of a Multi-segment Hybrid-Actuated Soft Origami Continuum Robot Through Variable Stiffness_supp4-3700107.mp4
authors: Erbao Dong
abstract: Soft origami continuum robots have attracted considerable attention because of their flexibility and adaptability, but they face challenges in control accuracy. This study presents a novel design and control scheme for large-deformation hybrid-actuated soft origami continuum robots to improve their motion performance in real tasks. An origami-based pneumatic chamber is used as the robot backbone to achieve a high extension ratio, and the hybrid variable stiffness principle combining antagonistic actuation and layer jamming further enhances the robot's overall structural stiffness. The robot performs precise movements by controlling tendons distributed in an external origami. An iterative training strategy based on long short-term memory is employed to model the inverse kinematics of the robot in consideration of the inherent hysteresis of origami robots. Step size features are introduced to improve model accuracy with limited data. The capability for variable stiffness enables the migration of the training model on the basis of a single segment, the accuracy of which is close to the submillimeter level. Decoupling control of each segment for a rear-driven multi-segment origami continuum robot is also achieved. Experiment results reveal that the trajectory tracking errors for single-segment and multi-segment of the robot are 1.58, and 2.81 mm, respectively, with relative errors of 0.71% and 0.63% over the robot length, demonstrating the good performance of the proposed design and control method. Orientation data can also be added to the dataset to achieve orientation control of the robot, with an angle error of 0.75°. The robot shows its stability and safety in a series of real tasks, including writing, LED tracking, board cleaning, and pick and place. This study demonstrates a potential solution for continuum robots performing precise tasks without any sensory feedback in confined spaces and specialized environments where electronic sensors fail.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: On the Sum of Inverse Gamma RVs and its Application to Wireless Communications Networks
authors: Petros S. Bithas, Liang Yang, Qiuming Zhu, Hector E. Nistazakis, Daniel Benevides Da Costa
abstract: Investigating the statistics of the sum of random variables (RVs) is fundamental in wireless communication systems, since it can be used to estimate the outage probability and error rates. In this paper, exact closed-form expressions are derived for the probability density function (PDF) and cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the sum of inverse-gamma (IG) RVs. The IG distribution is especially suitable for performance analysis purposes because it provides a tractable yet accurate model of shadowing/local mean power variations. Unlike prior studies on sums of shadowing RVs, the resulting formulas are simple to evaluate and numerically stable. Their utility is demonstrated in two practical settings: unmanned aerial vehicle (UAVs)-assisted networks and high-speed railway communications. Across all scenarios, the analytical results agree with Monte Carlo simulations to within machine precision, while the asymptotic approximations closely track the exact results.

title: Robust PI Control with Improved Performance for Nonlinear Systems under Saturated Actuation
authors: Zeqiang Li, Jason J. R. Liu, George A. Rovithakis, Yongduan Song
abstract: It is interesting and challenging to investigate the problem of prescribed performance constraints of uncertain nonlinear systems subject to inelastic control input saturation due to their potential conflicts. By designing a novel reference compensation mechanism that depends simultaneously on the input saturation error and the relative distance between performance boundaries and tracking errors, the reference signals are adaptively modified only when absolutely necessary, making the tracking control with pre-specified performance attributes affordable even in the presence of strict input constraints. In addition, a robust proportional-integral (PI) control scheme featuring with structure simplification and disturbance rejection is developed for a class of nonlinear systems with mismatched uncertainties. It is proved that all the closed-loop signals are bounded, and the proposed method can also ensure the output tracking performance. Numerical simulation results verify and clarify the effectiveness and superiority of the theoretical results.

title: A Multitask Crisscross Network for One-Shot Bearing Multiattribute Fault Diagnosis
authors: Fei Wang, Guangyu Jia, Jianbin Qiu
abstract: The scarcity of samples presents a significant challenge in data-driven fault diagnosis, particularly for bearing malfunctions where swift shutdowns are imperative to avert accidents. This operational constraint makes it difficult to obtain bearing fault data, resulting in catastrophic performance on the test set, particularly when only a single sample is available for each fault. This article proposes a multitask crisscross network (MTCCN) for one-shot fault diagnosis based on transfer learning theory. The semantic information of faults is treated as attributes, serving as knowledge to be transferred. A multitask learning approach is used to predict multiple attributes, which provides more valuable fault information. The horizontal structure of MTCCN is a task-sharing network, where global features are extracted for task-specific networks to predict attributes. The vertical structure consists of an attribute classifier chain (ACC), and the correlation between attributes is modeled by a directed acyclic graph (DAG) for the attribute prediction tasks. An information map integrates multiple heterogeneous sources of information to improve prediction accuracy. Finally, extensive experiments were conducted on the different datasets, and the transfer effect was quantified, which demonstrated the efficiency and robustness of MTCCN.

title: ETTJO: An Event-Triggered Two-Timescale Framework for Joint Caching, Trajectory, and Offloading in Maritime MEC
authors: Kai Yin, Yuxiang Zhao, Ruiting Wang, Xinxin Ye, Ting Gan, Zongpeng Li
abstract: In maritime Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) networks, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) serve as aerial relays to overcome offshore coverage limitations. However, sustainable UAV operation is challenged by the tight coupling among trajectory planning, content caching, and task offloading under strict energy constraints and stochastic maritime channels. Conventional periodic optimization further incurs excessive backhaul overhead and computational costs. To address these issues, we propose ETTJO, an Event-Triggered Two-Timescale Joint Optimization framework. Leveraging Lyapunov optimization, ETTJO decouples long-term energy constraints from instantaneous decisions without future network information. It further decomposes the problem into two timescales: event-triggered caching updates content only when service popularity changes significantly, while a hybrid short-timescale control algorithm integrates multi-agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for continuous trajectory control with matching theory for discrete task offloading. We provide theoretical analysis to support the proposed framework. Extensive simulations demonstrate that ETTJO achieves lower latency and improved energy efficiency compared with representative baselines.

title: Accelerating Top-K Subgraph Matching With Topology-Aware Pruning and Parallel Processing
authors: Linglin Yang, Yuqi Zhou, Yue Pang, Lei Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Given a query graph, top-k subgraph matching finds up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula> matches in a data graph with the highest scores according to a user-defined scoring function. It has wide applications across many fields, including knowledge graphs and social networks. Due to the enormous search space, existing methods are not efficient enough on large graphs. In this paper, we propose <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTAB</monospace>, an efficient serial algorithm for top-k subgraph matching. It traverses an efficiently pruned search space by topology-aware subspace score upper bounds computed from a novel hop index, which stores the range of node properties in a constrained multi-hop neighborhood of each node. Additionally, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTAB</monospace> integrates a cost-aware root selection strategy, which chooses query nodes leading to a search process that utilizes the pruning power of the hop index as much as possible. Furthermore, we use a novel edge-cut strategy to handle general query graphs with cycles. We also extend <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTAB</monospace> to a parallel version, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTABP</monospace>, which further improves performance via multi-threading and enhanced pruning techniques. Experimental results on real and synthetic datasets demonstrate that our methods outperform existing methods.

title: MELT-FSIL: Adaptive Hybrid-Loss Few-Shot Learning for Inclusive Sign Language Control in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Rahman Shafique, Erol Kina, Gyu Sang Choi
abstract: Despite significant advances in autonomous vehicle (AV) technologies, current interaction modalities remain insufficiently accessible to people with physical and communication impairments. Conventional AV interfaces, particularly voice-activated and touchscreen-based systems, may create usability barriers for users who are deaf, non-verbal, or have limited motor control, thereby limiting effective human–vehicle interaction. To address this gap, this study proposes a vision-based ASL recognition framework as an initial step toward accessible AV interaction. The proposed framework uses Few-Shot Incremental Learning (FSIL) to recognize American Sign Language (ASL) hand gestures from static RGB hand-sign images, enabling the model to learn new gesture classes from limited examples while maintaining performance on previously learned classes.The core contribution of this work is a hybrid loss function, termed Metric–Entropy Learning Tradeoff (MELT), which combines prototypical metric learning with cross-entropy-based discriminative learning within an FSIL training regime. By balancing prototype-based class separation with entropy-guided classification, MELT improves the model’s ability to adapt to newly introduced ASL gestures while reducing catastrophic forgetting. In addition, prototype normalization and episodic memory replay are incorporated to stabilize incremental learning performance. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed FSIL framework with MELT loss improves gesture recognition accuracy from 96.0% using the baseline CNN to 97.5% in the FSL setting and 97.9% in the FSIL setting after the integration of novel gesture classes. These findings demonstrate the potential of hybrid loss-based FSIL for ASL gesture recognition in assistive AV interaction scenarios. While the broader long-term goal is to develop a fully multimodal AV interface, the present study is limited to static RGB image-based ASL recognition; future work will extend the system by incorporating additional modalities such as depth, audio, wearable signals, facial expressions, and vehicle-state information. Thus, this work represents an early step toward more inclusive and accessible AV interaction systems.

title: Spatial-Frequency Structure-Aware Deep Hashing with Adaptive Gated Fusion (SF-AGDH) for Remote Sensing Image Retrieval
authors: Linglong Xiong, Yueshun He, Jie Chen, Zhenyang Hui
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Retrieving remote sensing images on a massive scale remains challenging because geospatial targets often display intricate scale variations and heterogeneous textures. While deep hashing offers a highly efficient retrieval metric, current methods predominantly rely on spatial convolutions. This makes it difficult for them to perceive the global frequency properties needed to separate visually similar classes. Moreover, standard multi-scale aggregation techniques are largely static and cannot dynamically adapt to different image contents. To solve these problems, this paper introduces the Spatial-Frequency Structure-Aware Deep Hashing (SF-AGDH) network. Utilizing an inference-efficient dual-stream design, our framework integrates a Dual-Domain Synergistic Attention (DDSA) module. By combining 2D Fast Fourier Transforms with coordinate attention, DDSA effectively captures both macroscopic periodic textures and localized geometric details. We also propose an Adaptive Gated Fusion (AGF) module that uses a learnable gating mechanism to balance shallow textures and deep semantics automatically. Finally, to combat the extreme positive-negative sample imbalance inherent in hash optimization, we apply a Weighted HashNet loss to dynamically recalibrate gradients. Extensive experiments on UCMD, AID, and NWPU-RESISC45 demonstrate that SF-AGDH achieves superior, state-of-the-art retrieval accuracy, especially at low bit rates.

title: Real-Time Output Linearization in Adaptive Predictive Control for Variable Rate Control of Agricultural Sprayers
authors: Deniver Reinke Schultz, Lucas Jonys Ribeiro Silva, Vilma Alves De Oliveira, Paulo E. Cruvinel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Precise flow rate control in agricultural sprayers is critical for variable-rate application, but is challenged by nonlinear dynamics, dead zones, and model variations due to operational changes and nozzle switching. This work aims to develop an efficient control strategy capable of handling these challenges while ensuring compliance with operational constraints. To this end, a generalized predictive controller (GPC) integrated with a dynamic output-linearization scheme is proposed. The dynamic linearization plays a key role to enable adaptive control under practical operating conditions, without requiring a detailed characterization of the hydraulic system. To support this framework, a simplified Kalman filter is employed, to estimate, in real time, the total equivalent fluidic resistance of the system. Experimental validation was conducted using an agricultural sprayer development system, demonstrating superior performance compared to traditional methods, with improved accuracy and adaptability in the presence of disturbances. The proposed output linearizing fuzzy GPC controller (L-FGPC) effectively handles nonlinearities and operational constraints, offering a practical solution for advanced agricultural spraying systems.

title: Robustness in TinyML: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Emanuel Pereira, Erick Barboza, Ícaro Araújo, Saulo dos Santos, Gabriel de Andrade, Itallo da Silva, Allan Martins
publish: ACM Transactions on Embedded Computing Systems

title: Unleash Your Potential

title: CaRLi-V: Camera-RADAR-LiDAR Point-Wise 3D Velocity Estimation
authors: Landson Guo, Andres M. Diaz Aguilar, William Talbot, Turcan Tuna, Marco Hutter, Cesar Cadena
abstract: Accurate point-wise velocity estimation in 3D is crucial for robot interaction with non-rigid, dynamic agents, such as humans, enabling robust performance in path planning, collision avoidance, and object manipulation in dynamic environments. To this end, this paper proposes a novel RADAR, LiDAR, and camera fusion pipeline for point-wise 3D velocity estimation named CaRLi-V. This pipeline leverages raw RADAR measurements to create a novel RADAR representation, the velocity cube, which densely represents encodes RADAR radial velocities within the RADAR's field-of-view. By combining the velocity cube for radial velocity extraction, optical flow for tangential velocity estimation, and LiDAR for point-wise range measurements through a closed-form solution, our approach can produce 3D velocity estimates for a dense array of points. CaRLi-V has been field-tested against on a custom dataset and achieves low velocity error metrics relative to ground truth while outperforming state-of-the-art scene flow methods

title: Federated and Privacy-Preserving Cross-Project Defect Prediction with Heterogeneous Features_supp1-3692541.pdf
authors: Meetesh Nevendra
abstract: Organizations are often reluctant to share defectlabeled source code because of privacy and intellectual property concerns, yet effective cross-project defect prediction (CPDP) depends on learning from diverse projects. This paper introduces FedCPDP, a federated and privacy-preserving framework that enables collaborative defect prediction without exchanging raw project data and without requiring a common feature schema. The approach employs feature hashing to unify heterogeneous metric spaces, integrates federated proximal optimization with representation alignment to mitigate distribution shift, and incorporates differential privacy mechanisms to safeguard sensitive updates. To further enhance robustness, FedCPDP explores adaptive server aggregation strategies (attention and graphbased) and seed ensembling, while addressing class imbalance through focal loss and threshold optimization. The framework is evaluated on thirty benchmark datasets across five CPDP families under leave-one-project-out validation, using both threshold-free and effort-aware metrics. Results show that FedCPDP improves F1 by up to 10-15% over FedAvg and 6-8% over FedDPI, while achieving 8-10% higher Popt under effort-aware evaluation. These gains are statistically significant, confirming that FedCPDP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods while uniquely supporting heterogeneous features and offering explicit privacy guarantees. These findings establish FedCPDP as a practical step toward enabling secure and deployable defect prediction in multiorganizational settings.

title: Intelligent Document Querying with LLMs and Speech Input: A Scalable Semantic Framework
authors: Ranganatha. K, Veeresh Biradar, Shilpa. B, Manjula. S, Gurusiddayya Hiremath, Narayan Naik
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: The information era has created challenges in terms of retrieving domain-specific information from voluminous unstructured documents (like Education and Businesses), whether via informer attacks or database attacks. Researchers have developed an information framework that includes LlamaIndex - which does intelligent document analysis; Gradient AI - which provides superior inferences; and Astra DB - which provides the appropriate storage solutions for these systems. More recently, we have expanded our research to include varied modalities of multi-modal data such as text and voice, using different configurations, e.g., BGE for embeddings, Wav2Vec2 for speech. We verified that the use of LlamaIndex, Gradient AI and Astra DB on varying document sets produced results averaging 2.7 seconds per query with an average of the “top 3” results indicating a semantic search performance rate of 91.2%. Overall, the proposed framework, when compared to existing technologies such as LangChain (88.4%) and Haystack <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(86.9 \%)$</tex>, has produced the highest level of performance. The response to user queries has been done in an informative and concise manner inline with the context and has been achieved via the use of RESTful APIs. The proposed model is ready to be tested in real-world applications and will be made available for additional trials beginning early next year.

title: Towards a Unified AI Framework for Autism Support: System Design and Pilot Evaluation
authors: Tajul Islam Tarek, Abhishek Dash, Barnali Sarker Shoumita, Mohammad Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental condition that affects social interaction. Early identification and developmental efforts can improve the state but early signs often go unnoticed due to a lack of effective and accessible tools. Although some digital systems provide tools to monitor different behaviors, the support for continuous development is limited. This work presents an AI-powered system to address these gaps. Our system combines five focus areas for autism support. It uses interactive games for tracking and training social, communication, and motor abilities by capturing multimodal information. The data includes eye tracking, gestures, facial expressions, speech, and full-body movement. Through these, parents can see early signs of autism, doctors can get help in screening and progress monitoring, and schools can easily monitor improvements. We performed a pilot test with 10 autistic children aged from 4 to 8. It showed high engagement and measurable improvements. This indicates that the system can be an effective tool for early screening and developmental support in children with ASD.

title: Research on Automatic Fault Diagnosis System for Marine Electrical Equipment Based on Computer Vision
authors: Shengjie Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Background: Marine electrical systems are subjected to severe environmental factors (kicking, high moisture levels, salt spray), and as such, marine faults are prevalent such as overheating, loose connections, etc. which pose a threat to navigation. Manual inspection is a process that is not efficient and cannot be sustained in continuous monitoring requirement in vessel automation. Approaches: A smart fault diagnostic system, which consists of deep learning and multimodal vision, is suggested and it is built over an extended ResNet-50. It applies use of multi-scale feature fusion and channel attention mechanisms to gain more fault recognition with a customized image enhancement strategy (defogging, illumination correction, denoising) to marine environmental interference. Results: The trained system with 8, 500 annotated samples of 7 common fault classes recognizes results with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 3 \%}$</tex> accuracy at an average of 0.18 seconds/image and outperforms the traditional CNN methods by 8.7 points. Tests aboard certify its close-to-perfect performance as a malfunction detector with 0 false omissions and a false alarm of 2.1 percent. In conclusion In this paper, it has been shown that the system is a dependable, real-time autonomous monitoring system of marine electrical equipment that will solve the major issues of environmental flexibility and diagnostic efficiency in maritime conditions.

title: Explainable and Trustworthy AI for Data-Driven Infrastructure Decision-Making
authors: Hemraj Parate, Kishore Bandela, Kishore Subramanya Hebbar, Amey Waikar, Suraj Balaso Desai, Anil Kumar Kotte, B. Nithya
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper will discuss how to develop Explainable and Trustworthy AI to decide on infrastructure in a data-driven way with a particular focus on Transportation Infrastructure and Highway Engineering. This paper suggests a Hybrid Explainable AI model combined with Federated Learning to overcome major challenges of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) and Smart Infrastructure. The proposed approach, based on the use of TensorFlow Federated (TFF), can guarantee privacy of data by training models in a decentralized manner, as well as increase the level of decision transparency with the help of such tools as SHAP and LIME. This blend of high-tech approaches will lead to the development of Decision-Centric AI Systems that are not only maximizing traffic flow and infrastructure planning but also in line with the concepts of Responsible Infrastructure AI. This research has shown that the hybrid solution can enhance scalability, interpretability, and real-time decision-making performance, being a strong solution to the implementation of AI-driven smart infrastructure systems in the city and highway context.

title: 1.2 kV 3D Quasi-Planar Trench SiC MOSFET with P-Body Layer as Trench Protection
authors: Mansha Kansal, Iulian Nistor, Kyrylo Melnyk, Giuseppe Capasso, Alessandro Borghese, Michele Riccio, Arne Benjamin Renz, Giovanni Breglio, Neophytos Lophitis, Andrea Irace, Marina Antoniou, Munaf Rahimo, Luca Maresca
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: In this paper, a new trench shielding concept against high electric fields is presented by means of an experimental demonstration backed up with 3D TCAD simulations. The novel concept has been applied on an advanced 3D Quasi-Planar Trench (QPT) SiC MOSFET rated at 1200 V and 50 A. The protection approach consists of two key design pillars by employing: 1) shallow trench depths embedded in deeper P-Well Channel regions which are accordingly 2) shallower than deeper highly doped P-Body regions under the source contact. This design provides a double protection scheme for enhancing the device blocking performance and shielding the gate oxide at the critical regions along the trench MOS gate structure. Three different QPT design structures were examined with varying protection levels, with each design having a consistent trench depth but varying trench dimensions across the JFET region (QPT1 with no embedded Trench in P-Well), P-body (QPT2 with partially embedded trench in P-Well), and into the N+ source region (QPT3 with fully embedded trench in P-Well). The blocking characteristics, including the off-state leakage behaviour, were analyzed for the proposed device structures. The optimized QPT structure performs showcases BV performance close to the best-in-class reference planar structure with a deviation of only ~ 0.3 %. The experimental results were further validated through advanced 3D TCAD simulations, which examined electrostatic behavior, and studied electric field distributions along different cross-sections of the trench.

title: Meta-Ensemble Transformer Framework for Multi-Class Mental Health Disorder Classification on Social Media
authors: Sayma Medha, Iffat Ara Babli, Md Abdul Masud
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing use of social media platforms such as Reddit has created new opportunities for analyzing user-generated text to support early identification of mental health disorders. Despite this potential, multi-class mental health classification remains difficult due to noisy language, overlapping symptom expressions, and contextual ambiguity commonly found in social media posts. This study introduces a meta-ensemble transformerbased framework that combines BERT and RoBERTa using a logistic regression meta-learner to enhance multi-class mental health disorder classification. The framework is evaluated on two publicly available Reddit datasets, Amurark and Mavinsao, which include up to eight mental health categories. The experimental results show that the proposed ensemble consistently outperforms individual transformer models. An accuracy of 0.8649 with a macro F1-score of 0.8646 is achieved on the Amurark dataset, while the Mavinsao dataset yields an accuracy of 0.8501 and a macro F1-score of 0.8502. Although the framework demonstrates improved performance, it is limited by its dependence on Englishlanguage text and the computational cost associated with transformer-based models. The proposed approach is suitable for automated mental health monitoring, early risk detection systems, and large-scale psychological research using social media data.

title: Comparative Load Flow Analysis of a 6-Bus Power System Using MATLAB and ETAP
authors: Fahmid Amin Zawad
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Load flow analysis is a fundamental and essential study in power system operation, planning, and optimization. It provide detailed insights into bus voltages, phase angles, real and reactive power flows, and system losses under varying load and generation conditions. In this study, a 6-bus power system is modeled and analyzed to evaluate its overall performance. The network is simulated in Electrical Transient Analyzer Program (ETAP) to obtain bus voltage magnitudes, phase angles, and line power flows, while a MATLAB implementation of the Gauss-Seidel iterative method is used for independent verification. A close agreement between the two approaches confirms the accuracy and reliability of the results. The analysis demonstrates the importance of combining software-based simulations with classical numerical methods to ensure both precision and practical understanding of power system behavior. Accurate load flow results help identify critical busses, evaluate line loadings, minimize system losses, and support informed decision-making in planning and operation. This study highlights the significance of load flow studies in achieving reliable, efficient, and safe operation of modern power networks, providing engineers with a foundation for further analysis, optimization, and contingency planning.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Based Tomato Leaf Disease Detection Using Transfer Learning
authors: Kashpia Tasnova, Wasifa Hoque, Uma Dhar, Zinnia Sultana
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In precision agriculture, it is a big problem to accurately and reliably detect diseases that affect tomato plants because all types of disease exhibit similar features to detect. Another major problem is that automated systems often do not provide a clear understanding of how they classify diseases, which makes these systems less useful. The purpose of this study is to create an efficient and easy to understand deep learning framework for multi-class classification of tomato leaf diseases. To address this problem, a comparative study is conducted using a baseline Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and three transfer-learning models-MobileNetV2, EfficientNetB3, and Xception-trained and evaluated on two publicly available datasets: PlantVillage and Tomato Leaf Disease Detection. All models are trained under consistent preprocessing, augmentation, and evaluation protocols to ensure fair comparison. To improve model interpretability, model-agnostic explainable AI techniques, namely SHAP and LIME, are applied to analyze prediction behavior at the region levels. Experimental data indicate that transfer learning models significantly outperform the baseline CNN. For example, Xception achieves a maximum accuracy of 99% for the PlantVillage dataset and 98% for the Tomato leaf disease detection dataset, and MobileNetV2 shows comparable performance, but has less computational complexity. Furthermore, the explainability analysis indicates that the models consistently concentrate their attention on disease-relevant areas, such as infected leaf areas and lesions, thereby confirming the accuracy of their predictions. These findings indicate that the proposed framework effectively combines high classification performance with interpretable predictions, making it suitable for real-world tomato disease diagnosis applications.

title: CompenKNet: An Intelligent State Estimation Method with Model Errors Compensation
authors: Bing Huang, Han Zou, Chao Ma, Yuan Huang, Miao Li, Weidong Sheng
abstract: Due to the absence of precise prior knowledge, model errors induced by model approximation are ubiquitous in state estimation. Such errors impose substantial degradation on the performance of conventional state estimation approaches. To address this issue, we propose CompenKNet, which incorporates KalmanNet with a dedicated compensation network to enable adaptive state estimation with explicit model error compensation. Featuring a modular dual-branch structure, the algorithm decouples yet jointly optimizes the learning of the Kalman gain and model error compensation, thereby alleviating the adverse impacts of model errors. Furthermore, a three-stage training pipeline is devised to optimize network parameters progressively via pre-training and joint fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets validate that CompenKNet achieves superior performance over state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: An Intelligent Machine Learning Approach to Guide Patients Toward the Appropriate Level of Healthcare in Bangladesh
authors: Md. Niamul Ferdous Rahi, Sudwip Roy Antor, Md. Arifuzzaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In Bangladesh, people often go to secondary level health care centers and tertiary level specialized hospitals when he have no severe health problem. As a result, these Secondary and Tertiary level healthcare centers face many challenges to provide treatment to their genuine respective patients. Here one key reason is, there is no referral-based health care system. To solve this problem, this paper proposes a machine learning based decision support system that recommends the appropriate healthcare level, where they should go - Primary, Secondary or Tertiary based on symptom-based inputted data. Our dataset is collected from a Kaggle repository which contains 131 symptoms related to disease prediction. 15 most impactful symptoms are selected through Random Forest feature importance to improve model efficiency and interpretability. Several machine learning models - Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, and XGBoost are implemented. XGBoost achieved superior performance of 95.73 accuracy when Logistic Regression achieves 88.82 accuracy, Random Forest 94.51 accuracy, SVM 95.33 accuracy. This model may helps to solve the problem of absence of strong referral based health care system.

title: Lecturify AI: Smart Lectures to Multiple Choice Questions Generator
authors: R Ranjana, S R Indurekaa, V Eswaramurthy, S Saran, V Tamilarasu, G Shanmuga Vishnu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: There is a lot of time taken to grade papers for students and the time for teachers to lose the ability to improve the accuracy of their grades through correction materials. Manual procedures have created inconsistencies in terms of quality of the assessment process, which contributes to delays in the ability to create assessments. This paper proposes a system called Lecturify AI, a technology that can convert audio, video and text lecture material into a structured way to create multiple choice questions (MCQs). This technology combines several components including Speech to Text (STT), Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and NLP to get the important concepts from the materials. The constructed MCQs will be assigned to different levels of cognitive learning as outlined by Bloom's Taxonomy or how deep a student can learn about any given concept with respect to the various cognitive processes used to evaluate readiness for the assessment process. The teacher is able to assess the constructed assessments from one web-based portal, and this will give the student an opportunity to use the assessments as study aids. Therefore, the purpose of this system will provide digital tools and resources will promote efficient and effective use of technology to allow for increased accessibility and scalability of learning be delivered using technology.

title: Hierarchical Risk-Aware Transformer Actor Critic Framework for Intelligent Financial Management and Decision Making
authors: Xinyue Quan, Chunfang Shi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, intelligent financial management decision making become important because of the market volatility, economic uncertainty and growing complexity of corporate financial structures. Moreover, the accurate capital allocation, risk control and liquidity management played an important role in ensuring organizational sustainability and long-term profitability. However, financial decision processes remained challenging because of high-dimensional financial indicators, temporal dependencies, multicollinearity and dynamic macroeconomic fluctuations which reduce model stability and predictive consistency. To overcome these challenges, a Hierarchical Risk-Aware Transformer-ActorCritic (HRAT-AC) framework is proposed for adaptive financial management optimization. Initially, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is applied for removing the redundancy and reduce dimensionality by preserving dominant financial variance patterns. Subsequently, temporal attentionbased feature extraction mechanism captured inter-period financial dependencies and volatility-sensitive representations. Finally, HRAT-AC model integrates hierarchical policy learning, transformer-based contextual encoding and risksensitive reinforcement learning with conditional value at risk to perform sequential financial optimization. The proposed HRAT-AC ensures robust profitability maximization, controlled leverage exposure, improved liquidity stability and enhanced risk-adjusted performance under dynamic economic conditions.

title: An Intelligent UPI Fraud Detection Framework Using Machine Learning for Secure Digital Payments
authors: Shabana Sultana, Harshitha H S
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The rapid embrace of the digital payment systems has greatly changed the nature of a financial transaction and it has become a high risk of fraud. Unified Payments Interface Transactions are large and real-time; therefore, they need smart and efficient fraud detection systems that can guarantee safe online payments. The authors present in the paper a smart framework of detecting fraud acts with the help of machine learning that helps detect fraud in connection with Unified Payments Interface transactions with great precision and consistency. The proposed model will consist of feature learning via deep learning and a robust classification model capable of aggregating complex transaction patterns and nonlinear relationships typically associated with fraud. Transaction data containing both legit and fraudulent transactions are used to train and evaluate the model. The structure is intended to be applied in close to real time; therefore, it applies to real-life implementation in digital payment systems. This has been demonstrated in the experimental results, where the proposed solution has been shown to outperform traditional fraud detection tools in terms of detection accuracy, resiliency, and scalability. The work is novel in that it possesses a hybrid learning architecture, offers a more robust representation of transaction behavior, and maintains excellent classification performance across different types of fraud. The received findings indicate that the proposed structure would contribute to reducing the financial loss by half and raising the user confidence, as it would enable them to identify the fraudulent conduct within the digital payment ecosystems in a timely manner and with a high level of accuracy and prevent the safety and maintenance of the modern cashless payment systems.

title: Adaptive Admittance Control for Conflict-free Climbing of a Dual-arm Robot in Space Stations
authors: Jin Sun, Siyong Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Traditional fixed admittance control methods suffer from significant internal force conflicts in dual-arm space robots during climbing maneuvers, which can damage actuators and destabilize the system. In this paper, an adaptive admittance climbing control method is proposed to reduce such force conflicts in dual-arm space robots. First, the kinematic and dynamic models of the dual-arm space robot are introduced. Next, a slavemaster control architecture is implemented to coordinate robotic arms, where one arm operates in position mode while the other is controlled by an adaptive admittance controller. Additionally, an adaptive admittance control method based on a backpropagation network is proposed to manage the slave arm. The virtual impedance gains are adjusted online using the backpropagation network. By dynamically tuning the impedance parameters, force conflicts during climbing tasks can be significantly reduced. Simulations in MATLAB/Simulink environment demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Configuration Design and Planning Algorithms for Modular Robots in On-Orbit Assembly
authors: Yao Jiang, Meibao Yao, Hongwei Zhang, Xueming Xiao, Kun Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In low-gravity environments, modular selfreconfigurable robots are attractive for tasks such as satellite reconfiguration, structural expansion, truss assembly, and robotic arm deployment, yet planning remains highly challenging due to the NP-hard nature of module assignment and the requirement to maintain both connectivity and reachability throughout reconfiguration. To tackle these difficulties, we establish an integer programming model with penalty functions that enforce connectivity and reachability, and we design a hierarchical mechanism to decouple global assignment from local motion planning. At the upper level, the proposed CMGA (Convolution-MST-Genetic Algorithm with Gaussian mutation) efficiently addresses the NP-hard assignment problem by integrating structural preprocessing, refined initialization, and Gaussian mutation to accelerate convergence and improve global exploration. At the lower level, an A* search generates feasible, connectivity- and reachability-preserving paths for individual module relocations. Experiments on truss-assembly and satellite-reconfiguration scenarios demonstrate that this layered framework significantly outperforms conventional methods in convergence speed and movement cost. Overall, the results highlight the effectiveness of combining a hierarchical optimization mechanism with cooperative spherical module design, offering practical computational tools for autonomous modular robotic reconfiguration in future on-orbit missions.

title: Physics–Learning Hybrid Adaptive Control for Soft Continuum Robots via ANCF Model and Sparse Online Learning Methods
authors: Yu Rong, Guoying Gu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: The intrinsic compliance and time-varying material properties jointly pose significant challenges in developing an accurate and computationally efficient controller for soft continuum robots. To address these problems, this work presents a physics-learning hybrid feedback controller that integrates an Absolute Nodal Coordinate Formulation (ANCF) model with lightweight data-driven methods, achieving online adaption and robust motion control. In this sense, the forward and inverse pose mappings are both decomposed into a physics-based component and a learning compensator. Specifically, the ANCF-based model physically characterizes the quasi-static motions and provides feedforward control predictions. Meanwhile, a sparse online Gaussian process (SOGP) is employed to compensate the residual error of the physical model. The SOGP can evaluate the novelty of the newly collected data and maintain a small training dataset, thus releasing the computational burden. Additionally, a radial basis function neural network (RBFNN) is used to compute feedback control signals for the desired goals. Parameter update for two modules completes within 25 ms, since they both operate on compact datasets and admit analytical expressions. Compared to the open-loop controller, the presented feedback controller reduces about 65% position error and 70% orientation error, showing online adaption and high robustness to diverse disturbances.

title: Sentiment Forecasting By Data-Driven Models
authors: Othoniel Joseph, Prathyusha Sukumar, Rayner Ulloa, Avimanyou Vatsa, Alexander Casti
abstract: Forecasting stock prices and corresponding emotions is a complex process. Economic and financial forecasting leverages financial planning and growth predictions for the country and the industrialist, significantly impacting investors’ lives and financial losses. Therefore, this study investigates the impact of integrating sentiment analysis data on the accuracy of LSTM sequential models and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for predicting semiconductor stocks’ daily closing prices. Also, the predictive performance of models trained solely on historical prices and technical indicators (Volume, RSI, MACD) versus models augmented with a general sentiment score derived from financial news, with missing sentiment values imputed using forward-fill. The performance metrics, Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Directional Accuracy, are used to evaluate the models. In conclusion, results indicate that incorporating forward-filled sentiment data marginally improved average directional accuracy across the tested stocks but did not consistently reduce RMSE or MAE compared to models without sentiment features. Moreover, the Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network (ST-GNN) outperforms sequential models. The ST-GNN enhances predictive performance through dynamic edge modeling, improved data fusion, and adaptive learning. It also demonstrates that fusing quantitative and qualitative data within a graph-based deep learning framework can yield more stable, accurate stock forecasts by incorporating financial indicators, stock data, and sentiment analysis.

title: Maximum Versoria Criterion Based Filtering for Robust SINS/GNSS Integration
authors: Yi Mao, Wei Gu, Feng Zhu, Pingping Ji, Tingting Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In practical applications of the Strapdown Inertial Navigation System/Global Navigation Satellite System (SINS/GNSS) integrated navigation system, complex operational environments and electromagnetic interference always lead to signal obstruction and disturbance, resulting in nonlinear mixed Gaussian noise in the signals received by satellite receivers. However, existing filtering algorithms designed for non-Gaussian noise often suffer from high computational complexity of kernel functions and low filtering accuracy. To address these limitations, a Maximum Versoria Criterion-based Unscented Kalman Filter (MVCUKF) algorithm is proposed, which introduces the Versoria function as the cost function and integrates it with the measurement update phase of the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) to derive the Maximum Versoria Criterion (MVC). MVC adjusts the weights of anomalous measurements in non-Gaussian noise environments to mitigate the impact of interference noise on state estimation, while reducing computational complexity without compromising filtering accuracy under non-Gaussian noise conditions. Finally, the results of simulation experiment demonstrate that the proposed filtering algorithm significantly mitigates the impact of non-Gaussian noise on SINS/GNSS integrated navigation, enhancing the accuracy of navigation filtering and proving the robustness.

title: A Novel Identification and Measurement Method for Transient External Heat Flux in Lunar Orbit
authors: Qinghua Gao, Shouwen Liu, Boying Lin, Dongliang Wu, Yanqiang Bi, Yan Zhou, Chenwei Hong
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Space external heat flux significantly affects the thermal design of spacecraft. In lunar orbit, external heat flux includes solar radiation, lunar infrared radiation, lunar albedo, and the cold black space environment. Generally, numerical simulation methods are used to calculate the external heat flux of spacecraft. Alternatively, infrared remote sensing technology or microwave radiosensors directly measure the lunar surface temperature. However, there is no precedent for directly measuring lunar orbit external heat flux using sensors. This paper proposes a transient radiant heat flux differentiation method. It relies on temperature differences caused by inconsistent surface radiation characteristics. This method enables the measurement of solar radiation, lunar infrared radiation, and lunar albedo in lunar orbit, filling the technical gap in this field. The physical processes of transient heat flux measurement for solar radiation and lunar surface infrared radiation are theoretically analyzed. A new measurement mechanism is proposed. Two sets of thermal control coatings with different solar absorptivity and infrared emissivity are selected to establish the transient identification and measurement scheme for external heat flux. The design scheme is verified through theoretical analysis and experiments. Results show that the transient infrared heat flux identification error is less than $3 \%$, and the solar radiant heat flux identification error is less than $6 \%$. This confirms the correctness of the proposed identification and measurement method.

title: Eye Focused Efficientnetb3 for Lightweight and Accurate Crossed Versus Normal Eye Classification
authors: S. Leela Lakshmi, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Md Ankushavali, Avani Mittal
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This research will be focused on the implementation of EfficientNetB3 in the automatic classification of Crossed Eyes and Normal Eyes using a dataset in Kaggle, which contains 534 images of the same cases. To improve the focus of the EfficientNetB3 model and minimize background bias, a strategy of region of interest was adopted to extract the region containing the eye, which was then resized to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224$</tex> and normalized. The dataset will be split into training, validation, and test sets at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 \%, 10 \%$</tex>, and 10 %, respectively. The transfer learning will be applied to the EfficientNetB3 model by training it using ImageNet and a light classification head for 25 epochs using the Adam optimizer and binary cross-entropy loss. On the independent test data set containing 53 images, the accuracy of the proposed model was 90.57 %, and the precision/recall/F1-score for Crossed Eyes and Normal Eyes were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.857 / 0.900 / 0.878$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.938 / 0.909 / 0.923$</tex>, respectively. The confusion matrix showed only four errors, and from this, it is confirmed that the model is stable in terms of generalization. The above results confirm that the proposed model, i.e., EfficientNetB3 along with ROI-based preprocessing, can be used to achieve an effective and computationally efficient solution for the problem of automated crossed-eyed

title: Lunar Regolith Volatile Induction Heating Extraction Device: Design and Validation
authors: Runqi Yuan, Junyue Tang, Zihao Yu, Shengyuan Jiang, Zhan Sun, Bo Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In-situ analysis of volatiles in lunar polar regolith represents an important direction in current lunar exploration. However, due to the relatively limited energy available in the lunar polar regions and the low thermal conductivity of lunar soil, traditional resistance heating methods for in-situ volatile extraction face challenges such as high energy consumption, slow heating rates, and failure to reach required temperatures. The Lunar Regolith Volatile Detection Payload onboard the Chang’e7 rover employs an induction heating method to extract water and other volatiles from lunar soil. Based on Faraday’s law of electromagnetic induction, this paper describes the design and working principle of the Lunar Regolith Volatile Induction Heating Extraction Device and establishes a performance test platform to experimentally validate its heating capabilities. Tests conducted in a vacuum environment demonstrated that the heating frequency and induction power exhibit a resonant characteristic-first increasing and then decreasing. Under a 15V power supply, a maximum heating power of 60 W was achieved, enabling precise temperature control between $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $700^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, with a temperature deviation of $\leq 50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and a heating time of $\leq 120$ seconds. These results meet the requirements for in-situ volatile detection in lunar regolith under orbital conditions. This study provides technical support for the in-situ extraction and analysis of volatiles in lunar polar regolith.

title: An Empirical Study of Version-Control and Validation Workflows for Enterprise AI Systems
authors: Abhijit Choudhary, Avimanyou Vatsa, Rohan Puppala, Aarav Rao
abstract: Today, AI systems have been embedded in enterprise software and are surrounded by a large array of infrastructure, supporting code, and processes of traditional software. An AI system inherits all the challenges of regular code, as well as AI-specific system-level challenges, thereby introducing risks of technical debt and costly future maintenance. This warrants a hybrid framework that, on the one hand, mitigates AI-specific issues through a validation approach that caters to both stochastic and deterministic behaviors typically observed in LLM-powered systems. On the other hand, it emphasizes adopting standard software version control practices, in which every feature promotion passes through quality gates that involve both automated checks and human-in-the-loop review, ensuring controlled evolution and backward compatibility of the system. Therefore, this study revisits the underpinnings of enterprise workflows across key system boundaries in software development and employs an LLM-as-a-Judge approach to mitigate AI system drift.

title: An Explainable and Uncertainty-Aware Deep Learning Framework for Histopathological Breast Cancer Detection
authors: Rajkumar Pandiarajan, Kailasam Selvaraj, Vimal Sekar, Venkadesh V, Sowmiya N, Anuja J
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Breast cancer diagnosis from histopathological images remains a challenging task due to complex tissue morphology and variability in visual patterns. This work presents an integrated deep learning framework designed to support automated and reliable classification of benign and malignant breast tissue samples. A transfer learning strategy based on a pre-trained ResNet50 network is employed to extract discriminative features while reducing training complexity. To improve decision reliability, uncertainty estimation is incorporated using Monte Carlo Dropout, enabling probabilistic confidence assessment through multiple stochastic forward passes. In addition, gradient-based activation mapping is applied to visualize spatial regions influencing classification outcomes, thereby enhancing interpretability and clinical transparency. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves stable convergence, improved generalization, and confidence-aware predictions. By combining feature learning, uncertainty modelling, and visual explanation within a unified architecture, the framework provides a trustworthy decision-support system for digital pathology applications.

title: Engineering Education on Professional Curriculum Practical Computer Science
authors: Igor Krepkov, Alexander Yermakov, Oleg Zhnyakin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article discusses a systematic approach to the development of professional educational curriculum on practical computing science. The authors justify the need for a profound transformation of curricula in response to the strategic objectives of technological sovereignty and digitalization of the economy, as well as global challenges in the field of digital transformation. The article proposes the use of the enterprise architecture (EA) concept as a methodological framework, which ensures the integrity and practical orientation of training. Based on an analysis of labor market trends and changes in students' educational motivation, the article formulates key principles for the development of the curriculum, as well as detailed educational trajectories for the main blocks of competencies. The article concludes with a model for training highly qualified IT specialists who are capable of immediately and effectively solving fullstack production tasks.

title: Explainable AI for Smart Irrigation using Soil Moisture and Climate Data
authors: Vignesh R, Janani C, Dhyanesh M, A. Syed Ismail
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Efficient planning of irrigation is very important for sustainable agriculture in Tamil Nadu as climate variability and growing water scarcity requires data-driven decision making. Conventional methods of scheduling irrigation are based on limited single station weather observations, does not consider spatial climate variability and even ignored soil heterogeneity often resulting in inaccurate estimates of water requirements, which are also not easily interpreted. This study focuses on proposing an explainable and spatially aware irrigation prediction system that incorporates multi-location satellite-derived climate data from the NASA POWER API) called POWER API, soil classification based on FAO framework of Food and Agriculture Organization International (FAO) and advanced machine learning regression models to predict irrigation requirements for a trajectory of days in litres per hectare. The area under study is Tamil Nadu, whole Each observation is collected at daily intervals establishing a complete data set of 54,810 observations over 30 spatial grid locations covering the period 2020-2024. Multiple regression models such as Linear Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest and XGBoost have been systematically analysed for ensuring robust model selection, to prevent bias induced by model dependence on a single method. Random Forest gave the highest overall performance with R2 value of 0.9999 with MAE of 279 L/ha and XGBoost performed best in terms of generalization with a cross validation R2 value of 0.9973. The reason for the high R2 values are explained to the structured nature of the data set, consistent environmental relationships, and feature engineering for the three spatial and temporal dimensions. Explainable Artificial Intelligence using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) that rainfall, longitude and temperature are the biggest predictors, but soil type has a significant moderating effect on water retention capacity. In addition, the system includes real-time simulation function to model dynamic irrigation demand, which can improve the practicality of the use. The framework proposed is to support precision irrigation, promote transparency and trust in the AI-driven recommendations, and contribute to sustainable water resource management in the smart agriculture systems.

title: Cross-Attention-Based Intelligent Video Temporal Grounding for CCTV-Based Crime Identification in Smart Cities
authors: Abdul Jawwad, Akil Krishna, Ch. Madhavi Sudha
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The key to automated processing of the long CCTV surveillance data in the intelligent cities is intelligent video temporal grounding, which allows detecting crimes on time and situational awareness. It is suggested in this paper that the adoption of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) as a context-aware query refinement system and frozen crossattention multimodal transformers (SlowFast R50 + CLIP) as a fine-grained temporal localizer would form a two-stage Multimodal Video Temporal Grounding (MMVTG) framework. Some of the major issues that have been tackled through the framework include sparse events, query bias, and cross-domain generalization. Experimental analysis of UCA dataset shows a large improvement in the accuracy of temporal localization (7388%), temporal Intersection over Union (tIoU 7889%), incident detection accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(86-90 \%)$</tex>, and response latency over baseline techniques. The suggested MMVTG system can be used in scalable real-time surveillance and offers a solid solution to intelligent smart city monitoring.

title: Self-Supervised Meta-Adaptive Multimodal Graph Attention Learning for Clinical Decision Support
authors: Sharmistha S, Shrinidhi C, Pandeeswaran C
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Clinical decision support a novel hybrid, multimodal, and graph-based learning, meta-learning, cloud-native AI system can be used to achieve better patient prognosis prediction and reduce prescription errors. The system uses modality-specific encoders that are contrastively trained to combine structured EHR information, modality-specific time-series vital signs and unstructured prescription text to generate strong patient embeddings. A capsule-based layer that explains higher-level features is represented by the model of Graph Attention Networks (GAT), in which the relations between the symptoms, the diagnoses, the labs, and medications are described. The knowledge graphs are used in a special prescription safety engine, which tells the drug-drug interaction, contraindication, and dosage aberrations. The meta-learning with prototypical networks is able to learn new patients or sparse-data clinical setting within a relatively short period of time. In self-supervised learning (SSL), one trains on large unlabeled data so as to strengthen the representational quality. Guided explainability Attention-guided explainability is used to induce the most significant factors influencing predictions and increase trust among the clinicians. It has been shown in experimental conditions that superior prognosis, security prescription and excellent generalization in various conditions of low information, is achieved which is a scalable, versatile and understandable clinical decision support system.

title: FBG-Based Wearable Sensors for Healthcare 4.0: A System-Level Review with AI, Generative AI, and XAI Integration
authors: Pradeep Kumar, Ruchi Antil, Eugene Su, Chao-Ching Ho
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Areas in Sensors
abstract: Wearable sensor (WS) technology has advanced rapidly in recent years, driven by the increasing demand for continuous physiological monitoring. Among various sensing approaches, fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensors have emerged as strong candidates for wearable systems due to their lightweight nature, electromagnetic immunity, multiplexing capability, and high sensitivity to strain, temperature, and motion. This review presents recent developments in FBG-based wearable sensors within the framework of Healthcare 4.0, where sensing, computation, and communication are integrated into a unified cyber-physical architecture. A structured literature review methodology is adopted to ensure comprehensive coverage and reproducibility. The fundamentals of FBG sensing and their implementation in wearable platforms are first outlined. Representative applications-including MRI-compatible cardiorespiratory monitoring, carotid pulse assessment, skin temperature mapping, gait analysis, and joint posture detection-are examined to highlight the performance advantages of FBG systems. A comparative analysis is further provided to distinguish FBG wearables from conventional electronic sensors in terms of sensitivity, robustness, and compatibility with clinical environments. Key challenges, such as interrogator size, power consumption, and placement-dependent sensitivity variations, are critically discussed. Emerging solutions are explored through the integration of artificial intelligence (AI), explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) for model interpretability, generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) for synthetic data augmentation and improved generalization, and singular value decomposition (SVD)-based signal decoupling and dimensionality reduction for efficient edge processing. Additionally, advancements in compact photonic interrogators and fog-cloud data processing frameworks are highlighted. Therefore, FBG-based wearable technologies demonstrate strong potential for enabling reliable, high-precision, and intelligent health monitoring in next-generation Healthcare 4.0 systems.

title: Development of a GIS Methodology for Assessing the Environmental Safety of Urbanized Territories
authors: Ilya V. Korolev, Natig M. Muradov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel, reproducible, and spatially explicit methodology for the comprehensive assessment of environmental safety in urban areas, grounded in Geographic Information Systems (GIS). The approach uniquely integrates three core components: (1) legally binding requirements of Russian sanitary legislation—particularly those concerning Sanitary Protection Zones (SPZs) and permissible emission thresholds; (2) multi-temporal remote sensing data (including satellite imagery and land surface temperature indicators); and (3) advanced spatial analysis techniques implemented in the open-source QGIS platform. The methodology employs a multi-criteria decision-making framework to construct an integrated environmental risk index that accounts for cumulative impacts from industrial sources, traffic emissions, green space deficiency, and proximity to hazardous facilities. The proposed method was pilot-tested in the Kapotnya district of Moscow—a historically industrialized and socially vulnerable area with documented air quality concerns. The GIS-based assessment revealed that approximately 12% of the district’s residential territory is exposed to elevated cumulative environmental risks that were not identified in official state environmental reports or urban planning documentation. These "blind spots" primarily resulted from the aggregation of multiple low-intensity stressors that, when considered individually, fall below regulatory thresholds but collectively pose significant health hazards.

title: Matching Comes First: Efficient Certificateless Lattice-Based Bilateral Access Control with On-Demand Matching
authors: Xin Gao, Huaqun Wang, Mengxue Yang, Hua Dai, Debiao He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The proliferation of data-driven services on cloud platforms, coupled with stringent regulatory frameworks like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), necessitates crypto-graphic solutions that ensure secure and efficient data exchange. However, simultaneously achieving post-quantum security, bilateral access control, data authenticity, simplified key management and efficiency remains a critical challenge. To address these issues, this paper introduces a certificateless lattice-based matchmaking encryption (CLLME) to provide post-quantum security while obviating key escrow and certificate management. The proposed scheme enforces bilateral access control, allowing both data senders and receivers to specify matching access structures; decryption is thus contingent upon mutual authorization. Moreover, to prevent the costly decryption of numerous irrelevant ciphertexts, CLLME embeds a lightweight authenticity operation, which enables retrieval of useful data, effectively creating a high-performance filter for encrypted data streams. We formally prove that CLLME achieves indistinguishability against chosen-plaintext attacks and existential unforgeability against chosen-message attacks under lattice-based assumptions. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that CLLME maintains favorable communicational and computational efficiency, confirming its suitability for practical and scalable deployment in regulation-compliant, large-scale data sharing environments.

title: Cyber Range for Cybersecurity Research in Digital Substation Networks
authors: Artyom Gavrilov, Anatoly Minzov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This article focuses on a training testbed that represents a secured local area network of a digital substation. The paper examines the testbed’s composition, its functional capabilities, and potential applications in the educational process. Additionally, the article outlines the procedure for organizing cybersecurity competitions using the developed testbed as a cyber range.

title: Automatic Reconstruction Algorithms for in vivo Skeletal Muscle 3D-SWEI Data
authors: Courtney Trutna Paley, Felix Q. Jin, Anna E. Knight, Ned C. Rouze, Mark L. Palmeri, Kathryn R. Nightingale
abstract: Rotational 3D Shear Wave Elasticity Imaging (3D-SWEI) offers many opportunities for the development of biomechanical biomarkers of muscle health. However, methods for material parameter reconstruction to date have involved manual processing steps, which are impractical for clinical implementations. In this work we describe and optimize algorithms for the automatic processing of 3D-SWEI skeletal muscle data and explore the most important factors affecting the success of automatic estimation of the shear wave speed (SWS) along and across the fibers. We compare the results of several automatic algorithms to the results obtained using manual processing across 130 in vivo 3D-SWEI datasets, evaluating the percent of valid acquisitions (non-gross-error), percent bias, and absolute percent error for each algorithm. We evaluate three major factors for algorithm optimization: wave detection method (comparing Radon Sum, time to peak, and cross correlation), lateral range selection (comparing a single range for all acquisition angles, changing the range with fiber orientation, and dynamic range selection, in addition to analyzing the start and end positions of the lateral range), and methods to improve robustness (exploring detecting the wave through 3D fits, and methods to isolate individual waves when multiple waves are detected). We conclude the optimal algorithm for passive muscle is a fixed lateral range (2-18 mm) Radon Sum method with multiwave detection and SWS fitting to the ellipse predicted in transversely isotropic materials, and we provide results that can guide algorithm choice for other applications. Additionally, we have made the implementation of all our algorithms publicly accessible at: DOI 10.5281/zenodo.18883176.

title: Real-Time Torque Gap Compensation for Electric Cars On Rough Terrain
authors: S. Divya, G. Sudha Ram, V. Ashok, M. Dharun Pandi, S. Tamizharasan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Challenges encountered when operating an electric vehicle include uneven ground surfaces as well as having large gaps between multiple torque sensors which negatively impact vehicle performance. The proposed model is designed to create a real-time torque compensation tool so that an electric vehicle travels smoothly. This is done by using an electric vehicle platform equipped with an integrated set of systems that provide the capabilities of performing real-time torque compensation. The torque compensation system uses tilt readings taken by an MPU6050 on the electric vehicle, to determine the characteristics of the terrain traversed by the electric vehicle, for real-time processing of readings accomplished using an ESP32 microcontroller that drives a DC motor to deliver an adequate level of energy to the axle of the electric vehicle. The proposed model has also utilized differential analysis of the tilted position of the electric vehicle to provide a means of adjusting the output from the torque sensor based upon the characteristics of the surface on which the electric vehicle is operated. Experimental results produced an improvement in the development of torque between motors, a decrease of 40% to overall slip at each wheel of the electric vehicle, an average pitch tolled at an acceptable level of ±2 degrees relative to the reference axis and an improvement in power supply efficiency.

title: Super-Resolution Assisted Mask-Aware Face Recognition for Low-Quality CCTV Surveillance
authors: Vijayalakshmi, Raghul S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The uses of the surveillance systems are important in the current smart city security systems, but the process of identifying the suspects with low-quality CCTV footage is a difficult task because of blur, noise, occlusion and partial visibility of the face by masks. This study presents a Super-Resolution Assisted Mask-Aware Face Recognition architecture that aims at reliably recognizing a person based on a low-quality surveillance footage. The system incorporates several modules of deep learning such as Real-ESRGAN to improve super-resolution reconstruction, YOLOv8-Face to find real-time face, Vision Transformer with mask-aware attention to extract features in an occlusion aware manner and ArcFace (ResNet100) to generate robust facial embeddings. Frames of CCTV footage are extracted at low resolution and super-resolution reconstruction is used to improve fine facial features. The detected faces are then processed by the mask-aware attention mechanism that lays emphasis in visible parts of the face like the eyes and forehead and ignoring the covered parts. Facial embeddings are extracted and compared to the suspect reference image on the cosine similarity and by using the Softmax-based confidence scoring. The proposed system is proved to have face recognition accuracy of 96.8%, detection precision of 97.2% and recall performance of 95.9% even in adversarial masked and low-resolution conditions through experimental evaluation. The findings indicate that image restoration should be combined with attention-based face analysis as it ensures greater reliability in recognition. The suggested framework presents a powerful automated approach to law enforcement agencies in terms of decreasing the workloads of manual CCTV surveillance and real-time surveillance real-time-based identification of the suspect in a faster and more accurate manner.

title: AI Applications in Information Systems and Their Role in Supporting Organizational Decision-Making: A Study on Jordanian Institutions
authors: Mohammed Moghazi Ezzeldein Ahmed, Hussain Ahmad Awad, Omar Mohammad Alquabeh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study investigates the role of artificial intelligence (AI) applications in information systems in supporting organizational decision-making within Jordanian institutions. Using a quantitative methodology, the findings demonstrate that AI applications significantly enhance both decision quality and timely decision-making. Furthermore, decision quality and timely decision-making substantially influence overall organizational performance, with timely decision-making exerting a particularly strong impact on institutional effectiveness. This study provides valuable empirical evidence for the literature on AI, information systems, and organizational decision support, highlighting the key factors of the proposed research model.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-based Link Optimization in MANETs over Time-Varying Fading Channels
authors: Khushbu Rai, Dr. Bhawana Pillai, Jitendra Agarwal, Dr. Bhupesh Gour
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Mobile Ad Hoc Networks are inherently dynamic and unpredictable with their nodes constantly moving about, as well as experiencing signal loss (fading) on their channels. In this research, a Deep Reinforcement Learning method for optimizing links in a time-varying Rayleigh fading environment for MANETs was developed. The problem was formulated as a Markov Decision Problem and solved using a Deep Q-Network (DQN). The simulation showed an improvement in throughput of the network, an increase in the number of packets delivered, and a reduction in energy usage of each node over other heuristic and classical methods that used Q-learning. The DQN's learned policy adapted well to both different fading and mobility conditions. Additionally, the spatial interference was minimized through the smart power adjustments. Overall, this research demonstrated that the DRL method is able to efficiently solve the complex wireless optimization problems even in environments which are very random and change frequently.

title: An Explainable Retrieval-Augmented AI Framework for Detecting Food and Nutrition Misinformation
authors: S Sreeraj, Gokul R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Misinformation about food has turned into a significant concern of popular health because of the rapid proliferation of unproven nutrition attributions in the Internet space. Dietary, food safety, and health advantage information is highly likely to be deceptive and affect the behavior and choice of the people. The currently used misinformation detection methods are mostly based on the key-word-match methods, rule-filtering, or other traditional machine learning classifiers which are not scientifically grounded and have no explaination. In response to this issue, this paper suggests NutriGuard, an artificial intelligence-based food misinformation detection system that is based on natural language processing, semantic embedding, and a Retrieval-Augmented Generation architecture.
