This system takes in nutrition claims submitted by users and converts the claims into semantic vectors, then finding suitable scientific support at an established knowledge base of peer reviewed nutrition literature. The evidence obtained is framed with the contextual thinking in order to categorize claims into true, false or misleading and give justifiable reasons supported by references. Experimental analysis on a dataset of online nutrition claims indicates that the given system reaches an accuracy of 96.85% and preciseness of 95.9%, recall of 96.3% and F1-score of 96.1%, which is better than the traditional misinformation detection models. The findings also prove that, when evidence-based retrieval is combined with the language model reasoning, the reliability and transparency of the food misinformation detection systems are greatly enhanced. NutriGuard is a framework that offers a sound platform on how to market scientifically sound nutrition facts and allow the informed decisions of the populace.

title: Entrepreneurial Culture in the Age of Artificial Intelligence: A Strategic Framework for Sustainable Development in the Mena Region
authors: Mohammed Salman Nimah, Basim Faisal Abdul Nayef, Khamees Naser Mohammed, Ibrahim Hadi Salem Aljumaili, Qayssar Khaleel Mhaidi, Marwan Abdeldayem
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This study aims to highlight the role of entrepreneurship in achieving sustainable development in MENA in its three dimensions: economic, social, and environmental. This is done in light of the global trend towards entrepreneurship culture in general, and in MENA in particular, and to assess MENA regional and global standing while analyzing its strengths and weaknesses. The study was based on several methodologies, including the descriptive analytical approach, which reviewed and analyzed previous studies related to the topic and highlighted the contribution of the current study. The deductive approach was used to analyze the study variables to clarify the role of entrepreneurship in achieving sustainable development. Additionally, the modern scientific approach was employed to construct and formulate a standard model to test the relationship between entrepreneurship and sustainable development during the period (2020-2024). The study recommends to support entrepreneurial projects through economic development policies, including public policies that minimize regulatory and administrative barriers, compliance costs and tax burdens, ensure market functioning and competition, and guarantee effective bankruptcy laws. Working to overcome difficulties in obtaining the necessary funds to finance projects. Increasing support for training and continuing education through the establishment of community centers for entrepreneurship. The necessity of directing entrepreneurial projects towards encouraging investment in clean energy that achieves environmental preservation and sustainable development in its environmental dimension

title: Aligning Perception, Reasoning, Modeling and Interaction: A Survey on Physical AI
authors: Kun Xiang, Terry Jingchen Zhang, Yinya Huang, Jixi He, Zirong Liu, Yueling Tang, Ruizhe Zhou, Lijing Luo, Youpeng Wen, Xiuwei Chen, Bingqian Lin, Jianhua Han, Hang Xu, Hanhui Li, Bin Dong, Xiaodan Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: The convergence of embodied intelligence and world models has catalyzed growing interest in integrating physical laws into AI systems. While prior surveys have examined world models and embodied intelligence separately, we focus on the progression that connects these capabilities as a unified developmental pathway from passive observation to active physical comprehension. This survey provides a systematic framework revealing how physical AI advances through four interconnected stages: perception transforms sensory data into structured physical representations, reasoning derives explanations from observed phenomena, modeling enables predictive simulation grounded in physical principles, and embodied interaction closes the loop through physical manipulation and environmental feedback. Each stage enables and enhances the next: perceptual grounding supports causal reasoning, reasoning unlocks predictive capabilities, and robust models drive genuine physical interaction. Through analysis of developments spanning architectural innovations, training methodologies, causal inference, and embodied systems, we synthesize how physical understanding emerges through cumulative integration across this progression. Our framework reveals the evolution from isolated, task-specific solutions toward integrated architectures that advance from pattern recognition toward causal reasoning and counterfactual prediction. This perspective provides foundations for next-generation physical AI systems with direct implications for safe, generalizable, and interpretable deployment across robotics, scientific discovery, and autonomous systems. We maintain a continuously updated taxonomy repository at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/AI4Phys/Awesome-AI-for-Physics</uri>.

title: STAIR-Tr: Transformer-based Gap Filling for Multi-source Satellite Data Fusion
authors: Kejie Zhao, Kaiyu Guan, Qu Zhou, Sibo Wang, Sheng Wang, Yizhi Huang, Alexander Schwing
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Satellite imagery offers consistent and scalable data for large-scale ecosystem monitoring and provides inputs for earth system modeling. However, two primary challenges hinder the applications of satellite images: (1) the presence of data gaps due to clouds or sensor malfunctions, and (2) the trade-offs between spatial and temporal resolutions of an individual satellite sensor. Multi-source satellite data fusion is widely adopted to address these challenges, such as ESTARFM and STAIR. These methods heavily rely on cloud-free images for reference and are either manually intensive or computationally expensive, potentially limiting their scalability and application to large regions. In this study, we proposed a novel transformer-based framework that addresses these challenges by efficiently filling gaps and fusing multi-source optical satellite data, STAIR-Tr (STAIR-Transformer). Our approach utilized a transformer-based model for gap filling, followed by a multi-sensor fusion model that generates high-resolution, daily, and cloud-free satellite data. Specifically, we tested STAIR-Tr for fusing Landsat-8 (16-day and 30-m) and MODIS (daily and 500-m) data at three regions in North and South America. Compared with STAIR, STAIR-Tr significantly improved the accuracy and efficiency of gap filling, with improvements in normalized Mean Absolute Error (nMAE) from 4.83% to 3.48%, the coefficient of determination (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathrm{R}}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) from 0.763 to 0.883, an 18-fold speedup with GPU acceleration, and a 2.3-fold cost reduction. Compared with ESTARFM, STAIR-Tr overcame the limitation of requiring cloud-free images and expanded the pool of available reference images, which further enhanced the fusion process, with an increase in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathrm{R}}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> from 0.51 to 0.64 and a decrease in nMAE from 23.2% to 20.4%. STAIR-Tr improves the accuracy and efficiency of multi-source satellite data fusion using a transformer-based gap filling architecture, making it more suitable for applications requiring frequent and high-resolution satellite observations.

title: Adaptive Endmember Bundle Extraction with a Modified Differential Evolution Algorithm for Addressing Spectral Variability in Geology-1 Hyperspectral Imagery
authors: Linpei Yang, Ruyi Feng, Banxiao Ruan, Kaijun Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: High-precision extraction of alteration mineral information provides crucial guidance for hyperspectral remote sensing mineral exploration. Currently, widely used hyperspectral datasets (such as ZY-1 02D and GF-5) lack targeted design in spectral coverage and sensitive bands for minerals. The successful launch of China's first dedicated hyperspectral satellite for geology and mineral exploration - Geology-1 - has addressed this gap and provides an optimal data source for detailed alteration mineral identification. However, mineral spectra are susceptible to variation under complex geological conditions. To effectively characterize this spectral variability, the endmember bundle method is adopted, which depicts an endmember category through a set of spectra. Existing endmember bundle extraction methods suffer from low computational efficiency and inadequate accuracy when processing high-dimensional hyperspectral data under complex geological conditions, which limits the effective interpretation of mineral information. To address this challenge, we propose an Adaptive Differential Evolution Endmember Bundle Extraction method (ADE-EBE). Based on a multi-stage optimization framework, this method transforms high-dimensional discrete space into low-dimensional continuous space for dynamic optimization. By employing an adaptive differential evolution algorithm to globally search for optimal endmember combinations, it significantly enhances the stability and accuracy of endmember bundle extraction. Experiments on simulated dataset and Geology-1 hyperspectral imagery show that ADE-EBE achieves superior overall performance compared to traditional and state-of-the-art methods in terms of spectral fidelity, abundance accuracy, and computational efficiency.

title: Prescribed Performance-Based Sampled-Data Adaptive Fuzzy Control for Incommensurate Uncertain Air Handling Units in HVAC
authors: Xinyao Li, Ying Zou, Chao Deng, Jian Cen, Feiqi Deng
abstract: This article studies the prescribed performance-based sampled-data control for air handling units formulated in fractional order dynamics with non linearities, system uncertainties, and extrinsic disturbances in the heating, ventilating, and air conditioning systems, which consumes about half of the energy of the electricity system for buildings, to achieve effective indoor temperature regulation. The unknown system nonlinearities are handled by approximation through the incorporation of fuzzy logic systems. Besides, taking the hereditary and infinite memory properties of fractional- order calculus into consideration, a refined control protocol is derived based on the integration of the prescribed performance bound technique and the adaptive sampled-data backstepping control scheme. With this control protocol, it can effectively lessen control-related resources while maintaining adherence to the specified indoor temperature tracking performance. Based on the Lyapunov stability analysis, it is confirmed that even under multiple time-varying system uncertainties and unknown disturbances in practice, the proposed method can assure the stability of the closed-loop systems with the indoor temperature tracking error to be constantly within the specified performance bound. Simulated and experimental results display the effectiveness of our developed scheme.

title: SafeBeat Rx—Moving ECG AI Beyond Diagnostics to Recommending Treatment
authors: Rachita Navara, Margaret Ferari, Walker McHugh
abstract: Drug-induced QT prolongation is the leading cause of FDA nonapproval and a key safety concern for many medications, including cardiac drugs, antibiotics, psychotropic drugs, and oncology treatments. Conventional automated QT algorithms have shown variable reliability in recordings with abnormal or irregular morphology, with misclassification rates up to 75%. This article presents SafeBeat Rx, an AI-powered ECG platform that provides beat-by-beat ECG analysis, enabling full transparency and optional adjustment of measurements for each individual ECG heartbeat. Validated against expert cardiologist adjudication, SafeBeat addresses a critical gap in postdischarge cardiac surveillance and represents a novel approach to guideline-adherent pharmacological disease management.

title: MMDOK: A Multi-modal and Multi-scale Disease-oriented Fusion Framework with Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
authors: Wei Li, Xun Gong, Lin Fan, Xinxin Li, Jiao Li, Xiaobin Sun
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Learning medical visual representations directly from image-report pairs has become an emerging topic in representation learning. However, the heterogeneity and complementary nature of medical reports and images pose challenges to adaptively fusing. We propose a Multi-modal and Multi-scale Disease-oriented fusion framework with Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (MMDOK). This method utilizes the multi-scale features naturally present in medical images and reports and explores feature fusion between multi-view or multi-modal images and textual reports. Specifically, MMDOK designs a text encoder and two image encoders to enhance the diagnostic model by adaptively integrating text supervision and additional image information. To enhance the model's ability to extract information across different semantic scales, we introduce a Bidirectional Cross-Attention (BCA) module and a Cross-Modal Clustering (CMC) module. The proposed CMC module effectively captures disease consistent latent features from both images and clinical reports, alleviating overfitting and improving generalization to out-of-distribution test sets. To reduce cross-modal redundancy, we introduce a Disease-Oriented Attention (DOA) module, which adaptively assigns modality-specific weights based on disease labels, enabling more effective and context-aware feature fusion. Additionally, we embed Kolmogorov-Arnold Network layers into various modules to enable efficient and accurate feature extraction through enhanced nonlinear representation capacity. We evaluate our model's performance, achieving an average accuracy of 94.27% and 83.67% with and without text supervision on a public lung disease dataset, and 87.63% and 82.1% on a private submucosal tumors dataset, outperforming state-of-the-art alternatives.

title: Agent xG Core: Agentic AI for Next-Generation Mobile Core Network
authors: Maria Katarine Santana Barbosa, Kelvin Lopes Dias
abstract: To meet the stringent requirements of emerging applications and the increasingly complex network management and operation, the Next Generation Mobile Networks (NextG), or 6G, will adopt an AI-native architecture on the Core Network (CN). In this movement, the Third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) has extended the cellular CN with new function as a first step toward integrating analytics, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and machine learning. However, those new functionalities are constrained by a centralized approach and managerial complexity. Furthermore, with the rise of Large Language Models (LLMs), a new era in network orchestration and management begins, leveraging and empowering the Intent-based Networking (IBN) paradigm. In addition, AI agents and Agentic AI integrate Reasoning and Acting (ReAct), enabling the usage of such intents to continuously interact with the network. Unlike state-of-the-art approaches that primarily employ Agentic AI to mitigate deployment and configuration complexity in the CN, this paper introduces AgentxGCore, which leverages an Agentic AI-Native layer to extend the 3GPP architecture and enable a system based on the existing APIs across the Beyond Next Generation Core (xGC) domain. This proposal establishes an AI-driven closed-loop for continuous optimization based on real-time information, enabling self-organization and self-adaptation. Our approach involves a multi-agent specialized system, divided into a network planner agent, capable of visualizing the network state and developing a plan to meet the intents, and a network executor, responsible for criticizing and executing the plan. To validate the proposed solution, an environment was built using an open-source CN, heterogeneous datasets, and different LLMs were employed to demonstrate its effectiveness.

title:
Nanoporous design for enhancing full-band solar-blind absorption in Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    photodetectors
authors: Xieyu Lin, Jialong Li, Dan Yang, Linghua Wang, Haizhong Zhang, Xiaoqiang Lu, Minmin Zhu
abstract: Gallium oxide, characterized by its ultra-broad bandwidth, high electron drift velocity, and excellent thermal stability, demonstrates significant potential for solar-blind ultraviolet photodetectors (SBUV-PDs) that can be applied in both military and civilian contexts, such as missile warning, fire detection, and secure communications. However, the conventional metal semiconductor-metal (MSM) structure of photodetectors results in suboptimal responsivity due to reflection and absorption at the metal-semiconductor interfaces, as well as the inability of the semiconductor to effectively optimize light absorption. In this work, we propose a periodic nanopore design in Ga2O3 thin films, which capitalizes the coupling between incident light and the electronic oscillations on the Ga2O3 surface, thereby increasing the effective absorption area as well as inducing optical interference within the structure. Compared to the structure without surface modifications, the photodark current ratio (PDCR) increases by an order of magnitude, from 2.39×10⁷ to 4.26×10⁸, and the responsivity is enhanced by a factor of 5.87, rising from 0.55 to 3.23 A/W. This study is expected to offer a reliable strategy for the fabrication of high-performance SBUV-PDs.

title: Toward Lived Metaphor: Exploring AR-Based Visual Metaphors for Instructing and Learning Embodied Knowledge in Aikido Practice
authors: Yuto Suzuki, Yuji Hatada, Laia Turmo Vidal, Rintaro Fujino, Daisuke Sakamoto
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Designing Interactive Systems Conference
abstract: None

title: Tractable Approximation of Labeled Multi-Object Posterior Densities
authors: Thi Hong Thai Nguyen, Ba-Ngu Vo, Ba-Tuong Vo
abstract: Multi-object estimation in state-space models (SSMs) wherein the system state is represented as a finite set has attracted significant interest in recent years. In Bayesian inference, the posterior density captures all information on the system trajectory since it considers the history of the states. In most multi-object SSM applications, closed-form multi-object posteriors are not available for non-standard multi-object models. Thus, functional approximation is necessary because these posteriors are very high-dimensional. This work provides a tractable multi-scan Generalized Labeled Multi-Bernoulli (GLMB) approximation that matches the trajectory cardinality distribution of the labeled multi-object posterior density. The proposed approximation is also proven to minimize the Kullback-Leibler divergence over a special class of multi-scan GLMB model. Additionally, we develop a tractable algorithm for computing the approximate multi-object posteriors over finite windows. Numerical multi-object tracking experiments, using a simulated social force model with uninformative observations, and a real-world social force pedestrian dataset are presented to validate the proposed approximation method.

title: IEEE Fedback
authors: N/A

title: Text-Driven Relation Manipulation of Diffusion Imagery
authors: Yiming Li, Peng Zhou, Hongwei Hu, Xiaokang Qin, Jun Sun, Yi Xu
abstract: Text-guided image manipulation has recently attracted significant attention. Prevailing algorithms predominantly focus on modifying the appearances of existing instances, such as texture and attribute editing, while they often fail to address the interactions between different instances or achieve fundamental structural changes, such as multi-object editing. This paper introduces a novel text-guided manipulation task named “relation manipulation”, aimed at fundamentally altering the structure of images. This task is capable of modifying the quantity of instances and, more importantly, enhancing the understanding and editing of interactions among diverse instances. Our approach comprises two main components: relation customization and multi-region guided diffusion. Relation customization fine-tunes specific relationships using a compact dataset of exemplary relations, facilitating nuanced understanding and implementation of instance interactions. Multi-region guided diffusion employs gradient optimization to update the generation process across multiple regions, integrating a fine-grained attention control strategy to minimize regional interference and conflict. Additionally, the demonstrated applications of our method in multi-region inversion underline its potential in practical scenarios, such as relation manipulation of real images and consecutive image manipulation. Compatible with different variants of Stable Diffusion models, our approach seamlessly integrates into the Stable Diffusion WebUI, enabling high-quality image generation and exceptional control over extensive manipulation. This makes it a robust tool for both academic research and creative industries.

title: On the Convergence, Implicit Bias, and Edge of Stability of Gradient Descent in Deep Learning: Reviewing recent progress [Special Issue on the Mathematics of Deep Learning]
authors: Hancheng Min, Lachlan Ewen MacDonald, René Vidal
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) trained via gradient descent (GD) with random initialization and without any regularization enjoy good generalization performance in practice despite being highly overparametrized. To theoretically understand this puzzling phenomenon, many works on convergence analysis for GD algorithms on NNs have been developed over the last half-decade. In this article, we review these research efforts and discuss how they address three specific questions related to this puzzle. The first question is why GD finds a global minimum efficiently, which the literature has addressed by studying what level of overparametrization (width, depth, etc.) and what type of initialization lead to a benign optimization landscape along the training trajectory, facilitating a linear convergence rate of GD. The next question is why the global minimum found by GD generalizes well, which has been addressed by showing that overparametrization induces an implicit simplicity bias along the GD trajectory. More recently, it has been observed that in practice, training DNNs with far larger learning rates than theoretically permissible results in faster convergence and better generalization. This leads to the third question of why faster convergence and better generalization can be achieved with a large learning rate, which has been recently addressed by identifying a self-stabilization mechanism and implicit bias toward flat minima.

title: AirDC: Adaptive Iterative Depth Refinement Framework for Full-Range Metric Depth Completion
authors: Hongyu Shi, Yunqi Du, Hongjuan Zhang, Wenzhuo Li, Zhen Dong, Bijun Li, Luliang Tang
abstract: Accurate metric depth completion across wide depth ranges is critical for autonomous systems. However, existing methods often struggle to efficiently capture depth features at both close and long ranges, primarily due to the inadequate modeling of fine-grained depth cues specific to different depth ranges. To address these limitations, we propose AirDC, an adaptive iterative depth refinement framework for full-range metric depth completion. The core contributions of our model lie in the design of two key modules. Specifically, we first construct an adaptive fine-grained stereo-LiDAR feature fusion module to fundamentally strengthen the model’s capacity to preserve original full-range depth information. Built upon metric-aligned depth volumes (i.e., a 3D representation composed of cubic voxels uniformly partitioned in real-world metric space), this module employs an adaptive sub-voxel depth attention mechanism to enhance sensitivity to subtle depth variations across the full range, thereby both avoiding long-range accuracy degradation introduced by conventional disparity conversion and alleviating the coarse near-range granularity inherent in metric depth representations. Second, we introduce an iterative hypothesis-guided depth refinement module to improve prediction accuracy while maintaining memory efficiency. By integrating multi-scale multi-modal guidance information from depth hypotheses, this module enables explicit and progressive refinement of the initial depth estimation with a small parameter overhead. Experiments on multiple mainstream real-world and synthetic benchmarks demonstrate that AirDC achieves state-of-the-art performance, providing an effective solution for full-range metric depth completion. The code and data are available at https://github.com/yunqidu/AirDC.

title: The Next Frontier for AI-Generated Kernels: Correctness
authors: Guido Martínez, Tyler Sorensen
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGPLAN International Workshop on Principles of Agentic Engineering
abstract: None

title: Secondary Throughput Maximization for an UAV Based Cooperative Jammer in RIS aided Energy Harvesting in Secured Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: S Akshay Ganesh, B H Vikas, R Ashok, Avik Banerjee, H. V. Ravish Aradhya
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The present work proposes a novel reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) aided energy harvesting (EH) based cognitive radio network (CRN) architecture wherein a single secondary user (SU) harvest energy and transmit data securely in an underlay mode in presence of primary user (PU) and an eavesdropper (Eav). The system model incorporates RIS aided nonlinear (NL) EH mechanism, where the RIS is configured with 'M' reflecting elements. A key feature of the proposed framework is the incorporation of an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) based cooperative jammer to countermeasure eavesdropping. Two non-overlapping frame structures are considered, where the first slot enables EH at SU transmitter via time-splitting protocol while the second slot is meant for secondary data transmission using SU transmit power (Ps) in presence of an Eav. The total power budget P is jointly utilized by SU transmitter and jammer with a ratio of ξ : (1 – ξ), respectively. The aim of the study is to maximize the throughput of the SU while maintaining energy causality, interferences at the PU transmitter, and secrecy capacity constraints. The present work makes use of an SU centric RIS design, where the RIS phases are configured to coherently enhance the received signal at the SU receiver. According to this design, the optimal phase and amplitude are determined for each RIS element. The Lagrangian optimization and Karush Kuhn Tucker (KKT) conditions are then used for the joint optimization of Ps, EH time slot (τ) and ξ.The impact of the placement of RIS on SU throughput, considering scenarios where the RIS is positioned near or far from the SU transmitter or receiver, is analyzed. Furthermore, the effect of changing the position of the UAV when located near or far from the SU receiver and the Eav is also investigated. SU throughput analysis with the variation in M value, phase shift of RIS, partial interference factor (Ω), ξ, τ , Ps values are analyzed. Simulation results demonstrate the efficacy of RIS in enhancing SU throughput, yielding gain of ~ 78:1% and ~ 24:3% at M = 36 over existing works under ideal unit-modulus reflection.

title: TransPortal: A Portable Portal-Based Locomotion Technique
authors: Daichi Hirobe, Shizuka Shirai, Jason Orlosky, Masato Kobayashi, Yuki Uranishi, Haruo Takemura
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: In VR video games, users can explore virtual worlds by physically walking in the real world or by using alternative locomotion techniques such as teleportation or steering. However, these techniques often pose challenges, including disorientation or sickness. This study presents TransPortal, a novel portal-based locomotion technique for VR video games. The proposed technique allows users to manipulate a marker object to specify a destination and to move there through a portal that can be freely activated or deactivated. To evaluate the proposed method, we conducted a within-subjects experiment with 30 participants, comparing it with traditional teleportation and steering. The results show that TransPortal induces lower VR sickness in a complex scenario without reducing immersion or entertainment quality.

title: Confidence-Driven Sensorless Adaptive Optics for Target Recognition Using SPGD Optimization
authors: Xin Li, Huizhen Yang, Zhiguang Zhang
abstract: Adaptive optics (AO) compensates wavefront distortions caused by atmospheric turbulence; however, conventional wavefront sensor-based systems are limited in extended target imaging. Wavefront sensorless adaptive optics (WFSless AO) addresses this issue by optimizing image quality, with stochastic parallel gradient descent (SPGD) widely adopted. However, traditional metrics (e.g., variance and weighted high-frequency components) are often unstable under complex backgrounds or target motion and show weak correlation with recognition performance.This paper proposes a novel SPGD metric based on YOLOv11 recognition confidence and develops an integrated framework that combines turbulence compensation, object detection, and closed-loop control. Experiments on UAV targets under both static and dynamic turbulence demonstrate improved image clarity and detail, along with enhanced robustness compared to conventional metrics.

title: Model-Free End-to-End Learning for Probabilistic Shaping in IM/DD Systems
authors: Guifen Chen, Fengshuo Dong, Yijun Wang, Ruixin Miao
abstract: In high-speed IM/DD optical communication systems, conventional probabilistic shaping methods often have limited adaptability to complex channel impairments, restricting their information-rate gains. This is particularly relevant to optical transmission in space-air-ground integrated information networks with heterogeneous channel conditions. This paper proposes a model-free end-to-end (E2E) probabilistic shaping framework for complex IM/DD channels, which directly learns the transmit symbol distribution and jointly optimizes receiver equalization via GMI maximization, offering improved adaptability under realistic channel impairments without requiring explicit channel modeling. Simulation results show that, under identical channel conditions and a unified receiver structure, the proposed Learned PS scheme achieves a GMI of 4.72 bit/symbol, outperforming uniform 64QAM and the optimal MB-64QAM baseline by 0.16 and 0.08 bit/symbol, respectively. These results indicate that the proposed framework can effectively improve the information-rate performance of complex IM/DD systems and provide a feasible data-driven solution for probabilistic shaping design in practical optical communication links.

title: Domain Adversarial Neural Networks for Gender-Fair Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Hasan Md. Shahriare, Nursadul Mamun
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) systems often exhibit gender-based performance gaps due to spurious correlations between acoustic features and biological sex. Dataset balancing alone is insufficient, as bias arises from feature-level dependencies, necessitating algorithmic solutions. Therefore, this paper introduces GF-DANN, a domain adversarial learning framework employing a shared CNN–Transformer feature extractor and a Gradient Reversal Layer to learn emotion-discriminative yet gender-invariant representations. Evaluated on EARS and IEMOCAP under strict speaker-independent protocols, GF-DANN achieves 64.30% accuracy with a 4.87% gender gap on EARS. On IEMOCAP, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda=0.003$</tex>, accuracy improves from 49.65% to 53.35% with an 87.2% gap reduction; with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda=0.05$</tex>, near-complete bias elimination is achieved (0.64% gap, 93.3% reduction). The gender discriminator converges to chance level, confirming effective suppression of gender-related information. These results demonstrate that adversarial unlearning provides a tunable mechanism for enhancing fairness without sacrificing accuracy.

title: Agent-Based Model for Real-Time Forest Fire, Early Warning and Alerting System
authors: Qurat Ul Ain Aini, Sana Nasri, Nadeem Ahmad, Zaki Uddin, Iqra Ashfaq
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: Forest fires pose significant challenges for prediction and early detection, necessitating advanced technologies to mitigate their impact. The research proposes the design and development of an early warning and alert system for forest fires using an agent-based model. This study analyzes communication protocols, the Fire Weather Index (FWI) model, and system performance to enhance fire detection accuracy and response scalability. Key findings indicate that the proposed agent-based model demonstrates improved performance compared to existing technologies, such as wireless sensor networks and camera-based systems. However, the system’s performance could be further optimized by implementing customized communication protocols, which would enable agents to communicate and coordinate more effectively in real-time environments.

title: AI-Powered Secure e-Voting System with Facial Recognition for Real-Time Voting Authentication
authors: Sheikh Latifur Rahman, Sabbir Hossain Talukder, Sarafat Hussain Abhi, Md. Minhazul Islam, Md. Munsur Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Electronic voting systems face challenges with ensuring security, accuracy, and transparency. Many current systems rely on manual identity verification or paper-based processes that are vulnerable to human error, delays, and trust issues. This paper presents a novel AI-driven e-voting system that integrates facial recognition with embedded hardware control and a webbased dashboard for real-time monitoring. The system utilizes the InsightFace MobileFaceNet model for voter authentication and a Raspberry Pi for controlling the voting booth hardware. A Flask web application displays real-time voter participation and party vote counts, while the system ensures that voters can only vote once by using a secure local SQLite database. The system's performance is evaluated through simulation results, demonstrating robust real-time operation and accurate vote tracking, with performance improvements over conventional voting systems. This approach highlights the potential of combining deep learning with embedded systems to provide a secure, scalable, and transparent e-voting solution.

title: Secure Ultrasound Video Steganography Using Fractal Star Sweep and Deep Learning
authors: Sheikh Thanbir Alam, R. Badlishah Ahmad, Md. Maruf Hassan, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: What if a video could conceal secret data safely while still looking completely normal? This capability is vital for medical, defense, and surveillance fields. This paper proposes a new method called Fractal Star Sweep (FSS) video steganography using deep learning. First, secret messages are encrypted using RSA and the video frames are shuffled to increase security. A deep neural network finds safe areas in each frame where small changes to pixels are not noticeable. The encrypted data is then hidden following a fractal star sweep pattern across RGB channels, using only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6}-\mathbf{7} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> of the pixels. Metadata including keys and encryption information is stored in the video itself, so no external key exchange is needed. Tests on a 5-second, 25 FPS ultrasound video show that the method keeps the video quality high and can resist compression, noise, and other attacks. This method provides a simple, safe, and reliable way to hide data in videos for sensitive applications.

title: An IoT-Based System for Parkinson's Disease Detection Employing Hybrid Machine Learning Models
authors: Mst Jesmin Khatun, Md Ikramul Haque, Md Sabbir Hossain, Md Alimul Nishat Shantu, Md Farman Ali, Md. Harun-Ar-Rashid
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a progressing brain condition that influences movement, speech and thinking skills. In the initial stage, PD frequently creates speech problems. This work introduces an IoT-enabled hybrid machine learning model for the detection of PD. A total of 512 voice samples are initially collected using a mobile microphone, out of which 303 samples are collected from healty people and 209 samples are collected from PD affected patient at Shaheed Tajuddin Ahmad Medical College Hospital and Dhaka University of Engineering & Technology, Gazipur. For better result dataset was increased to 1000 samples by augmentation techniques and SMOTE was used for class balance, SelectKBest used for feature selection and parselmouth was used to extract acoustic features. This study integrates Random Forest, SVM, and XGBoost as primary estimators and logistic regression as top-level integrator. It delivered Notable results: 99% accuracy, 99% precision, 98% recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex>-score, and a near-perfect <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99 \% \text{ROC}$</tex> AUC. In general, early identification of PD is difficult in clinics with limited resources and limited diagnostic tools but the results demonstrate how this technique could be a reliable, low-cost method.

title: Intelligent Algorithm-Driven XR Film Content Production and User Precision Reach Mechanism
authors: Haodong Zhao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: History: Background Extended Reality (XR) films are a new paradigm in immersive storytelling that combines Virtual Reality, Augmented Reality and Mixed Reality technologies, but experiences widespread issues in production inefficiency and poor audience targeting. The existing production processes involve a lot of manual efforts and do not utilize computational intelligence to optimize them. Approaches: This work suggests a smart system that will utilize Generative Adversarial Networks, Reinforcement Learning and Deep Neural networks as a three-layer model that consists of content generation, optimization of production, and matching with users. The framework fully integrates script parsing based on LSTM, automated optimization of 3D asset generation, resource scheduling using genetic algorithm and multi-modal collaborative filtering to optimize the entire workflow. Findings: Experimental validation using 500 samples of 500 XR film proves the ability to reduce the production time by 67 percent, increase the quality measures of content by 43 percent and predict user preference by 82 percent, which is much better than the conventional methods. The practical applications of the system are confirmed in the real-time personalized recommendations, latency of 45ms and user satisfaction rate of 89%. Final Thoughts: The suggested algorithmic intervention is effective in increasing the efficiency of creativity and increasing the possibility of reaching the audience of the XR film ecosystem without sacrificing the quality requirements.

title: Federated Graph-Transformer Synergy for Early Alzheimer's Detection: A Dual-Modal Approach
authors: M.Rakinul Alam, Abdullah Al Abrar Chowdhury, Obayed Mashfi, Safid Bin Azad, Sabbir Hossen Meahjee, M. K. Abir
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The early diagnosis of Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is a critical challenge in neuroinformatics, requiring the integration of complementary neuroimaging modalities such as Diffusion Tensor Imaging (DTI) and functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI). While multimodal deep learning offers high diagnostic precision, the deployment of such models is severely constrained by data privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR) that prevent the centralization of patient data. Existing Federated Learning (FL) solutions often struggle with the heterogeneity of multimodal data distributions across institutions. To address this, we propose FedNeuro-Trans, a novel privacy-preserving framework that synergizes Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for structural connectivity with Vision Transformers (ViTs) for functional spatiotemporal features. We introduce a Cross-Modal Attention Alignment (CMAA) mechanism to dynamically fuse disparate modalities within local clients, coupled with a Federated Contrastive Aggregation strategy to mitigate non-IID data shifts. Evaluated on the ADNI dataset, our method achieves an accuracy of 94.7 % and an F1-score of 93.2 %, outperforming state-of-theart centralized and federated baselines.

title: Analysis of College Students' Physical Exercise Behavior Characteristics based on Convergence-Feedback Ant Colony Optimization
authors: Chunming Xiao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Nowadays, the physical exercise behavior plays a crucial role in shaping the health and wellbeing of college students. However, the existing models have analyzed student activity patterns by using clustering methods but they face premature convergence which led to unstable and suboptimal clustering results. Hence, this research proposes a ConvergenceFeedback Ant Colony Optimization (CF-ACO) framework for analyzing the college student's physical exercise behavior characteristics. Initially, the input data is collected from institutional physical exercise records and surveys that provide behavior data of students. Then, the data is preprocessed with mean imputation, min-max scaling and categorical encoding to prepare clean, complete and numeric data suitable for clustering. After that, the behavioral feature vector construction is employed to transform the preprocessed data into multidimensional numerical vectors by capturing key behavior characteristics. Further, the CF-ACO groups the students into clusters based on similarity of feature vectors for preventing the premature convergence. Finally, the clusters are analyzed to extract meaningful physical exercise behavioral patterns and interpret behavioral profiles per cluster through statistical analysis and characterization. From the results, the proposed CF-ACO framework achieved better results in terms of Silhouette score (0.78) and Davies-Bouldin Index (1.15) than the existing ACO-based clustering model.

title: Predictive Analysis of Nifty Stock Trends Using Ensemble Methods
authors: Chennampalli Keerthana, Kotha Sai Anandini, S. Pushpa Rani
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: The stock market prediction process becomes difficult because market conditions and price changes create unpredictable patterns which make it hard to predict future market movements. The NIFTY index needs accurate forecasting models because it serves as the main benchmark for investment decision-making and risk management activities within the Indian equity market. This research presents a hybrid ensemble framework which combines Random Forest and Gradient Boosting with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to study financial time-series data through its ability to model nonlinear connections and extended time relationships. The researchers used historical NIFTY data together with essential technical indicators for their model training and assessment. The experimental results demonstrate that the ensemble-LSTM model achieves 92.9 percent classification accuracy while maintaining a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.024 and a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.035 which exceeds all baseline models. The results show that the proposed framework delivers better predictive accuracy which establishes it as a dependable system for market trend prediction.

title: Trap-Based Modeling and Mechanisms of Non-Monotonic Hot-Carrier Degradation in Cryogenic CMOS Devices_supp1-3701063.docx
authors: Ran Cheng

title: COSMO: Contrastive Satellite Image Embedding Techniques Reflecting Expert Weather Bulletins
authors: Min-Hyoung Kim, Ha-Myung Park
abstract: A similarity search of geostationary meteorological satellite imagery is a basic tool for analyzing atmospheric conditions. However, if one simply reuses vision encoders pretrained on natural-image datasets, similarity is computed in the embedding spaces they induce; thus, the resulting representations emphasize visual appearance and dataset-specific semantics rather than the meteorological similarity implied by expert analyses. To mitigate this mismatch between visual and meteorological similarities, we propose COSMO, a contrastive representation learning framework that reshapes satellite image embeddings using operational weather bulletins as weak textual supervision. COSMO keeps image and text encoders frozen and learns lightweight projection heads that align images and bulletins in a shared embedding space with a multi-positive contrastive loss regularized by an auxiliary reconstruction term that encourages the projected image embeddings to preserve the structure of the original visual features. Using a time-aligned corpus constructed from COMS geostationary imagery and robustly preprocessed weather bulletins provided by the Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA), we evaluated the learned embeddings on retrieval-style tasks, including image-to-image and image-to-text searches based on bulletin-derived semantic similarity. COSMO consistently outperforms purely visual baselines and non-contrastive ablations on these retrieval tasks, and analyses of the learned embedding space further suggest that bulletin-guided contrastive learning makes satellite image embeddings more consistent with meteorological similarities.

title: FedDPI-SH: A Quality and Similarity Aware Federated Learning Framework for Medical Image Analysis
authors: Gadha Lekshmi P, Govind A, Ajo Babu George, Veera Manikantha Rayudu Tummala, Abhishek Hazra
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables decentralized medical image analysis while preserving data privacy. However, conventional methods overlook client data heterogeneity and inter-client feature similarity, resulting in suboptimal performance. In this paper, our proposed FedDPI-SH framework addresses these limitations through quality and similarity-aware weighted aggregation. The framework introduces a Data Performance Index (DPI) quantifying client reliability through dataset size, image resolution, label distribution balance, duplication levels, and cross-client generalization accuracy. Client Representation Similarity Matrix (CRSM) measures inter-client feature alignment via cosine similarity. FedDPI-SH combines these components to compute aggregation weights, prioritizing high-quality clients during feature extractor updates while maintaining personalized classifiers. Evaluation across three medical imaging datasets (Medical MNIST, PathMNIST, HAM10000) under severe non-IID conditions demonstrates improvements, with HAM10000 achieving 81.24% balanced accuracy, outperforming MOON (58.85%), FedAvg (45.22%), FedAvgM (17.66%), and FedProx (12.64%).The framework addresses data quality heterogeneity through explicit assessment of duplication, label balance, and resolution in federated medical imaging.

title: Reliability Assessment of Multi-state Performance Sharing Systems with Lag Dependence under Epistemic Uncertainty
authors: Yefang Chen, Hui Xiao, Rui Peng
abstract: With the rapid advancement of cloud and edge computing, cloud–edge collaborative systems have been widely deployed in applications such as smart manufacturing and the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT). These systems exhibit multi state operational behavior with task migration among nodes, and their reliability is jointly influenced by state lag effects and epistemic uncertainty. This research develops a reliability model of a multi-state performance sharing system with epistemic uncertainty. A discrete-state continuous-time (DSCT) Markov process is employed to characterize component degradation, and a time-lag threshold interval is introduced to represent lag dependence in component states. Based on the Evidential Network (EN), a reliability assessment algorithm of a multi-state performance sharing system with time-lag dependence under epistemic uncertainty is proposed. Numerical results show that the common bus capacity has a significant effect on system reliability. The epistemic uncertainty of lag dependence impacts the degree of uncertainty in system reliability, but the impact decreases over time.

title: TNOD: A High-Precision Tri-Modal Dataset for Non-Cooperative Object
authors: Chaofu Wei, Taiquan Zhang, Guifeng Guo, Yuxiang Guo
abstract: Multimodal object detection holds important application value in tasks such as military reconnaissance, security surveillance, and complex environment perception. Visible images can provide abundant texture and structural information, infrared images characterize the thermal radiation features of targets, and SAR images possess all-time and all-weather imaging capabilities. The three modalities exhibit prominent complementary advantages.Nevertheless, most existing non-cooperative object datasets focus on single-modal or visible-infrared bimodal scenarios, while high-quality aligned tri-modal data integrating visible, infrared and SAR images remain insufficient. In particular, there is a lack of precisely aligned samples for non-cooperative targets. To address this limitation, this paper constructs a high-precision tri-modal non-cooperative object detection dataset named TNOD (Tri-Modal Non-cooperative Object Dataset). The dataset contains 10,630 groups of aligned tri-modal samples with a total of 31,890 images, covering six categories of time-sensitive targets, including land armored vehicles, rocket launchers, air defense missiles, and aerial fighter aircraft.Given the huge differences in imaging mechanisms between SAR and optical sensors and the difficulty of spatial registration, a unified alignment method based on the world coordinate system is adopted to achieve effective spatiotemporal registration of tri-modal data. Furthermore, fusion and object detection experiments are conducted to validate the dataset effectiveness. The experimental results demonstrate that the aligned data significantly outperforms artificially misaligned data in fusion evaluation metrics, which verifies the reliability of the tri-modal alignment strategy. Meanwhile, utilizing the TNOD dataset, the multimodal fusion detection and recognition performance in complex environments is improved by 15% compared with the optimal single-modal baseline model.Consequently, the spatiotemporally aligned TNOD dataset can provide crucial data support for researches on multimodal fusion perception, object detection in complex scenarios, and military target recognition.

title: Reconfigurable Wideband-to-Narrowband Filter Low-Noise Amplifier With Intrinsic Switch-Off Operation
authors: Andrés Fontana, Steven Matthew Cheng, Dimitra Psychogiou
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A reconfigurable, multimode, and continuously tunable radio frequency (RF) codesigned filter low-noise amplifier (FLNA) is presented. It can be reconfigured between a narrowband and a wideband bandpass response (Mode I and II) with a continuously tunable center frequency and bandwidth (BW). Intrinsic switch-off functionality (Mode III) is also included to disable the signal transmission without the need for extra elements. Mode reconfigurability is obtained through a new type of complex-matched tunable load. The multimode FLNA concept has been validated at sub-6 GHz through a manufactured printed circuit board (PCB) prototype with the following RF performance characteristics: 1) Mode I: gain $\ge 11.9$ dB, center frequency tunability $\Delta f_{cen} = 2.78$ –3.82 GHz, tunable fractional BW $\Delta $ FBW= 4.9%–11.2%, and noise figure (NF) $\le 1.4$ dB; 2) Mode II: gain $\ge 13.7$ dB, $\Delta $ FBW = 29.9%–32.9% at ${f} _{cen}=3.35$ GHz, and NF $\le 1.23$ dB; and 3) Mode III: signal attenuation >35.4dB.

title: Distributed Scenario Q-Learning Control for Multiagent Systems with Uncertain Cost Functions
authors: Jing Wang, Khanh D. Pham
abstract: This paper presents a distributed scenario Q-learning algorithm for cooperative tracking control of multiagent systems with uncertain cost functions. First, we introduce a scenario-based Q-function that replaces the nominal stage cost with a log-sum-exp soft-maximum aggregation over independently sampled uncertainty scenarios, providing probabilistic robustness guarantees without requiring knowledge of the uncertainty distribution. Second, we design three dynamic average consensus estimators—Π<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</sup>, Φ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</sup>, and Ψ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</sup>—that allow each agent to track the global scenario cost sum, the global minimum Q-value, and the global current Q-value, respectively, using only one-hop neighbor communication. Third, we establish rigorous convergence guarantees: the estimator disagreement decays geometrically at a rate governed by the spectral gap of the network weight matrix, and the temporal-difference weight updates converge in probability to the unique projected Bellman fixed point under standard Robbins–Monro and feature-independence conditions and a slowly-varying behavior-policy assumption. Numerical simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed design.

title: Design of Adaptive Steady-State Fading Memory Filter for Servo Drive Systems Under Maneuvering Conditions
authors: Can Wang, Shengming Liu, Jianfei Pan
abstract: To address the state observation requirements of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) servo systems under complex maneuvering conditions, this paper proposes an artificial intelligence–enhanced state observation approach. First, by integrating the fading memory filter (FMF) model with covariance steady-state conditions, the state observation equation for servo systems based on the steady-state fading memory filter (SSFMF) is established for the first time. Second, comprehensive transfer function analysis reveals the influence of filter gain on the observer's responsiveness and smoothness, providing theoretical guidance for parameter optimization. To enhance the algorithm's adaptive capability, the innovation sequence is employed as a maneuverability assessment metric, while an optimized Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network architecture is implemented to preserve estimation accuracy under computational efficiency constraints. This framework enables dynamic filter gain adaptation, robust state estimation performance, and high computational efficiency. Comprehensive simulation and experimental results demonstrate the superior performance characteristics of the proposed adaptive steady-state fading memory filter (ASSFMF) method.

title: On usage of artificial intelligence on pulmonary diseases in neonates: a systematic literature review
authors: Stephany Paula Da Silva Canejo, Flávio Leandro De Morais, Anna Beatriz Silva, Anthony Alexandre Morais Militão, Katia Maria Mendes, Emanuela Batista Ferreira E Pereira, Wellington Pinheiro Dos Santos, Patricia Takako Endo, Waldemar Brandão Neto
abstract: The study aimed to discuss howArtificial Intelligence (AI) has been applied to improve the care of pulmonary diseases in preterm neonates. Following the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) 2020 guidelines, a Systematic Literature Review(SLR)was conducted across six scientific databases, covering studies published between 2014 and 2024. From 7,565 initially identified studies, 35 were selected for analysis. Most studies focus on predicting Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia (BPD), whereas others address chronic and acute pulmonary diseases and additional clinical outcomes. There is a predominance of well-established AI models, particularly Random Forest and Logistic Regression, likely due to their recognition in the literature and consistent performance across clinical applications. Their familiarity may facilitate adoption in clinical contexts, especially when combined with commonly used evaluation metrics such as the Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC-ROC), sensitivity, and accuracy. The most frequently used data for model training include gestational attributes (e.g., gestational age and gestational diabetes), followed by neonatal attributes (e.g., birth weight and sex) and clinical attributes (e.g., comorbidities and indicators of pulmonary function). Recent studies demonstrate promising progress in applying AI to predict pulmonary diseases in preterm neonates. However, methodological limitations, including small sample sizes and inconsistent handling of missing data, remain prevalent and hinder clinical implementation. Despite these challenges, the trends and findings identified in this review may contribute meaningfully to future research.
