title: State Space Models over Directed Graphs_supp1-3702346.pdf
authors: Rong-Hua Li
abstract: Directed graphs are ubiquitous across numerous domains, where the directionality of edges encodes critical causal dependencies. However, existing GNNs and graph Transformers tailored for directed graphs face two major challenges: (1) effectively capturing long-range causal dependencies derived from directed edges; (2) balancing accuracy and training efficiency when processing large-scale graph datasets. In recent years, state space models (SSMs) have achieved substantial progress in causal sequence tasks, and their variants designed for graphs have demonstrated state-of-the-art accuracy while maintaining high efficiency across various graph learning benchmarks. However, existing graph state space models are exclusively designed for undirected graphs, which limits their performance in directed graph learning. To this end, we propose an innovative approach DirEgo2Token which sequentializes directed graphs via k-hop ego graphs. This marks the first systematic extension of state space models to the field of directed graph learning. Building upon this, we develop DirGraphSSM, a novel directed graph neural network architecture that implements state space models on directed graphs via the message-passing mechanism. Experimental results demonstrate that DirGraphSSM achieves state-of-the-art performance on three representative directed graph learning tasks while attaining competitive performance on two additional tasks with 1.5× to 2× training speed improvements compared to existing state-of-the-art models.

title: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
authors: N/A

title: Strengthening Treatment Adherence in Clubfoot Management Through AI-Supported Co-Production: A Conceptual Perspective
authors: Md Akramul Bari, Zauwiyah Ahmad, Kamarulzaman Ab. Aziz
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Congenital talipes equinovarus (clubfoot) stays as one of the main reasons of preventable childhood infirmity in many low- and middle-income countries, it is because of delayed diagnosis, poor care pathways, and poor long-term treatment adherence. The Ponseti method is widely known as the gold standard for non-surgical treatment; its success significantly relays on the sustained caregiver engagement and continuous brace rules compliance, which stay challenging in resource-constrained and rural settings. In response to these research gaps, this study proposes an integrated framework that combines artificial intelligence-enabled digital health technologies with co-production principles to strengthen treatment adherence in clubfoot management. Drawing on the concept of co-production, patient engagement, and human–AI collaboration in healthcare, the current study develops a multistakeholder conceptual model where caregivers, clinicians, and practitioners collaboratively design and implement AI-supported treatment pathways. The framework conceptualizes the role of intelligent mobile health systems in facilitating early detection, personalized educational guidance, real-time adherence monitoring, and constant feedback between families and healthcare contributors. By embedding AI tools within a co-produced care ecosystem, the model aims to improve trust, accountability, and shared responsibility through the treatment duration. This paper contributes to the literature by advancing a theoretically grounded and practice-oriented model that integrates digital innovation with participatory healthcare delivery. The proposed framework offers a scalable approach for improving adherence-driven outcomes in clubfoot treatment and provides conceptual guidance for the design of AI-enabled co-production tactics in pediatric orthopedics and other chronic childhood circumstances. The paper will suggest suitable research designs for future work.

title: Enhancing Security in Multicasting Over Nakagami-0.5 Fading Channel with Opportunistic Relaying and SC Diversity Scheme
authors: M. T. Tania, M. Z. I. Sarkar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This Paper presents a comprehensive analysis of secrecy performance in a multicast wireless communication network operating over Nakagami- 0.5 fading channels. The Probability of non-zero secrecy multicast capacity (PNSMC) and the secure outage probability for multicasting (SOPM) are analytically developed under the influence of multiple relays, legitimate users, and eavesdroppers. To ensure enhanced secrecy reliability, an opportunistic relay selection approach is integrated with selection combining (SC) diversity at the destination. Verified the analytical results through extensive Monte Carlo simulations, exhibiting a strong correspondence with theoretical predictions. Numerical evaluations indicate that an increased number of relays and destination antennas notably strengthen secrecy performance, whereas stronger eavesdropper channels deteriorate it. In general, the framework presented provides useful design guidelines for cooperative secure multicast communication systems operating in severe fading environments.

title: Brain Tumor Detection Using Vision Transformer and CNN with Explainability
authors: Nilesh Dhannaseth, Harsh Kalbande, Harsh Bhagat, Shahid Sameer, Mohit Agarwal
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Explainability and clinician trust were enhanced by incorporating explainability techniques such as GRAD-CAM, LIME as well as SHAP. Brain tumors represent one of the most dangerous clinical conditions requiring early and correct diagnosis in order to heal patients effectively and guarantee their survival. One common technique for diagnosis is the manual interpretation of MRI scans, but consumes a lot of time, is subject to errors, and is highly dependent on radiological expertise. For addressing these problems, in this paper, we propose a deep learning-based model that usages the VGG16 and VGG19 models to automatically detect brain tumors through transfer learning. Both of them were trained and hyperparameter-tuned using an MRI dataset and their performance was calculated by model's accuracy, loss, precision, recall and F1-score. These outcomes indicate that VGG19 has slightly higher precision, yet VGG16 offers faster convergence and computational efficiency, which is also highlighting the pros and cons of both architectures. The trained models, along with XAI techniques, were then deployed within a Flask-based web application, which enabled users to upload MRI scans, obtain predictions, and visualize explanations in real time. The suggested model explains how the combination of explainable AI, deep learning, and deployment can help provide more rapid, consistent, and understandable diagnostic support for detection of brain tumor.

title: A DFT Clock Control Block Design and Implementation for Scan-Based Testing
authors: Pradeepkumar G, Vimal Raj R K, M Karthikkumar, Santhiya Palanisamy
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Design for Testability (DFT) is a critical post-fabrication methodology used to evaluate and ensure the integrity of integrated circuits. As semiconductor technologies scale, embedding these test architectures becomes increasingly complex, particularly regarding the precise regulation of test-mode clocks. This research introduces an innovative clock control architecture designed to accelerate scan shift operations and significantly reduce overall testing duration. The proposed frame-work manages DFT timing dynamically by configuring specific Test Data Registers (TDRs). Key features of this system include a centralized clock entry point for each tile, TDR-controlled clock halting, capture routing that mirrors functional paths, and staggered pulse mechanisms for both Launch-On-Shift (LOS) and Launch-On-Capture (LOC) strategies. Furthermore, the module facilitates dedicated clock interception for scanning and debugging operations. Within this architecture, a tile's scannable logic receives its clock directly from the control unit, which relies on pulse-generating circuitry to govern an internal tile-level clock gater. Comparative evaluations against existing clock topologies demonstrate that the proposed design achieves a minimal dynamic power consumption of just <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0}$</tex> milliwatts. This substantial reduction compared to conventional on-chip clock controllers makes the proposed approach highly advantageous for large-scale System-on-Chip (SoC) deployments that demand reliable, customizable, and energy-efficient test clock infrastructures.

title: Benchmarking Local Vision-Language Models on an Edge Device for the Visually Impaired
authors: Fatima Sarwar, Baseer Ayaz Raja, Muhammad Ibrahim, Ayesha Zeb, Umar Shahbaz Khan, Usman Asad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: Visually Impaired People (VIP) require reliable scene understanding for safe navigation in complex environments. While state-of-the-art Vision-Language Models (VLMs) can describe scenes in great detail, they typically rely on cloud servers, raising issues such as privacy risks and continuous internet dependence. This paper explores a local solution by benchmarking five open-source VLMs running entirely on NVIDIA’s Jetson Orin Nano (8 GB) via the Ollama (llama.cpp) framework. The models were tested on the VizWiz-VQA dataset, which consists of real images captured by blind users. The models’ performance was evaluated on hardware metrics such as latency, throughput, power consumption, etc., as well as semantic reliability using CLIPScore and POPE benchmark to detect hallucinations. The results demonstrate that while the larger models (gemma3:12b & ministral-3:8b) fall short in hardware benchmarks, they are significantly less likely to provide hallucinatory responses.

title: A Hierarchical Transformer–CNN Fusion Model for Classification of Mustard Leaves: Distinguishing Healthy, Abiotic Disorder, and Alternaria Leaf Blight Disease
authors: Debapriya Dhar, Pinki Debnath, Tanoy Paul
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: One of the most devastating mustard diseases is Alternaria leaf blight, which causes huge yield loss if it is not identified accurately. Moreover, diseases and disorders are often misunderstood. Misdiagnosis and the improper application of pesticides have adverse effects on both the food chain and the environment. Therefore, to enable early prediction and precise identification of Alternaria leaf blight disease and abiotic disorders in mustard, this study employed a Transformer-based Vision Transformer (ViT) and a CNN-based EfficientNet-B0 model. A total of 1122 mustard leaf photos were captured from several farmers' fields in the Sylhet region. We chose 853 photos and categorized them into three classes: Healthy, Disorder, and Alternaria. The ViT and EfficientNet models were fine-tuned using the collected dataset to fit the specific task of classification. Using 5-fold cross-validation, both models were evaluated, achieving mean accuracies of 95.08 % for ViT and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 4 \%}$</tex> for EfficientNet on the three-class dataset. Two additional binary sub-datasets were prepared: (i) Healthy-Unhealthy (ii) Alternaria-Disorder. The study then compared the performance of ViT and EfficientNet on the sub-datasets (i) and (ii) using 5-fold cross-validation. ViT outperformed EfficientNet on sub-dataset (i), achieving a mean accuracy of 98.59 % compared to 97.42 % of EfficientNet. In contrast, EfficientNet outperformed ViT, attaining a mean accuracy of 97.50 % versus 96.79 % on subdataset (ii). Considering these, our study proposes a Hierarchical Transformer-CNN Fusion model, which uses both ViT features trained on the sub-dataset (i) and EfficientNet features trained on the sub-dataset (ii). The proposed model achieved a mean accuracy of 96.95% across 5-fold cross-validation, outperforming ViT and EfficientNet models' performances on the three-class dataset. Overall, this study establishes a novel approach to classify mustard Alternaria leaf blight disease, abiotic disorder, and healthy leaf through machine learning, without the need for expert intervention.

title: Anomaly-Based Network Intrusion Detection Using Random Forests
authors: Mohammed Shakawat Hossen, Amad Uddin Osama, Mohsin Abdullah Chowdhury, Mohammed Shifath Hasan Tanbir
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of cloud computing, IoT, and high-speed networks has increased the complexity of cyber threats, demanding accurate and efficient intrusion detection systems. This research examines anomaly-based detection using the UNSW-NB15 benchmark by implementing a consistent pipeline across 13 supervised models. These models include linear classifiers, tree-based ensembles, gradient boosting techniques, and deep learning frameworks such as MLP, GRU, and LSTM. The data preprocessing steps involve cleaning, encoding, normalization, and addressing class imbalance with SMOTE, which is applied solely to the training dataset. The models' performance is evaluated based on accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, and runtime to assess both their detection capabilities and computational efficiency. Random Forest achieves the best overall trade-off, reaching 94.98% accuracy, 94.98% F1-score, and 0.99 ROC-AUC, while maintaining moderate execution time. SMOTE provides limited improvement for top ensemble models, and deep learning approaches, particularly GRU, incur higher computational overhead despite competitive recall. Feature-importance analysis highlights key traffic attributes, supporting interpretable and scalable intrusion detection.

title: Optimization of Artificial Intelligence Algorithms Based Federated Graph Learning
authors: Yunbo Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, rapid growth in cyber-attacks has driven need for optimizing Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms for improving intrusion detection accuracy, speed and adaptability. Traditional models had faced challenges like it often depended on centralized data aggregation and static classification strategies which struggle with scalability and evolving attack patterns. To overcome these challenges, this research proposed Federated Heterogeneous Attention-based Graph Neural Network (Fed-HAT-GNN) for high-performance intrusion detection. The system first models network traffic relationships by using graph-structured data to capture complex attribute interactions. A federated learning enables model training across multiple decentralized participants without sharing raw data, thereby ensuring confidentiality and compliance with security constraints. After that, the HAT-GNN architecture applies hierarchical attention for extracting global and local discriminatory features essential for identifying malicious behavior. Subsequently, Golden Eagle Optimization (GEO) optimization process tunes model's hyperparameters by enhancing detection accuracy and reducing false-positive instances. The optimized Fed-HAT-GNN better results in terms of accuracy, recall, precision and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F}$</tex>-measure in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 5 0}$</tex> iterations when compared to existing model.

title: LatentGW-RiskNet: A Latent Fingerprint–Based Autoencoder Framework for Three-Class Groundwater Risk Assessment
authors: Rumman Shahriar, Anika Afrin, Shinthi Tasnim, Shirin Sultana, Natasha Tanzila Monalisa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Groundwater contamination is a significant environmental and public health issue in urban areas with industrial, commercial, and residential activities. Accurate assessment of water quality is challenging due to multiple physicochemical parameters and small sample sizes. It limits the effectiveness of conventional methods for classifying water into Safe, Contaminated, and High-Risk categories. We collected 78 groundwater samples from three functional zones in Rajshahi City. We measured twelve parameters which are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, turbidity, electrical conductivity, TDS, TSS, TS, COD, Fe, and Mn. We also considered two derived features, MetalRisk and ExceedCount. It reflects metal contamination levels. We proposed LatentGW-RiskNet model along with mitigation strategies to address this issue. A shallow autoencoder based framework that compresses the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> dimensional input into a 2-D latent fingerprint. It captures water quality information and reduces noise. Latent representations are analyzed using Gaussian Mixture clustering and classification is performed using a Random Forest classifier. The model was evaluated with Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation. It achieved 92% accuracy and F1-score It outperformed with models trained on raw features and standard baselines such as SVM, KNN, and Decision Tree. These results highlight that latent fingerprint representations provide a compact, interpretable, and robust approach for small sample groundwater risk assessment. It enables effective three class classification and supporting informed decision-making for water risk assessment.

title: Minimization of Reversible Logic Circuits Using A* with Lookahead
authors: Hyunsu Choi, Takashi Hirayama, Katsuhisa Yamanaka
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: This paper presents an approach for minimizing reversible logic circuits composed of Toffoli gates, employing the A* with Lookahead (AL*) algorithm. Previous A*-based optimization methods have suffered from excessive memory requirements, which limit the size of circuits that can be handled. To overcome this issue, we employ AL*, an algorithm that incorporates the advantages of depth-first search (DFS) into A*. By predicting the expansion priority of each node through a DFS-based lookahead, the proposed method achieves a significant reduction in memory usage. Consequently, our approach optimizes larger circuits that previous A* methods could not handle due to memory overflow, and we present comparative results in this paper.

title: Intelligent Macrocell-Femtocell Handover Management Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Md. Mahade Hasan, Mostafa Zaman Chowdhury, Md. Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Wireless communication forms heterogeneous networks (HetNets) by integrating macrocells with femtocell base stations(BSs). These are an effective architecture for improving wireless coverage and capacity in high-user-density environments. In such networks, macrocell BSs provide wide-area connectivity, while femtocell BSs support localized service enhancement and traffic offloading. However, maintaining quality of service (QoS) in HetNets became a critical challenge, particularly due to user equipment mobility, unpredictable interference patterns, and dynamic traffic demand, which can degrade network performance. Frequent unsuccessful handover (HO) between macrocells and femtocells leads to increased latency, signalling overhead, and decreased user experience. This paper presents an intelligent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{H O}$</tex> management framework utilizing deep Q-learning, enabling adaptive and context-aware HO decisions in HetNets. The decision-making process of deep Q-learning involves three network-state parameters: the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR), the velocity of the user equipment (UE), and the distance of the UE from the serving and neighboring BSs. The goal of this study is to train a deep Q-network (DQN) to find an optimal HO policy that lowers HO latency while raising the HO success rate (HSR) and SINR in a HetNet. The simulation results indicate that the proposed method surpasses traditional hybrid <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{H O}$</tex> (HHO) schemes, which integrate reference signal received power (RSRP) and SINR, in terms of HSR, HO latency, and SINR. The method enhances HSR, SINR, and latency greatly, showing the potential for nextgeneration HetNets.

title: Role of Best Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface in Enhancing Secure Outage Performance of Rayleigh Fading Channels
authors: Md. Fahmid Ibne Afzal, Md. Zahurul Islam Sarkar, Ahshan Habib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper describes the enhancement of physicallayer security in Rayleigh fading channels through the deployment of the best reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). The system model under consideration includes one legitimate receiver and one eavesdropper, both operating over Rayleigh fading channels. The effect of key system parameters, such as the average signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of the eavesdropper, the quality of the reflecting elements at the RIS, and the number of available RISs, is thoroughly examined. To evaluate the secrecy performance of the system, closed-form analytical results for the probability of non-zero secrecy capacity (PNSC) and the secure outage probability (SOP) are derived. The numerical results demonstrate that the deployment of RIS significantly improves both PNSC and SOP, thereby providing valuable insights for future research and potential real-world applications.

title: Zero-Shot Personalized Speech Synthesis via High-Fidelity Speech Guidance
authors: Shengya Yu, Long Zhang, Yiyao Gao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Personalized speech synthesis technology can significantly improve the naturalness and user experience of human-computer interaction. However, existing methods often face problems such as high data acquisition cost, insufficient generalization ability caused by data dependence, and decreased timbre fidelity in zero-shot scenarios. To solve these challenges, this paper proposes a zero-shot personalized speech synthesis method based on high-fidelity speech guidance. The proposed framework combines a high-fidelity speech synthesis model based on high-quality speech data training with any-to-any speech conversion technology, and realizes fine-grained modeling and effective transfer of target speaker timbre under data-limited conditions. Experimental results show that even under the condition of scarce reference speech, the proposed method can maintain stable performance in timbre consistency, speech naturalness, and text fidelity, outperforming the traditional zero-shot method in both subjective and objective evaluation. This method provides an efficient and scalable solution for zero-shot personalized speech synthesis.

title: Space-Filling Experimental Design and Optimization Strategy for Complex Constrained Environments
authors: Qinglong Zhu, Boyi Liu, Xiaoning Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: Space-filling experimental design faces significant challenges in engineering applications characterized by complex constraints. Traditional approaches exhibit limitations: they are primarily developed for unconstrained domains and struggle to simultaneously optimize spatial uniformity, strict feasibility, and computational efficiency, leading to sample clustering and reduced model accuracy in high-dimensional non-convex domains. This paper proposes a multi-objective experimental design framework that systematically integrates constraint mapping with space-filling optimization. By establishing a mathematical model to describe the coupling relationship between the design space and constraints, developing a feasible domain mapping mechanism that transforms irregular domains into normalized spaces, and employing Pareto-based evolutionary algorithms to achieve multi-objective balance, the proposed framework overcomes the aforementioned limitations. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to conventional methods, the proposed approach exhibits superior and more balanced performance in complex constrained environments, providing a reliable foundation for intelligent sampling and optimization.

title: Deep Hybrid Forecasting Engine for Real-Time Enterprise Revenue Prediction and Business Trend Analysis
authors: D Divya Kalpana, Abdelhalim Mohammad Jubran, Pernedi Koteswara Rao, Sourabh Kumar, P Deepthi, Ashley Mejolsha A
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Non-stationary business dynamic, asynchronous key performance indicators, and uncertainty of the external market have made accurate real-time enterprise revenue forecasting a hard task. The traditional statistical and deep learning models are typically not able to adjust to abrupt changes in structure, volatility bursts and late causality between subsystems in the enterprise. In order to overcome these difficulties, the present paper suggests the concept of Deep Hybrid Forecasting Engine as the real-time prediction of enterprise revenue and business trends. The framework combines the empirical wavelet-based signal decomposition, adaptive Bayesian change-point-driven forecast windowing, multivariate time-warped attention fusion, quantum-coded market sentiment injection, and volatility-conscious Transformer-Kalman hybrid framework. Also, a topologically rewired deep echo state network is used to model dynamic memory and cross-graph contrastive learning is used to embed financial and operational dependencies. Massive experimental studies on the datasets of multi-year enterprises reveal that the proposed solution performs substantially better than the classical and deep learning baselines. The model has a stable and overall forecasting accuracy of 93.0 and a MAPE of 9.2, which is better than LSTM, Transformer, and Temporal Fusion Transformer models on the same conditions, whether stable or high-volatility. The findings establish the strength, flexibility and extensiveness of the suggested framework to real time enterprise decision support systems.

title: A Hybrid Stacking-Based Framework for Multiclass Road Accident Severity Prediction with Interpretable SHAP Analysis
authors: Maksuda Akter, Obaidul Haque, Mariha Tabassum, Rajib Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Road traffic accident severity prediction is challenging due to extreme class imbalance and the rarity of fatal cases. Many existing studies report high accuracy using evaluation pipelines that introduce data leakage, leading to overly optimistic results. This paper proposes a leakage-aware hybrid stacking model for multiclass accident severity prediction, focusing on improved detection of Minority cases. Using the Addis Ababa road traffic accident dataset (2017-2020) this study tried SMOTE-Tomek resampling strictly applying within the training pipeline, and out-of-fold probability stacking is employed to prevent data leakage. The proposed model integrates RUSBoost, LightGBM, and Decision Tree base learners with a neural network meta-learner, followed by class-specific threshold optimization. The framework achieves an accuracy of 81.9% and a Macro F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50.03 \%$</tex>, outperforming multiple machine learning, deep learning, and ensemble baselines under leakage-controlled evaluation. SHAP-based interpretability further provides transparent insights into model decisions for safety-critical applications.

title: CT-CoherXAI: A Coherence-Driven, Explainable Ensemble Framework for Lung Cancer Classification from Chest CT Images
authors: Mursheda Nusrat Della, Shahid Uz Zaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Diagnosis at an early stage of lung cancer is crucial to increase patient's survival by allowing early intervention and better treatment options. While deep learning models have shown promise in analyzing CT scans, their blackbox nature limits clinical trust. Post-hoc interpretability techniques attempt to address this but often rely on external explanation models. In this research, a coherence-aware and inherently interpretable ensemble framework CT-CoherXAI for lung cancer detection from chest CT scan images has been proposed. The proposed method utilized CLAHE-based contrast enhancement followed by a multi-glimpse attention mechanism, which extracts fixed spatial crops (center and topleft, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right) to simulate varied diagnostic views. An automatic cancer detection model has been developed with a joint loss function and combining CrossEntropy. Also, a Grad-CAM based coherence regularization, enforcing consistent spatial focus across crops. In proposed method, deep features are compressed via Truncated SVD and fed into a soft-voting ensemble of Random Forest, SVM, and Naive Bayes classifiers. Unlike post-hoc methods, the proposed approach integrates interpretability directly into the training phase without requiring pixel-level supervision. The proposed CT-CoherXAI method is evaluated on the IQ-OTHNCCD dataset, and it can be seen that the CT-CoherXAI achieves 99% classification accuracy and generates spatially coherent, clinically meaningful attention maps, offering a robust and transparent tool for clinical decision support.

title: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Automated Identification of Indigenous Flowers of Bangladesh
authors: Sayeda Sazia Kalam, Kamrun Sumaiya, Mohammad Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Bangladesh is home to a varied range of local flowers holding ecological, cultural, and economic significance. Despite the significance, there remain a limited research in automated flower detection and Identification in Bangladesh. To address this gap, this study works with the advanced deep learning object detection models to identify and detect Bangladeshi flower species in complex natural and diverse environments. This study utilizes a composite dataset which was construccted with images sourced from the Kaggle ColoredFlowersBD dataset, and self-captured photographs to reflect on real world conditions. For automated flower detection, we have used state-of-the-art detection models YOLO11, YOLO12 and RT-DETR to implement and evaluate. Performance was evaluated using standard performance metrics of object detection models including mAP@0.5, precision, recall, and F1-score. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLO12 achieves the highest overall performance with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 91.30 %, precision of 88.79 %, recall of 85.43 %, and an F1-score of 87.08 %, followed closely by YOLO11 and RT-DETR. Despite strong performance, challenges such as inter-class similarity among visually similar flower species, intra-class variability, and species class limitation remain. The findings highlight the effectiveness of object detection models for Bangladeshi flower detection and provide valuable insights for future improvements.

title: Vision Transformer-Based Detection of Lung Diseases from Chest X-Ray Images
authors: Mohammad Isfar Hossain, Md. Rakibul Islam, Mome Ashrafe, Md. Hikmotier Rahman Hamim, Md. Farhan Tanvir Nasim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Early and reliable detection of lung diseases—including pneumonia, viral pneumonia, lung opacity, COVID-19, and tuberculosis—is vital for timely intervention and improved patient outcomes. Vision Transformers (ViTs) have recently shown strong potential in medical imaging due to their ability to capture global contextual information. However, ViTs are highly sensitive to optimization strategies, making optimizer selection a crucial factor in achieving stable and accurate performance. This study evaluates the effectiveness of six widely used optimizers—Adam, AdamW, Nadam, RAdam, SGDW, and Momentum—for fine-tuning a ViT-small (patch16-224) model on multi-class chest X-ray classification. The dataset was constructed from publicly available CXR repositories covering six classes: Normal, Pneumonia, Viral Pneumonia, Lung Opacity, COVID-19, and Tuberculosis. Among all optimizers, SGDW demonstrated the most stable convergence and highest overall performance, achieving an accuracy of 86.2%. It also produced more balanced class-wise predictions and superior ROC–AUC values across the six disease categories. The comparative analysis confirms that optimizer selection substantially influences ViT performance in multi-class medical image classification. SGDW emerges as the most effective and reliable optimizer for transformer-based CXR disease detection.

title: Fish Farm Pro: Sustainable Vertical Aquaculture Practices for Improving Fish Farming Productivity in Bangladesh
authors: Md. Minhaj Rowfun Rabbi Anik, Stephen Argho Das, Fahim Hassan, Anika Aziz Khandoker, Noor Uddin Md Mursalin, Mehedi Hasan Rafid, Md. Sajid Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Maintaining the quality of water environment is important for fish to grow well in fish farming. This research is about an IoT based system that checks the water quality all the time actively and analyzes the important things in the water using special sensors that collect data to detect real time anomaly detection and monitor. If we manage the water well we can grow fish and that helps people get the food they need. We are also looking at the way of farming fish called fish farming, which is a good way to use space and can make a lot of money. If we use our free space in the home to produce fish then we have to keep the water environment clean and healthy for the fish. The data from the sensors helps us check if the fish are healthy and makes sure the water is okay for them which is good for the environment. We use IoT based devices to look at the data and see how the fish are doing how fast they are growing and if they are sick so we can make decisions. By looking at all the data, we have we can see what we need to work on and make the fish farming system even better. We can see what is going on with the fish and water on devices and also on the internet which helps us make decisions and take care of the fish. Water quality is important for fish growth in aquaculture systems and we need to keep checking the water environment to make sure it is healthy for the fish.

title: A Coupled-Charge Driven Reverse-Recovery SPICE Model for SiC MOSFETs Considering Quasi-Floating Channel Effects
authors: Fu-Jen Hsu, Ting-Fu Chang, Hsiang-Ting Hung, Chung-Ju Yu, Chih-Fang Huang, Cheng-Tyng Yen
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel SPICE model for SiC MOSFETs that accurately reproduces reverse recovery characteristics, specifically addressing the Quasi-Floating Channel (QFC) effect. Conventional models often neglect the significant impact of negative gate bias V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GS</inf> on conductivity modulation in SiC devices. We introduce a Coupling-Charge drive Reverse-Recovery (CCRR) approach that employs a voltage-controlled current source to decouple the carrier lifetime emulation network from the main circuit. This method eliminates parasitic capacitance artifacts while precisely predicting Q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rr</inf> variation across different temperatures and gate biases. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed model achieves excellent agreement with measurements across a wide range of temperatures. The simulation results confirm the model’s ability to accurately reproduce the reverse recovery dynamics, reducing the Q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rr</inf> estimation error to less than 5%.

title: Mapping the Landscape of Machine Learning Research Through Clustering
authors: Md. Rawha Siddiqi Riad, Sharier Mahmud Akash, Md. Nuruzzaman, Abdus Salam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper explores a data-driven approach to analyze research trends in the machine learning (ML) domain. The main objectives are to identify the subdomains within the ML domain and to find their popularity over time. A dataset of 10,000 research papers (collected by web scraping) from arXiv was used, containing titles, abstracts and dates related to ML. The text data were processed using TF–IDF vectorization and reduced through Principal Component Analysis (PCA) before applying K-Means clustering to group similar papers. A predetermined set of keywords is used to automatically label each cluster. It helps to identify machine learning subdomains, including deep learning, computer vision, reinforcement learning, and natural language processing. In addition, trend analysis and visualizations were performed to examine changes in research focus from 2021 to 2025. The results show that Time Series and Forecasting has become the most popular and fastest growing area. At the same time, traditional fields like Optimization and Computer Vision continue to remain important. This research shows that text based data science methods can effectively analyze the research trend in ML.

title: Lower-Voltage-Stress Reduced-Switch 31-Level Cascaded Inverter for EV and Aircraft Systems
authors: Yeaser Arafat Prantik, Md. Kawsar Hossain, Ahanaf Ahmed, Faysal Amin Tanvir, Md.Shazzad Hossain Shajib, Bivas Sarkar, Omar Farrok
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: For modern electric vehicles (EVs) and aircraft, a high-power quality and efficient power conversion system is crucial. This study proposed a novel reduced-switch 31-level cascaded H-bridge (CHB) multilevel inverter (MLI) for aircraft and EV power system applications. The proposed inverter reduced the number of switches by 68.33 %, and minimized the gate drivers compared to the conventional CHB 31-level MLI. As a result, the proposed topology reduced the switching losses and the voltage stress on the semiconductor devices. The inverter generates an excellent rated output voltage of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1 5} \mathrm{V}_{\text{rms}}$</tex> at 400 Hz frequency, while the calculated total harmonic distortion (THD) of 3.01 %, which is below the requirement of the EV and aircraft standard. To ensure the system robustness, inverter harmonic performance, and voltage regulation, an extensive performance assessment was carried out under dynamic load conditions, which range from (1 kW to 10 kW). MATLAB/Simulink was used for simulation, which confirms that the proposed inverter has stable operation, near-sinusoidal output voltage, and improved efficiency under load variation.

title: A Machine Learning Stacking Ensemble for Cerebral Stroke Prediction and Early Risk Assessment
authors: Shahariar Hossain, Saifuddin Sagor, Jahidul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Cerebral stroke is a critical neurological disorder where early and proper risk forecasting is necessary to minimize death and permanent disability. Conventional diagnostic methods can be manual and time consuming which restricts their applicability in the timely intervention. In the present paper, a machine learning framework based on stacking ensembles is suggested to use in the prediction of cerebral strokes. The framework combines the use of CatBoost, Decision Tree, and Random Forest as base learners to utilize their relative predictive advantages. An end-to-end machine learning pipeline is built that involves the pre-processing of data, features encoding, and normalization, minimization of class imbalance with the help of the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE), applied to the training data only to avoid data leakage. Eleven machine learning models were first tested on an imbalanced Kaggle stroke dataset and the best models were chosen in building an ensemble, in a performance comparative analysis. As it is experimentally proved, the proposed stacking ensemble is better than the individual classifiers and results in the overall prediction accuracy of 98.64 percent and the enhanced precision, recall, and F1-score. In order to achieve practical usefulness, the trained model is implemented in the form of a web-based application, which can make real-time predictions of the risk of stroke based on patient demographic and clinical characteristics. The suggested framework has a promising future as a dependable decision-support platform of smart healthcare systems and prompt stroke risk detection.

title: IoT-Based Smart Aquaculture Monitoring and Automation System
authors: Sudipto Ghosh, Faysal Ahmmed, Md Sakibul Hassan Rifat, Mehedi Hasan Anik, Tanha Saeera Tamanna, Mahafuza Khatun, Touhidul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Fish farming in rural Bangladesh relies heavily on traditional manual methods, which often lead to inefficient water quality management. This paper presents a low-cost IoT based smart fish farming system that automates aeration based on water temperature, monitors <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> to detect contamination, and schedules automatic feeding four times daily. The system employs an ESP8266 microcontroller, DS18B20 temperature sensor, analog pH sensor, DS1302 real-time clock, a servo motor for feeding, and a DC motor for aeration. Data is logged and visualized in real-time on Firebase, with remote control via the Blynk mobile app. By using water temperature as an indirect indicator of dissolved oxygen and issuing alerts for abnormal pH levels, the system offers an affordable solution tailored for small-scale rural farmers. Testing under simulated conditions demonstrates reliable performance and ease of use.

title: Spectral-Thermal Dynamics and Urban Heat Island Evolution in New Damietta, Egypt
authors: Mohamed Kamel Elshaarawy, Hatem A. Khater, A. A. Elsharkawy
abstract: Land surface temperature behavior in coastal cities is controlled by surface composition, emissivity, moisture availability, and maritime advection. New Damietta has undergone rapid conversion from coastal and sparsely vegetated surfaces to impervious urban fabric, altering radiative forcing and surface energy partitioning. This study analyzes seasonally matched summer Landsat 8 OLI/TIRS Collection 2 Level-1 scenes acquired in 2018 and 2025 to retrieve emissivity-corrected land surface temperature (LST) and to derive the normalized difference vegetation index (NDVI) and normalized difference built-up index (NDBI). The analysis addresses surface urban heat island behavior expressed by satellite-derived LST rather than near-surface air temperature. Results showed stronger 2025 thermal anomalies over zones of expanded built-up response and reduced vegetation. Pearson correlation and ordinary least squares regression confirm persistent positive LST-NDBI coupling and negative LST-NDVI coupling, with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values of 0.648 and 0.624 for LST-NDBI and 0.661 and 0.564 for LST-NDVI in 2018 and 2025, respectively. Because one summer scene was used for each epoch, the inter-annual mean LST difference is interpreted together with possible meteorological variability; the strongest evidence therefore lies in the repeated spatial coincidence of hotspots with higher NDBI and lower NDVI.

title: High-Speed Multi-Level Signaling Using a Two-Dimensional Symbol Map and Intermediate-Level Inserted Symbol Representation
authors: Yosuke Iijima, Yasushi Yuminaka
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: Multi-level signaling techniques are being increasingly implemented in high-speed digital communication systems, as they can limit the required symbol rate and mitigate the noise effects caused by high-frequency components. In this study, we present a multi-level signaling scheme that suppresses symbol transition-dependent inter-symbol interference (ISI) using a two-dimensional (2D) symbol map and an intermediate level–inserted multi-level representation. We propose a PAM-5 signaling scheme that introduces a new intermediate level to reduce transition amplitudes, thereby mitigating ISI sensitivity and enhancing robustness against sampling timing variations. Furthermore, for transitions that remain difficult to decode, the selected symbols are considered as one-bit erasures even at the intermediate level. The simulation results obtained for the measured characteristics of a two-meter microstrip line indicate that the proposed PAM-5 scheme suppresses cluster spreading in the 2D symbol map and reduces distortion when compared with PAM-4.

title: NeuroFusionNet and CNN-Based Models for Detecting Cassava Leaves Disease: Insights using Grad-CAM Explainability
authors: Md. Azim Uddin Nahid, Md. Fahim Tahmid Rupak, Noman Al Badhon, Mohammed Shamsul Alam, Taohidur Rahman, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Cassava is an important food crop in a wide range of tropical and subtropical regions; however its productivity is badly affected by a number of leaf diseases including Cassava Bacterial Blight, Brown Spot and Green Mite infestation and Mosaic Disease. Early and accurate identification of these diseases is crucial for efficient crop management but the traditional diagnosis techniques are time-consuming and heavily rely on expert knowledge. In this paper, an automated framework a Deep Larning (DL) based framework is proposed for automated cassava leaf disease classification using several convolutional neural network architectures, including DenseNet121, InceptionV3, MobileNetV2 and a hybrid fusion model called NeuroFusionNet (EfficientNetB0 + MobileNetV2). Extensive data preprocessing and augmentation techniques is learned to better the robustness and generalization under the real world scenarios. Experimental results show that the overall accuracy of MobileNetV2 is the highest of 93.14 %, which is heavier than other heavier models and still has computational efficiency. Furthermore, Explainable AI (XAI) with Grad-CAM is adopted to visualize model attention to ensure that the predictions are due to biologically relevant regions of the disease. The proposed framework brings an accurate, interpretable, and lightweight solution for real-time application of agriculture.

title: Development of an IoT Based Transformer-Centric General-Purpose Thermal and Electrical Safety System
authors: S. M. Khalid bin Zahid, Abdul Hasib, Md. Abu Horaira Ansary Tanvir, Raheeb bin Nazmul, Mahadi Hassan, M.D. Habibullah
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Now-a-days, fire accident due to electrical short circuit is very common. This often happens because of failure of any electrical component due to excessive heat, current or voltage. To avoid these accidents, we developed a system which can monitor and control of a transformer's condition and provide realtime data using Internet of Things (IoT). Transformer's lifetime and dependability are significantly reduced by their extreme sensitive to abnormal voltage situations, overcurrent, and heat stress. This paper presents the design and implementation of a general-purpose IoT-based transformer protection system capable of monitoring temperature, current, and voltage in real time and can take preventive measures to avoid any accident. The proposed design uses an ESP32 microcontroller interfaced with an INA219 current-voltage sensor and a Dallas DS18B20 digital temperature sensor to continuously acquire and process important operational data. To ensure steady relay operation and avoid frequent switching under variable conditions, a memorybased decision method is presented. The system automatically activates cooling mechanisms and isolates the transformer load when predefined safety thresholds are exceeded. By the time, all operational data are transmitted to a cloud platform for remote visualization and supervision. Experimental results demonstrate accurate sensing and effective protective response under varying operating conditions. Here, the DS18B20's average accuracy is about 99 % and INA219's average accuracy is about 97 % with more than 96 % of accurate protective response. Although the prototype is validated on a distribution transformer, the developed architecture is scalable and can be deployed for thermal and electrical protection of a wide range of industrial and household electrical equipments.

title: Design of an External Sensing Configuration for a Broad Range of Circular PCF SPR Biosensors
authors: Md. Milon Ali, Md. Faysal Ahmed Anik, Monjila Afrin Dorothi, Mst. Suraia Yesmin, Mst. Umme Habiba
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The proposed study demonstrates a very sensitive surface plasmon resonance sensor based on a circular photonic crystal fiber for accurate refractive index readings between 1.33 and 1.40. Numerical simulations using the finite element method with appropriately matched layer boundary conditions show that the proposed structure has better sensing capabilities. The sensor has a robust sensitivity of amplitude <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$591.94 \text{RIU}^{-1}$</tex> and a high sensitivity of wavelength <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4000 ~\text{nm} /$</tex> RIU, and it achieves high resolution of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.50 \times 10^{-5} \text{RIU}$</tex> for the refractive index range, which makes it suitable for exact biochemical detection. The proposed design achieves a high figure of merit, which equals 666.667 RIU <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$^{-1}$</tex>, showing it has high sensing efficiency. The structure makes it simple to integrate external sensors, improving analyte accessibility and streamlining device development while maintaining system affordability.

title: Bank Customer Churn Prediction Using a Reproducible and Explainable Machine Learning Framework
authors: Ripa Sarkar, Ratna R. Sarkar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Predicting customer churn is crucial for banks because retaining existing customers is more cost-effective than acquiring new ones. This study presents a transparent and reproducible machine learning framework for predicting bank customer churn using a public dataset of 10,000 customers. Unlike previous work, the study resolves inconsistencies in the target variable and properly encodes categorical features such as Geography and Gender. Six machine learning models, including Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, KNearest Neighbor, Naive Bayes, and ensemble voting methods are evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The Bayesian Network model is applied with proper discretization of continuous features to ensure reproducibility. Explainable AI tools such as LIME and SHAP are used to interpret model predictions, providing insight into which features influence churn. The final model is integrated into a Flask-based interface for real-time prediction, offering banks a practical and reliable tool to reduce churn and improve customer retention. This framework emphasizes methodological rigor, reproducibility, and explainability, making it a novel and actionable approach to prediction churn in banking.

title: Inter-Arrival Time Driven Intrusion Detection for DoS Traffic in Cloud Networks Using Smote-Enhanced Deep Learning Optimization
authors: Mohammad Anwar Hossain, HM Alvee Hasan, Noshin Un Noor, Ahsan Ullah, Jannatul Naeem, Md. Shahariar Sarkar, Kh. Mustafizur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks are still a serious threat to cloud computing systems and the large volume of malicious traffic can deteriorate network performance and cause service unavailability. Conventional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are mostly based on packet-content features and have difficulty to identify temporal traffic patterns, especially in the imbalanced traffic scenarios. This paper presents a time-sensitive ID framework that uses packet IAT and traffic features to achieve efficient detection of DoS in the cloud. Specifically, a Mininet integrated dataset was created comprising normal data, ICMP flood traffic (attack type 1), TCP flood traffic (attack type 2), and Smurf traffic (Other) from which we built nine models using traditional machine learning and deep learning approaches. To avoid the influence of imbalanced class and improve learning results, we utilized Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) technology and Grid Search (GS) optimization. Experiment results show that although traditional methods are able to obtain moderate performance, deep learning models achieved superior macro-averaged performance across all classes and reach 97 % overall accuracy as well as nearly perfect precision and recall in all types of attacks (including the challenge case, the Smurf traffic) after applying our RNN-SMOTE-GS model. The findings demonstrate that the use of spatio-temporal traffic behavior information, as well as optimization strategies, demonstrates the effectiveness of temporal modeling combined with imbalanceaware optimization our detection capability. This platform offers the necessary underpinnings for real-time intrusion detection in cloud computing environments.

title: DAPT-XLMR: A Domain-Adaptive Transformer Framework for Binary Depression Detection in Bangla–English-Banglish Code-Mixed Social Media Texts
authors: Md. Sazid Hassan Rahat, Raeysa Nur, Shimul Dey Katha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Depression is a common psychological disorder that greatly influences the mental states of people. Early detection of depression is difficult, especially in resource-constrained environments where code-switching occurs. Social media plays an important role in understanding the psychological states of people. However, analyzing the psychological conditions of people using Bangla-English-Banglish texts is challenging because of code-switching, informal language use, and spelling variations. This paper presents a novel approach called DAPT-XLMR for binary depression classification using publicly available social media content that contains multilingual expressions. DomainAdaptive Pretraining (DAPT) helps to improve the adaptation of the XLM-RoBERTa model to the domain-specific texts. The proposed approach has an accuracy of up to 98%, better than the best transformer models. Furthermore, the proposed approach uses a post-hoc explainability technique to identify the depression-related words that are affecting the predictions. Overall, this proposed framework offers an effective solution strategy in understanding depression in a multilingual codemixed social media setting.

title: Spatio-Temporal Impact of Agroclimatic Variables on Rice Yield in Bangladesh: A Comparative Machine Learning Approach
authors: Tawfiqul Hasan, MD Ashfi Ar Raihan, Sabrina Islam Tesa, Antu Chakrabarty, Mhamud Hasan Kwoshick, Shaila Sharmin, Sultanul Arifeen Hamim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In climate-affected areas, farming is strongly affected by environmental changes over time and place. The forecast of rice yield in Bangladesh remains mysterious due to complicated relationships among agroclimatic parameters indicating strong divisional variations. In this context, the present study proposes a machine learning framework to estimate and separate yield at the sub-national level that overcomes the limitation of national-level yield modelling, by using 24 years (2000-2024) worth of data across eight administrative divisions. Four representative regression algorithms (Linear Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting and SVR) were evaluated to analyze 15 climatic and agricultural variables. Linear Regression yielded the best performance, with an average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> greater than 0.97 and a minimum MAE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.03 \text{ton} / \text{ha}$</tex> (Rajshahi: 0.9958). Photosynthetically Active Radiation and Root Zone Soil Wetness were identified as the dominant yield drivers. A Pythondata processing and evaluation pipeline implementation of the framework is available, providing a generalizable and scalable decision-support tool for regional food security planning.

title: Research on quality prediction of industrial processes based on deep learning with missing data
authors: Guangxiang Zhang, Xudan Cui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: A quality prediction method based on data interpolation and mixed gate units is proposed to address the quality prediction problem caused by data loss in industrial processes. Firstly, use Generative Adversarial Interpolation Network (GAIN) for data interpolation of time series; Secondly, redesign the loss functions of the GAN generator and discriminator to enable them to learn the original distribution of missing data and effectively fill in the missing parts; Finally, a mixed gate unit (MIXGU) quality prediction model is constructed to predict the quality of the interpolated samples. Through predictive experiments, it can be concluded that the problem of insufficient generalization in different data can be solved, further improving the accuracy of quality prediction.

title: State-of-the-Art Deep Learning Architectures for Brain Tumor Segmentation: A Review
authors: Mahjabin Hossain, Al Hossain Rafi, Umme Niraj Mahi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Brain MRI scans consist of multiple slices capturing the 3D brain anatomy on which the segmentation of the tumor becomes manually difficult and time-consuming. Automated classification using MRI provides a non-invasive safer technique than that of biopsy. Since the late 1990s and 2000s, there has been large-scale research work committed to creating automated method for classifying and segmenting brain tumors. Various studies have explored segmentation techniques over deep learning (DL) techniques. In parallel, significant improvement has also been realized in the classification of tumors by conventional types with high accuracy often. The present paper will offer a systematic review of recent advancement in brain tumor segmentation and classification (BTSC) using deep learning (DL) techniques. In addition, the strength and limitation of each technique as well as relevant information such as preprocessing, model architecture design, utilized datasets, preprocessing, feature extraction techniques, classification models, various architecture designs, different comparison metrics.

title: Explainable Multi-Modal Ensemble Learning for Flood Prediction from SAR and Optical Satellite Imagery
authors: Jannathul Maowa Hasi, Sagufta Sabah Nakshi, Mallika Sultana
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Timely and accurate flood detection using satellite imagery plays a critical role in disaster management and emergency response. Beyond accuracy, real-world flood monitoring demands models that are reliable, interpretable, and actionable for emergency responders. To address this, we present a sensor-aware deep learning framework for flood detection that jointly leverages Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical imagery. We benchmark a broad spectrum of approaches - from classical machine learning to advanced deep learning and ensemble methods - to identify what works best in practice. Tuned SVM and Random Forest models provide solid baselines, but deep learning architectures consistently advance. EfficientNet-B0 stands out among individual models with 93.57% accuracy, and combining predictions through XGBoost stacking pushes performance further to 95.43%, illustrating how complementary models collectively outperform any single approach. Beyond quantitative evaluation, we incorporate Explainable AI techniques - Grad-CAM (PCA = 0.73) and saliency maps <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{PCA}=0.66)$</tex> - to ensure model transparency. The visual explanations and quantitative evaluation confirm that the models focus on hydrologically meaningful regions such as water bodies, rather than irrelevant background features. Overall, this study provides a practical framework that balances accuracy and interpretability. It shows that careful preprocessing, advanced models, ensemble learning, and explainable AI work together to create reliable flood monitoring systems.

title: Real-Time Detection Algorithm of Metal Surface Minor Defects Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Chunya Wang, Zhiwei Chen, Huiyang Cai
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In industrial production, small defects on metal surfaces are common. Traditional inspections rely on manual inspections or simple machine vision leading to low accuracy in detecting defects and missing small cracks and pores, limiting the quality of a product and affecting production efficiency. This paper presented improvements to YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once version 8) that allow for accurate, real-time detection of small defects on metal surfaces. First, it introduced multi-scale feature enhancement coupled with improved FPN (Feature Pyramid Network) +PAN (Path Aggregation Network) to capture information in different scales and improve the ability to identify minor defects. Second, this paper incorporated a CBAM (Convolutional Block Attention Module) attention mechanism to the backbone network to weight attention in the spatial and channel direction to increase the distinguishability of defect features. Next, it implemented lightweight convolution and depthwise separable convolution to optimize the computational efficiency of the network to allow for industrial real-time detection while ensuring accuracy. The experiment results show that the improved YOLOv8 surpassed the comparison algorithm based on the PR (Precision-Recall) curve, with an F1-Score above 0.8 for the 5 types of defects; the improved inference speed reaches 142.5 FPS <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(7.0 \text{ms})$</tex> on the RTX 4090 and 68.2 FPS <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(14.7 \text{ms})$</tex> on the RTX 3060, improving real-time performance while ensuring accuracy and meeting the industrial high-speed needs for defect detection.

title: Deep Relational Knowledge Distillation Hashing via Relaxed Masking Triplet Optimization for Large-scale Image Retrieval

authors: Haohao Qu, Liangbo Ning, Rui An, Wenqi Fan, Tyler Derr, Hui Liu, Xin Xu, Qing Li

title: Leveraging AI Ecosystem for Portable and Sustainable GPU Kernels in HPC
authors: Yanbo Zhao, Zhaonan Meng, Sai Krishna Teja Varma Manthena, Xu Liu, Ajay Panyala, Jiajia Li
