This study conducts a comprehensive literature review and in-depth analysis to systematically summarize the effects of SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> polishing slurry on semiconductor materials during CMP, and deeply reveals the corresponding action mechanisms. Specifically, it focuses on the influences of key components in SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> slurry, which including SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> abrasives, alkaline pH regulators, oxidants, and other additives, on the CMP performance of semiconductor materials. Through theoretical analysis, the research elaborates on the chemical reactions of each component at the molecular or atomic level and clarifies the micro-mechanisms of interaction between abrasives, chemical components, and semiconductor material surfaces during the polishing process. This review not only establishes a theoretical framework for understanding the relationship between SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> polishing slurry and IC semiconductor materials but also provides constructive references for optimizing the polishing process of large-sized silicon wafers.

title: Catching Hackers by Watching Watts: A Measurement Study of Power-Only Attack Detection on Consumer IoT Devices
authors: Yuanyuan Zhou, Hashim Zia, Jiahui Qin, Sandra Siby, Anna Maria Mandalari
abstract: Security analysis of consumer Internet of Things (IoT) devices is increasingly constrained by limited visibility into encrypted traffic and closed firmware, which motivates the exploration of observable and privacy preserving external signals. Prior work has shown that adversarial activity can induce measurable changes in device power usage, indicating that power based intrusion detection is feasible, yet its stability and reliability under realistic operating conditions remain unclear. We address this gap through a systematic measurement study of 33 commercial IoT devices, collecting per second power traces and complete background traffic logs during idle operation and realistic active use under Denial of Service (DoS) and Reconnaissance attacks. Our analysis shows that power usage provides clear separation between benign and adversarial behavior in stable idle states, while legitimate activity introduces masking that varies across devices. We identify 4 behavioral regimes that describe how activity suppresses or alters attack related increments and analyze power stability and background traffic characteristics to explain the observed differences in detection strength. Our findings provide the first systematic large-scale characterization of when power-based detection remains reliable and when it degrades, highlighting the physical and network conditions that govern its robustness in realistic deployments.

title: Learn to Generate Complementary March Tests
authors: Ruiqi Zhu, Houjun Wang, Chongtao Guo, Yindong Xiao, Zhijian Dai, Rui Wu, Wanyu Yang, Geoffrey Ye Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: March tests require balancing fault coverage (FC) and test length in embedded memory testing. This brief proposes a novel reinforcement-learning (RL)-based framework that learns optimal March tests by exploiting undetected fault statistics. First, we analyze March tests using a scalable state-tuple-based fault analyzer. Then, we formulate March test generation as a problem to maximize expected FC (EFC) under budget limits. By modeling this as a Markov decision process (MDP), we leverage deep Q-learning to find the optimal operation sequences in March tests, with rewards derived from the proposed fault analyzer. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework generates complementary March tests achieving complete residual FC across a broader range of fault models than the existing methods, with generation efficiency significantly improved through fault analysis completing in milliseconds.

title: Decentralized MNO Resource Management for B5G HetNets
authors: Faisal Zaman, Abdelhakim Senhaji Hafid, Dimitrios Makrakis, Abdallah Jarray
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Dynamic spectrum management in beyond-5G heterogeneous networks requires efficient inter-operator coordination under time-varying demand. Existing centralized solutions, such as Spectrum Access Systems (SAS), rely on heartbeat-based authorization and polling-driven updates. This introduces enforcement latency and transient spectrum under-utilization. These architectures also lack native support for real-time multi-operator spectrum transactions across licensed, shared, and unlicensed spectrum bands. In this paper, we propose a blockchain-enabled decentralized spectrum management framework that decouples local optimization from global coordination and policy enforcement, enabling event-driven spectrum transactions without centralized control. We model spectrum reconfiguration as a traffic-driven process where capacity shortfalls or cost-efficiency opportunities generate control transactions. The rate of these events defines the transaction density. We quantify coordination delay using spectrum reaction latency and transient capacity loss, and derive a break-even condition under which decentralized coordination outperforms centralized operation. Blockchain acts as a coordination layer that validates transactions, enforces constraints, and maintains a consistent global state, while optimization remains local to each operator. Simulation results across licensed, shared, and unlicensed bands show that decentralized coordination reduces reaction latency and transient loss, improving time-averaged utilization and cost under non-negligible delay. Gains are limited at low transaction density but increase as transaction density grows, establishing it as a key system design parameter.

title: Hybrid CNN-BiLSTM with Attention Mechanism and Ensemble Learning for Multi-Symptom Disease Prediction: A Comparative Analysis
authors: D. Sungeetha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Proper prediction of disease based on symptomatic presentation is a major issue in medical diagnosis especially in situations where resources are limited such as in contexts where advanced diagnostic facilities are not readily available. This research will suggest a new hybrid deep learning framework, which is a combination networks to predict multiple diseases on the basis of multi-symptom patient data. The suggested model of hybrid CNN-BiLSTM got a high accuracy of 99.39% with precision of 99.46, recall of 99.39 and F1-score of 99.40. K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) recorded the highest accuracy of 99.49 percent but the proposed model performed better than the conventional ones such as Support Vector Machines (93.29 percent), logistic regression (92.28 percent), and Naive Bayes (85.37 percent). The ensemble top five algorithm did 99.39% accuracy. The deep learning hybrid technique using attention mechanism has a strong contribution to the prediction accuracy in diseases and retains the interpretability in terms of attention weights. The high performance of the model in a wide range of disease classes evidences its viability in the real-life medical application especially in the cases of initial screening and triage.

title: PCA-Enhanced Logistic Regression Model Framework for Intelligent EV Battery Charging Duration Classification with Comparative Machine Learning Analysis
authors: Ashwin Jacob, Sibi Kumaran K, Saran Adithyaa S, Thamizharasan R, S. Nalini Jayanthi, T. Rajesh
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The fast rate of electric vehicle (EV) integration requires the use of smart battery control systems that are able to predict optimal charge times to ensure the battery life, grid stability, and energy consumption. Conventional methods of machine learning do not typically handle the non-linear and complicated nature of the relationship between charging parameters (SOC, voltage, current, temperature, degradation rates) and optimal charging categories. Largescale experimental analysis of real-world EV battery charging data reveals that the PCA (Principal Component Analysis) enhanced logistic regression provides much better results (97.5% accuracy, Precision: 0.975, Recall: 0.975, F1-Score: 0.975), but GMM provides similar performance (93.5% accuracy, Precision: 0.935, Recall: 0.935, F1-Score: 0.935) with the benefit of having prob The effectiveness of the probabilistic modeling approaches is attested by the comparative study against the Support Vector Machine (92.0% accuracy), Gaussian Naive Bayes (87.0% accuracy) and K-Nearest Neighbors (83.5% accuracy). The framework gives quantification of uncertainty in the predictions, which is extremely good in the real-time charging station management systems where the confidence intervals are crucial. Our contribution makes computational battery analytics advance with an effective and explanatory framework of optimal classification of EV charging duration that is probabilistically based.

title: Adaptive Gradient-Weighted Stacking Ensemble for Electric Vehicle Energy Efficiency Prediction: A Novel Meta-Learning Approach
authors: Ashwin Jacob, H Mohammed, Gokul Krishnan, K. Saranya, V.Usha Rani, Parameswaran R
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: One of the most important and complex issues of sustainable transportation is the energy efficiency prediction of electric vehicles (EVs), which is associated with intricate interdependences between the vehicles characteristics, motor capacity, charging ability, and performance efficiency. Conventional methods of machine learning do not always explain the complex non-linear dynamics and multi-scale relationships of EV energy consumption. This study presents a new meta-learning algorithm called Adaptive Gradient-Weighted Stacking (AGWS) Ensemble that combines both the gradient-based optimization and adaptive weight learning to achieve better energy efficiency prediction. The suggested system works with detailed EV data covering model year, manufacturer, vehicle type, motor power (kW), recharge time (hours) as well as car energy efficiency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{km} / \text{kWh})$</tex>. AGWS has three levels of architecture (1) the heterogeneous base learners that render various facets of the efficiency landscape, (2) the adaptive optimization of weights depending on the prediction confidence, and the error pattern, and (3) Ridge regression meta-learning to combine the ensembles effectively. The comprehensive comparison of the results of AGWS with seven state-of-the-art algorithms indicates that it has an outstanding predictive accuracy with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R} 2=0.9153, \text{RMSE}=0.2456, \text{MAE}{=}$</tex> 0.1694, and MAPE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.0055 \%$</tex> indicating a 0.21 % improvement over the best model. The algorithm has identified motor power <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{kW})$</tex> as the most significant predictor having 35 percent feature importance, then vehicle make, and model characteristics. A consistent analysis of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0}$</tex> visualization frameworks shows that AGWS has a consistent prediction accuracy in all of the vehicular classes and efficiency ranges, with high consistency in the region of mid-range efficiency vehicles <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4-5 ~\text{km} / \text{kWh})$</tex>. The real-world EV selection, fleet optimization and policy-building in the sustainable transportation ecosystem are effectively deployed with the foundation model architecture.

title: Optimization of Fault Diagnosis Algorithm and Real Time Monitoring System Based on Electronic Information Equipment
authors: Fengqi Jia, Shanshan Lin, Xiaoli Shan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Background: Traditional electronic fault diagnosis relies on manual feature extraction, is insensitive to early weak faults, and lacks real-time monitoring. This paper optimizes fault diagnosis algorithms and constructs a real-time monitoring system. Methods: A 1D-CNN adaptively extracts multi-level fault features from vibration signals, with residual connections improving model stability and convergence. Genetic algorithms optimize deep neural network parameters to enhance generalization and accuracy. An adaptive threshold strategy reduces false positives, while online incremental learning captures new fault modes without catastrophic forgetting. An edge-cloud collaborative architecture enables local fast inference and cloud-based global management. Results: The method achieves 95.12% diagnostic accuracy and 93.72% recall under six fault scenarios, with a 1.21% false alarm rate and 15 ms response delay. Conclusion: This study integrates deep learning and optimization algorithms to improve fault diagnosis accuracy, real-time performance, and long-term reliability for predictive maintenance.

title: A High-Sensitivity Multilayer SPR Biosensor for Early Detection of Dengue Infection Using Plasma and Platelet Samples
authors: Khandoker Tauhid Imam, Nafis Nur, Dewan Mohammad Tausif
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Surface plasmon resonance (SPR) biosensors offer high sensitivity and real-time, label-free detection. This work proposes a multilayer SPR sensor with a ZBLAN/BiFeO <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{3}$</tex> /Ag/InN/WSe <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{2}$</tex> /analyte stack for early dengue diagnosis using plasma and platelet samples. A high-index ZBLAN prism and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{BiFeO}_{3}$</tex> perovskite improve light coupling and plasmon excitation. Sensor performance is evaluated using both the transfer matrix method (TMM) and the finite element method (FEM), and the close agreement between them validates the design. The optimized structure achieves a sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$267.27^{\circ} /$</tex> RIU, an FWHM of 4.98°, and a detection accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.20 \text{deg}^{-1}$</tex>. The penetration depth is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1935 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, and the peak electric field in the WSe <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{2}$</tex> layer is about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$280 \text{kV} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, indicating strong field confinement near the sensing interface. Parametric studies also examine different prisms, oxides, and 2D materials, as well as the effect of additional layers. The results show that the proposed SPR biosensor is a strong candidate for precise, early dengue detection in future biomedical sensing applications.

title: IoT-Based Electricity Consumption Prediction with User-Controlled Appliance Shutdown
authors: Haripriya V, Fathima Rania M, Raghavi S
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The energy consumption in households has been continuously growing as a result of the use of more electrical and electronic appliances. Traditional smart meters cannot anticipate the future and control the work of appliances and provide only basic indicators of their use. To enhance the state of domestic electricity consumption, the proposed study introduces an Internet of Things-based energy management system entailing real-time monitoring, predictive modelling, and automated cost estimation. Smart switches and sensors track the real time energy consumption of every appliance, and the data are processed by machine learning models to predict demand trends in the short- run. The system allows its users to remotely switch off appliances to reduce unnecessary use of energy and sends notifications when the expected amount of consumption exceeds the set limits. It also gives households a clear picture of their spending in relation to electricity by approximating the rates of electricity cost. As per the experimental analysis, an integration of IoT equipment with predictive forecasting brings about improved awareness of energy consumption, reduction of waste, and ease of making well- informed decisions in the day-to-day management of energy consumption at the household level.

title: The Effect of Advertisement Strategy on Business Performance Moderated by AI
authors: Jansen Wiyanto, Okky Rizkia Yustian
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Advertising, or commonly referred to as advertising, is a business strategy often used to promote a product or service offered. In addition to making the product well known in the community, another use is to make the business or brand name famous and make consumers aware of the business or brand name. The purpose of this study is to see the effect of advertising strategies moderated by AI on business performance, whether it has a positive impact or vice versa. The research method used is a quantitative technique using a questionnaire with Google Form platform. The target is retail companies engaged in the electronics sector such as PCs and laptops in Bandung with a population of 4,800 companies. Specific criteria for the sample are companies that have been operating for at least 3 years, still using advertising strategies for marketing, have experience in advertising strategies, use Purposive Sampling techniques to be able to take samples according to the specified criteria, for the data processing method using PLS SEM, the results obtained in general are many companies that use AI in their company's advertising strategy.

title: Understanding Learner Perceptions in Distance Education Using Sentiment Classification
authors: Md. Saiful Islam, Siyam Ul Alam, Syeda Zakia Nayem, Saurav Chandra Das, Md. Morshed Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Distance learning has become a crucial method of education, especially in the period of the COVID-19 pandemic, which also allows learning and teaching to take place outside regular classrooms in both synchronous and asynchronous environments. It is fundamental to ensure an improvement in the effectiveness of this modality by gaining an understanding of the perception of this modality among the population. The work is an analysis using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and machine learning models on a Kaggle dataset of user-created comments expressing their sentiments about distance learning. Sentiment labeling of the comments was done using VADER (Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner), which groups comments into positive, negative, and neutral. The positive sentiments related to words such as “accessibility” and “flexibility,” whereas words that indicate challenging issues of technology and infrastructure included negative sentiments. A variety of models were tried to compare the sentiment classification, including SVM, logistic regression, CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM, random forest, and BERT. Out of them, BERT established the best accuracy (95%), followed by BiLSTM (94%; and SVM/LSTM (93%). The results reveal that although distance learning is mostly valued because of it's flexibility and it's ability to be inclusive, there is some concern around the limitations of technicality and quality of learning experiences. These observations suggest a good implication for educators, institutions, and policymakers to improve the digital learning systems. In the future, aspect-based sentiment analysis and real-time feedback can be incorporated to enhance the learner.

title: Trading Policy Optimization Using Deep Reinforcement Learning for the Dhaka Stock Exchange
authors: Md. Rafid Tasnim Nabil, Fahim Ahmed Patwary, Ekram Hussain, Md. Shahriar Mahbub
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The paper introduces a portfolio management strategy that uses a meta-learning approach to adaptively integrate the performance of multiple deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based stock trading agents. Instead of relying solely on the decision of a single agent or using heuristic selection, the proposed strategy uses a meta-learning approach that considers the recently performed policies. Three actor-critic agents A2C, DDPG, and PPO are separately trained as expert learners. The neural network architecture that includes recurrent and attention layers is designed to assign the combination parameters. These parameters are required to adaptively weigh the portfolio allocation decisions of the three agents. The proposed approach is applied to the Dhaka Stock Exchange, an emergent stock market with the quickest stock price movement in the global industry. The experimental results indicate that the MAM-DRL (Meta-Adaptive Mixture of Deep Reinforcement Learning) approach significantly outperformed the standalone DRL agents and the non-adaptive ensemble approach in terms of risk-adjusted return.

title: Multi-Temporal Cloud Removal for Remote Sensing Images Based on Spatial-Temporal Convolutional Self-Attention
authors: Rui Sun, Ziqi Zeng, Junjie Xu, Yu Shi
abstract: The cloud cover in the optical remote sensing images will obscure the ground information to varying degrees, which causes the blurring and loss of the surface observation information and greatly affects the imaging quality of remote sensing images. Existing cloud removal methods mainly focus on spatial restoration from single-temporal images and insufficiently exploit complementary information from multi-temporal observations, which limits reconstruction quality under complex cloud conditions. In this article, we propose a multi-temporal cloud removal network based on spatial-temporal self-attention. A 3D convolutional residual block is introduced to capture dynamic changes across multi-temporal images and enhance temporal correlation learning. An adaptive dynamic weight fusion module is designed to adaptively aggregate temporal features and improve the utilization of complementary information from different temporal phases. In addition, a ConvTransformer is constructed to combine global contextual modeling with local structural perception, thereby improving the recovery of fine-grained details. Experimental results on the SEN2_MTC_Old dataset show that the proposed method achieves 27.800 dB PSNR and 0.842 SSIM with only 4.87M parameters, outperforming CTGAN, STGAN, and MCGAN. These results demonstrate that the proposed network can effectively exploit complementary multi-temporal information while maintaining a lightweight structure.

title: Deep Transfer Learning for Date Palm Leaf Disease Classification Using DenseNet201
authors: Raisul islam Ove, Ramisa Anjum Oishi, Tofayet Sultan, Md. Ariful Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Date palm cultivation is crucial to the agriculture and economy of numerous nations. However, leaf diseases threaten yield and quality, and manual detection methods are time-consuming and require expert knowledge. In this work, we introduce a deep learning-based method that uses the DenseNet201 architecture and transfer learning to classify date palm leaf diseases. The dataset was pre-processed with resizing and normalization, and data augmentation was applied to improve generalization. Two experimental setups were conducted: i) a baseline model with the pretrained DenseNet201, and ii) a finetuned model with a new classification head. A variety of criteria were used to train and assess the models, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix, and ROC-AUC curves. The fine-tuned DenseNet201 model performed better, according to the results, with an F1-score of 0.90 and a test accuracy of 91.2 %, outperforming the baseline version. The proposed framework demonstrates the effectiveness of transfer learning for agricultural image classification and provides a step toward automated, real-time plant disease detection systems.

title: Restoration-Guided Multi-Hazard Detection in Hazy Remote Sensing Images
authors: Ran Tian, Lijian Yu, Xiangchao Meng, Haoyong Qi
abstract: Geological hazard segmentation in remote sensing images is challenging due to the large variation in morphology and scale among different hazard types, as well as the severe degradation caused by haze. In particular, large region-like hazards such as landslides and collapses require strong contextual modeling, whereas crack-like hazards depend more on fine structural preservation. To address these issues, we propose a restoration-guided framework for multi-hazard segmentation in hazy remote sensing images. The proposed network integrates an auxiliary dehazing branch with a coarse-to-fine segmentation head, enabling joint learning of degradation-robust representations and structural features for hazard extraction. Specifically, restoration-aware features are fused with encoder features to enhance segmentation under degraded conditions, while the coarse and fine branches are used to model large-scale hazard regions and slender local structures, respectively. Experiments on a self-constructed hazy multi-hazard dataset containing multiple geological hazard categories and degraded haze scenarios demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Performance and Efficiency Analysis of Modern Filesystems on Consumer-Grade NVMe SSDs Under Real-World Mixed Workloads
authors: Sajeeb Khan, Sikder Moynul Hasan, Sagor Roy, Anowar Kabir
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: A filesystem is the core software that manages data storage and retrieval, determining the overall efficiency of I/O operations. Modern NVMe (Non-Volatile Memory Express) SSDs (Solid State Drives) are exceptionally fast, but for consumergrade hardware, the operating system often struggles to cope with this speed, becoming a bottleneck. Most existing research has focused on powerful servers and enterprise systems; performance behavior on devices with limited resources, like 8GB RAM laptops, has been largely neglected. In this research, we present the first comparative study of EXT4, XFS, BTRFS, ZFS, and NTFS on a single consumer-grade NVMe SSD focusing on CPU efficiency rather than raw throughput. We evaluated the performance and efficiency of five major filesystems-EXT4, XFS, BTRFS, ZFS, and NTFS- on a setup with standard Linux running on an AMD Ryzen 5 processor. Instead of measuring only data transfer speed, we focused on the efficiency gap, quantified by the hardware resource overhead required to achieve a specific throughput. In our tests, an important trade-off was observed: although ZFS provides advanced features, it utilizes up to 99% of the CPU (and up to 254% multi-core usage) in consumer laptops to maintain its advanced features, rendering it unsuitable for general mobile use. Conversely, for write operations, XFS delivers the best performance with minimal resource usage. For the mixed workloads typical of development environments, BTRFS proves to be a balanced option. We conclude that for general consumergrade hardware, the primary performance metric should be CPU efficiency-specifically, minimizing CPU overhead-rather than raw speed.

title: Based on Improved Ant Colony Algorithm Identification of Abnormal Nodes in Fiber Optic Communication Networks
authors: Hanyi Wang, Jushang Li, Ya'nan Zhang, Shicheng Zuo, Ziming Fang, Jialin Ren
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: When abnormal nodes appear in the optical fiber communication network, it affects the network communication efficiency, so it is crucial to quickly detect these abnormal nodes. We proposed an improved ant colony algorithm for identifying abnormal nodes. By setting a limited domain for the input data, the computational load within the data path range is significantly reduced, allowing for quick calculation of the optimal path within the limited domain. In the experiment, different nodes in the fiber optic network were tested, and the average accuracy of the system was 97.9%. The improved ant colony algorithm is insensitive to the total number of samples in the process of identifying abnormal nodes, and can adapt well to applications with large amounts of test data. In the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 0}$</tex> sample test, the detection time was less than 5 s, and the performance was significantly improved.

title: DeTailNet: Scene Graph Generation with Selective Causal Debiasing and Generative Tail Enhancement
authors: Yin YeChen, Zhong Zhenshu, Sun Qian
abstract: Scene graph generation aims to jointly model object entities and their semantic relationships in images. However, existing methods are often affected by the joint influence of long-tailed data distribution and contextual statistical bias during relation prediction, which leads to an over-reliance on high-frequency relation priors and weak recognition of tail predicates, thereby limiting the completeness and accuracy of scene semantic representation. To address these issues, this paper proposes a scene graph generation method that integrates selective total direct effect debiasing with generative tail enhancement. In the relation classification stage, a selective total direct effect debiasing mechanism is introduced to adaptively regulate the intervention strength on biased predictions, suppressing erroneous relation inference caused by object co-occurrence statistics and language priors while preserving discriminative visual semantic cues. In the tail predicate modeling stage, a generative adversarial learning based tail enhancement module is designed to compensate and enrich low-frequency relation features, alleviating insufficient feature learning caused by tail-sample scarcity. Experimental results on the PredCls task of the Visual Genome dataset show that the proposed method effectively improves tail predicate recognition while maintaining competitive overall recall, demonstrating its effectiveness in suppressing contextual bias and mitigating long-tail effects. Although validated on a general natural-image benchmark, the proposed framework also shows promising transfer potential to remote sensing imagery and spaceborne Earth observation scenarios, providing a useful reference for semantic scene understanding, object relation reasoning, and intelligent image interpretation in aerospace information processing.

title: Hidden Elo: Private Matchmaking through Encrypted Rating Systems
authors: Mindaugas Budzys, Bin Liu, Antonis Michalas
publish: Proceedings of the Sixteenth ACM Conference on Data and Application Security and Privacy
abstract: None

title: Exploring Bodily Phenomena through Code: A Research Through Design Inquiry of Sketching with LLMs
authors: Irene Kaklopoulou, Kit Kuksenok, Daniel Saakes, Pedro Sanches
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Designing Interactive Systems Conference
abstract: None

title: Re-Envisioning Instant Photography using Generative AI: An Exploratory Design Probe Using the UnReality Camera
authors: Michael Yin, Angela Chiang, Robert Xiao
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Designing Interactive Systems Conference
abstract: None

title: Automatic generation method of large model code based on associated AST revision
authors: Yubo Wei, Xiaodong Du, Jianfei Cao, Jiyang Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: With AI’s rapid development, large-model-based automatic code generation has great potential in software development, but existing methods lack functional correctness, code quality and maintainability. This paper proposes a large-model automatic code generation method based on Association Syntax Tree (AST) revision to improve overall performance. It combines NLP and AST structural information to revise large-model-generated code, optimizing grammatical structure, logical clarity and maintainability. Specifically, it uses a pre-trained model to generate initial code from natural language, identifies code problems via semantic association analysis between code and natural language, then revises code with AST structure to ensure correctness and clarity. It further enhances code performance through algorithm selection optimization, automatic code optimization, etc. Experiments on Human Eval, MBPP, APPS, DS-1000 and a custom dataset, using indicators like Pass@k, code complexity and execution efficiency, show this method outperforms existing ones in correctness, quality and maintainability while keeping high efficiency, providing a reliable solution for automatic code generation.

title: Influencer Attractiveness, Expertise, and Trustworthiness: Effects on Brand Image, Relational Quality, and Premium Price Purchase Intention in Skincare Products
authors: Savira Rahmatullah Pahlevi, Devi Anisatul Mawadah, Fanni Yuliani, Yuniarty
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Influencer marketing presents numerous significant opportunities for premium brands to increase purchase intentions at premium prices. This study examines the key factors influencing purchase intention for premiumpriced skincare products by investigating interconnected variables, including influencer characteristics, brand image, and relational quality. Using purposive sampling, data were collected from 313 respondents in Indonesia who were over 18 years old and had watched influencer content promoting a premium skincare brand. The analysis technique used was Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) with the Partial Least Squares (PLS) approach. This study aims to provide insights for premium brands to understand consumer behavior in response to influencer marketing, highlighting how influencer characteristics shape positive perceptions of brand image and relational quality. By selecting influencers based on appropriate traits, the brand can increase premium price purchase intention. The results of this study show that trustworthiness is a primary determinant of relational quality and significantly affects the purchase intention for premium-priced products, followed by brand image. This suggests that premium brands must be selective in choosing influencers who possess trustworthy characteristics, characterized by dependability, honesty, reliability, sincerity, and trustworthiness, to drive premium-priced purchase intentions.

title: An AI-Driven Gamified Framework for Predictive Habit Formation and Personalized Wellness Management
authors: S Sreeraj, Dhanaseelan S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The growing need in the digital wellness has triggered the creation of intelligent platforms that can enhance the stability of behavior in the long term. This paper introduces HappyHabits, an intelligent AI-based gamified wellness and habit changing application created to help individuals keep their daily habits with the help of adaptive analytics and motivational systems. The system combines habit tracking, gamification rewarding, engagement with virtual pets and behavioral prediction on the basis of machine learning to improve user activity. A predictive model is made lightweight, and the historical data of habit completions, activity patterns, and behavioral indicators are analyzed to predict the likelihood of successful habit execution. The personalized recommendation mechanism has been proven to be reliable with an accuracy of prediction of 91.3% and the precision and recall of more than 0.88 documenting the positive results of the evaluation carried out on simulated datasets of user activities. The gamification model also enhances the interest in streak consistency and milestones of achievements being rewarded. Altogether, the suggested platform has potential in terms of maintaining habit adherence and behavioral change. The next steps in the work will include integrating behavioral analytics based on deep learning patterns, integrating wearable sensors in real-time, and adapting emotional feedback to further personalize the process and engage the user in the long term.

title: Multi-site Complementary Solar Networks for Continuous Power with Minimal Storage at the Lunar South Pole
authors: Wengan Sun, Qiliang Wang, Junke Zhang, Honglun Yang, Xianze Ao, Gang Pei
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: Ensuring a continuous energy supply is critical for sustaining the long-term operation of Lunar research stations. The diurnal variation in solar irradiance presents a major challenge to relying solely on solar power. This is particularly evident in the Lunar South Pole, where low solar elevations and terrain-induced shadowing create highly time-varying illumination. Nevertheless, these conditions also create opportunities for innovative solutions. This study conducts a detailed investigation into the strategy of multi-site complementary solar energy nexus, that exploits the natural temporal complementarities among nearby illuminated areas to deliver near-continuous power with little or no bulk energy storage. In this work, the complementary potential of 12 landing sites near the Lunar South Pole were evaluated, and network configurations within radii of 20 km and 50 km were systematically analyzed. Results indicate that connecting some landing site with one or two adjacent high-illumination areas can achieve a continuous solar energy supply without the need for energy storage systems. Moreover, interconnecting more than a dozen regions with moderate solar resources can enable a solar power system supplemented only by micro-energy storage. A comparative analysis with the solar-tower concept demonstrates that the multi-site complementary strategy offers greater feasibility for landing sites with relatively low illumination levels. In conclusion, the proposed strategy reframes south-polar solar power from single-site optimization to local network design, reduces storage dependence through temporal complementarity and expands the range of potential landing site selections in the Lunar South Pole.

title: Social Media Recommendations and Impulsive Buying: The Roles of Fear of Missing Out, Stalking, and Digital Fatigue
authors: Kurnia Puji Smith, Domingos Elisito Do Espirito Santo, Ridho Bramulya Ikhsan, Dara Savor, Hartiwi Prabowo, Teguh Sriwidadi
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The immense digital transformation and rapid growth of e-commerce have changed customer interaction and decision-making practices. One notable phenomenon is customers' tendency to buy trending products recommended on social media, often influenced by exposure to others' lifestyles, recommendations, or achievements. As digital engagement increases, consumers are constantly exposed to persuasive content that shapes their perceptions of what is desirable or necessary. This constant connectivity not only fosters social comparison but also amplifies emotional triggers such as the fear of missing out. This study aims to investigate the effect of fear of missing out, social media stalking, fatigue, and online social comparison on impulsive buying behavior. A quantitative method was used with purposive sampling, through a survey of 388 social media users in the Greater Jakarta area. Data analysis was performed using “structural equation modeling-variance-based” to test the relationships among the proposed variables. The results revealed that fear of missing out significantly influenced social media stalking, fatigue, online social comparison, and impulsive buying. These findings provide empirical evidence of the psychological mechanisms behind social media experiences that can trigger unplanned buying behavior. Theoretically, this study contributes the understanding of customer behavior in the digital age. It also has practical implications for digital businesses, including designing ethical communication strategies, fostering responsible consumer engagement, and increasing customer awareness of digital vulnerability management.

title: Development of a Machine Learning Model for Assessing Risks of Delay in IT Project Management
authors: Levon V. Agamirov, Vladimir L. Agamirov, Igor S. Vlasnev, Arseniy M. Sampilov, Natalia V. Toutova
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: The problem of missed deadlines in IT projects remains one of the most pressing issues in the software development industry. The lack of publicly available data on completed projects including detailed descriptions of project parameters, risks, and execution dynamics significantly hinders the application of machine learning methods for predicting delays. This paper proposes an approach based on synthesizing a realistic dataset that mimics the structure and dependencies of real IT projects, and building a CatBoost gradient boosting model for classification and regression. On a generated dataset of two thousand projects, the model achieved a classification accuracy of 79.25% and a recall of 88.7%. The developed approach has been implemented as source code, available for use and further training on real-world data.

title: Experimental Study on End-Effector Performance in Simulated Extraterrestrial Surface Soil Conditions
authors: Hao Wang, Yafang Liu, Jian Gao, Dong Pan, Jie Ji, Yufei Li, Yiliang Chen, Wenming Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: The capability of robotic end-effector to interact with extraterrestrial soils is fundamental to planetary sampling and in-situ exploration. This study conducts an experimental validation of end-effector performance in simulated extraterrestrial soil environments, focusing on measurable sampling indicators. Soil simulants replicating the physical and mechanical characteristics of Martian regolith, including particle size distribution, bulk density, and cohesion, were employed in controlled laboratory tests. A robotic arm and corresponding sampling end-effector with distinct structural designs were fabricated and evaluated. Key performance metrics, including motor current variation during soil penetration and excavation, as well as the mass of collected samples, were systematically measured and analyzed. Results demonstrate that average motor current remained within $26.32 \%$ of the rated value under all tested soil conditions, ensuring stable energy consumption. Furthermore, the average sampling mass achieved by the optimized end-effector exceeded $\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ g}$ per trial, indicating robust collection efficiency. The results highlight the strong influence of soil mechanical properties on end-effector energy efficiency and sampling effectiveness, providing experimental evidence to support the design and optimization of robotic soil-interacting tools for future and Martian exploration missions.

title: Application Practice and Future Concept of MBSE in the Design of Deep Space Exploration Spacecraft Systems
authors: Yu’ang Liu, Yuanming Miao, Haitao Fan, Fuming Zeng, Xiangdong Chen, Fan Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: In response to the challenges of high complexity in the design and validation of asteroid probe, high interdisciplinary coupling, and difficulty in flight mission design, Model Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) was introduced to conduct research on MBSE methods such as requirement analysis and modeling, key process modeling, and task-level system function simulation. The application of MBSE methods in detector system design was also practiced, effectively improving the design quality and efficiency of the detector. On this basis, research and ideas were conducted on the application modes and methods of MBSE method in subsequent model development.

title: An Autoencoder and Gradient Boosting Framework with Advanced SMOTE Variants for Disaster Prediction
authors: J. Avanija, Rama Krishna Sankavaram, Dharani Palla, Chandana Saladhi, Akhilesh P
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Natural disasters such as floods, earthquakes, and wildfires threaten human lives, infrastructure, and economies. Accurate and timely prediction is vital for preparedness and risk reduction, yet current models face major limitations. Traditional approaches like Random Forest, SVM, and Logistic Regression often fail to capture nonlinear patterns in heterogeneous data and perform poorly on imbalanced datasets, where floods dominate while wildfires and earthquakes are underrepresented. Hybrid methods such as Neural-XGBoost improve accuracy but remain constrained by generic feature extraction, overfitting risks, and reliance on basic oversampling techniques. To address these gaps, this work proposes a hybrid Autoencoder–LightGBM framework with advanced SMOTE variants for multi-class disaster prediction. Autoencoders learn compact, noise-resilient representations from complex disaster records, enhancing feature quality. To correct imbalance, advanced oversampling techniques such as SMOTE-ENN generate informative synthetic samples and filter noisy boundaries. LightGBM is then applied for classification, leveraging its speed, scalability, and interpretability while effectively handling tabular data with missing values. The proposed framework was evaluated using the EM-DAT disaster dataset and was compared with baseline models such as Random Forest, SVM, Logistic Regression, and Neural-XGBoost. The results showed improved accuracy and balanced F1-scores across all disaster categories, particularly enhancing the prediction of underrepresented classes such as wildfires and earthquakes. By integrating deep feature extraction, robust resampling, and an efficient boosting classifier, the study provides a balanced, interpretable, and practical solution for disaster prediction that can strengthen disaster preparedness and management strategies.

title: A Real-Time Verification Framework for Hallucination and Bias Detection in AI Generated Text
authors: Rithika R J, Uma Bharathi S, G.S. Devi Lakshmi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The growing ease of usage of Large Language Models (LLM) has released anxieties about hallucinations, factual inconsistency, prejudgment, and moral dependability of AI-created answers. Current methods are mainly aimed at the detection of hallucinations or binary factual verification without adequate multi-dimensional reliability evaluation. In this paper, a multi-layered AI response assessment model is suggested, organizing AI creation, multi-source fact checking (Wikipedia and Google), bias/toxicity identification using ToxicBERT, and the organization of results. The system allows the use of text and speech as inputs, giving out quantitative credibility scores, issue detection as outputs, and interpretability-based visualizations. Whether compared to previous evaluation-only designs, the given architecture is a combination of factual verification and ethical risk check in a deployable pipeline. The experimental analysis of it shows high validation and strong cross-verification results on different datasets. The framework offers a scalable, interpretable and holistic solution towards reliable assessment of AI responses.

title: Evaluation of the Quality of VLSI Component Placement Using the linear Segment Metric
authors: Vladislav I. Danilchenko, Nikolay D. Posrednikov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This study addresses the NP-hard problem of VLSI block placement under technological and physical constraints through the formulation of a multi-objective optimization framework incorporating a comprehensive set of design metrics. In addition to conventional geometric measures—total wirelength, placement density, area utilization, and crossing index—a novel topological metric based on linear segment count is introduced. This metric facilitates not only the minimization of interconnect length but also the structural regularization of routing geometry, thereby improving signal integrity, reducing parasitic effects, and enhancing energy efficiency. To solve the formulated problem, modified bio-inspired heuristics are proposed: a genetic algorithm enhanced with a hybrid evolutionary strategy integrating Lamarckian local adaptation and De Vries stochastic mutations, and an improved stem-cell behavior model incorporating mechanisms for crossing minimization and density-aware placement refinement. A dedicated software framework was developed to conduct simulation-based experiments. The efficacy of the proposed algorithms and the informativeness of the metrics were evaluated using statistical analysis, including correlation studies and Pareto-optimality assessment. The experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in key design parameters such as power dissipation, thermal profile, and routability. Based on the findings, methodological guidelines are provided for integrating advanced topological metrics into contemporary VLSI CAD/EDA design flows, thereby contributing to enhanced design quality, manufacturability, and parametric yield. This work is situated within the domains of physical design automation, computational intelligence in electronic design, and multi-criteria engineering optimization.

title: Intelligent Parcel Monitoring and Security System for Smart Apartments using IoT and AI Technologies
authors: Kanimozhi S, Thanush S, Vishwa S, Sanjai K, Naveenkumar M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The rapid growth of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies is improving smart security systems for modern residential environments. With the increase in online shopping, apartments face challenges such as unattended deliveries and parcel theft. This project proposes an AI-assisted IoT-based delivery monitoring system that uses an ESP32 microcontroller, cloud connectivity, and sensors to ensure secure parcel management. AI techniques analyze sensor data to detect parcel placement and identify unusual activities. The system sends real-time notifications to users through a mobile application, enabling remote monitoring and secure access to the delivery box. This approach enhances parcel security, improves delivery verification, and provides a reliable solution for smart apartment delivery management.

title: API-First Architecture: Achieving Enterprise-Wide Operational Unity and Real-Time Data Flow in Complex Digital Ecosystems
authors: Vishesh Goel, Astha Bhatiya, Kshitij Khare
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: The API-first architecture has become a core methodology of developing flexible, scalable, and interoperable digital systems in modern enterprises. This paper discusses the use of the API-first strategy to achieve operational coherence across the enterprise and real-time data flow across the complex digital ecosystems of cloud platforms, microservices, legacy systems, and third-party applications. By prioritising APIs as the core building blocks of system design, organisations can achieve consistent data exchange, faster integration, and improved cross-functional collaboration. The research investigates essential architectural principles, such as standardised interface contracts, service decoupling, and real-time communication mechanisms, and evaluates their contribution to improving system agility and resilience. Moreover, the paper also emphasises how API-first architecture can be used to facilitate real-time analytics, automation, and scalable digital transformation programmes. Challenges related to governance, security, and version management are also discussed, along with best practices to address them. The results indicate that the API-first approach is a fundamental enabler of next-generation digital ecosystems because it produces a high level of efficiency and speed in innovation and facilitates uninterrupted information flow in distributed enterprise settings.

title: Exploring the Impact of Artificial Intelligence Applications in Information Systems on Organizational Sustainability in Banks
authors: Anwar Al-Gasaymeh, Mohammad Mahmoud Saleem Alzubi, Fadi Ahmed Mohamed Abutabanjh, Nour Ibrahim Kurdi, Hani Jazza Irtaimeh
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a strategic enabler of digital transformation, particularly in sectors characterized by intense competition, high data dependency, and strong sustainability pressures. In the banking industry, AI applications embedded within information systems are increasingly used to enhance decision-making, automate operations, and manage organizational knowledge; however, their direct contribution to organizational sustainability remains underexplored, especially in emerging economies. This study examines the impact of AI applications in information systems on organizational sustainability in Jordanian banks, drawing on the Resource-Based View (RBV) and Dynamic Capabilities Theory (DCT). A quantitative research design was employed, and data were collected through a structured questionnaire administered to managers and employees in Jordanian commercial banks. A total of 322 valid responses were analyzed using Structural Equation Modeling (SEM). The results reveal that AI-based data analytics and predictive intelligence, AI-enabled process automation in banking operations, and AI-supported knowledge management and decision support systems all have significant positive effects on organizational sustainability. Among these dimensions, AIsupported knowledge management and decision support systems exert the strongest influence. The findings indicate that AI-driven information systems enhance financial stability, operational efficiency, governance quality, and stakeholder trust, thereby supporting sustainable banking performance. This study contributes theoretically by extending RBV and DCT to the context of AI-enabled information systems and sustainability, and it offers practical insights for bank managers and policymakers seeking to leverage AI as a strategic tool for sustainable organizational development.

title: A Soft Robotic Gripper With an Embedded Bistable Elastic Mechanism for Pneumatic Actuation and Passive Locking
authors: Efrem Olivio Gomes, Shyang-Jye Chang, Ilham Saputra, Chin-Yi Cheng
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: This paper presents a soft robotic gripper with an embedded bistable elastic mechanism combined with pneumatic actuation. The proposed design enables controlled opening through pneumatic pressure while achieving passive grasping via elastic energy release after pressure removal. Unlike conventional pneumatically actuated soft grippers that require continuous air supply to maintain a grasp, the proposed gripper decouples the opening and holding functions, improving energy efficiency and operational simplicity. The soft gripper is fabricated using a multi-stage silicone rubber casting process from CN-251 A and CN251B with an embedded bistable elastic element and an internal pneumatic chamber. Experiments are conducted using a simple open-loop control scheme with a maximum operating pressure of 0.25 bar. The gripper achieves a maximum opening of 75 mm, with both opening and grasping motions occurring in less than 1 second. Grasping tests using six objects with masses ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 g}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5. 9 g}$</tex> demonstrate stable passive holding without pneumatic input. The results confirm the effectiveness of the bistable elastic mechanism for fast, energy-efficient, and adaptive soft robotic grasping.

title: STEM Technologies Support for Small Groups
authors: Kirill S. Katkov, Anton I. Tikhonov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article discusses STEM technology support system for publishing heterogeneous static content and interactive web applications (active content) prepared using the Dash, marimo, and Solara frameworks. An analysis of the system architecture, types of supported content, methods of developing e-textbooks with active content, and various scenarios for its application in the educational process is provided. It is shown that the use of the FastHTML framework has made it possible not only to integrate heterogeneous active content, but also to reduce time and effort required to create support for STEM technologies in the educational process. In conclusion, the possibilities for enhancing the functionality of the system are examined.

title: Comparative Study of LLMs and BERT for Informal Indonesian TikTok Sentiment Analysis
authors: Chayun Fadila, Nur Aini Rakhmawati
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: TikTok Live Streaming has become an important live streaming platform for real-time audience interaction, generating massive volumes of comments and emoticons that carry nuanced sentiments across multiple aspects. Conventional sentiment analysis approaches are inadequate for analyzing detailed opinions in informal texts that are rich in emoticons. This article proposes a hybrid framework in Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) on TikTok Live Streaming comment data, integrating BERT refinement using LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) and Large Language Model using LoRA. This system targets four aspect categories, namely Host, Content, Interaction, and Topic, which will classify sentiment into eight emotion classes with three levels of polarity. The results of this study show that BERT+LoRA achieves a higher F1 score in the domain, while the LLM-based approach offers greater flexibility without the need for retraining.

title: Specific Features of Teaching the “Lean Production” Discipline in the Engineering Education
authors: Irina Sazonova, Maria Znamenskaya, Natalia Ketoeva
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: The article is devoted to analyzing the specifics of teaching the "Innovations in electric transport" section within the "Lean manufacturing" discipline in the framework of the system of higher engineering education. The concepts of lean innovation and frugal innovation are examined and compared as tools for the development of electric transport. The article considers the features of cultivating innovative thinking in students, taking into account the differences between lean and frugal innovations. Examples of the implementation of these types of innovations in electric transport are provided, as well as the necessity of reflecting them in educational programs is justified. One of the main objectives of this study is to explore the essence and distinguishing characteristics of the lean innovation and frugal innovation concepts. The study has shown that integrating lean and frugal innovation concepts into the educational process contributes to the development of innovative thinking and practice-oriented competencies in students in the field of electric transport. The developed model for teaching the "Innovations in electric transport" section within the "Lean manufacturing" discipline will help promptly address challenges in preparing competitive engineering professionals capable of designing and implementing effective innovative solutions in the electric transport field.

title: Temporal Swarm Intelligence Driven Enhancement of LEACH-C for Energy Neutral and Self-Healing Wireless Sensor Networks Enabled by Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting
authors: S P Vimal, Harshini R, Mithra K M, Nakshathraa N B
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: To be deployed in the long term in remote and energy sensitive environments, Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) need to develop into self sustaining, adaptive, and fault tolerant schemes. In an effort to reach a smart and energy efficient network management, this paper identifies an Ant-Bee Temporal Switching Protocol that enhances LEACH-C protocol and is complemented with the piezoelectric energy harvesting. Ant inspired routing based on pheromone communication can be used to ensure reliable and sparse intra-cluster communication in the case of low energy states, and bee inspired optimisation can be used to cope with inter-cluster load balancing and efficient data aggregation in the case of plenty energy. Resource reconfiguration and prevention of hotspots in global clusters by swarm intelligence is achieved by periodic LEACH-C optimisations and hotspots prediction by a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) based health classifier. An unbroken adjustment to energy variations, node failures and traffic disproportions is additionally guaranteed by a closed loop feedback process. These innovations go a long way in enhancing network performance indicators including lifetime of the network and survival rate of the node not to mention the communication indicators like throughput and packet delivery ratio (PDR). Self healing capability has high rates of recovery of path failures as well as node death in reaction to active load balancing and safeguarding of energy intensive nodes. This work is innovative in its own right due to the smooth combination of two major innovations: (1) temporal swarm intelligence switching to dynamically choose between ant inspired conservative routing and bee inspired exploratory optimisation based on instantaneous energy access and (2) closed loop self healing of failures of pathways, node degradation and load imbalance with no human intervention. It has been proven through a comparative analysis that the integrated approach is more superior to the conventional clustering protocols in all the key performance indicators, and therefore it is considered as an effective solution to various long term deployment scenarios which require less maintenance and highest levels of reliability.

title: VisionSpice: A Scalable Attention-Augmented Deep Learning Framework for Fine-Grained Indian Spice Classification
authors: D. Viji, Sriram R, Meyyappan L
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Automated recognition of Indian spices is a challenging fine-grained visual classification problem. Morphologically similar varieties, such as cumin and fennel, are often difficult to distinguish under real-world imaging conditions, while intra-class variations caused by illumination, capture angle, and physical form (whole, cracked, or ground) further increase the complexity of classification. This study presents VisionSpice, an attention-augmented deep learning framework evaluated on a 19-class benchmark dataset containing 10,991 images collected from two public Mendeley repositories. The proposed system integrates EfficientNet-B3 with additional stage-level Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention modules placed at macro-stage boundaries. The model is trained using AdamW optimization, cosine annealing learning rate scheduling, and label smoothing. A systematic ablation study is conducted to assess the contribution of each component. Experimental results demonstrate that the final model achieves a test accuracy of 96.8% with a macro-F1 score of 0.967, outperforming previously reported spice classification methods that were evaluated on smaller class sets. Furthermore, the model processes images in 19 ms per image on a consumer-grade GPU while maintaining a compact size of 12.5 million parameters, making VisionSpice suitable for both industrial inspection systems and edge-based deployment.

title: The Usability Analysis of PYTHON Optimization Libraries in the Context of Student Research Projects
authors: Anna Klimenko, Vadim Zhepan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel model for evaluating the usability of PYTHON optimization libraries in the context of student research projects. Based on the analysis of four popular libraries (Mealpy, Optuna, DEAP, and SciPy), we introduce a set of objective metrics specifically designed for educational settings. The evaluation focuses on three key aspects: visualization capabilities, data processing capabilities, and optimization algorithms customization. Our experimental case demonstrates that Mealpy provides the most user-friendly visualization capabilities with minimal implementation complexity, while DEAP offers greater flexibility for algorithm customization. The results show that appropriate library selection can reduce implementation effort by up to 80% while maintaining similar research outcomes. The proposed model helps educators and students make informed decisions about optimization tools for academic projects, potentially improving student motivation and productivity in research activities.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Accounting and Finance: A Systematic Review of Emerging Technologies and Practical
authors: M. Sreedevi, M.D.Raj Kamal, Nagendar Yamsani, Samridhi Sharma
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Accounting and finance are being fundamentally transformed by Artificial Intelligence (AI): intelligent automation, sophisticated analytics, and data-driven decisions can be achieved. Conventional financial systems, which have long relied on manual approaches and rule-based solutions, are undergoing more and more additions of Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing, and Robotic Process Automation. The technologies are in support of automated bookkeeping, intelligent auditing, fraud detection, credit risk modeling, portfolio optimization, and financial forecasting. AI systems enhance efficiency, accuracy, and realtime generation of insights after analyzing large volumes of structured and unstructured financial data. The current review paper is a systematic study of recent advances in accounting and finance of AI use, where methods are divided into categories, and key practical applications are identified. It also examines the latest trends, such as explainable AI, financial reporting using generative AI, and systems that are powered by AI and support decision-making. In spite of major improvements, issues can still be encountered regarding the interpretability of the model, regulatory compliance, data privacy, ethical aspects, and the risk of cybersecurity. The paper proposes research gaps that are critical and maps the directions in the future to facilitate transparent, reliable, and scalable intelligent financial systems that add to the research in academia and industry practice.

title: A Deep Transfer Learning Framework Based on EfficientNetB4 for Robust Neem Leaf Disease Diagnosis
authors: M Amreen, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Disha Garg
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Neem is a critical medicinal plant, and the leaves of this plant are prone to diseases that may affect the health of the plant and the quality of therapy. Manual detection of the diseases is time-consuming and subjective, and this has provided the motivation to implement automated image-based detection of diseases. This research focuses on the EfficientNetB4 network to detect and classify diseases in neem leaf images. A dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 3 8}$</tex> RGB images of four classes (Alternaria, Healthy, Leaf Miners, and Powdery Mildew) was used. Images were preprocessed by resizing to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$380 * 380$</tex>, normalization, removal of background focusing on the leaves, and ImageNet-based standardization for transfer learning. The model was trained with Adam optimization using categorical cross-entropy, while an independent test set was used for evaluation. The proposed framework was found to have an accuracy of 88.39 % and significant class wise performance (F1-scores: Alternaria 0.87, Healthy 0.89, Leaf Miners 0.84, Powdery Mildew 0.90). Confusion matrix analysis revealed little misclassification overall, with the majority of misclassifications occurring between visually similar symptoms. The results show that EfficientNetB4 is an effective and computationally efficient solution to the early diagnosis of neem leaf disease in order to support timely intervention and improved agricultural management.

title: Investigating the Robustness of Machine Learning Models Against Data Poisoning in Industrial Control Systems
authors: Soumya Ranjan Bej, Kamal Mann, Abhilash Pakalapati, Bhawana Pillai
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: In the course of this research, the researchers determined how well Machine Learning (ML) algorithms functioned when subjected to poisoning of their training data on behalf of Industrial Control Systems (ICS). The researchers created a simulated testing environment utilizing synthetic signal patterns to determine how the accuracy, confidence, and feature patterns generated from the ML algorithm would be altered with differing degrees of poison attack. Results indicated that a minor amount of poisoning will begin to create instability in the model over time, whereas increased degrees of poisoning will result in unstable operation and generate inaccurate outputs. Additionally, the researchers found that the poisoning caused the model's feature importance to move in an incorrect direction and that the clusters that had been established via PCA began to collapse. Therefore, it can be seen that the researchers experienced a high degree of ambiguity regarding what the model learned. In general terms, the researchers identified that combinations of both noise and poisoning attacks will reduce the model's performance at a much higher rate than either type of individual attack. Ultimately, the findings from this research support that Machine Learning-based systems within ICS need greater security protections against the adverse effects of poisoning since poisoning could potentially disrupt an ICS prior to identification of an operational problem.

title: Experimental Comparison of Recurrent Neural Networks for Text Classification Under Noisy Conditions
authors: E. S. Markovskiy, M. R. Nikiforov, N. V. Markovskaya
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper examines the main architectures of recurrent neural networks used for text classification. Since real-world text data often contains distortions, the aim of the study was to compare the performance of vanilla RNN, LSTM, and GRU models in the presence of noise. To address this issue, an experimental design was developed that included data preparation and model training. The results of the experiment revealed that the GRU model clearly demonstrated the best classification quality and the greatest resilience to distortions, while LSTM showed similar but lower results, and vanilla RNN was significantly inferior in accuracy and resilience to noise.

title: Deploy Low-Latency Convolutional Neural Networks for Autonomous Embedded Applications: Real-Time Object Recognition on AI-Integrated Microprocessors
authors: T. S. Balaji, Ali Hussein Ali Abu Zaidline, V.G Dharsana Dharani, Sami Anand, N Krishna Chythanya, Jeevitha R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Autonomous embedded systems increasingly leverage real-time perception for safe and efficient operation in dynamic conditions. Perception in robotics, unmanned vehicles, smart sensors, and industrial automation requires neural network-based object recognition. CNN models on embedded devices are difficult to implement because to computational, power, and latency constraints. High-performance CNN designs for cloud or desktop settings rarely meet real-time performance requirements on embedded microprocessors. This project implements autonomous embedded applications with low-latency CNN topologies using AI-integrated microprocessors. For real-time object recognition with minimal latency and energy consumption, the suggested solution optimizes model architecture, computation flow, and hardware use. On-chip accelerators, parallel processing, and hardware-aware model design enable efficient inference without compromising recognition accuracy. Optimized low-latency CNNs outperform conventional models in inference speed and power economy while retaining robust object detection. Co-designing embedded intelligence algorithms and hardware is essential, and this study proposes a resource-constrained autonomous system paradigm for real-time vision.

title: Enhancing Team Improvisation Through Boundary Spanners' Metaknowledge: Evidence from IT Project Teams_supp1-3702823.docx
authors: Sanjun Jiang
abstract: Team improvisation is critical for engineering project teams operating under uncertainty, interdependence, and time pressure, yet research has paid limited attention to the cross-team micro-foundations that enable such adaptation. Addressing this gap, we examine how boundary spanners facilitate team improvisation. Drawing on a twowave, multi-source field study of 93 software project teams (N = 496) within a large, project-based technology enterprise in China, we develop and test a dual-path model in which boundary spanners' metaknowledge enhances team improvisation through two knowledge integration mechanisms: inter-team knowledge acquisition and intra-team knowledge transfer. We further show that organizational power strengthens both indirect effects by enabling boundary spanners to legitimize coordination, mobilize resources, and accelerate knowledge integration across unit boundaries. A comprehensive set of robustness checks confirms the stability and reliability of these findings. Together, these results identify boundary spanners' metaknowledge as a critical micro-foundation of team improvisation in complex project environments.

title: Cognitive AI in Managerial Decision-Making: Redefining Leadership and Control in Data-Driven Organizations
authors: Saed Adnan Mustafa, Sakher M.A Alnajdawi, Mahmoud Alghizzawi, Malek Ali Alnajdawi, Hazem ALKasassbeh Marketing
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: The breakneck pace of artificial intelligence development has changed the aspect of managerial decision-making to cognitively augmented intelligence. This paper discusses the transformation of cognitive artificial intelligence in managerial decision-making by improving leadership and organizational control in data-driven organizations. The research is based on human-AI collaborative and intelligent system viewpoints and formulates a conceptual framework to be empirically tested with the help of the Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The results show that AI capability of cognition is a great benefit to the quality of decisions in two main ways, i.e. better human-AI interaction and reinforcement of the organizational control systems. The human-AI collaboration also emerges to be a key mediating factor as it allows leaders to fuse algorithmic information with experience analysis. The findings also show that successful leadership and governance are also required to achieve the strategic worth of cognitive AI. This paper adds to the growing body of writing on AI-based management by establishing cognitive AI as more of a decision-enhancing tool than a replacement of managerial authority, which can provide more practical advice on the responsible use of AI in the organization.

title: Magnetic Parametric Optimization of an Electromagnetic Harvester Tuned to In-Situ Stochastic Railway Vibrations
authors: Muhammad Mansattha, Roger Dixon, Edward Stewart
abstract: Conventional electromagnetic energy harvesters often fail in industrial environments because they are designed for idealized sine-wave vibrations that rarely occur in operation. This paper presents the magnetic design and optimization of an Electromagnetic Vibration Energy Harvester (EVEH) tuned specifically to field-measured vibration patterns due to railway operation. Unlike standard approaches, this study integrates in-situ MEMS accelerometer data identifying a dominant 91 Hz resonance directly into the simulation workflow.
