A Design of Experiments (DoE) framework was executed in Ansys Maxwell to model the non-linear magnetic flux distribution and maximize the flux linkage gradient within the coil-magnet assembly. The analysis correlates critical geometric parameters, such as magnet volume and coil aspect ratio, with the harvester’s electromechanical damping and power density. The resulting optimized magnetic circuit yields a predicted average power of 1.40 mW, which is sufficient to drive wireless Condition Monitoring Systems.

title: Canonical-Volterra-Based Model Generation for Digital Predistortion of Wideband PAs
authors: Juan A. Becerra, Kevin S. Chuang, Elías Marqués-Valderrama, Liam Platt, Miguel Nogales, María J. Madero-Ayora
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: High-efficiency power amplifiers (PAs) exhibit pronounced nonlinear and memory effects when driven by wideband signals. Classical Volterra-based behavioral models, including generalized memory polynomial (GMP) and dynamic deviation reduction (DDR) structures, tackle the combinatorial growth of the Volterra series by imposing predefined structural constraints. However, these fixed model forms often fail to capture hardware-specific impairments, while unconstrained Volterra expansions remain computationally prohibitive. This work introduces the genetic-based Volterra-subspace generator (GVG), a novel framework that shifts the paradigm from coefficient selection within predefined models to data-driven structure discovery. The proposed approach relies on a canonical Volterra formulation in which regressors are decomposed into delayed input, conjugate, and envelope components. A genetic algorithm is employed to systematically explore the search space, evolving candidate regressors through crossover and mutation operators. The fitness of each regressor is evaluated using doubly orthogonal matching pursuit (DOMP), ensuring sparsity and numerical robustness. Unlike conventional approaches, the GVG does not impose a priori structural constraints on the model. Instead, it autonomously discovers complex cross-terms and novel basis functions tailored to the device under test, including those required to compensate for I/Q impairments. Experimental validation is conducted on three scenarios: a Doherty PA with I/Q impairments, a high-efficiency integrated PA module (PAM), and a high-power multiband (MB) PA, demonstrating that the proposed GVG technique achieves superior performance across all cases. These results demonstrate the capability of the proposed framework to identify the most relevant features without prior knowledge of the system.

title: Blockchain based Financial System for Jewel Loan Disbursal and Bond Issuance
authors: Sudarsanam, C. Ashokkumar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Gold-backed lending has been considered a secured form of lending by many banks and financial institutions. When compared with housing loans, the recovery of the loan amount is easier for jewel loans. Hence, many financial institutions prefer this type of secured loan. However, traditional lending practices rely on centralized databases, which limits transparency. Another disadvantage is that the disbursed loan amount could not be accessed until the borrower repays the loan in full, which is usually at the end of the loan tenure. This imposes a liquidity constraint for many financial institutions. This paper proposes a blockchain-based financial system for managing gold-backed loans and the reuse of the disbursed loan amount by issuing bonds. Smart contracts automate the process of loan origination, bond issuance, and capital inflows. The financial system is evaluated using two performance metrics, such as gas consumption per operation and transaction latency under increased system load. Obtained results show that the gas consumption was not abnormal. Loan approval consumed gas fees at an average of 90,000 units, while bond purchases consumed 65,000 units. When the number of concurrent transactions was increased, the transaction latency also increased from 1.2s to 4.6s.

title: No Eigenvalues Outside the Limiting Support of Generally Correlated and Noncentral Sample Covariance Matrices
authors: Zeyan Zhuang, Xin Zhang, Dongfang Xu, Shenghui Song
abstract: Spectral properties of random matrices play an important role in statistics, machine learning, communications, and many other areas. Engaging results regarding the convergence of the empirical spectral distribution (ESD) and the “no-eigenvalue” property have been obtained for random matrices with different correlation structures. However, the related spectral analysis for generally correlated and noncentral random matrices is still incomplete, and this paper aims to fill this research gap. Specifically, we consider matrices whose columns are independent but with non-zero means and non-identical correlations. Under high-dimensional asymptotics where both the number of rows and columns grow simultaneously to infinity, we first establish the almost sure convergence of the ESD for the concerned random matrices to a deterministic limit, assuming mild conditions. Furthermore, we prove that with probability 1, no eigenvalues will appear in any closed interval outside the support of the limiting distribution for matrices with sufficiently large dimensions. The above results can be applied to different areas such as statistics, wireless communications, and signal processing. In this paper, we apply the derived results to two communication scenarios: 1) We determine the limiting performance of the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio for multi-user multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems with linear minimum mean-square error receivers; and 2) We establish the invertibility of zero-forcing precoding matrices in downlink MIMO systems, providing theoretical guarantees.

title: Non-contact Cross Domain Fault Diagnosis via Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data Fusion and Global Imbalance Awareness
authors: Yanrun Zhou, Guangrui Wen, Zihao Lei, Yu Su, Zhenyi Chen, Qing Ni, Yongbo Li, Ke Feng
abstract: Gas storage facilities have long faced bottlenecks in the core control systems of high-power compressors, where operational safety, efficiency, and automation under complex conditions remain key challenges. Traditional single-sensor monitoring offers limited perception and low data utilization, falling short of ensuring reliable operation and intelligent decision-making. With AI engineering advancing toward multi modal and heterogeneous industrial applications, research has increasingly focused on multi-sensor fusion and intelligent diagnostics. Although multi-sensor networks provide complementary information and enhanced perception, issues like data heterogeneity, class imbalance, and cross-domain distribution differences continue to constrain diagnostic performance. To address these issues, a Dual-branch Heterogeneous Synergistic Network (DHSNet) is proposed to achieve cross-modal fusion of vibration signals and infrared thermal imaging signals. The framework incorporates a cross domain adaptation strategy to enhance domain-invariant feature learning and a dynamic focal loss function to adaptively adjust class weights based on real-time output indicators, mitigating the impact of sample imbalance. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an average diagnostic accuracy of 93.27% in cross-load transfer tasks, outperforming other methods used in imbalanced scenarios by over 4%. Besides, the proposed method maintains robust diagnostic performance exceeding 90% accuracy across most load transfer scenarios, even under moderately imbalanced data conditions. Feature contribution analysis further validates the effectiveness of multi modal synergy, and revealing the complementary mechanism of multi-source data. This study enhances Prognostics and Health Management (PHM) systems' engineering applicability and perception capabilities in multi-sensor industrial environments, providing reliable multi-modal diagnostics to advance intelligent maintenance.

title: Advanced Task Intelligence for Automated Employee Performance Assessment
authors: Kanagamalliga S, Srigitha S Nath, Winaayag Amit J, Ramya J
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV)
abstract: Employee performance evaluation plays a crucial role in improving organizational productivity and decision making. Traditional evaluation methods are often affected by human bias, delayed feedback, and limited scalability, which reduce fairness and efficiency. With the growing complexity of workplace tasks, the need for intelligent and automated evaluation mechanisms has become increasingly significant. In this study, advanced task intelligence concepts are explored to support automated employee evaluation in modern organizational environments. Performance metrics such as task completion time, accuracy, consistency, and workload handling capability are emphasized to address existing evaluation limitations. Data driven analysis is leveraged to ensure objective assessment and real time feedback generation. Automation is utilized to minimize manual intervention and improve consistency across evaluations. Challenges related to data quality, adaptability to diverse tasks, system scalability, and trust in automated decisions are highlighted. Ethical considerations such as transparency, accountability, and reduction of bias are also considered essential factors in intelligent evaluation systems. The study aims to demonstrate how intelligent task analysis can enhance evaluation reliability while reducing managerial workload. The adoption of task intelligence is shown to be a promising direction for achieving efficient, accurate, and scalable employee performance evaluation in dynamic work environments.

title: A Multi-Scale Monetary Precision Framework for Distributed Banking Systems
authors: Bhaskar Manchuri
publish: 2026 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems (ICETSIS)
abstract: Modern banking systems simultaneously maintain monetary values across computation (8-10 decimal places), ledger posting (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}-\mathbf{8}$</tex> decimal places), and customer presentation (2 decimal places) scales. In distributed microservice and event-driven architectures, unmanaged transitions between these scales introduce rounding drift, ledger divergence, reconciliation breaks, and non-deterministic replay risks that threaten audit compliance and regulatory reporting accuracy. This paper investigates how distributed banking systems can manage multi-scale monetary precision while preserving deterministic balances and auditability. The proposed Multi-Scale Monetary Precision Model (MSMP) defines three scale classes, three explicit precision boundaries, and four correctness invariants governing deterministic posting, ledger conservation, presentation consistency, and fractional carry forward. A taxonomy of three canonical failure modes, namely early rounding, boundary truncation, and nondeterministic aggregation, is presented together with four architectural patterns designed to ensure precision-safe monetary propagation. Evaluation on a synthetic bankingrealistic workload (10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> accounts over 365 days) demonstrates that MSMP reduces cumulative monetary drift by up to 99.9 %, eliminates reconciliation breaks entirely, and achieves 100 % replay determinism, with computational overhead of approximately 18 %. These results establish monetary precision governance as a first-class architectural control for audit-ready distributed financial systems.

title: A Self-Aligning Hip Exoskeleton for Lateral Walking Assistance
authors: Peihua Li, Xinbo Zhu, Meng Yin, Changyu Li, Xinyu Wu, Wujing Cao
abstract: Hip exoskeletons have been widely investigated for human motion assistance; however, most existing systems primarily target sagittal-plane motions during forward walking, while frontal-plane hip abduction/adduction (HA/A) assistance remains relatively underexplored. Lateral walking, which is essential for balance maintenance and many daily activities, exhibits gait characteristics fundamentally different from forward walking and therefore imposes distinct requirements on exoskeleton design and control. This article presents a hip exoskeleton specifically designed for lateral walking assistance, with a focus on HA/A motion and human–robot misalignment. The system incorporates a self-aligning sliding mechanism to accommodate kinematic misalignment, along with a structural gravity compensation mechanism intended to reduce the gravitational load on the HA/A actuators. A phase-dependent control strategy is implemented to deliver assistive torque while ensuring zero output torque during double-support phases. Experimental results demonstrate stable lateral walking assistance and show that the proposed exoskeleton reduces normalized muscle activation by an average of 23.17% compared with normal walking and 28.71% compared with a passive exoskeleton mode, despite the added device mass. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed task-specific design for lateral walking and support its potential for frontal-plane gait assistance and rehabilitation.

title: FedAdapt: Unsupervised Federated Domain Adaptation for Cross-Satellite Hyperspectral Image Classification_supp1-3702427.pdf
authors: Maoguo Gong
abstract: Multi-satellite hyperspectral image classification is increasingly expected to be performed on-orbit to reduce downlink and annotation costs. However, existing unsupervised domain adaptation methods typically rely on the closed-set assumption that source and target domains share the same label space, which is often violated in real multi-satellite deployments due to temporal land-cover changes and sensor upgrades. When the target domain contains previously unseen classes, directly aligning the two domains may induce negative transfer, making it difficult to learn a reliable decision boundary between known and unknown categories, especially under privacy constraints that prohibit raw data sharing. To address these challenges, we propose FedAdapt, an open-set unsupervised federated domain adaptation framework for cross-satellite hyperspectral image classification. FedAdapt first constructs class prototype vectors to induce a discriminative metric space for known classes and estimates an instance-level reliability weight for each target sample by its similarity to the prototypes. Based on this reliability, a relevance-driven weighting strategy is introduced to perform cautious adversarial domain alignment, emphasizing likely-known target samples while suppressing potential unknowns to mitigate negative transfer. In addition, an energy-based dynamic thresholding scheme is learned to adaptively separate known and unknown target samples without manual threshold tuning. Experimental results on multiple cross-scene HSI classification tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of our method.

title: From Felt Experience to Robotic Design: Embodied Techniques for Expressive Movement and Shape
authors: Chang Shu, Joseph Whitmore, Zhining Jiang, Cheng Chang, Diana Alina Serbanescu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 Designing Interactive Systems Conference

authors: Alejandra Gómez Ortega, Joo Young Park, Anna Ståhl, Marianela Ciolfi Felice, Nadia Campo Woytuk, Shradha Shivaji Retharekar, Airi Lampinen, Madeline Balaam

authors: Ricarose Roque, Jaleesa Trapp, Alexis Hope

title: Training Neural Networks at Any Scale: An exposition
authors: Thomas Pethick, Kimon Antonakopoulos, Antonio Silveti-Falls, Leena Chennuru Vankadara, Volkan Cevher
abstract: This article reviews modern optimization methods for training neural networks (NNs) with an emphasis on efficiency and scale. We present state-of-the-art optimization algorithms under a unified algorithmic template that highlights the importance of adapting to the structures in the problem. We then cover how to make these algorithms agnostic to the scale of the problem. Our exposition is intended as an introduction for both practitioners and researchers who wish to be involved in these exciting new developments.

title: Guest Editorial: Introduction to the Special Section on Near-Field Antenna Systems for Communications and Sensing
authors: Manuel Arrebola, Sérgio Matos, Álvaro F. Vaquero, Mauro Ettorre
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation

title: Unveiling Hidden Convexity in Deep Learning: A sparse signal processing perspective [Special Issue on the Mathematics of Deep Learning]
authors: Emi Zeger, Mert Pilanci
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved groundbreaking success in various fields. Yet, the inherent nonconvexity of their training objectives and the complexity of their architectures present challenges for optimization and theoretical understanding. Our article examines the hidden convex structure within DNNs by drawing upon frameworks from sparse signal processing, specifically least absolute shrinkage and selection operator (LASSO), group LASSO, and nuclear-norm regularized models. By leveraging these established techniques from sparse linear models, we demonstrate how convex formulations can yield global optima and facilitate the interpretability of deep networks. Furthermore, we explore convolutional neural networks (CNNs), transformers, diffusion models, and generative adversarial networks from this convex regularization framework. We show how hypercomplex structures, such as geometric algebra, emerge in this analysis, offering new insights into the generalization to unseen data. We aim to engage the signal processing community in exploring these convex perspectives to better understand deep learning and its practical applications.

title: Compact Radiator Shared MIMO Array of Port and Radiation Pattern Decoupling Magnetoelectric Dipoles
authors: Guang-Yao Liu, Kwok Wa Leung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a port-decoupled and radiation-pattern-decoupled (RPD) technique for designs of multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) magnetoelectric-dipole (ME-dipole) arrays. The ME-dipole elements share the patches with each other to obtain a compact design, with the element spacing given by 0.43 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub>. In general, each array element has excited and coupled ME-dipole modes. The proposed design suppresses the coupled ME-dipole mode, leaving only the excited ME-dipole mode. As a result, the RPD characteristics can be obtained for each array element. For demonstration, two 1×3 and one 2×2 MIMO arrays operating in the 10-GHz band are designed, fabricated, and measured. Measured results show that the overlapping -10 dB impedance bandwidth of the elements covers a new 6G frequency range from 10.0 GHz to 10.5 GHz (4.9% bandwidth). Reasonable agreement between the measured and simulated results is observed.

title: Study on the Peeling Mechanism of Aged Layer Removal from Silicone Rubber Sheds Using Pulsed Laser
authors: Yulin Wang, Yuyao Hu, Xingliang Jiang, Xuening Xing, Dongdong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Silicone rubber possesses excellent anti-pollution flashover performance and insulation properties, making it widely used as a shed material for composite insulators. With increasing service time, silicone rubber materials gradually degrade under the influence of adverse factors such as ultraviolet radiation and salt spray, leading to the formation of an aged layer on the surface. To remove the aged layer from the silicone rubber surface without damaging the substrate, this study elucidates the peeling mechanism of pulsed laser on silicone rubber through simulation and experimental validation. The peeling process of silicone rubber by pulsed laser is accomplished through the combined effects of thermal ablation and stress field. In the initial stage of laser action, the surface temperature of the silicone rubber rises sharply, dominated by the thermal ablation mechanism. In the middle and later stages, the surface temperature stabilizes, while the stress field gradually propagates into the depth of the silicone rubber and increases in magnitude, at this point, the process is governed by the combined action of thermal ablation and the stress field. Both laser fluence and the number of scans are positively correlated with the peeling depth. After five scans, the contact angle on the aged layer surface of the silicone rubber reaches 142°–156°, the atomic percent ratio of Si and C elements increase by 2.1%–3.4% and 5.1%–10.9%, respectively, while the O element content decreases by 7.1%–14.2%.

title: Building Intelligent ECG
authors: Leslie Mertz
abstract: New technology by HeartSciences Inc. is using signal processing and a cloud platform driven by artificial intelligence to gather frequency and energy data in electrocardiogram signals; retrieve information beyond visible in a standard ECG; and identify early signs of heart disease in an easy-to-read format. The technology includes the MyoVista Insights platform and the MyoVista wavECG device.

title: Transient Voltage Stability Analysis and Enhancement of Grid-Following Converters Considering DC-link Voltage Control Dynamics
authors: Yongpeng Shan, Xinyu Liu, Huanhai Xin, Di Zheng
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy resources through grid-following converters, the voltage support capability of the system is weakened, thereby increasing the risk of voltage instability. Existing studies mainly focus on the transient voltage stability of induction motors (IMs), where the dynamics of voltage source converters (VSCs) are often neglected. However, VSCs with DC-link voltage control (DVC) also face the risk of transient voltage instability under grid faults, even when the voltage-supporting effect of VSCs during low-voltage ride through (LVRT) is considered. Such an instability phenomenon poses a tremendous threat to the stable operation of VSC-dominated power systems, which has not been addressed in existing research. To fill this gap, this paper provides a theoretical explanation of the VSC's transient performance under DVC and LVRT, and reveals the transient voltage instability mechanism of modern power systems with high penetration of VSCs. To mitigate such instability, this paper proposes an adaptive current-limiting control strategy after analyzing the impact of current limitation on transient voltage stability. Simulation results verify the correctness of the theoretical analysis and the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: High-Efficiency Multi-MHz Class E Push-Pull Converter with Resonance-Tuned Series-Connected Architecture for 13.56 MHz Industrial Applications
authors: Hassan Mujtaba, Stefan Liccardi, Faheem Ahmad, Jannick Kjær Jørgensen, Stig Munk-Nielsen
abstract: High-frequency resonant inverters operating in the 13.56 MHz ISM band are attractive for industrial RF power systems because of their high power density. At such frequencies, repeatable Class-E operation is limited by the nonlinear GaN output capacitance and low reflected load resistance under weak transformer coupling, which increases the effective loaded system quality factor, whereas transformer leakage and layout parasitics further degrade the soft-switching margin. This paper presents a safety-factor-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula>-based, resonance-tuned, series-connected Class-E push–pull architecture for 13.56 MHz operation. The main contribution is an interface-design criterion for preserving ZVS by shaping the drain-source termination such that the drain-network quality factor remains moderately above the loaded system quality factor. Based on this principle, two interface-compensation realizations are developed for high-quality-factor systems. An impedance-based design methodology is established using measurements, LTspice time-domain simulations, and MATLAB linearized transfer-function (pole–zero) analysis. Sensitivity studies quantify the effects of transformer coupling and parasitic resistance on the efficiency and system quality factor. Experimental prototypes demonstrate scalable voltage stacking and consistent soft switching: a five-cell implementation delivers 1 kW from a 21.2 V supply with ><inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$90\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> efficiency, and a four-cell implementation delivers approximately 3 kW from a 42.5 V DC bus with ><inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$92\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> efficiency.

title: Haptic Tracing: A New Paradigm for Spatialized Vibrotactile Haptic Rendering
authors: Tom Roy, Yann Glemarec, Gurvan Lecuyer, Quentin Galvane, Philippe Guillotel, Ferran Argelaguet
abstract: Haptic technology enhances interactive experiences by providing force and tactile feedback, improving user performance and immersion. However, despite advancements, creating tactile experiences still remains challenging due to device diversity and complexity. Most available haptic frameworks rely on trigger-based or event-based systems, and disregard the 3D scene information to render haptic information. This paper introduces Haptic Tracing, a novel method for spatial tactile haptic rendering that simplifies the creation of interactive haptic experiences without relying on physical simulations. It uses concepts from visual and audio rendering to model and propagate haptic information through a 3D scene. The paper also describes how our proposed haptic rendering method can be used to create a vibrotactile rendering system, enabling the creation of perceptually coherent and dynamic haptic interactions. Finally, the paper discusses a user study comparing haptic rendering techniques that combine different propagation strategies and modulation functions. The results show that our approach significantly enhances the realism and expressivity of the haptic feedback, showcasing its potential for developing more complex and realistic haptic experiences.

title: Not All Encoder Layers Are Essential: Shallow-Feature Fusion for Infrared Small Target Detection
authors: Qiang Li, Jiangbin Zheng, Wenbin Zou, Yong Zhao, Bingshu Wang, Han Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep learning has revolutionized infrared small target detection. However, prevailing encoder-decoder architectures, which maintain symmetrical depths, incur unnecessary computational cost. We challenge this design with a key insight: for this task, deep encoder layers are largely dispensable in the decoder feature fusion process, as shallow features alone can achieve an optimal accuracy-efficiency balance. Driven by this insight, we propose TADNet, a Target-Aware Dual-branch Network, to translate this insight into an efficient and accurate model. Its core components incorporate: (1) an effective dual-branch encoder that collaboratively extracts high-resolution spatial details and rich semantic contexts, and (2) an efficient dynamic fusion decoder that strategically utilizes only the first two shallowest features— pruning the traditional fusion backbone. Beyond the model, we introduce the NPU-SIRST benchmark to address multi-scale and multi-scenario limitations. Rigorous experiments on four datasets show that our method achieves state-of-the-art results with significantly enhanced efficiency, demonstrating robust transferability under infrared cross-dataset testing. The code is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/searchfresh/TADNet</uri>.

title: Multimodal Domain Generalization for Inter-Turn Short-Circuit Fault Diagnosis of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors
authors: He Bing, Shi Jin, Guanglong Jia, Ming Jing, Fuqi Li
abstract: The current characteristics of inter-turn short-circuit (ITSC) faults in permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) typically exhibit continuous evolution characteristics with changes in operating conditions. However, industrial data acquisition usually only covers limited and discrete operating conditions, making it difficult to fully characterize fault evolution information across operating conditions. To address this issue, this article proposes a multimodal domain generalization diagnostic framework. The proposed framework constructs feature representations from three physical perspectives, namely the time domain, frequency domain, and time–frequency domain, based on the same stator current signal. By introducing an operating-condition evolution prior, the proposed framework extends conventional modeling approaches that treat operating conditions as discrete domains to a continuous evolution representation, thereby recovering the progressive trajectories of fault features in the latent space. In addition, semantic-guided multimodal feature fusion and a hierarchical domain alignment mechanism are jointly employed to mitigate modality heterogeneity and cross-condition distribution shifts. Experimental results on both private and public datasets demonstrate that the proposed method maintains stable and accurate diagnostic performance under unseen operating conditions, validating its effectiveness for cross-condition ITSC fault diagnosis in PMSM.

title: Towards Cross-Satellite Change Detection: A Spatial-Spectral Dual-Alignment Joint Learning Network
authors: Kai Zhang, Jian Xiao, Feng Zhang, Jiande Sun, Lorenzo Bruzzone
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Despite boosting change detection performance, most deep neural networks (DNNs) are designed for multi-temporal remote sensing images acquired by the same satellite. However, it is difficult for a single satellite to acquire continuous data for high-frequency change detection over a large area. Although it is feasible to conduct CD on multi-temporal remote sensing images from different satellites, differences in spectral response and spatial resolution cannot be ignored. To mitigate these issues, we propose a spatial-spectral dual-alignment joint learning network (SSDA-Net). The proposed framework introduces a dual-resolution joint learning strategy that exploits LR features from the SR network to guide HR feature alignment in the CD network, thereby suppressing spectral inconsistencies caused by different sensor responses and spatial mismatches induced by resolution differences between satellites. Specifically, for multi-temporal inputs with different resolutions, we first send the LR one into the SR network to improve its spatial resolution, and then the super-resolved result and the HR one are fed into the CD network to extract HR features. To exploit the spatial and spectral information in both LR and HR features, we design dual-alignment learning modules (DALMs), which are composed of a spectral alignment block and a spatial alignment block. Through the two modules, the spectral and spatial differences between multi-temporal images from different satellites are suppressed. Finally, features from DALMs are used to generate the change map. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed SSDA-Net, we construct a real cross-satellite change detection (SD-CSCD) dataset. Experimental results on the newly-built SD-CSCD dataset, the DE-CD dataset, and a synthesized dataset demonstrate that our SSDA-Net achieves better performance than state-of-the-art methods.The SD-CSCD dataset and the code of SSDA-Net are available at https://github.com/XxxJ0909/SSDA-Net.

title: AI-PHIXR: AI-Powered Photonics-based Interference-Resilience Next Generation Robust Radar
authors: Bikash Nakarmi, Bikash Nakarmi, Bikash Nakarmi, Bikash Nakarmi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The widespread deployment of radar systems across autonomous navigation, aerospace, defense, and consumer electronics has resulted in severe spectral congestion, making electromagnetic interference the fundamental bottleneck limiting radar detection accuracy, range resolution, and operational reliability. Conventional electronic-domain interference mitigation solutions are constrained by inherent bandwidth limits, high computational overhead, and poor adaptability to dynamic and complex interference environments. Microwave photonics, with its intrinsic advantages of ultra-wide bandwidth, electromagnetic interference immunity, and low transmission loss, has emerged as a promising pathway to overcome these limitations. In this keynote, we present AI-PHIXR, a comprehensive framework developed through our long-term research efforts in microwave photonics technologies with a focus on photonics radar to provide safe, secure and robust (S2R) radar environment. In this talk, we first outline our foundational contributions to photonics radar system including the expansion of single-mode Fabry-Perot laser diodes (SMFP-LD) for microwave photonic applications and the development of interference-robust reconfigurable radar waveform advanced optical injection locking. We will then present our comprehensive photonic radar framework, which combines a photonics and AI to ensure safe secure and efficient in dynamic environment through reconfigurable photonic waveform generation platform with hardware-aligned adaptive processing workflows, enabling dynamic waveform adaptation, signal mismatch correction, and high-fidelity signal recovery in complex spectral environments. By bridging the MWP and AI, AI-PHIXR establishes the scalable pathways towards S2R radar operation in safety-critical scenarios. The keynote concludes with a discussion on practical deployment opportunities and future research directions including the hardware and AI optimization, edge deployable photonics intelligent and resilient radar for nest generations.

title: Hybrid Precoding Revisited: Low-Dimensional Subspace Perspective for MU-MIMO Systems
authors: Mintaek Oh, Jinseok Choi
abstract: This paper presents a low-complexity hybrid precoding framework for multiuser multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems by leveraging a low-dimensional subspace property. Under the low-dimensional subspace perspective, we first identify an unconstrained optimal radio-frequency (RF) precoder. We then optimize a digital precoder via a reduced-complexity precoding method. We further extend the proposed framework to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(i)$</tex-math></inline-formula> a dynamic-subarray antenna partitioning algorithm that adaptively allocates subsets of antennas associated with RF chains, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(ii)$</tex-math></inline-formula> a channel covariance-based approach to exploit statistical channel state information at a transmitter (CSIT), ensuring robustness with partial CSIT. Simulations validate that our proposed algorithms achieve superior performance while significantly reducing complexity compared to existing methods.

title: Power Prediction and Anomaly Detection Using Statistical Thresholds and Satellite-Based Solar Data in Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Michelle M Cavalcante, João Lucas de Souza Silva, João Frederico Souza de Paula, Juliana de Souza Granja Barros, Tárcio A. S. Barros
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society
abstract: The increasing scale and complexity of photovoltaic (PV) power plants require reliable and cost-effective monitoring strategies capable of identifying operational anomalies under diverse climatic conditions. However, a challenge in this context lies in the strong dependence of many existing approaches on high-quality local solar irradiance measurements, which are often unavailable, incomplete, or economically unfeasible in real-world PV installations. This paper proposes an integrated methodology for PV power prediction and anomaly detection using satellite-based solar data combined with statistical thresholding techniques. Satellite-derived meteorological datasets are used to train supervised regression models to estimate PV power, which is subsequently employed as an external and physically consistent reference for anomaly detection. Monthly characteristic curves are constructed from the predicted power using statistical measures, enabling the definition of adaptive operating thresholds that account for seasonal and intraday variability. Anomalies are identified based on both statistical deviations from these dynamic limits and physically inconsistent behaviors between irradiance and measured power, such as power drops under increasing irradiance conditions. The methodology is evaluated using two independent satellite datasets with different temporal and spatial characteristics, allowing a comparative analysis of linear and tree-based machine learning models in terms of prediction accuracy, robustness, and explainability. In this way, results demonstrate that satellite-based power prediction provides a stable and scalable baseline for anomaly detection, particularly suitable for PV plants with limited instrumentation or without local irradiance sensors. The proposed approach effectively captures seasonal patterns of operational stress and distinguishes different types of anomalous behavior, offering a practical and interpretable solution for large-scale PV monitoring and performance assessment.

title: Deep Learning–based Glaucoma Screening using Smartphone-Captured Low-Quality Fundus Videos
authors: Solomon Gebru Abay, Melkamu Hunegnaw Asmare, Julie Jacob, Lucca Geurts
abstract: This study investigated the feasibility of deep learning–based glaucoma screening using fundus videos acquired with an ultra-low-cost, smartphone-based imaging system designed for resource-limited settings. Fundus videos were collected during routine clinical examinations using the glaucoma screening-on-phone (GSoP) adapter. A red-channel intensity-based strategy was employed to select diagnostically relevant frames from videos. Optic disc (OD) localizationwas performed using a fine-tunedYOLOv8n object detector, followed by OD-centered cropping and preprocessing. Glaucoma classification was conducted using MobileNetV3-Small model, fine-tuned via five-fold stratified group cross-validation. Performancewas evaluated using accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC), with additional testing on independent dataset. Comparative and ablation analyses assessed architectural choices and OD localization impact. Despite GSoP’s limited optical quality, the model achieved strong performance. On the independent test set, the single-frame MobileNetV3-Small reached an AUC of 0.91, with 93% sensitivity and 88% specificity. This semi-automated approach outperformed a fully automated multi-frame strategy (0.90 vs. 0.81 accuracy). Ablation analysis demonstrated that OD localization improved sensitivity and specificity by 13.3% and 9.5%, respectively, on MobileNetV3-Small model. The lightweight model performed comparably to deeper networks with significantly lower computational complexity. These findings indicate that clinically meaningful glaucoma-related features can be extracted from low-quality fundus images acquired with ultra-low-cost smartphone hardware, with semi-manual single-frame selection currently proving more effective than fully automated multi-frame-based prediction. This work serves as an encouraging proof-of-concept for combining affordable imaging systems with efficient deep learning pipelines to achieve scalable glaucoma screening in underserved settings.

title: H-VSJ: A Hybrid Stiff-Compliant Variable Stiffness Joint With Decoupling Position and Stiffness Modulation_supp1-3701570.mp4
authors: Lei Yan
abstract: The wide spectrum of robotic applications motivates the intrinsic stiffness modulation of the robot besides compliance control, especially for highly dynamic manipulation. Variable Stiffness Actuators (VSAs) are designed for improving impact resistance, stiffness adaptability, and dynamic performance, while many existing variable stiffness mechanisms still face coupling between position and stiffness modulation. This paper presents a Hybrid Stiff-Compliant Variable Stiffness Joint (H-VSJ) that employs a decoupling mechanism to achieve independent control of joint position and stiffness as well as hybrid stiff-compliant modes. By adjusting the force-transmission ratio between the elastic element and the output link along the joint axis, H-VSJ achieves the decoupled and accurate stiffness modulation. The decoupling mechanism also allows the joint to smoothly switch between the stiff mode and compliant mode. The stiffness model of H-VSJ is established, and the influence of key design parameters on stiffness is further analyzed. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed H-VSJ is validated through experiments on buffering motion range, stiffness-position decoupling, stiffness analysis, dynamic stiffness modulation, and impact-buffering capability, which demonstrate H-VSJ has a maximum deflection of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$30.20^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the low-stiffness state, an average stiffness modulation error of 7.00%, a stiffness range of 23.57–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$260.36~\mathrm{N\ m/rad}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the compliant mode, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8630.25~\mathrm{N\ m/rad}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the stiff mode.

title: Neuromorphic Hyperdimensional Computing for Efficiently Processing Event-Based Data
authors: Tianyang Yu, Bi Wu, Ke Chen, Chenggang Yan, Gong Zhang, Weiqiang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The neuromorphic sensor’s event-based data output offers significant benefits, including minimal data redundancy and exceptional time resolution, which guarantee low power consumption and heightened sensitivity during the data acquisition process. Spiking neural network (SNN), with its inherent event-driven characteristic, is well-suited for processing event-based data, and its spike-based computing mechanism enhances the efficiency of data processing. Recent studies are exploring the integration of brain-inspired hyperdimensional computing (HDC) with SNN to leverage HDC’s advantages, including the low inference and training complexity, aiming to further reduce hardware overhead associated with SNN deployment. However, existing works have not effectively harnessed the information output by SNN during hyperdimensional encoding, leading to considerable area and energy overhead. In this article, an efficient neuromorphic HDC method is proposed, featuring a simplified hyperdimensional encoding approach that considers the temporal dynamics of SNN. In addition, a lightweight accelerator design matching the proposed method is also given. Experimental results show that the proposed accelerator achieves over 50% area reduction and reduces energy consumption by 20%–90%.

title: Daily Physical Activity Recognition and Energy Consumption Estimation based on Smartphone IMU Sensors and Lightweight CNN
authors: Ge Dai
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Daily physical activity recognition and energy consumption estimation are hotspots in sports health, yet wearable-based solutions suffer from high costs and poor portability. Existing IMU-based models using traditional ML algorithms underperform in complex taekwondo movements, while deep CNNs face computational challenges on mobile devices. We built an IMU dataset, designed MobileActiveNet with depthwise separable convolutions and channel attention, and integrated action recognition with physiological parameters for energy estimation. Lite ActCNN reduced parameters to 39.5% of MobileNetV2, achieving real-time mobile performance. Conclusion: Our approach outperforms heart rate-based methods in accuracy and efficiency.

title: Two-Stage Noise-Reduced Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Ray Images using Denoising Autoencoder–CNN Integration
authors: Md Abdul Mutalib, Kopil Das, Mohammad Hossain, Shahriar Hussain, Avijit Deb Nath, Md Mahmud Zaman, Md. Alamgir Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Pneumonia remains a major cause of morbidity and mortality worldwide, with chest X-ray imaging serving as a primary tool for early diagnosis. However, the presence of noise, artifacts, and low-quality acquisitions significantly degrades image interpretability and limits the effectiveness of automated deep learning–based detection systems. Conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are particularly sensitive to such degradations, leading to reduced classification reliability. To address this limitation, we propose a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates a denoising autoencoder (DAE) with a CNN for robust pneumonia detection from chest X-ray images. The DAE is trained to reconstruct clean images from noisy inputs, enabling the suppression of noise while preserving diagnostically relevant features, and is subsequently coupled with a CNN classifier in a unified pipeline. The proposed model is evaluated on the publicly available Kaggle chest X-ray dataset using data augmentation, class-weighted loss, and early stopping to mitigate class imbalance and overfitting. Experimental results demonstrate that the DAE-CNN framework consistently outperforms a CNN-only baseline, achieving an accuracy of 92.9%, an F1-score of 94.6%, and a balanced accuracy of 90.9%, compared to 86.1% accuracy and an F1-score of 89.8% obtained without denoising. These results highlight the effectiveness of incorporating learnable denoising into deep learning pipelines and underscore its potential for improving the robustness and reliability of automated pneumonia detection in clinical imaging scenarios.

title: TCMF: Tensor-Based Cross-Modality Fusion Network for Multimodal Remote Sensing Object Detection
authors: Ke Liu, Yunqi Zhao, Zhe Zhang
abstract: Multimodal remote sensing object detection benefits from the complementary characteristics of heterogeneous sensors, especially RGB and infrared (IR) imagery. However, effectively exploiting cross-modality relationships remains challenging, since simple feature aggregation may fail to fully capture the structural correlations between different modalities.Building on our previous exploration of tensor decomposition for multimodal fusion, this paper presents a Tensor-based Cross-Modality Fusion (TCMF) network for remote sensing object detection. Different from conventional tensor-based fusion strategies with relatively shallow structures, the proposed method embeds tensor interaction modeling into a deep detection framework and further introduces a cross-attention mechanism for spatially adaptive feature refinement.In the proposed framework, the Tensor Fusion module models channel-wise high-order interactions while preserving spatial layouts, and the Cross Attention module generates modality-aware weights to enhance discriminative regions. Experiments on the DroneVehicle dataset show that TCMF achieves better overall performance than single-modality baselines, traditional early and mid-level fusion strategies, and the MF module in SuperYOLO. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of combining tensor-based interaction modeling with attention-guided feature refinement for multimodal remote sensing object detection.

title: Difference in Overvoltage Stress Behavior between CIS and UIS in Ohmic p-Gate GaN HEMTs
authors: Wataru Saito, Shin-Ichi Nishizawa
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: The behavior under overvoltage stress was compared between clamped inductive switching (CIS) and unclamped inductive switching (UIS) in Ohmic p-Gate GaN-HEMTs. In both cases, failure causes hole current to flow from the drain to the substrate. However, the changes in drain voltage at failure, peak voltage shift under repeated stress, and drainsource capacitance shift showed opposite trends. These differences between CIS and UIS are attributed to the distinct effects of electron and hole traps on electric field concentration at the drain electrode edge. However, regardless of the trap polarity, impact ionization at the drain electrode edge destructs the drain–substrate heterostructure, and thus current always flows between them at failure.

title: Advancing AI in Health through Meta Learning for FewShot Disease Prediction on Cross Heterogeneous Biomedical Graphs
authors: Sudipta Kumar Saha, Aftab Mughal, Shujjat Khan, Abdullah All Ahhad, Sanjana Afrose Ritu, Md Khairul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Biomedical datasets are inherently heterogeneous, containing diverse entities such as patients, genes, proteins, and diseases connected through multi-relational structures. Conventional deep learning and graph-based methods often fail to generalize effectively across such domains, especially in low-data or few-shot scenarios. This study introduces a novel framework that integrates meta-learning principles with heterogeneous graph representation learning to achieve transferable and data-efficient disease prediction. The proposed model jointly captures intradomain relationships and cross-domain dependencies using a meta-adaptive optimization strategy, enabling rapid adaptation to new biomedical contexts. Experimental evaluations conducted on five heterogeneous biomedical graphs demonstrate that the model attains an average classification accuracy of 93.8 % and an F1-score of 92.6 %, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines such as CNN, HAN, CGFL, and Meta-GNN. Despite operating on a limited dataset, the framework shows robust generalization and interpretability, making it suitable for few-shot disease prediction and biomedical knowledge transfer. This work provides a significant step toward trustworthy, scalable, and generalizable AI in biomedical graph learning.

title: YOLOv8-Based Deep Learning AI Framework for Real-Time Bird Dropping Detection & Autonomous Solar Panel Cleaning
authors: Kamrul Hasan Emo, Jakariya Sajib, Md. Sabbir Alam, Mohammad Asif Ul Haq, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Environmental contaminants, particularly bird droppings, reduce solar panel efficiency by causing localized soiling and current losses. Manual cleaning is time-consuming and costly, highlighting the need for autonomous solutions. This study presents a real-time bird-dropping detection and automated cleaning system using YOLOv8, an IP camera for image capture, Firebase for cloud monitoring, and an Arduino-controlled cleaning mechanism. A custom dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 8 9}$</tex> labeled solar panel images under diverse lighting and contamination conditions was used to train the YOLOv8 model with transfer learning. The model achieved a mean average precision (mAP@50) of 87.7%, precision of 90.1%, and recall of 82.0%, demonstrating robust real-time detection. Detected contamination triggers autonomous cleaning cycles, forming a closed-loop feedback system that restores panel output to 96% of nominal levels, even under heavy contamination. Energy analysis confirms that recovered energy far exceeds cleaning energy, ensuring high energy efficiency. The mobile app provides real-time monitoring, historical trend visualization, and remote control of cleaning operations, making the system suitable for sustainable, low-maintenance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> installations.

title: Research on Multi-Level Collaborative Decision-Making Mechanism in Power Material Supply Chain
authors: Jiang Qian, Huigang Wu, Junya Zhou, Guoliang Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing complexity and uncertainty of the material supply chains of power necessitate a smart system of coordination to provide resilient operations and performance. The proposed study conducted and tested a multi-level collaborative decision-making mechanism that improves the coordination and performance within hierarchical levels in the power material supply chain. The quantitative simulation-based approach was conducted with Python (Google Colab), where synthetic operational data were created based on three modes of collaboration (none, delayed, and real-time). High-level machine learning algorithms, e.g. XGBoost, Support Vector Classifier (SVC) or Deep Q-Network (DQN), were used to evaluate the predictive accuracy and policy optimisation. The XGBoost model had the lowest RMSE (1.70) and MAE (8.49) with the highest cost prediction accuracy, but the SVC and Gradient Boosting models produced good classification results (AUC = 0.881 and 0.913). Learning through reinforcement also helped decrease the overall cost of operation (841,707 to 835,856), which attests to the benefit of adaptive decision-making. The research concludes that real-time collaboration helps stabilise the service reliability greatly (mean service = 1.0) and reduces stockouts of all SKUs. It advises supply-chain players to introduce data-driven coordination frameworks that consider simulation and AI-based analytics. In practice, the framework makes it possible to proactively control costs and withstand disruption resilience during power supply activities. But the utilisation of artificial data and idealised types of collaboration restricts the externalisation of results.

title: A Bio-Engineered Adaptive Multi-Modal Deep Learning Framework for Real-Time Neural Prosthesis Control
authors: Neelambika B. Hiremath, Vasanthakumar. G U, Vinny Kuriakose, Priya. L, Yathin. C
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Brain-driven prosthetic systems (BPS) have improved the quality of life of individuals with limb loss through their ability to control prosthetic systems via the brain. Traditional BPS Control technologies such as PID and shallow learning methods have very limited adaptability to changes in the neural signals of an individual, low robustness against variability in the neural signals, and no interpretability of the neural signals. To address these challenges, proposed a bioengineered adaptive deep learning framework for neural prosthesis control that integrates multi-modal neural information with explainable intelligence. The proposed methodology consists of six sequential stages: Multi-Modal Neural Bio-Signal Acquisition which captures EEG and sEMG bio-signals. Essential Bio-Signal Preprocessing and Normalization to suppress noise to reduce variability. Further, Deep Feature Extraction from Neural Bio-Signals through modality-specific convolutional networks. Later AttentionBased Multi-Modal Feature Fusion to dynamically weight complementary neural features, Temporal Modeling and Adaptive Learning of Neural Intent using sequential deep learning for personalized intention decoding and Explainable Prosthetic Decision-Making and Actuation Control to ensure transparent and stable movement execution. The proposed framework enhances robustness, adaptability, and clinical trust, enabling accurate, real-time, and user-specific neural prosthetic control. The proposed BAM-DLNP model achieved higher results in terms of accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(92.3 \%)$</tex>, when compared with exiting Motor Imagery.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Deep Ensemble Learning for Enhanced Multi Class Skin Disease Classification
authors: Md Parvez Kabir, Jhion Adbar Opi, Md Tamim Hasan, Md Awal Hadi, Sazzadul Islam, Md Assaduzzaman Assaduzzaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Making accurate diagnoses concerning skin diseases is difficult because of the subjective visual inspections that medical professionals conduct to diagnose them. Though many deep learning models for diagnosing skin diseases exist, many research works still report poor classification results for several skin diseases simultaneously. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes to study an ensemble learning approach by combining several powerful models for improved results through accurate diagnoses. Six of the most powerful models, DenseNet121, VGG16, ResNet18, ResNet50, ViT-B/16, and EfficientNetB3, will first be tested independently for establishing baselines. On the basis of these baselines, two approaches to ensemble learning would be developed. In the first case, the soft voting combination of ViT-B/16 and ResNet50 reports 87.86 % accuracy, with precision of 88.02 % and 87.86 % recall, which is a relative improvement of 2.43 % over the best-performing individual model. In the second case, the integration of the best three models, including ViT-B/16, ResNet50, and DenseNet121, reports 88.11 % accuracy, reflecting a relative improvement of 3.40 %. From these results, it is clear that combining different models is beneficial for improved classification outcomes by considering diverse representations. This method presents a resilient avenue for automated multiclass skin disease recognition.

title: Smart Aqua Guard: IoT-Enabled Framework for Real-Time Monitoring and Sustainable Freshwater Aquaculture
authors: MD Sakib Hasan Biplob, Nushrat Jahan, Nusrat Jahan Chadni, Mustakim Ahmed, Afroza Nahar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Freshwater aquaculture plays a vital role in global food security, yet it continues to face challenges such as inefficient feeding practices, unstable water quality, and delayed disease detection. These issues reduce productivity, increase production costs, and create environmental risks. This paper proposes “Smart Aqua Guard” as a solution to these problems that is an integrated aquaculture management framework, a combination of Internet of Things (IoT)-based real-time monitoring, Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based analytics, and blockchain-based security. The system uses a distributed sensor network to measure key parameters including pH, dissolved oxygen, temperature, turbidity, and ammonia with data sent to an ESP32 microcontroller to preprocess and send it wirelessly in a secure manner. AI models, such as Random Forest to predict disease, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) to predict time-series, and fuzzy logic to optimize feeding are produced to generate proactive insights and automated control reactions. Immutability and traceability of operational data is guaranteed by a blockchain layer, which increases trust and compliance throughout the aquaculture supply chain. A case study of Tilapia farm in Bangladesh reveals that the farm had realized high level of improvement such as reduced mortality risk by 22 %, increased feed ratio by 15, and response time was improved more than 90 % as compared to when they operated manual. These results indicate the power of Smart Aqua Guard, an intelligent, scalable and sustainable concept of freshwater aquaculture management.

title: 0.15 μm 120 V Si P-epi BCD Technology on Latchup immunity with Lowest Ron nLDMOS, HV ESD of combined PNP Structure for Automotive Application
authors: Feng Qiu, Xiaodong Yang, Zhuang Wang, Shijie Wang, Fengsong Liu, Linhui Hu, Liming Liao, Qiang Zhang, Hongtao Jiang, Pengcheng Yu, Hao Gu, Shijin Ding, Chunlei Wu, D. W. Zhang, Haiou Li, Hong Zhou, Jincheng Zhang, Linna Zhao, Xiaofeng Gu, Wei Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper proposes an advanced 0.15μm 120 V P-epi BCD technology platform applied for automotive applications. The 60-120 V n/p LDMOS adopts controllable RESURF technology and deep drain profile engineering, achieving best in class breakdown voltage (BV) & specific on-resistance (Ron,sp) of 146 V/155 mOhm*mm2, and maintaining high safe operating area (SOA) performance from -40 °C to 125 °C. The deep trench isolation (DTI) is used to suppress latch-up and reduce noise coupling, which significantly reduces the injection ratio (α) of the parasitic PNP from 0.93 to 0.41 and the current gain (β) of the parasitic NPN from 47 to 3.49. On the other hand, a novel composite PNP-based ESD (CPNP-ESD) is proposed to improve the capability of the failure current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T2</inf>), which constructs a composite current path combining lateral and vertical paths by removing the HVNW, and thereby effectively mitigates surface current crowding. The base structure is designed to tune trigger voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T1</inf>) and holding voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">h</inf>) by modulate PNP current gain. It achieves an I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T2</inf> exceeding 3 A at 25 °C and 2.5 A at 125 °C, maintains a stable holding voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">h</inf>), and significantly reduces the latch-up risk.

title: A Multimodal OCR-Aware Approach for Classifying Bengali Political Memes into Religious, Gender-Based, Neutral, and Political Categories
authors: Shahi Pervez Pulok, Md. Rakibul Islam, Md. Masud Rana, Arifa Tur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Bengali political memes have become one of the most popular forms of online communication that integrate visual with noisy and code-mixed Bangla English texts to express satire, criticism, and focus on political or social messages. It is also difficult to automatically recognize the object of such memes, religious organizations, gender organizations, political organizations, or neutral messages because of the nature of the interaction between visual and textual information. In this paper, it is suggested to present a multi-modal OCR-sensitive framework of target-based classification of Bengali political memes. The images of memes containing text are first processed with optical character recognition and then with a trained BERT model and encoded, but the visual features are extracted with a pretrained ResNet-152 convolutional neural network. The visual and textual representations are combined by an entirely connected layer to anticipate the target category. As the experiments on the PoliMemeDecode dataset show, the suggested multimodal approach has better performance in comparison with unimodal baselines. The model has a validation rate of 87 % and a macro-average F1-score of 0.83 and the classes show high performance, especially that of a Religious and Political meme. These findings point to the efficiency of OCR-aware multimodal combination in strong target classification in Bengali political memes.

title: Recurrent Box Supervision for Robust Remote Sensing Object Detection with Noisy Bounding Boxes and Missing Annotations
authors: Yawen Zhang, Guangshuai Gao, Yan Dong
abstract: Remote sensing object detection is sensitive to annotation quality, yet high quality labels are difficult to obtain in practice. Due to the characteristics of remote sensing imagery, such as small object scale, dense distribution and complex background, training data often suffer from two types of label noise: noisy bounding boxes and missing annotations. Existing methods usually rely on teacher student learning and target correction strategies to alleviate noisy supervision, but most of them focus on spatial correction at the current training round and lack explicit modeling of the temporal instability of corrected supervision across training epochs. As a result, the supervision target of the same instance may still fluctuate during training. To address this issue, we propose a teacher guided recurrent box supervision method for robust remote sensing object detection under noisy bounding boxes and missing annotations. Specifically, box correction at the current round first refines noisy annotations with teacher predictions to reduce local spatial errors. In the box branch, recurrent evolution then fuses the original noisy box, the historical supervision target, and the current corrected box to stabilize supervision across training rounds. Finally, missing target compensation mines unlabeled objects on top of the stabilized box supervision set, improving the completeness of the final supervision. Experiments on NWPU VHR-10 under multiple settings with noisy bounding boxes and missing annotations show that the proposed method consistently outperforms the baseline, demonstrating the robustness of the proposed method.

title: An Information Security Risk Assessment Method for Intelligent Space-Air Networks
authors: Zhaoguo Zhu, Zhicheng Xu, Xiaodong Ling, Zhidong Cao
abstract: Intelligent space-air networks are characterized by cross-domain heterogeneous interconnection, rapidly changing topology, link fluctuations, and constrained onboard resources, which make their information security risks highly coupled and dynamic. Existing studies have established security indicator systems for satellite Internet from the perspectives of functionality, performance, availability, reliability, and supportability, and have analyzed major threats at the physical, operational, and data layers. However, these studies mainly focus on security state description and lack a unified framework for quantitative and dynamic risk assessment. To address this issue, this paper proposes an information security risk assessment method for intelligent space-air networks. The proposed method first quantifies and normalizes multidimensional security indicators and combines the analytic hierarchy process with the entropy weight method to determine indicator weights. It then computes system security capability, maps vulnerability, and constructs a comprehensive risk model by integrating threat intensity and impact severity. On this basis, a dynamic correction mechanism is introduced by incorporating topology variation, link fluctuation, and real-time attack alerts. Four representative scenarios, namely normal operation, physical-layer disturbance, operational-layer attack, and data-layer compound attack, are designed for case-based validation. The results show that the proposed method can effectively distinguish risk differences across scenarios and capture the amplification effect of dynamic factors on risk values. The method can support risk warning and security protection for intelligent space-air networks.

title: Boundary Guided Refinement U-Net for Edge Preserving 2D LGG Tumor Segmentation in Brain MRI
authors: Kazi Mubashir Tazwar, Anika Rahman Shapnil, Juhayer Mahtab Tasin
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Early detection of brain tumors is important for proper treatment planning. Accurate delineation of tumor-related regions in FLAIR MRI is a key step for quantitative analysis of lower-grade gliomas (LGG). However, LGG lesions are often small and diffuse, which can lead to unstable optimization and ambiguous boundaries in standard segmentation pipelines. This paper presents a leakage-safe 2D LGG segmentation framework combining a U-Net backbone with explicit boundary supervision and a lightweight boundary-guided refinement head that corrects coarse mask predictions using predicted edge cues. To ensure reliable evaluation for clinical deployment, the framework enforces strict patient-level data splitting, applies tumor-slice oversampling only during training, and determines the segmentation decision threshold exclusively on the validation set. We conducted experiments on two publicly available datasets which are Brain MRI segmentation dataset derived from TCGA-LGG dataset and BRISC 2025 dataset. Both of these are available on Kaggle. On the Brain MRI segmentation dataset, our proposed pipeline achieves a Dice score of 0.8646 and an IoU of 0.8307 on the test set. On the BRISC 2025 dataset we attained a Dice score of 0.8277 and an IoU of 0.7327. The results demonstrate the framework produces a very competitive result on two different datasets and also proves its capabilities for future clinical deployment. Because through this tumors are accurately localized with clear masks and also the evaluation is reliable and reproducible.

title: Advancing Dental Care with Deep Learning: Classification and Detection of Dental Diseases in OPG X-Ray Images
authors: Sabrina Akter Esha, Miftahul Jannat, Md. Jahidul Hasan Jahid, Anika Bushra
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Using Orthopantomogram (OPG) X-ray images, which are frequently utilized in clinical dentistry to offer a thorough view of the jaws, teeth, and surrounding structures, our work intends to construct a deep learning-based dental disease diagnosis system. Deep learning approaches have been used by several studies to diagnose dental diseases such as cavities, infections, impacted teeth, fractures, and implants using OPGs. These studies have shown that OPG images can greatly improve diagnostic accuracy and help doctors with early identification and treatment planning when combined with automated detection systems. We merged two datasets, one from Kaggle and another from AIUB, into a unified dataset that covers a wide range of dental abnormalities. After applying image enhancement techniques and class-wise cropping, multiple transfer learning and object detection models were trained and tested. The best performance was achieved using a fine-tuned Inception-based model, which reached an accuracy of 93.7% ± 0.6% (95% CI: 92.6% – 94.8%) accuracy, and a hybrid model combining Inception and EfficientNet, which also delivered the same accuracy as InceptionV3. Furthermore, Object detection was explored by converting the dataset annotation to YOLO format and implementing different YOLO models. Performance across all approaches was evaluated using standard metrics and statistical analysis, confirming the effectiveness and robustness of deep learning for dental disease detection from OPG images.

title: An Explainable Ensemble Learning Framework Optimized by MSSO for Kidney Disease Prediction
authors: Dil Afroz Mahfuza, Swekhaynu Marma, Md. Sadiq Iqbal, Muhammad Minoar Hossain, Mohammed Ibrahim Hussain, Mohammad Mamun
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a long-term clinical situation associated with gradual deterioration of kidney function, increased risk of morbidity if not identified at an early stage. Reliable classification of CKD using clinical data can assist healthcare professionals in timely diagnosis and treatment planning. The application of, machine learning (ML) techniques approaches a significant potential in medical decision-support systems due to their ability to analyze complex and heterogeneous datasets. In this study, a systematic ML-based framework is presented for CKD classification using supervised learning models, including Logistic Regression (LR), Decision Tree (DT), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Gradient Boosting (GB), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB), and Random Forest (RF), integrated with the proposed Feature Optimization (FO) Multi-Stage Stacking Optimizer (MSSO). Comprehensive data preprocessing is performed to address missing values, categorical feature representation, and feature scaling. All models undergo 10-fold cross-validation (CV) to provide a thorough and unbiased measure of their predictive performance. Model performance is evaluated through a comparative analysis using standard classification metrics. Experimental findings indicate that the RF classifier optimized through MSSO achieves the highest predictive performance with an accuracy of 99.50%, demonstrating superior stability and generalization compared to the other models. The results suggest that ensemble learning methods, when combined with appropriate data preprocessing and the MSSO optimization strategy, can provide effective and reliable support for automated kidney disease classification, offering practical relevance for clinical decision-making and digital healthcare systems.

title: Explainable Deep Learning and IoT Fusion Strategy for Early Indoor Fire Safety Monitoring
authors: Md. Numanur Rahman, Tahzib Mahmud Rifat, Md. Jalish Mahamud, Suman Saha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Early and reliable indoor fire detection is essential for minimizing damage and ensuring critical safety. Many existing systems depend particularly on vision or sensor-based approaches, which often fail to provide reliable detection in complex indoor environments. Traditional smoke or heat sensors often trigger false alarms, especially in kitchens, workshops, or other busy indoor spaces. To address this problem, we propose a vision and IoT fusion based framework that combines the computer vision decisions with IoT sensors data. The vision module uses YOLO-based deep learning model to detect fire and smoke in real time. The sensor module measures temperature, gas, and smoke levels to understand the surrounding environment. A simple fusion rule then combines both outputs to decide whether the environment is safe or hazardous. We trained and evaluated YOLOv8n, YOLOv8m and YOLOv11n, using the same settings and compared their accuracy, speed, and ability to detect small flames. Experimental results show that YOLOv8n performed slightly better in our tests and required fewer resources, making it suitable for cost-constrained indoor deployment. To further analyze detection failures and improve model transparency, an explainable AI technique based on Grad-CAM is implemented to visualize model attention patterns in challenging fire and smoke scenarios. Our findings show that adding sensor information helps reduce false alarms and improves decision-making in indoor conditions.

title: XAI-Powered Vision: Making CNN Malware Classifiers Transparent for Cybersecurity Frontlines
authors: Showkot Hosen, Turja Talukder, Md. Azad Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This work introduces a CNN-powered malware classifier on the Malimg dataset that has incorporated the use of saliency explainability. This makes the work capable of handling the opacity issue which exists within security systems developed using deep learning techniques. This model operates on grayscale 128 × 128-sized images belonging to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5}$</tex> Windows PE families with 3.3M parameters. SOC analyst's trust in SOC solutions is made possible by saliency maps focus on the discriminative pixels that represent PE structure components such as the ‘.text’ and ‘.data’ sections, as opposed to abstract features. Such mapping gives SOC analysts insight into which parts of the malicious file are being used in decision-making by CNNs. The performance analysis on the entire Malimg test dataset of 1,868 images clearly brings out the strength of the model on various families. In addition, the visualizations of the saliency maps discussed above help in gaining better insights into the triage processes. Such a model helps fill the gap between performance and explanation.

title: Revisiting the Avalanche Breakdown Criterion in High-Dislocation-Density GaN Power Devices
authors: Zineng Yang, Yifan Wang, Hehe Gong, Jiaqi He, Hang-Ming Zhang, Kai Cheng, Han Wang, Yuhao Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: Avalanche capability is desirable for power devices to ensure nondestructive breakdown and surge energy dissipation via impact ionization. Conventionally, a positive temperature coefficient (PTC) of the breakdown voltage (BV), extracted at low-current compliance of the temperature (T) dependent leakage I-V characteristics, is used as a useful criterion for identifying avalanche capability. Here, we challenge this criterion by investigating quasi-vertical GaN p-n diodes fabricated on patterned sapphire (PSS) and sapphire substrates with high dislocation densities (~10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup>). While the GaN-on-PSS diode exhibits a weak PTC with a flattened reverse I-V, the GaN-on-Sapphire diode yields a negative temperature coefficient (NTC). Unclamped inductive switching (UIS) tests demonstrate robust avalanche capability in both devices, featuring stable voltage clamping ~1.5 kV and avalanche current densities approaching 1 kA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, as well as capability of sustaining at least 10,000 avalanche cycles. This contradiction could be attributed to the superposition of the dislocation-mediated leakage current onto the avalanche current, where the former’s NTC property could mask the latter’s PTC characteristics in the leakage I-V characterization.
