These results suggest that a leakage I-V PTC is likely not the prerequisite for avalanche especially in high-dislocation-density devices, establishing circuit-level energy dissipation as the reliable validation criterion.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: Design and Implementation of Intelligent Image Processing Algorithm based on Computer Vision and Microelectronics Technology
authors: Yiqun Zhang, Feng Xu, Luyao Feng
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: This study proposes an intelligent image processing solution that integrates computer vision (CV) and microelectronics technology to address the problems of low recognition accuracy, poor real-time performance, and high hardware resource consumption of existing image processing algorithms in complex scenes. By constructing a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture, this paper adopts depthwise separable convolution to reduce computational complexity, and combines it with Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) for hardware acceleration optimization. The algorithm design includes three core modules: image preprocessing, feature extraction, and object detection. Batch normalization and residual connections are used to improve convergence speed. Implement pipeline parallel processing on FPGA platform and use fixed-point quantization technology to compress model parameters. The experimental results show that the algorithm achieved an mAP @ [0.5:0.95] index of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 7 \%}$</tex> on the COCO dataset, a processing speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 3. 1}$</tex> FPS at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1920 \times 1080$</tex> resolution, and a peak power consumption of only 2.7 W, verifying the significant advantages of the proposed method in accuracy, speed, and energy efficiency.

title: Performance Analysis of Quantum GKP Error Correction Code Under Noisy Environment
authors: M. Samin Salek, M.A.Mosabbir Antor, Md. Saifur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Gottesman-Kitaev-Preskill (GKP) Codes tend to address one of the most challenging tasks for Quantum Communication of correcting the erroneous quantum bits. This paper focuses on analyzing the performance of this code under thermal displacement noise. GKP-encoded quantum memory under repeated error-correction cycles was simulated using the Strawberry Fields Bosonic simulator via finite energy approximation of the GKP state. We evaluate the logical-state fidelity as a function of correction cycles and noise strength, and analyze the effectiveness of error correction. Our results illustrate the robustness limits of GKP-based memory under realistic noise and highlight the dependence of logical performance on displacement noise variance, providing insight into practical continuous-variable quantum error correction.

title: Sequential-Spatial CNN-LSTM Framework for Network Flooding Attack Detection
authors: Praveen Kumar L, Rajshekar R, Shivag N R, Sinchan H, Rashmi S
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) flooding attacks continue to pose a serious threat to modern networks due to the growth of cloud infrastructures and IoT devices, making traditional rule-based detection methods ineffective against dynamic and low-rate attacks. Methods: This paper presents a sequential–spatial deep learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for spatial feature extraction with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal traffic modeling using flow-level network data. Results: Experiments conducted on benchmark datasets including CICDDoS2019, CICIDS2017, and UNSW-NB15 demonstrate high detection performance, achieving accuracy and F1-scores above 97% with low inference latency suitable for near real-time deployment. Conclusion: The proposed framework effectively detects both high-volume and low-rate flooding attacks while maintaining computational efficiency, though challenges related to dataset bias and real-world deployment remain.

title: An Interpretable Hybrid CNN-Vision Transformer Framework for Pneumonia Detection from Chest X-Ray Images
authors: Manik Kumar Saha, Sarwar Hosain, Najmul Hossain, Mohammad Shorif Uddin
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In clinical screening, when reducing false-negative diagnoses is crucial, accurate and prompt diagnosis of pneumonia using chest X-ray pictures is crucial. Long-range contextual dependencies may be difficult for convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to represent, despite their capacity to catch localized problematic patterns. Vision Transformers (ViTs), on the other hand, are excellent at learning global representations but may miss fine-grained spatial features. This research suggests an interpretable hybrid CNN-Vision Transformer framework that combines learning local patterns and global relationships through a justified late-fusion method in order to address these complimentary shortcomings. Focal Loss is used during training to explicitly highlight screening sensitivity and reduce class imbalance. Strong performance is demonstrated by experimental evaluation on a publically accessible chest X-ray dataset, with a macroaveraged <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 0.89, an accuracy of 0.90, and a pneumonia recall of 99.7 %. In addition to quantitative results, a dual explainability framework combining CNN-based Grad-CAM and transformer attention maps is employed to ensure predictionaligned local and global interpretation. The suggested method is a viable tool for automated pneumonia screening since it strikes a fair compromise between performance, interpretability, and clinical relevance.

title: Notes on Proof Systems for Intermediate Logics
authors: Norihiro Kamide, Sara Negri
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: G0- and G3-style sequent calculi are defined for the propositional intermediate logics Dummett logic and Jankov logic. The equivalence between the G0- and G3-style sequent calculi for these logics is then proved, from which cut elimination for these calculi follows. Natural deduction systems with general elimination rules are also defined for these logics, and a full normalization theorem for these systems is established. This result is obtained by means of bi-directional translations between the G0-style sequent calculi and the natural deduction systems. Additionally, alternative cut-free sequent calculi and a fully normal natural deduction system are obtained for classical logic by generalizing the proposed proof systems.

title: Multi-Architecture Comparative Framework for Bangla Sign Language Classification Using CNN and Transformer Models
authors: Nazifa Tabassum, Sadia Sultana Jese, Mahedi Hasan, Moumitu Tasnim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Bangla Sign Language (BDSL) plays an important role to communicate with hard of hearing or deaf people of our country. Due to the limitations of technology support most of the time these deaf community faces difficulties in academic, financial or job field. Even if there are several studies on this filed but most of them are done by using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) models. This model often faces major issues such as overfitting, poor generalization on small dataset or limited recolonization system. This work proposed a robust model to detect Bangla Sign Language (BDSL) using Vision Transformer architecture. The proposed model uses the customized Vision Transformer model which is already pretrained on ImageNet-21k along with fine tuned on Shongket Bangla Sign Language Letter dataset. This dataset contains nearly 5,820 images across 36 Bangla letter classes. For better performance this model applied data augmentation, resizing image and normalize the images. At the end partial fine tuned applied on the selected dataset. Final experimental result demonstrate that proposed custom Vision Transformer(ViT) model successfully recognize the different hand gestures also capture the relationship between image patches. The proposed model managed to achieve 99.54% validation accuracy, 99.56% Recall value and F1-Score along with 99.57% precision score. Also the gap between the training validation performance also very minimal. This result proves that the proposed model is able to solve the overfitting issue. The proposed model outperformed compared to many existing CNN models such as ResNet50, VGG16 and MobileNetV2. The main purpose of this study is to highlight the importance of transformer based study for Bangla Sign Language recognition and also suggest a robust alternate for traditional CNN-based methods. This proposed work can be a pioneer for future research on word level and sentence level Bangla Sign Language recognition.

title: A Retrieval-Grounded and Novelty-Aware Framework for Research Idea Generation using Large Language Models
authors: Hazrat Ali, Md. Abdur Rahman, Md. Ferdaus Hossen, Md. Rajibul Palas, Md. Shoab Alam, M. A. Quader
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Large language models have rapidly been adopted but provided new opportunities of automated research ideation.Current LLM-based systems can usually find it difficult to get out of coming up with similar, poorly differentiated, or ideas. Lack of a proper base in previous research. In realworld academic and such limitations can make innovation slow especially in industrial research settings add to redundancy, and impose more cognitive burden on researchers that have to evaluate newness and viability manually. To address this challenge, this paper is introducing a novelty-aware project idea recommendation framework integrating external academic retrieval with LLMs (based on transformers). The framework employs augmented, dense semantic generation of retrieval dings and top-K similarity search in order to base idea generation on applicable scholarly articles. Training is made through retrieved papers. Measures an explicit novelty scoring system, LLM reasoning eschews the same by penalizing semantic congruence to other text and learning new concepts in similar studies. Comprehensive assessments across various research fields indicate that the proposed framework consistently produces more innovative project concepts while preserving significant semantic relevance in comparison to leading benchmarks. These results show how useful it is to combine structured novelty modeling with external knowledge retrieval. This is a scalable and effective way to use AI to help with research ideas in modern computer and information technology workflows.

title: Few-Shot Multimodal Medical Imaging: A Theoretical Framework
authors: Md Talha Mohsin, Ismail Abdulrashid
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Medical imaging often faces the challenge of limited annotated data, especially when it comes to rare diseases in lowresource clinical settings. While different kinds of multimodal and meta-learners have been shown to improve performance in such scenarios, they often lack strict theoretical foundations that explain the conditions under which they work. In this work, we develop a comprehensive theoretical framework for few-shot multimodal medical imaging that simultaneously characterises the complexity of the sample, quantifies the uncertainty, as well as the interpretability. By leveraging PAC learning theory, Vapnik-Chervonenkis (VC) dimension analysis, and PACBayesian bounds, we derive lower bounds on the required number of labeled samples for reliable performance and show that different modalities of data complementing each other attenuate effective capacity through an information gain term. Furthermore, we suggest a formal metric of explanation stability and prove that the variance of explanations falls off at the rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 / n$</tex>. To empirically validate the theoretical arguments above, we build a multimodal dataset and test an additive convolutional neural network with multilayer perceptron fusion in few-shot regimes. The results not only support the predicted multimodal performance improvements, but also show modality interference when the sample size is larger and corresponding reduction in predictive uncertainty. Collectively, our framework provides a principled foundation for the design of data-efficient, uncertainty-aware and interpretable diagnostic models for lowresource environments.

title: Performance Analysis of 6T and 8T Sram Cells at Low Supply Voltage in 65-nm CMOS
authors: Abu Talha Haque Miah, A. S. M. Kibrya, Miss Al Ive, Ratin Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of six transistor and eight transistor SRAM cells implemented in 65 nm CMOS technology and simulated in LTspice across thirty supply voltage points ranging from 0.85 V to 1.5 V. Performance characteristics evaluated were access times during read/write operations, static noise margin (SNM), dynamic power and leakage power, and area efficiency under identical operating conditions. SNM was extracted using the conventional butterfly curve method; access delay measurements were taken from the 50% VDD point at the time of transition between active and inactive states. Based on simulation data, it was found that the 8T cell had 25% higher read SNM (125 mV vs 100 mV) and 17% shorter read access delay than the six-transistor cell (approximately 240 ps vs 290 ps), primarily due to the decoupled read path architecture of the eight-transistor cell to its sixtransistor equivalent. Although the six-transistor cell consumed lower dynamic power than the eight-transistor cell design (14%) and was more area efficient, these findings demonstrate the natural trade-offs between stability, speed, and area. Thus, this work is very important for low-voltage and energy-restricted applications.

title: An Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Pancreatic Cancer Biomarker Prediction with End-to-End Application Deployment
authors: Md. Nazmus Sakib, Md. Arafat Islam Nazmul, Md. Yeasin Arafat, Ananna Rani Das, Nowshad Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Early detection of pancreatic cancer remains a major clinical challenge, largely due to overlapping biochemical marker patterns across benign and malignant stages. This study presents a clinically oriented, explainable machine learning framework that integrates serum and urinary biomarkers such as CA19-9, CEA, bilirubin, LYVE1, REG1A/B, and creatinine to support early risk stratification. A structured preprocessing pipeline was applied, including robust imputation, outlier control, and feature standardization to ensure data reliability. Among several evaluated classifiers, XGBoost achieved the strongest performance, reaching an overall accuracy of 81.5% and demonstrating consistent discrimination across low-, medium-, and high-risk groups. To promote transparency in clinical decisionmaking, SHAP and LIME were incorporated to provide both global biomarker relevance and patient-specific explanatory insights. The results indicate that combining multi-biomarker profiling with interpretable machine learning can enhance early risk identification while preserving clinical interoperability. This framework is designed to function as a decision-support tool, assisting healthcare professionals in identifying high-risk patients who may benefit from further diagnostic evaluation and closer monitoring.

title: Phishing Detection using Machine Learning and Explainable Fusion
authors: K. Rajesh Kambattan, Mammula. Arjun, Atla. Ravindra Reddy
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Phishing sites are one of the biggest threats to cybersecurity as they mimic the appearance of valid online services in order to steal confidential data. The current paper discusses PhishGuard AI, which is a explainable phishing URL detector developed as a feature-level fusion. This system identifies 35 lexical, domain, and security related features and uses an XGBoost classifier to predict the probability of phishing. An impersonation detector module is an improvement of contextual risk measure based on similarity-informed domain comparison. The last result is calculated as a weighted combination of the brand risk score and classification probability. An experimental assessment of a balanced corpus of 55,000 URLs indicates an accuracy rate of 92.3, precision of 89.7, recall of 94.1 and a false positive rate of 1.2. Model stability is given by five-fold cross-validation. The framework results in real-time detection and interpretability based on SHAP based explanations.

title: Low-altitude Electromagnetic Spectrum Sensing Acceleration Method based on FFT and Parallel Pipeline
authors: Hongcan Huang, Zhenjia Chen, Hongpeng Chen, Mengqiang Li, WanchaoLi WanchaoLi
abstract: Aiming at the real-time and efficiency requirements of spectrum sensing in low-altitude information monitoring scenes, a low-altitude electromagnetic spectrum sensing acceleration method based on fast Fourier transform and parallel pipeline was designed and implemented. In this method, the self-made RF front-end and ADC module are used to complete the digital processing, and the data are directly input to the FPGA for stream calculation. On the FPGA side, the parallel pipeline structure is used to realize the FFT, threshold decision and other key algorithms. As the main control platform, RK3568 is responsible for system control and result processing, and an efficient hardware/software cooperation architecture is constructed. Experimental results show that the proposed method performs well in terms of real-time performance, energy efficiency and scalability, and provides an efficient and low delay implementation scheme for low-altitude electromagnetic spectrum sensing.

title: Intelligent Basketball Shooting Training System Using Dynamic Shooting Evaluation Network and Internet of Things-Based Motion Technology
authors: Quan Li, Hongyan Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Background: In recent years, an accurate evaluation of basketball shooting mechanics has become increasingly important for Internet of Things (IoT)-based athlete training, performance monitoring and biomechanics research. Where shooting actions involve complex wrist-arm ball coordination, athlete-specific execution patterns and rapid motion transitions. Challenge: The existing Light Gradient Boosting Machine with Bayesian Optimization (LightGBM-BO) model effective for motion recognition. However, it fails to capture inter-joint coordination quality and fine-grained temporal fluctuations essential for reliable shooting performance evaluation. Proposed model: To address these limitations, this research proposes a Dynamic Shooting Evaluation Network (DSEN) for intelligent basketball shooting evaluation and the design of the basketball shooting system. Initially, Inertial Measurement Units (IMU)-based motion data were collected from 16 athletes using smart basketball embedded with IMUs and a wrist-worn IMU sensors and complimented by dual-view video recordings for crossvalidation. After that, preprocessed through Adaptive Motion Curves (AMC) for temporal smoothing, Cross-Modality Noise Recalibration (CMNR) for sensor fusion, and Personalized Pose Normalization (PPN) to standardize joint coordinates. Then, the kinematic signals are transformed into Kinematic Entropy Embeddings (KEE), which captures joint-level variability, motion stability and cross-joint coordination. Finally, the DSEN model integrates a Temporal Pattern Encoder (TPE) with recurrent dynamics and computes a differentiable Sequence Alignment Score (SAS) to quantify deviations from expert reference motions. Results: The experimental validation demonstrates that DSEN achieved superior alignment accuracy of 99.1 % Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.815 when compared to existing LightGBM-BO model.

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Quantifying Sea-Level Rise: A Conservative Baseline Forecast through 2030 Using Prophet Architecture
authors: Shahadat Hossain, Towfiq Bin Hasan, Hasibul Hasan, Md. Farseen Islam Sattique, Muhammad Firoz Mridha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Global Mean Sea Level (GMSL) rise is a critical variable for evaluating climate change threats to coastal stability. This study leverages 32 years (1993-2025) of high-resolution satellite altimetry data from NASA Earthdata to examine historical trends and project future elevations through 2030. The non-stationary nature of the time series was statistically validated using the Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) test <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p=0.8038)$</tex>. A decomposable machine learning architecture, Facebook Prophet, was implemented to model annual seasonality and long-term deterministic growth. Our results estimate a sea-level rise of 9.59 mm by 2030, corresponding to a conservative annual rise rate of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.9 \text{mm} /$</tex> year. Compared to the IPCC AR6 accelerated scenarios (3.5−4.5 mm/year), this research establishes a robust “Conservative Baseline” essential for risk-averse coastal infrastructure planning and environmental sustainability management. These findings have significant global implications, emphasizing the necessity of accounting for non-local drivers and anthropogenically forced climatic alterations in regional adaptation strategies. Comparing with IPCC climate models that are often nebulae of uncertainty, this work presents a tangible realistic empirical “Safe Lower Bound” for sea-level rise based on 32 years of high-resolution satellite altimetry data.

title: Distinguishing Mainshocks from Foreshocks Using Spatiotemporal Labeling and Interpretable Ensemble Learning
authors: Kazi Sakib Hasan, Most. Afia Anjum Borsha, Faiyaz Bin Zaman, Azra Humayra Alam Prova, Mahir Hasan Chowdhury
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Rapid identification of whether a seismic event is a mainshock or a foreshock is crucial for minimizing public panic and enabling timely disaster response in earthquake-prone regions. However, this task remains difficult due to the physical similarity of seismic waveforms and the stochastic nature of earthquake sequences. This work presents a data-driven framework for mainshock classification based on an 88 -year USGS catalog, incorporating strict chronological separation and a bidirectional spatiotemporal labeling algorithm. Ensemble classifiers including XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and AdaBoost—were optimized using Bayesian methods. Physics-informed features such as local seismicity rate, cumulative energy release, and magnitude differential capture the evolving stress state preceding each event. Among the evaluated models, CatBoost demonstrated the strongest sensitivity to mainshocks, achieving an AUC of 0.965 and recall of 0.97 for safety-critical detection. SHAP interpretability further confirms that magnitude contrast relative to recent local seismic history is the dominant predictor of mainshock behavior. The proposed framework establishes a scalable foundation for real-time seismic role classification and supports the development of operational early-warning and disaster-response mechanisms.

title: Design and Analysis of a Cell Phone Mounted Dual-Band Patch Antenna for 5G/6G Applications
authors: Md Faruk Muntasir Banna, Mehedi Hasan Munna, Md Aminul Islam, Syed Md Hasin Tasnim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In this paper, we proposed a design and analysis of a dual-band microstrip patch antenna aimed at next-gen Wi-Fi and 5G/6G mobile applications. Nowadays, microstrip patch antennas are commonly used in wireless systems because they are low profile, easy to fabricate, and compatible with printed circuit boards. The proposed antenna is an inset-fed dual band rectangular patch antenna. It was designed on an FR4 substrate which operates at 5.2 GHz and 6.96 GHz with return losses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-24 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-45 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex>, respectively. To verify its practical usability, the antenna was placed on the back of a smartphone model, and a full-wave simulation was done using CST Studio. The results indicate that the antenna maintains stable operation in both bands. It has acceptable gain and radiation characteristics, even in the presence of the phone body. Furthermore, a Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) analysis was done in CST using a multilayer human body phantom model. The obtained SAR values were found to be well below internationally accepted safety limits. So, it is safe to use with any smartphone models. Overall, the proposed antenna has a small and practical design, making it simple to manufacture and easy to integrate into modern smartphones. It is well suited for research purposes and supports Wi-Fi as well as emerging sub- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 \text{GHz} 5 \mathrm{G} / 6 \mathrm{G}$</tex> wireless technologies.

title: Look Out for New Spiders: Araneae Species OOD Detection Using Ensemble Pooling
authors: Zi Kang Deng, Jeffrey J. Rodriguez
publish: 2026 IEEE Southwest Symposium on Image Analysis and Interpretation (SSIAI)
abstract: The order Araneae, commonly known as spiders, comprises more than 53,000 described species. In the last decade, roughly 9,000 species have been added and discovered, and the pace of discovery shows no signs of slowing down. Species discovery can help in biodiversity evaluation. Existing spider species discovery methods do not commonly incorporate machine learning, which we believe has promising potential to enhance discovery outcomes. To that effect, we present a new method for out-of-distribution (OOD) detection based on Mahalanobis distance scoring using ConvNeXt as a feature embedding model. Our method, OspideR, creates a separate anomaly detection model for each class in the training dataset. At inference time, a separate anomaly score is generated by each model, and then the minimum score is used to determine the final output score of the model, known as the OspideR score. Performance was evaluated using a test set containing in-distribution and out-of-distribution spider images, distinct from the training images. The experimental results show that OspideR achieves better AUROC, F1, and accuracy compared to the other methods tested.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Identifying Endangered Animals in Bangladesh
authors: Tuhin Hossain, Jahidul Islam, Ahmed Ainun Nahian Kabir, Anik Pramanik, Md Sadekur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Preserving endangered animals from extinction is an overwhelming challenge for researchers throughout the globe. Monitoring wildlife in their natural habitat is crucial. This proposed work develops an automated system that can detect endangered animals in nature. It might be challenging to manually identify endangered animals since there are so many distinct species. For that, we constructed a custom dataset carefully consisting of 1,880 images representing 10 endangered animal species, sourced from multiple online platforms. The images performed preprocessing, including resizing, normalization, and data augmentation techniques such as random shear, zooming, and horizontal flipping to enhance model generalization. We assessed five deep learning(DL) models on this dataset: Custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), VGG16, InceptionV3, MobileNetV2, and DenseNet121. DenseNet121 showed the best results, achieving 99.67% accuracy on both the training and testing datasets, which means it generalizes better, learns faster, and avoids overfitting compared to the other models. Furthermore, to improve model transparency and interpretability, we used Explainable Artificial Intelligence(XAI) approaches, notably LIME, which highlighted the key image regions that contribute to the model's predictions. These findings demonstrate how well DenseNet121 can identify endangered animal species, offering a dependable tool to aid in conservation and wildlife monitoring.

title: A Multi-Modal Intelligent Virtual Assistant for Extended Reality Environments Based on Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing, and Large Language Models
authors: Md. Saiful Islam, Maslinia Sujnus Shifa, Md. Sajib Al Masud, Md. Nazaf Wahid Omi, Rubayat Zaman Eashan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Extended Reality (XR) environments increasingly demand natural and adaptive interaction mechanisms beyond traditional menu-driven interfaces. This paper presents a multi-modal intelligent virtual assistant for XR that jointly understands user intent and contextual information by integrating computer vision, natural language processing (NLP), and large language models (LLMs). The key novelty of the proposed approach lies in a context-grounded reasoning framework that fuses real-time visual perception with conversational state to generate reliable and explainable assistant actions. Visual information, such as objects, spatial cues, and user activities, is transformed into a structured scene representation, which is combined with linguistic intent signals to support accurate decision-making in immersive environments. Unlike conventional virtual assistants that process visual and textual inputs independently, the proposed system employs confidence-aware multi-modal fusion and retrieval-augmented LLM reasoning to maintain contextual consistency and reduce ambiguous responses. The framework is designed in a modular and engine-agnostic manner, enabling seamless integration with common XR platforms for applications including object-aware guidance, interactive task support, and contextual information retrieval. Experimental evaluation is conducted using task-completion scenarios and user experience metrics, demonstrating improved interaction naturalness, reduced user effort, and higher contextual accuracy compared to unimodal and loosely coupled multimodal baselines. These results highlight the effectiveness of multi-modal grounding for developing intelligent, trustworthy, and user-centric virtual assistants in extended reality environments.

title: AI Driven CNN-LSTM Hybrid Neural Network for Robust Heart Disease Prediction
authors: Md Abdulla Hasan, Zaffar Abdullah, Faiza Saiara Nity
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Heart disease is one of the major causes of death worldwide and predictive models that are applicable within a wide range of patients are needed; however, existing machine learning and deep learning approaches rely on shallow network structures or single-source datasets and lack robustness in heterogeneous clinical settings. This paper presents a Hybrid Deep Neural Network (HDNN) which combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with hierarchical interaction of feature with long short-term memory (LSTM) networks with gated dependencies together with a relevance-sensitive weighting scheme and risk-sensitive loss to achieve enhanced optimization stability and clinical sensitivity. The framework is evaluated based on the dual-scale validation framework that includes the UCI Cleveland benchmark dataset and a multi-source dataset constructed by integrating of five publicly available heart disease repositories. The proposed HDNN evaluates at 97.75 % (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.9885$</tex>) accuracy on Cleveland and 98.86 % (AUC = 0.9978) on the multi-source dataset and, overall, it demonstrates superior performance over the baseline machine and deep learning models with clinically relevant indicators. These results demonstrate that the suggested framework provides a robust and reliable generalizable method of heart disease risk prediction with promising implications for clinical decision support.

title: Predicting the Risk of Post-Chikungunya Arthritis Using Hybrid Ensemble Model
authors: Md Wakil Ahmed, Khushnor Rahman Meem, Md Shahnewaj Limon, Sadia Afrin, K.M.Nure Tanvir Siddique, Shahrin Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Chikungunya is an abroviral disease caused by the mosquito, which frequently deteriorates into chronic inflammatory rheumatism, including post-Chikungunya arthritis, resulting in permanent incapacity in 2 5 - 6 0% of the infected individuals. This paper addresses the absence of early risk prediction by developing an AI-possessed hybrid ensemble model of XGBoost and Logistic Regression. It was trained using a combined dataset of 1001 real samples provided by Kaggle and 1129 more samples created through probability distributions using clinical summaries. The key features were demographics (age, gender) and symptoms (fever, headache, myalgia, arthralgia, rash, vomiting, swelling, joint stiffness, pain duration). The initial data was skewed (59% with arthritis); a combination of these and SMOTE eliminated this. The model scored 0.73 macro F1score and 0.761 AUC on the merged dataset with preprocessing, non-linear relationship using polynomial feature engineering, SMOTE balancing, and hyperparameter optimization, which was significantly higher than the baseline macro F1-score of 0.46. The SHAP analysis showed that among the clinical evidence arthralgia type, duration of pain, swelling were the best predictors. Robustness was verified by Sensitivity analysis. Results validate the importance of augmented datasets in data-scarce diseases. Weaknesses are synthetic biases and future efforts include deep learning and external clinical validation.

title: Characterization, Analysis, and Modeling of Quasi-Saturation in High-Voltage Superjunction 4H-SiC Power DMOSFETs
authors: Zhaowen He, Collin Hitchcock, Stacey Kennerly, Reza Ghandi, T. Paul Chow
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: We have characterized, analyzed, and modeled quantitatively the quasi-saturation (QS) phenomenon in high voltage (HV) 4H-SiC superjunction (SJ) DMOSFETs and compared to the conventional counterparts. We introduce a QS figure of merit (FoM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">QS</inf>) and saturation ideality factor (η) for these quantitative comparisons. Our results show that QS has weak temperature dependence (<6%) for both SJ and conventional (Conv) DMOSFETs. At room temperature, the FoM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">QS</inf> of the 3.3 kV and 4.5 kV SJ DMOSFETs decreases by 44% and 47%, respectively, compared with the Conv device. In addition, tested SJ DMOSFETs exhibit voltage rating, gate and temperature insensitive QS due to the dominance of the inherent DMOS JFET region. Finally, a power-law relation is employed to model the quasi-saturation voltage drop vs. drain current density, showing good agreement with measured data.

title: Adoption of Artificial Intelligence in Customer Relationship Management: Insights from Bangladesh's Digital Marketing Sector
authors: Md Mehedi Hasan Emon, Md. Adnan Rahman, Sharmin Jahan, Soeb Md Shoayeb Noman, Zerin Tasnim, Javed Mahmud
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The main objective of the study is to examine the effect of technological, organizational, and environmental factors, namely, PU of AI, OR, TC, and CP, and their interplay with each other on the adoption of AI-powered CRM in the context of digital marketing in Bangladesh. The quantitative research design was used with primary data being collected by using a structured questionnaire with a 5-point Likert scale. The marketing managers, digital marketing professionals, and CRM specialists were the targeted group and the analysis of 149 valid responses was conducted with the help of Smart PLS 4 and PLSSEM to assess the hypothesis about the existing relationships. The results indicate that all the four determinants have a positive and significant impact on AICRM adoption, with CP having the strongest impact, and then OR, TC, and PU. The findings underscore the interactive effects between technology, organizational and environmental variables in determining the behavior to adopt AI, which justifies the relevance of the TOE framework in a developing country setting. In practice, the study is actionable in helping managers to improve AI adoption through investing in organizational preparation, harmonizing technology systems, and tracking competitor forces. The study highlights socially the prospects of AI adoption to enhance customer experiences and advance more effective digital marketing practices. It is innovative in that it incorporated several determinants in the framework of TOE in the specific case of AI-powered CRM adoption in Bangladesh. The constraints are the convenience sampling and cross-sectional data, which indicates that longitudinal studies should be used in further research.

title: Enhancing Deep Learning-Based Parkinson’s Disease Detection from Sustained Phonation Voice Signals using SMOTE for Data Imbalance
authors: Osama Zulfiqar, Hammad Nazeer, Urooj Abid, Noman Naseer
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: Parkinson’s disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative disorder that is progressive for which, early and timely diagnosis is clinically demanding. Speech-based analysis is an effective method of automated PD detection that is non-invasive. This study explores deep-learning classification of Parkinson disease with high-dimensional speech features on the UCI Machine Learning Repository dataset, which is a set of 756 long phonation samples of 252 participants. The samples are represented by 754 features using Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) features, Tunable Q-layer Wavelet Transform features, and traditional perturbation and nonlinear features. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Bidirectional LSTM (Bi-LSTM) and Peephole LSTM (P-LSTM) are compared before and after data balancing. Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was used to correct the imbalance. It is shown that following SMOTE, the results of the experimental models show significant performance increments with LSTM yielding the highest accuracy of 94.06%. Statistically significant t-test results validated the observability of performance improvements, which shows that data balancing is effective in the classification of Parkinson disease using deep learning.

title: Evaluating Stable Diffusion with LoRA for Class-Imbalanced Medical Image Augmentation
authors: Sabib Ahmed, Jannatul Ferdus Aspia, Bithi Rani Kapali, Khalilur Rahman, Md. Samir Murad
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Deep learning models for medical image analysis are frequently constrained by severe class imbalance and data scarcity, especially in clinical settings where positive cases are rare. Although GAN-based synthesis and classical augmentation have been investigated to address these problems, they often have poor scalability under real-world restrictions, low diversity, and training instability. In this study, we thoroughly assess text-to-image Stable Diffusion as a useful data augmentation technique for class-imbalanced medical imaging. Using renal stone CT imaging as a case study, we optimize Stable Diffusion with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to produce high-quality synthetic samples exclusively for the minority class. Instead of suggesting a new classifier, we evaluate the created data's usefulness using many standardized downstream evaluation backbones, such as CNN and transformer-based models, to guarantee robustness among representation learning paradigms. Comprehensive tests and ablation studies on synthetic-to-real data ratios show that while excessive dependence on synthetic data worsens performance due to distributional bias, moderate diffusion-based augmentation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{2 5 - 5 0 \%})$</tex> consistently increases recall and F1-score. Qualitative analysis and Fréchet Inception Distance enhance the realism of the generated visuals. Overall, this study demonstrates that, under realistic data and computational restrictions, Stable Diffusion, when modified via LoRA, can function as an efficient but limited data augmentation technique for class-imbalanced medical imaging.

title: IIoT-TOPSIS: A Novel Heterogeneous Network Handover Algorithm in Smart Factory
authors: Desheng Liu, Yue An, Chen Liang, Yunxiang Zhang, Dequan Kong, Xiaowei Chen, Zihan Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) is central to realizing smart factories under Industry 5.0, requiring communication that is both low-latency and highly reliable. While heterogeneous networks (HetNets) improve coverage and reliability, they face challenges in network handover, such as frequent switching and the "Ping-Pong effect," which degrade service quality. To mitigate these issues, this paper proposes the IIoT-TOPSIS handover algorithm. Our method enhances traditional TOPSIS by integrating industrial data types and network congestion levels, introduces a handover threshold and a license function, and leverages HetNets to provide multiple transmission alternatives. Simulation results demonstrate that IIoT-TOPSIS significantly reduces the Ping-Pong effect and shortens handover time compared to existing methods, while also minimizing data loss during network fluctuations. The proposed algorithm thus improves handover efficiency and supports more reliable IIoT communications in smart factory environments.

title: A Subject-Independent Hybrid Autoencoder–CNN–LSTM Framework for Boosted Alzheimer’s Disease Detection Using Scalp EEG
authors: Muhammad Ali Tahir, Nasir Rashid, Ayesha Zeb, Hassan Elahi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disorder with abnormal oscillatory brain activity, leading to cognitive decline in individuals. The early diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease is still an open problem, particularly in environments where the use of expensive neuro-imaging techniques is not feasible. The use of electroencephalogram (EEG) is an effective technique for the diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease, as it is an inexpensive, non-invasive, and widely available technique for the detection of brain function-related changes in individuals with dementia. The current study proposes an effective subjectindependent approach for the diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease using a hybrid approach involving autoencoder-based learning of latent representations, convolutional neural networks(CNN), and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks. The effectiveness of the proposed technique is illustrated by employing publicly available EEG dataset, where subject-wise 5-fold cross-validation is performed with strict evaluation measures to avoid data leakage. The results obtained using the proposed approach are found to have an accuracy of $89.7{\%} \pm 3.2$, sensitivity of $88.4{\%} \pm 4.1$, specificity of $91.1{\%} \pm 3.8$, and an area under the curve of $0.92 \pm 0.04$.

title: Explainable AI for Neonatal Seizure Detection: A Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Approaches Using Multi-Channel EEG
authors: Sagar Kundu, Apurbo Saikat Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Neonatal seizures often indicate underlying neurological problems and require prompt, accurate identification to avoid adverse developmental outcomes. This study introduces a patient-independent seizure detection system that utilizes machine learning techniques on multi-channel electroencephalography (EEG) data. The research uses a publicly available dataset containing EEG recordings from 79 newborns. To address the issue of class imbalance, oversampling was applied to the training set. Dimensionality reduction was performed using principal component analysis (PCA) with component counts of 20, 50, 70, 100, and the full feature set. To enhance interpretability, SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) analysis was used to identify key features and electrodes contributing to seizure prediction. Eight classifiers were evaluated, with XGBoost demonstrating the best overall performance, achieving the highest accuracy of 93.75%, recall of 93.00%, F1-score of 93.00%, and AUC of 95.80%. These results highlight the potential of interpretable, data-driven methods for reliable and scalable neonatal seizure detection.

title: AI-Driven Sentiment and Context Analysis in English Literature for Enhanced Interpretation and Literary Criticism
authors: Priyadharshini. K, V. R. Yasu Bharathi, J. Mercy Navis Abinaya, Revathy J, Angala Parameswari D, C. Suganya, Kunal Shejul
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This is a novel method to literary criticism which is based on transformer-based contextualized sentiment analysis using BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers). Unlike the analysis tools which have been used for centuries, BERT registers the nuances of the emotions and the motion of emotions in the literary text along with complexity in sentiments present in the context through bidirection attention mechanisms. The methodology is an adaptation of the pre-trained version of BERT (which is an extremely powerful image recognition model) on the literary corpora specialized in numerous domains, with the goal to deep interpret character psychology, narrative arcs and evolution of thematics in diverse genres. By plotting the distribution of sentiments, and the dynamics of emotions, such an approach makes possible to find some patterns in the author's intentions and the way in which they are received. The framework has demonstrated excellent results in terms of the capacity to detect irony, the use of metaphorical language and the use of polysemous expressions in comparison to the traditional lexicon-based approaches. Results confirm the effectiveness of BERT for computational literary research that will allow the construction of quantitative analytical tools for better understanding of the texts in literary research, without going against qualitative traditions of literary analysis.

title: Design and Development of an IoT Based Environmental Oxygen Monitoring Device for Healthcare Application
authors: Shivani Venkatesan, C. Arun, R. Chandrasekaran, Shree Jananni
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In healthcare environments such as hospitals, ICUs and labs, where deviations from normal oxygen level could result in severe safety hazard, it is necessary to track the ambient oxygen concentration, real time ambient oxygen concentration monitoring is presented in this paper. To measure oxygen levels precisely and send data wirelessly, the suggested system combines an AO-03 electro-chemical oxygen sensor with an ESP32 micro-controller and a high-resolution ADC and Continuous data logging at particular intervals and remote with HTML and CSS. The sensor's sensitivity to ambient conditions is highlighted by variations seen in high temperature environments, while experimental results show steady and accurate readings under controlled indoor conditions. The system is small, inexpensive, energy efficient, and ideal for medical settings where regulated environmental monitoring is crucial. The suggested gadget promotes future development of multi parameters air quality monitoring systems and provides a workable way to improve environmental safety.

title: MaizeXNet: A CNN-Based Explainable AI Framework for Maize Pest and Disease Classification
authors: Arif Rahman Samad, Sizen Mahmud, Moin Uddin Sayem, Munshaibur Rahman Mahin, Sakib Hussen, Gourab Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Global food security depends heavily on agriculture, yet crop yield is still severely hampered by pest and disease infestations, especially in the cultivation of maize (Zea mays L.). Accurate and timely identification of such conditions is vital for sustainable crop management. This study introduces MaizeXNet, a lightweight and efficient Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture integrated with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) for automated classification and interpretation of maize pest and disease classes. The framework employs EfficientNet B0 as a pretrained backbone with a Depthwise Separable Convolutional Block to enhance feature extraction while reducing computational complexity. The dataset used in this study includes diverse maize pest and disease classes, covering both pest infestations and pathogen-induced infections. A balanced dataset was achieved through preprocessing and augmentation to improve generalization and mitigate overfitting. Comparative experiments with EfficientNet B0, ResNet50, and MobileNetV2 demonstrated that MaizeXNet achieved superior performance, attaining 99.58 percent validation accuracy and 99.38 percent test accuracy, along with higher precision-recall statistics, F1 measure, and AUC-ROC values than baseline models. Furthermore, GradCAM++ and LIME were applied to interpret MaizeXNet's predictions and visualize key regions influencing classification. The results confirm that MaizeXNet provides a reliable and interpretable framework for intelligent maize pest and disease detection in modern agriculture.

title: Adaptive Cognitive Vision System for Intelligent Industrial Automation
authors: Sathwik Shetty. B, P. Thiyagarajan, Ladly Patel, K. Anguraj, G. Reeteka
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, cognitive vision systems for industrial automation have evolved as crucial component of smart manufacturing which allows intelligent perception, adaptive decision-making and scalable process monitoring in industrial environments. However, existing Semantic Integrated Vision System (SIVS) based on Deep Learning (DL)-based vision with semantic event interpretation struggled with static reasoning, restricted temporal modeling and inadequate explainability of deep vision decisions. To address these challenges, this research presents Adaptive Cognitive Vision System for Industrial Automation (ACVS-IA). Primarily, realtime visual data collected from industrial cameras and unified Spatio-Temporal Attention Vision Module (STAVM) is employed for feature extraction and concurrently captures temporal dependencies which produces attention-based explanations. Further, Adaptive Cognitive Policy Module (ACPM) uses Reinforcement Learning (RL) for continuously updating event interpretation mechanisms based on feedback. Furthermore, Context-Aware Event Reasoning Layer (CAERL) is introduced which converts cognitive decisions in semantic manufacturing events and automation ready control actions. Finally, proposed ACVS-IA framework allows unified visual perception, temporal process understanding and adaptive cognitive reasoning. Therefore, by combining attention-based vision, learning-driven cognition and semantic event interpretation, proposed ACVS-IA framework delivers intelligent, scalable and explainable industrial automation.

title: Behavior-Aware Edge-Based Zero-Trust Cybersecurity Framework for Securing Internet of Things Enabled Smart Home Environments
authors: Bhupendra Singh, Ravi Shankar Garapati, Chandrayee Kumar, S. Madhubalan, Nalini Chekuri
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: With rapid explosion of Internet of Things (IoT) enabled smart homes, ensuring robust cybersecurity has become important challenge because of its resource constraints, heterogeneous devices and evolving attack surfaces inherent to such environments. Traditional security mechanisms often depend on static rules or cloud-centric analysis which are insufficient for detecting zero-day attacks and preserving user privacy. To overcome these challenges, a Behavior-Aware EdgeBased Zero-Trust Framework (BEZT) is proposed for securing smart home IoT ecosystems. The proposed framework integrates lightweight cryptographic authentication, secure communication and edge-based behavioral intelligence for continuously monitoring the device activities and dynamically evaluate trust levels. Then, a behavior-aware anomaly detection mechanism is deployed at smart home gateway for identifying the malicious activities by learning normal device behavior patterns which enabled real-time intrusion detection with low latency. Furthermore, zero-trust access control model is enforced using dynamically computed trust scores derived from behavioral deviations, authentication strength and historical reliability by ensuring continuous verification rather than static trust assumptions. Finally, the automated incident response and adaptive policy updates are incorporated for isolating compromised devices and mitigate threats without human intervention.

title: Efficient Facial Emotion Recognition Using CNN Features with Margin-Aware Feature Selection and SVM Classification
authors: Sunil S. Harakannanavar, Roddam Sai Likitha, R. Disha, Srinivasan. P
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Facial emotion recognition (FER) is a crucial aspect of human-computer interaction, healthcare monitoring, and affective computing. Even though convolutional neural networks (CNNs) give very detailed deep representations, the feature vectors are often high-dimensional and contain extra information. This makes classification more expensive and takes longer. This paper presents an efficient FER framework that combines CNN-based feature extraction with margin-aware feature selection to keep only the most useful features and get rid of the ones that are not useful. We use three machine learning classifiers including Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Random Forest (RF) to test the chosen feature subset. The CK+ extended dataset, which has eight emotion categories, is used for experimental validation. The proposed feature reduction strategy makes computations more efficient by lowering the number of features and the amount of work needed to classify them, all while keeping the recognition performance high. The system is built in Python, and TensorFlow/Kera's is used to get deep features and Scikit-learn is used to choose, train, and test features. The SVM classifier did the best in the experiments, with an accuracy of 87.44%, beating KNN and Random Forest. These results show that margin-aware feature selection makes it easier to separate classes and speeds up and makes FER more reliable, making it ready for real-world use.

title: Adaptive QoS-Aware Smart Grid Communication using Cloud-Native Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Rohit Tewari, Shubhankar Shilpi, Prateeksha Siddhanti, S. Madhubalan, Ranjitha. R
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Background: In recent years, cloud enabled smart grid communication system have become a significant factor for supporting automation, control and large-scale monitoring. The huge growth of event-driven grid applications and distributed sensing devices has resulted in heterogeneous communication traffic and highly dynamic, quality-of-service (QoS) management challenges in workload conditions and time-varying network. Moreover, traditional cloud-based learningdriven communication frameworks include Cloud hive, deliver the capacity control for communication of smart grid. Challenges: however, these methods face difficulties in delayed feedback utilization, reduced scalability and limited stateawareness when operating cloud-native deployment constraints and bursty traffic which affects the queue stability and latency control. Proposed Methodology: to overcome these challenges proposes Centralized Deep Reinforcement Learning with Quality of Service (CDRL-QoS) a cloud-native deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based communication control framework for smart grid environments. Initially data was collected from distributed grid devices, followed by stream management and cloud-native message ingestion to enable traffic organization and scalable buffering. In particular Elastic resource orchestration confirms the steady execution services under dynamic loads. Moreover, DRL-based communication controller and then control the handling of message by observing system states include communication delay, arrival rate, resource utilization, queue length and optimizing the action control using reward-driven learning. Finally, cloud analytics modules calculate the performance metrices and deliver the continuous feedback to allow the closed-loop adaptation.

title: Data Analytics in Medicaid: Revolutionizing Service Delivery and Patient Care
authors: Anshul Sharma, Srinivas Gadam, Nabeel Sheikh, Deepak Kole, Sai Rakesh Reddy Dontireddy, Karthik Veeravalli, Balaji Shesharao Ingole
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Data analytics has emerged as a critical enabler of transformation in Medicaid service delivery and patient care as healthcare systems increasingly rely on large-scale data for decision-making. This paper examines the application of advanced analytical approaches, including predictive modeling and machine learning, to improve operational efficiency, resource allocation, and health outcomes for Medicaid beneficiaries. Using datasets encompassing demographic information, treatment histories, and service utilization patterns, the study demonstrates how analytics-driven insights can support evidence-based policy decisions and program optimization. The paper also evaluates the role of real-time data access in strengthening care coordination and enabling more personalized interventions. Through empirical analysis and illustrative case studies, this work identifies both the benefits and implementation challenges associated with analytics adoption in Medicaid programs. The findings highlight data analytics as a key mechanism for addressing structural constraints, enhancing care delivery, and advancing a more efficient, responsive, and equitable Medicaid healthcare system nationwide impact.

title: Temporal Random Forest to Predict Dengue Hotspots in 11 Districts of Bangladesh Using Meteorological Data
authors: Samia Jahan, Nazneen Akhter, Afif Daiyan Laskar, Md. Sazzadur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Dengue fever poses a significant and recurring public health threat in Bangladesh, with its transmission dynamics being heavily influenced by meteorological conditions. The effective prediction of dengue hotspots is critical for enabling timely public health interventions. This study presents a lightweight machine learning framework to predict monthly dengue patient counts, which is a key indicator for identifying potential hotspots. In this research, a Random Forest Regressor model is developed to train on historical meteorological data including rainfall, temperature, and humidity and dengue patient records from 2011 to 2021. This approach presents a temporal feature engineering strategy that structures the data to explicitly teach the model long-term seasonal patterns by using the same month from previous years as input features. On a hold-out test set for the year 2020, this model has achieved a notable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> score of 0.335 and an accuracy of 96.97% for predictions within a 2 patient tolerance. The proposed model significantly has outperformed several alternative architectures, including those based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). The results demonstrate that this approach can serve as a valuable and reliable tool for public health authorities to proactively identify high-risk areas, facilitating efficient resource allocation and targeted vector control measures.

title: Comprehensive Analysis of Computational-Based Analytical Approaches for Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia Detection and Classification
authors: Veena M. Naik, Ashwinkumar Motagi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) is unique case of cancer characterized by abnormal production of white blood cells mainly in children as well as it is also reported among adults too. Existing system has witnessed various form of screening approaches where varied form of methodologies have been presented towards diagnosis of ALL. However, inspite of massive number of research archives presenting diagnostic solution of ALL, the problem has not been solved in practical world. On the other side, it is also noted that there is an increasing adoption of computational-based analytical approaches which mainly uses radiological images for facilitating detection and classification of ALL. However, it is still unclear about the current state of effectiveness of such approaches. Hence, this manuscript contributes towards this gap by reviewing the current computational-based analytical approaches where learning-based approaches are found to be quite dominant and witnessed increased pace of research adoption. The novelty of this review is its compact and crisp investigational insight towards guaging the effectiveness of recent study approaches followed by updated highlights of research trends and research gap to showcase higher opportunity of improvement in this study domain.

title: Interpretable Ensemble Framework for Fake News Detection
authors: Isma Binte Shafik, Salsabil Binte Kashem, Nahin Binte Azad, Taspia Binta Yousuf, Syeda Tabassum Nadia, Fatema Binte Emran, Rashedul Arefin Ifty
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of misinformation on digital platforms has intensified the demand for fake news detection systems that are accurate, interpretable, and efficient. This study presents a lightweight ensemble framework that integrates handcrafted textual features with layered fusion strategies. Structural, stylistic, semantic, and readability-based features-such as punctuation frequency, capitalization ratio, and title-body similarity-were extracted and represented numerically. These features were used to train diverse classifiers, including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Extra Trees, XGBoost, and LightGBM. A two-level ensemble was designed: first, base models were combined through soft voting; second, their outputs were stacked using Logistic Regression as a meta-learner. Experiments on the “Fake and Real News” benchmark dataset achieved 91% accuracy and an AUC of 0.968, surpassing the performance of individual classifiers and deep learning baselines. The proposed approach offers a practical and interpretable solution for realtime fake news detection where transparency and computational efficiency are essential.

title: Demonstration of High Voltage (>1.7kV) GaN Negative-Capacitance Ferroelectric HEMTs Using HZO Gate Stack Achieving 47.89 mV/dec SS and Reduced ON-Resistance
authors: Thi My Hanh Dao, Yu-An Chen, Hsien-Yang Liu, Tian-Li Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: In this work, we demonstrate the first kilovolt-class GaN negative-capacitance ferroelectric HEMTs (NC-FeHEMTs) using an Hf<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>Zr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> (HZO) gate stack, and systematically clarify the critical role of atomic-scale interfacial-layer (IL) engineering in enabling stabilized NC operation for power devices. By comparing a conventional MIS-HEMT (Sample A), an HZO device with a 1-nm Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> IL (Sample B), and an optimized HZO device employing a single-cycle (~0.1 nm) Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> IL (Sample C), we show that aggressive IL scaling effectively suppresses depolarization fields and strengthens polarization coupling to the GaN channel. Structural analyses confirm a well-defined TiN/HZO/Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> stack with mixed orthorhombic/tetragonal HZO phases, while electrical characterization reveals that only the optimized device (Sample C) exhibits near frequency-independent C–V characteristics and negligible transfer hysteresis, evidencing hysteresis-free stabilized NC operation rather than trap-assisted effects. As a result, the optimized NC-FeHEMT achieves a minimum subthreshold swing (SS) of 47.89 mV/dec, a 47% reduction in specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON,sp</inf> = 3.14 mΩ•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), and a high OFF-state breakdown voltage exceeding 1.7 kV at room temperature. Furthermore, a blocking capability above 1.2 kV is preserved up to 200 °C. In sum, GaN NC-FeHEMTs with a single-cycle (~0.1 nm) Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> IL is promising for high voltage power switching applications.

authors: Seungheon Lee, Wonhyuk Yang, Seonyeong Heo, Gwangsun Kim

title: Artificial Neural Network Model for Predicting OAM Mode Transmission Characteristics in Multicore Fiber
authors: Rosni Sayed, Abdul Khaleque, Sumaya Akter, Kumary Sumi Rani Shaha, Sadman Sakib, Shaila Alam Urmi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This work presents a deep learning-assisted framework for the optical performance prediction of a six-core orbital angular momentum (OAM) mode supporting multicore fiber (MCF). An artificial neural network (ANN) model is developed and configured to map structural inputs to optical output characteristics, including supported mode count, effective refractive index, refractive index difference, chromatic dispersion, and crosstalk. The model achieves a low prediction error (mean square error = <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.11 \times 10^{-4}$</tex>), demonstrating strong agreement with finite element method (FEM) simulation results while requiring only a fraction of the computational cost. Compared to conventional COMSOL Multiphysics simulations, the proposed model significantly accelerates evaluation, making it suitable for rapid parameter exploration, optimization, and inverse fiber design. The demonstrated approach highlights the potential of deep learning as a fast, accurate, and resource-efficient tool for next-generation OAM mode supporting fiber development.
